title: Adaptive Graph Echo State Network Based on Island Transpeciation Co-Evolution
authors: Zhaozhao Zhang, Zhijun Deng, Yingqin Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address concept drift caused by changes in graph topology and node attributes, this paper proposes an adaptive Graph Echo State Network (Is-GESN), integrating island migration co-evolution into GESN design. Is-GESN constructs a dynamic multi-level system with multiple parallel optimization “islands”, achieving global search in the hyperparameter space via periodic “island transpeciation” to adaptively match the optimal architecture. Experiments on dynamic graph infectious disease models show that Is-GESN outperforms conventional networks in stability and generalization; in time window experiments simulating concept drift, it exhibits excellent temporal adaptability and robustness.

title: A Control Strategy for Non Capacitor Voltage Sensor of Offshore DC Transformer
authors: Kanghang Lv, Lijun Zhang, Jiajun Qin, Yang Fu, Jinbin Zhao, Zhenkun Li
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: DC transformers, as key equipment for collection and transmission, usually adopt an isolated topology structure. However, the intermediate frequency isolation transformer is constrained by magnetic materials and cannot achieve high power, making it difficult to meet the demand for large-scale offshore wind power transmission. Therefore, a non isolated DC transformer topology is proposed to cope with high-power scenarios. The T-type DC transformer is a new type of DC transformer structure with development potential in non isolated topology structures. This structure adopts a modular design approach, which is easy to expand and maintain. This article proposes a capacitor voltage self balancing strategy for a new type of T-type DC transformer. This strategy does not require a capacitor voltage sensor and is based on matrix theory. By presetting a switch matrix, the capacitor voltage is autonomously balanced, reducing the control complexity of the T-type DC transformer. Finally, the feasibility and effectiveness of the strategy were verified by establishing a simulation model of a T-shaped DC transformer.

title: Vehicle Detection and Traffic Counting Alarm System Based on Improved YOLOv8
authors: Jia Tian, YuanFeng Guo, JunHuai Zeng, YingLu Chen, YongJie Peng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)

title: Optimization of Mixed Vehicle Platoons Using Bilateral-Constrained Model Predictive Control
authors: Longqing Xia, Rui Zhang, Peng Xia, Haixing Du
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address high energy consumption and poor dynamic coordination in mixed vehicle platoons, this paper proposes an energy-efficient control strategy based on bilateralconstrained Model Predictive Control (MPC). An energy proxy term is embedded in the longitudinal platoon control framework to balance control smoothness and energy usage, and a bilateral speed constraint is introduced to enhance intra-platoon speed coherence. A five-vehicle mixed platoon is tested on a TruckSim/Simulink co-simulation platform under two configurations with the human-driven vehicle (HDV) located at the front and in the middle. Simulation results show that the proposed controller reduces overall energy consumption while preserving platoon stability, improving both energy-saving performance and dynamic coordination.

title: Research on Large Language Model Text Source Detection Based on Domain Adaptive Fine-Tuning
authors: Zhe Nan, YanFeng Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of the text generation capabilities of large language models, machine-generated text has found widespread application across various industries. However, this proliferation has also introduced risks associated with the misuse of such text. Consequently, effectively determining the origin of such text has become a significant research topic. However, existing AI text detectors often struggle to achieve high accuracy rates in practical scenarios involving machine-generated text from specific domains and diverse models. This paper uses the Machine Generated Text Detection in the Wild (MAGE) detection platform and its pre-trained language model detector. It conducts domain-specific fine-tuning and experimental validation across three specialised domains (Comment, Q&A and News texts) and a mixed Multi-domains setting. The experimental results demonstrate that, following fine-tuning using domain-specific custom datasets, the detector achieves significantly improved accuracy and other metrics across all three domains and the mixed domain.

title: Research on Aligning Large Models with Human Values Using Lora Fine-Tuning
authors: YouQi Wang, Ning Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the widespread application of Large Language Models (LLMs) in various fields, ensuring their outputs align with societal moral norms has become a critical AI safety issue. To address this, this paper proposes an efficient value alignment method based on LoRA fine-tuning of the Llama3-8B model. Using a dataset of 10,000 value-aligned examples, we conducted fine-tuning and evaluated the model across four key dimensions. Experimental results demonstrate that this approach significantly enhances the model's ethical alignment and safety: safety improved by approximately 11.48%, the alignment score increased by 6.24%, and the refusal rate decreased by 10.6%. This indicates a strengthened ability to identify and reject high-risk requests, offering a promising alternative that reduces the reliance on human annotation inherent in RLHF.

title: A Frequency-Shift Real-Time Slow-Transient Compensation Method Based on Parallel Digital Frequency Conversion Processing
authors: Jia Zheng, Linyu Guo, Zhengguo Wu, Yin Kuang, Xun Han, Xin Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: In temperature-sensitive, non-uniform clock satellite-to-ground relay communication systems, when the frequency error of the onboard digital relay equipment's frequency source is significant, this paper investigates a real-time gradual frequency shift compensation method based on parallel digital frequency conversion processing. This method ensures the relay signal frequency meets the demodulation frequency range and Doppler shift threshold of downstream receiving equipment while reducing the system bit error rate. This method addresses real-time gradual frequency compensation for source-generated signals within ultra-wideband digital relay channels, enhancing output signal frequency accuracy, reducing output signal Doppler shift rates, and significantly lowering system communication bit error rates. Verification was conducted on a V7 series FPGA test platform. The frequency deviation of the relay signal across the full operating temperature range was reduced from ±50KHz to ±1KHz, while the output signal's Doppler shift rate decreased from 1.5KHz to 363.8Hz.

title: MAHDDPG-Based Optimisation of Vehicle-UAV Edge Task Offloading
authors: Longfei Huang, Yingying Yu, Xi Huang, Hongliang Ma, Wenxun Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The growing need for low-latency computing in intelligent transportation systems has boosted the development of vehicular edge computing technology. However, traditional offloading strategies struggle to efficiently coordinate heterogeneous nodes due to the limited resources of individual vehicles in dynamic vehicle-to-everything (V2X) scenarios. To address this challenge, this study proposes an innovative multi-agent hybrid deep deterministic policy gradient (MAHDDPG) algorithm to optimise edge task offloading for vehicle-drone collaboration, which fully considers the idle resources of both vehicles and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). In the algorithm, each task vehicle is modelled as an agent, with its hybrid action space designed to encompass both discrete and continuous actions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy reduces the average task processing time of the system by 39 %, which satisfies the requirements of delay-sensitive applications.

title: Experimental Study of Capacitive Distance Sensors with Parallel Coplanar Electrodes for Control Airgap in Capsule Hydrogenerator
authors: Ievgen Zaitsev, Viktoriia Bereznychenko, Anatolii Levytskyi, Sergey Zakusilo, Vadim Rassovskyi
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: In this paper, the design, circuitry, technological aspects, and experimental study of a capacitive sensor for measuring the air gap between the stator core bore and the rotor poles in a capsule hydrogenerator are discussed, using a system of parallel coplanar strip electrodes. The electrical capacitance between the high-potential and low-potential electrode systems changes as a function of the distance between the common plane of the sensor electrodes and the rotor poles, which indicates the air gap size. The paper describes a method for experimentally determining the sensor's conversion function, as well as the equipment and measuring instruments used. The research methodology simulates changes air gap value through the use of a specialized device that facilitates the parallel plane movement of a flat, grounded plate, imitating the rotor pole surface in the machine. The plate moves along the device carriage in relation to the sensor electrodes' common surface, which is fixed on the device. The movement and its measurement are performed by a micrometer head. The experimentally determined transformation function is presented in both tabular and graphical formats. A comparison between the experimental and theoretical results confirmed the accuracy of the proposed sensor construction principles. The application of the sensor allows for more precise measurement of air gap in a hydrogenerator, which in turn improves the evaluation of the machine's current technical state.

title: A YOLO-Based Visual SLAM Algorithm for Dynamic Indoor Environments
authors: Zhou Shan, Shuangfeng Wei, Ming Guo
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: To address the degradation of visual simultaneous localization and mapping (VSLAM) pose estimation accuracy caused by extensive moving objects and the resulting contamination of feature points in indoor dynamic environments, this study proposes a dynamic feature point rejection strategy that integrates a You Only Look Once (YOLO) family detector, YOLO11, with the Lucas–Kanade (LK) optical flow method within the ORB_SLAM3 framework. First, YOLO11 is employed to detect potentially dynamic targets such as pedestrians in the image, and ORB features extracted within the detection bounding boxes are labeled as high-risk dynamic points. Subsequently, based on pyramidal LK optical flow, the motion consistency of feature points is analyzed across multiple frames, and a secondary discrimination is performed on features located at the edges and in the vicinity of the detection boxes. In this way, feature points that conform to a static motion model are maximally preserved, thereby alleviating the excessive removal and misclassification of static points caused by oversized detection boxes. On this basis, a new map is reconstructed using the filtered static feature points, and the pose estimation process is further refined. Experiments on multiple indoor dynamic scenarios demonstrate that, compared with the original ORB_SLAM3, the proposed method effectively suppresses dynamic interference, enhances the stability of feature points, and significantly improves pose estimation accuracy and trajectory continuity, thus providing a valuable reference for robust VSLAM applications in dynamic environments.

title: A Chatter-Free Predefined-Time Spacecraft Attitude Control Method Based on Accurate Disturbance Observer
authors: Ruidong Xie, Xiangkun Fu, Yingmin Yi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In this paper, a chatter-free predefined-time spacecraft attitude control method based on an accurate disturbance observer is proposed. It aims to solve the chattering issue in sliding mode control and the attitude control problem with uncertain inertia of spacecraft, where the uncertainties in the dynamic model of the spacecraft attitude system are treated as external disturbances. The control scheme consists of a predefined-time disturbance observer and a two-stage predefined-time sliding mode controller. The former can accurately observe the disturbances of the spacecraft system within the first stage of predefined time, while the latter ensures that the system states remain bounded within the first stage and guarantees system stabilization within the second stage. Finally, the effectiveness of the designed controller is demonstrated through simulation experiments.

title: A Review of Bio-Behavior-Inspired Coordination and Control in Unmanned Swarms
authors: Qing Lan, Hao Yuan, Qinghua Hao, Xing Meng, Yu Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper presents a review of bio-inspired cooperative control in unmanned swarm systems. Starting from the key behavioral characteristics of biological collectives, the study explores their underlying scientific principles and the corresponding mappings to unmanned system coordination mechanisms. By examining the self-organization, distributed intelligence, and adaptive behaviors observed in bird flocks, fish schools, and ant colonies, the paper elucidates the theoretical foundations and algorithmic implementations of representative bio-inspired models, including the Vicsek model, Boids model, Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), Fish School Search (FSS), and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) Algorithm. Furthermore, typical applications of bio-inspired unmanned swarms in military penetration, agricultural monitoring, and disaster rescue are summarized. Finally, emerging research trends and development prospects are discussed, providing theoretical insights and practical guidance for advancing cooperative behavior modeling and control in unmanned swarm systems. [1]–[4]

title: A Hybrid Ant Colony Optimization Algorithm for Single Batch Processing Machine Scheduling Problem with Two-Dimensional Job Sizes
authors: Xiaoyu Huang, Feng He, Maoyan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper considers a single batch processing machine scheduling problem with non-identical two-dimensional job sizes. The objective is to assign jobs to batches considering the machine capacity constraint such that the makespan is minimized. A mixed-integer programming model is established to minimize makespan, for which a Hybrid Ant Colony Optimization (HACO) algorithm is proposed. HACO utilizes wasted volume and EMR for effective packing and assignment. Experimental results demonstrate that our HACO algorithm outperforms four other classical heuristics in achieving a lower makespan. Notably, HACO demonstrates superior robustness (smaller error bars) in scenarios with smaller job sets and larger machines, though variability increases with more jobs and smaller machines.

title: Sensor Fault Diagnosis for Lithium-Ion Battery Management System Based on Capacity Estimation
authors: Yu Ma, Tinglong Pan, Dezhi Xu, Weilin Yang, Jinlei Pei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Capacity estimation, an integral component of battery management system (BMS), is intricately linked with state of charge (SOC) and state of energy (SOE) estimation. And their precise estimations are not possible without voltage and current measurements. This paper proposes an extended Kalman filtering (EKF) algorithm improved by considering aging factors. To improve modeling accuracy and state estimation, this study enhances the traditional second-order RC equivalent circuit model by integrating a hysteresis module, thereby refining parameter identification. Additionally, the extended Kalman filter algorithm is optimized through capacity calibration and a forgetting factor to account for battery aging effects on both model parameters and actual capacity. Subsequently, the estimated capacity is obtained jointly with the estimation of SOE to identify battery sensor faults. Experimental simulation results demonstrate that the method has commendable stability, estimation accuracy and robustness, and can effectively detect voltage and current sensor faults.

title: Vibration Trend Prediction Method-Based CNN-Transformer Hybrid Model for Transmission Systems
authors: Xintong Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: As the core component for power transfer, the transmission system is prone to cascading failures caused by vibration-related faults, which seriously endangers flight safety. This paper conducts research on vibration prediction methods for the transmission system. A combined method is proposed, which consists of a CEEMDAN feature extraction, signal timefrequency domain decomposition, and a CNN-Transformer prediction model. This method can effectively realize the feature extraction and accurate prediction of the vibration signals of the transmission system. Through simulation, the effectiveness of the proposed vibration prediction method is verified.

title: TMFN: A Triple-Branch Attention Network for Multi-Modal Remote Sensing Classification
authors: Yong Mei, Xiangsuo Fan, Jinlong Fan, Qi Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Multimodal remote sensing image classification is widely used in land refinement classification, but cross modal feature fusion faces challenges. To address this issue, this paper proposes a three branch attention fusion network (TMFN) for HSI and LiDAR/SAR images. The network first achieves early information exchange through feature extraction branches and lightweight fusion modules, while maintaining model lightweighting while ensuring complementary spectral spatial features; Obtain local and global spatial dependencies through the backbone network of parallel Transformer and multi-scale dual attention convolution module; Introducing an adaptive SE attention fusion module when integrating multimodal features to achieve cross modal semantic deep level feature fusion. Our model achieved overall classification accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$88.99 \%, 99.63 \%$</tex>, and 74.89 % on three typical open-source datasets, Muufl, Trento, and Berlin, respectively, outperforming representative algorithms in recent years. The ablation experiment further validated the effectiveness of each module. This method not only improves classification accuracy but also considers model running efficiency and scalability, providing a new solution for multi-source remote sensing image fusion.

title: Mamba-Lip: Revolutionizing Lip Reading Generation with Selective State Space Models
authors: Xu Wang, Yunpeng Jiao, Jiayi He, Yaxiong Wang, Lechao Cheng, Shengeng Tang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Lip reading generation, which aims to synthesize realistic facial landmark sequences from textual input, represents a crucial task in visual language processing. While existing methods predominantly rely on Transformer or RNN architectures, they often struggle with capturing long-range dependencies in sequential data and face efficiency challenges when processing lengthy sequences. In this paper, we propose Mamba-Lip, a novel framework that revolutionizes lip reading generation through selective state space models (SSMs). Our approach features two key components: (1) Mamba-Enc, a text encoder that leverages selective SSMs to effectively capture linguistic structures and long-range contextual dependencies; (2) Mamba-Dec, an autoregressive decoder that generates fine-grained facial landmark sequences through causal state space modeling combined with cross-modal attention. The selective mechanism enables our model to dynamically focus on relevant linguistic contexts while efficiently processing long sequences. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Mamba-Lip achieves state-of-the-art performance in both generation accuracy and temporal coherence, while exhibiting superior efficiency compared to Transformer-based counterparts.

title: Attention-Guided Wavelet Aware Few-Shot Back Recognition
authors: Yuhao Hu, Lina Zhang, Shanshan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of intelligent surveillance and security systems, pedestrian re-identification has gained increasing significance. However, existing datasets exhibit notable limitations in back-view recognition scenarios, particularly under few-shot conditions, where models struggle to effectively capture key multi-scale structural features in back-view images. Current methods suffer from intermixed frequency-domain features and a lack of physically interpretable attention mechanisms, leading to suboptimal robustness in complex scenarios. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel few-shot backview recognition framework that integrates wavelet transforms and heatmap-guided attention mechanisms. The framework employs multi-level wavelet decomposition to extract frequency-domain features and innovatively fuses Grad-CAM heatmaps with sub-band energy distributions to dynamically focus on discriminative regions. To advance research in this domain, we construct a specialized dataset for multi-scenario few-shot back-view recognition, covering cross-view, occlusion, and varying illumination conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves classification accuracies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$56.49 \%, 69.43 \%$</tex>, and 73.39 % under 1-shot, 5-shot, and 10-shot settings, respectively, outperforming state-of-the-art fewshot learning approaches. These results validate the framework's effectiveness and superiority, providing an efficient solution for security systems.

title: A Geometric Unwrapping-Based Quantitative Analysis Method for CT Image Defect Evaluation
authors: Lian Changxin, Li Liangliang, Wang Peng, He Chuchao, Di Ruohai
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: The structural integrity of solid rocket propellant grains directly affects the reliability and performance of the engine. Traditional CT inspection methods rely on two-dimensional slice observation, which struggles to accurately depict the morphology of defects extending along the circumferential direction. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a CT image unfolding method based on geometric mapping, unfolding the cross-sectional image in cylindrical coordinates into a Cartesian coordinate representation. This method combines automatic grayscale optimization with polar-to-Cartesian coordinate transformation to achieve adaptive enhancement of image contrast and precise preservation of structural information, allowing for the continuous presentation of defect distribution in both the circumferential and radial directions. Experimental results show that this method can effectively eliminate the geometric distortion problem in direct measurement of the original CT image, enabling quantitative measurement of defects. The circumferential length of porosity defects is $0.6-1.4 \mathrm{~mm}$, and the radial length is $1.6-6.0 \mathrm{~mm}$; the circumferential distribution length of debonding defects can reach 9.1 mm. Research indicates that image unfolding is a key technology for achieving accurate defect quantification and structural integrity assessment, providing reliable technical support for non-destructive testing of solid propellants.

title: Robust Edge Segmentation via Threshold-Aware Attention and Dynamic Feature Selection
authors: Zhiyang Guo, Jiejia Wang, Baigan Zhao, Shun Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Accurate boundary segmentation of fruits is essential for robotic harvesting in smart agriculture, yet remains challenging due to scale variation among fruits and complex field conditions involving occlusions and overlapping boundaries. To address these challenges, we propose Agro-EdgeNet, a novel boundary attention network that integrates three key components: an Adaptive Context-Aware (ACA) module to dynamically adjust receptive fields and capture multi-scale features, a Boundary Attention (BAP) module to focus on edge-related information and preserve structural details, and a Multi-level Complementary Refinement module to iteratively fuse region and boundary features. Extensive experiments on strawberry and tomato datasets demonstrate that our proposed Agro-EdgeNet achieves superior performance compared to other recent state-of-the-art methods, attaining a remarkable accuracy of 92.86% and recall of 90.61%.

title: Emergency Control Method for Energy Storage Participation in Frequency Under Large-Scale Wind Energy
authors: Mengxuan Shi, Ling Zhu, Dejun Shao, Yu Wang, Youping Xu, Hongyang Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: This study proposes an emergency active power control strategy involving energy storage systems to address the potential occurrence of large-scale wind power under penetration caused by power grid faults. Firstly, the short-term impact effect of wind power low penetration on system frequency was analyzed, and the dynamic variation characteristics of frequency were considered. Based on energy storage as a regulation method, this article establishes an optimization model with the goal of minimizing the lowest point of the inertia center frequency. This model takes the emergency control quantities of energy storage stations at different nodes as optimization variables and solves the optimal allocation scheme through an improved particle swarm algorithm. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method was verified through IEEE-39 node system simulation.

title: Research on the Evaluation Method of System-Level Electromagnetic Pulse Protection Effectiveness
authors: Keyu Guo, Wei He, Yuebo Li, Shuang Deng, Jiuliang Xiong, Jie Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: To investigate the evaluation methods for system-level electromagnetic pulse (EMP) protection effectiveness, a specific water supply and drainage system was taken as the study object. An evaluation model for EMP protection effectiveness was established, and the corresponding evaluation results were calculated. These results were then validated through experimental testing. The findings indicate that the maximum deviation between the calculated and experimentally tested EMP protection effectiveness of the water supply and drainage system is 14.77%, with a minimum deviation of 0%. This demonstrates that the evaluation method for EMP protection effectiveness is accurate and reliable.

title: Smooth Switching Between Grid-Connected and Islanded Modes of Operation
authors: Boxuan Si, Xingwu Yang, Ziqi Wang, Yang Fu, Jiaxuan Yan, Zhengxiong Deng
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: In the process of unplanned islanding transition for a master-slave grid-connected microgrid inverter, delays in islanding detection and static transfer switch disconnection lead to an intermediate state where the inverter remains in current source control mode, resulting in voltage and frequency instability. To address this issue, a control strategy suitable for seamless grid-connected and islanding transitions is proposed. By introducing the islanding control structure in advance during the intermediate state, the stability of load voltage and frequency is maintained. This method effectively suppresses frequency drop and voltage deviation during the mode transition. A hybrid controller is designed based on the proposed strategy, achieving smooth switching between gridconnected and islanding operations. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed control strategy exhibits excellent dynamic performance under unplanned islanding conditions, verifying its feasibility and effectiveness.

title: Analysis and Evaluation of the Internal Electromagnetic Field of Unmanned Vehicle
authors: Rongxia Duan, Zhuoqiao Ji, Nan Li, Tingwei Jia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: This paper focuses on the electromagnetic field inside the intelligent unmanned vehicle, with the core of multi-condition tests such as starting, stable running, and braking state, and uses high-precision instruments to measure the electromagnetic field at key parts in the vehicle. Due to the difference in functions and the layout of electronic equipment, the magnetic induction intensity also has obvious distinctions. Quantitative analysis and evaluation are made based on the test data, which provides strong theoretical basis and decision-making reference for electromagnetic compatibility optimization in the vehicle design stage and quality control in the production process.

title: Axial Vibration Model Accounting for Cumulative Effects
authors: Guangwei Liu, Zhen Hao, Zhaoyang Pan, Huan Li, Deliang Cheng, Fan Zhang, Guodong Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: This paper investigates the cumulative effects of short-circuit impacts on power transformer windings, with particular focus on axial vibration characteristics. Through cyclic loading tests on insulating pressboards, we demonstrate that plastic deformation accumulates with increasing stress cycles. The stress-strain curves exhibit stabilization after approximately 100 loading cycles, with elastic modulus increasing by approximately 15-20% due to material compaction. We establish an axial vibration model incorporating cumulative effects, revealing a 4.1% decrease in natural frequencies while mode shapes remain largely unchanged. The proposed model enables iterative assessment of transformer short-circuit withstand capacity throughout its operational life by accounting for the evolution of clamping force and spacer stiffness. Experimental results and numerical simulations confirm that electromagnetic force accumulation dominates the cumulative effects, while thermal accumulation remains negligible due to short circuit durations. This research provides a foundation for evaluating transformer reliability under repeated short-circuit conditions.

title: Sensitivity Analysis and Intelligent Optimization Collaborative Photovoltaic Power Generation Parameter Identification Method
authors: Xutao Li, Yong Ren, Lei Zhou, Hongqiang Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: This paper proposed the improved parameter identification method for the transient model of photovoltaic generators. Firstly, based on the calculation method of parameter trajectory sensitivity, analyze the impact of different disturbances (perigee fault, apogee fault, illumination disturbance) and different observations (active power, reactive power) on the observability of the system, and select the system's observations based on this. Built a joint simulation experimental platform between Matlab and PSASP, configured the interface, and implemented loop calling between the two for joint simulation. Research on the optimization process and principles of particle swarm optimization and genetic algorithm, as well as their application in parameter identification of photovoltaic power generation system models. We built a PSASP three machine nine node time-domain simulation model to verify the effectiveness of the identification method, and compared and analyzed the identification accuracy and error based on different optimization algorithms according to the simulation data of the example. Provide accurate model parameters for the analysis of the impact of photovoltaic power generation integration on the stability of synchronous units in the following text.

title: Sensor for Detecting Quasi-Elastic Bodies in a Visco-Plastic Medium
authors: Olexandr Salenko, Viktor Cherniak, Vadym Orel, Bohdan Salenko, Yurii Danylchenko, Volodymyr Pavlenko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: A special sensor for diagnosing the presence of solid quasi-elastic bodies in a viscous medium is under consideration, which is a mechanical elastic flexible rod that fits into a protective soft tube and is connected to a hermetic chamber, one of the walls of which is a membrane capable of converting the vibrations of the rod into sound waves that, propagating in the camera, are recorded by the microphone. The latter is connected to sound frequency filters (up to 15 kHz) through an amplifying link, which allows you to isolate a useful signal and record the contact of the rod with a foreign body during its movement in a visco-plastic environment. In addition, the specified sensor can be equipped with a mechanical vibration exciter, which, working during contact with a foreign quasi-elastic body, generates signals that can indirectly allow determining the approximate dimensions of the body, its type (according to dynamic characteristics). The proposed sensor can be used in medical practice, in disaster medicine, when the speed of detection of a patient's damage by shrapnel is important, and there are no accepted means of diagnosis. Since a wound channel is used, no additional traumatization of the patient occurs.

title: An Induction Generator Based Electrical Generator Set for Dual DC Arc Welding and AC Electric Power Supply
authors: Leonid Mazurenko, Olexander Dzhura, Andriy Kotsiuruba, Maksym Shykhnenko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: In this paper, a schematic of a welder genset using squirrel cage induction generator shunted by compensating capacitors and voltage source converter is considered. The generator supplies electrical power to inverter welding machines. Both dc-link and AC side voltage magnitudes are controlled by controllers of the voltage source converter. A schematic solution of an inverter welding machine with boost voltage mode is proposed for quick arc ignition. Two vector control algorithms for voltage control are considered. The first algorithm is based on hysteresis current control and the other is developed using space vector pulse width modulation technique. Simulation of the genset under fixed speed and variable dc-link load has been performed and discussed. Voltage waveform distortion of the generator controlled according to hysteresis current control algorithm was compared with voltage waveform distortion of the generator with space vector pulse width modulation control. The further direction of research has been formulated.

title: Investigating the Impact of AI-Powered Tools on Academic Activities: A Quantitative Study of University Students in Jakarta and Tangerang
authors: Daven Karim, Louis Aaron Sumendap, Natalia Limantara
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: Advances in artificial intelligence (AI) technology have had profound effects on numerous sectors, particularly higher learning. With the rise of ChatGPT and other AI software, their integration into student learning has been on the increase. Though AI research has come very far globally, such empirical evidence within the Indonesian setting remains limited. This study aims to investigate the drivers that trigger and influence the adoption and appropriation of AI-based tools by university students in Jakarta and Tangerang using an extended version of the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT) model. Data were collected from 202 respondents and analyzed using the Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) method. The findings state performance expectancy, effort expectancy, social influence, facilitating conditions, service reliability, and student satisfaction as factors having significant impacts on behavioral intention, while behavioral intention significantly predicts AI appropriation in learning activities. The model is determined to have good explanatory power, hence making theoretical contributions towards AI technology adoption studies and offering practical implications for instructors, policymakers, and developers of AI to encourage effective and sustainable AI adoption in higher institutions of learning.

title: An Optimized Fourier Neural Operator for the 2D Fractional Poisson Equation
authors: Wei Xiao, Shanshan Wang, Lexiang Yang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Recent advances in operator learning have opened new possibilities for solving partial differential equations (PDEs) directly in function space, bypassing the need for mesh-based numerical solvers. In this work, we introduce an Optimized Fourier Neural Operator (O-FNO), which integrates improvements such as residual connections and enhanced spectral resolution to improve convergence stability, training efficiency, and predictive accuracy in solving the 2D fractional Poisson equation with fractional order. Through a systematic comparison against DeepONet and the Base-FNO, we highlight the significant improvements of O-FNO in both stability and accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed O-FNO achieves a test relative accuracy of over 98%, significantly outperforming both the Base FNO and DeepONet. These findings confirm the effectiveness of the Fourier-based operator formulation for non-local PDEs and highlight the potential of O-FNO as a robust and efficient surrogate solver for fractional-order problems.

title: MCP-E: Improving Tool Execution and User Experience in Model Context Protocol Systems
authors: Feng Zhang, Ying Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Model Context Protocol (MCP) has emerged as a foundational framework for managing tool invocation and contextual interactions in AI systems. However, traditional MCP implementations suffer from performance bottlenecks, limited support for long-running tasks, and rigid user input mechanisms. In this paper, we present MCP-E (Model Context Protocol-Enhanced), an enhanced protocol architecture that addresses these limitations through three key innovations. First, MCP-E introduces a local tool mounting mechanism that bypasses JSON-based remote calls, significantly improving invocation latency and throughput. Second, it adopts an asynchronous task submission model, enabling users to initiate long-duration tasks and query their progress via task identifiers, thereby improving responsiveness. Third, MCP-E integrates UI-aware input controls that allow large models to trigger structured widgets based on input context, enhancing user experience and reducing input friction. Results from pytest-benchmark and other evaluation methods, demonstrate that MCP-E outperforms traditional MCP across multiple dimensions, including execution speed, task management efficiency, and interaction quality.

title: Surface defect detection algorithm of aero-engine blade based on improved YOLO11n model
authors: Jiayi Ren, Xinwei Wang, Li Mu, Wenlong Li
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Aero-engine blades inevitably develop surface defects such as cracks, corrosion, and wear during service, which requires efficient and accurate automated detection methods. To address the limitations of existing approaches that struggle to balance accuracy and speed, this paper proposes an improved single-stage object detection algorithm based on You Only Look Once version 11n (YOLOv11n). The network integrates C3k2_ConvFormer for fine-grained feature extraction, a bidirectional weighted feature pyramid network (BiFPN) enhanced with an edge-guided attention module (EGAM) for multi-scale feature fusion, and Scylla-IoU (SIoU) loss for faster convergence and more precise localization. Experimental results on a public aero-engine blade defect dataset demonstrate consistent improvements over the baseline YOLOv11n, with mean Average Precision increased by 5%, Precision improved by 3.3%, and Recall enhanced by 3.7%, while maintaining real-time inference speed. Compared with existing methods, the proposed model achieves a better trade-off between accuracy and efficiency in detecting small and lowcontrast defects. In addition, cross-dataset testing confirms that the model exhibits a certain degree of generalization ability. This work provides a practical and effective solution for safe and efficient aero-engine blade inspection and offers methodological insights for the application of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) in manufacturing, particularly in industrial scenarios involving weak-feature defect detection.

title: Performance Comparison Analysis of Conventional and Advanced Machine Learning Algorithms for APT Malware Detection
authors: Alifya Kayla Shafa Susanto, Ilham Achmad Fiqrin, Ravi Kumar Patchmuthu, Sharul Tazrajiman Bin Haji Tajuddin, Darwis Setiawan Waruwu, Ofelius Laia
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) malware can remain undetected for extended periods and inflict substantial damage. This research compares the performance of conventional machine learning (ML) with that of advanced ML algorithms, which include ensemble methods for detecting APT malware. This study utilizes three different datasets: DAPT2020, CIC-IDS 2018, and Unraveled. Further, CatBoost was selected for feature extraction and tested using conventional ML models: Random Forest (RF), Decision Tree (DT), Naïve Bayes (NB), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Logistic Regression (LR), and advanced ensemble models: XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, and Gradient Boosting to find the best model. All models’ performance is evaluated using key metrics, including accuracy, recall, precision, and F1-score, specifically for APT detection. LightGBM performs best on all datasets, with accuracy, recall, and F1-score values of $98.96 \%, 99.21 \%$, and $98.27 \%$ on DAPT2020; 98.82%, 95.51%, and 93.83% on Unraveled; and $98.33 \%, 92.37 \%$, and $92.57 \%$ on CIC-IDS2018, respectively. This shows that LightGBM is a highly effective model for detecting APT malware.

title: Characterization of CNT Schottky Diode and Model-based RF Mixer Design
authors: Zeyu Ma, Yanwen Wu, Simin He, Defu Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: This paper presents the fabrication and characterization of a carbon nanotube Schottky diode and its use in model-based radio frequency mixer design. A CNT Schottky diode with a 120 nm channel length was designed, fabricated, and measured, demonstrating good agreement with the model. The diode exhibited a maximum responsivity of 2.51 mA/W and a cutoff frequency exceeding 200 GHz. Direct current and S-parameter measurements of the diode were used to extract key parameters for an equivalent-circuit model, which was fitted to the measured data and validated up to 40 GHz. The achieved performance highlights the potential of CNT technology for high-frequency applications. With a 9 dBm local oscillator drive, the single-diode RF mixer simulation without impedance matching networks achieved 21 dB conversion loss at 33 GHz and a 1 dB compression point of 5 dBm. The results demonstrate that measurement-based CNT Schottky diode models can effectively guide the design of radio frequency mixers using emerging low-dimensional semiconductor technologies. This work represents one of the first functional demonstrations of a measurement-based CNT Schottky diode mixer in the Ka-band, establishing a benchmark for future radio frequency circuits.

title: Identification of Topic Areas by Scientific Publication Using K-Means on LDA
authors: Mada Rekadarma Septianda, Widodo, Murien Nugraheni
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of mapping diverse research themes in faculty publications from computer-related departments at the State University of Jakarta, where many publications do not explicitly state their thematic area. To overcome this, Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) was applied for topic modeling and K-Means clustering was used to group hidden topics into coherent research fields. A dataset of 130 Scopus-indexed publications was processed through text preprocessing and TF-IDF weighting. Evaluation metrics, including the Davies–Bouldin Index (DBI) and Silhouette Coefficient, confirmed that 10 clusters provided the most optimal grouping. The results revealed dominant research areas such as student learning analytics, machine learning, and IoT applications, which reflect the main directions of faculty research. These findings can support curriculum development, research strategies, and institutional policy. However, the study is limited by its relatively small dataset and reliance on a single clustering algorithm. Future work will expand the dataset, incorporate multiple institutions, and compare results with alternative topic modeling and clustering approaches.

title: Sitting on Two Chairs: Optimized Mobility Management with Selective Dual Connectivity
authors: Anna Prado, Fidan Mehmeti, Wolfgang Kellerer
abstract: The deployment of 5G networks, which are characterized by high-frequency bands, dense cell deployments, and users with diverse application demands, presents significant challenges for efficient mobility management. Frequent handovers lead to transmission interruptions and reduced network capacity, affecting both the user experience and overall network performance. The current standard handover algorithm selects target base stations solely based on signal strength, without considering the availability of resources, which can result in overloaded cells. To address these challenges, in this paper, we formulate a multi-objective optimization problem that reliably captures the network support for dual connectivity. The objective is to maximize network throughput (of interest to the operator), and to minimize service interruptions (of interest to users). Given the computational complexity of the optimization problem, we develop a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-based algorithm to perform user-to-BS assignment and wireless resource allocation. We evaluate our approach through realistic simulations in two scenarios: an indoor factory floor that uses Frequency Range (FR) 2 and a two-tier outdoor network that operates in both FR1 and FR2. The proposed algorithm demonstrates significant improvements over state-of-the-art methods, achieving better sum throughput and zero handover rates while operating within 10% of the optimum. Additionally, our algorithm ensures a 100% user rate satisfaction. Instead of performing handovers, it utilizes dual connectivity initiations and keeps their rate 20−30% lower than the handover rate.

title: Reliable Transmission in FBMC-Based OTFS Systems with TF domain Pilot-Aided Channel Estimation and Equalization
authors: Ying Wang, Qiang Guo, Jianhong Xiang, Yu Zhong
abstract: Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation can effectively support high-mobility communication scenarios. However, Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) based OTFS suffers from high spectrum leakage. Implementing channel estimation and equalization that simultaneously supports both OTFS and OFDM also faces challenges. In this paper, we adopt Filter Bank Multi-Carrier (FBMC) modulation as an alternative to OFDM and propose a TF-domain pilot-aided channel estimation and equalization scheme, which can improve spectrum leakage and enhance compatibility. Specifically, first, based on the core function of the prototype filter, we choose the Hermite prototype filter with symmetric properties to construct the FBMC-based OTFS system, enhancing adaptability to dynamic channels. Second, considering the dynamic characteristics of fast time-varying channels, we construct Bessel fitting or priori information-assisted channel extrapolation mechanisms to achieve accurate tracking of channel parameters. Finally, we derive the criterion for determining the channel wide-sense stationarity time interval, which provides a basis for the update mechanism of prior information. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme can work robustly on doubly-selection channels. Compared to classical OTFS, FBMC-based OTFS significantly improves reliability in high mobility scenarios.

title: Survey of Latest Advancements in Deep Learning for Point Cloud Completion
authors: Alessio Romanelli, Michaela Servi, Roberto Magherini, Monica Carfagni, Yary Volpe
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Point clouds have become a widely used data format in computer vision, driven by the increasing availability of 3D scanning devices in applications such as robotics and autonomous driving. However, challenges in data acquisition, such as occlusion, reflection, and limited sensor resolution, often result in incomplete scans. Consequently, point cloud completion, the task of reconstructing the complete geometry of a point cloud object from partial data, has emerged as a key area of research. Numerous deep learning-based methods have been proposed to tackle this problem. Although some surveys on shape completion exist, the rapid growth of research in recent years calls for an updated review. In this work, we present a comprehensive survey of recent advancements in deep neural networks for point cloud completion, covering literature from 2024 up to December 2025. After establishing a reference context by analyzing the major foundational methods up to the year 2023, we review the most recent methods with an analytical perspective based on how they have improved each specific component of the network. Furthermore, we present a comparative analysis of the performance of the reviewed methods on the main benchmark datasets, and finally, we discuss open challenges, offering insights and suggestions for future research directions.

title: AmphiHip Exo: A Cable-Driven Amphibious Hip Exoskeleton With Physics-Informed Intention Perception for Lower Limb Augmentation
authors: Longwen Chen, Xieyuanli Chen, Junpeng Xu, Fangge Cui, Ke Gui, Chuang Cheng, Huimin Lu
abstract: Developing an amphibious hip powered exoskeleton (AHPE) for lower limb augment in amphibious environments is crucial, which is facing the challenges, such as mechanical design, effective human-exoskeleton interaction, and simulating scenarios, for performance validations. To tackle these issues, this work proposes a design of a cable-driven AHPE named AmphiHip Exo for the hip flexion/extension assisting. To achieve an effective human-exoskeleton interaction, a physics-informed neural network named PILaN is proposed to realize hip motion intention perception (HMIP)-based control of the AmphiHip Exo. Trained by a self-collected small-scale dataset containing hip states of human activities, the proposed PILaN can automatically proceed HMIP, including joint trajectory and velocity estimations, with real-time dynamics parameter estimations and the Lagrangian dynamics model. Prototype tests are conducted on various wearers under four designed task scenarios: daily handling, climbing stairs, underwater gait assistance, and water rescue, and the performances are validated according to our designed evaluation metrics: HMIP accuracy, power assisting effect (PAE), and mechanical reliability. Experimental results prove the accuracy and efficiency of PILaN, the PAE among different wearers and task scenarios, and the waterproof effect as well as battery endurance, demonstrating the feasibility of the designed AmphiHip Exo.

title: Hybrid Free-Space Optical / cmWave Transmission over Shared Antenna Aperture
authors: Bernhard Schrenk, Florian Honz, Michael Hentschel, Fotini Karinou
abstract: Hybrid signal transmission over both free-space optics (FSO) and radio-frequency (RF) carriers can highly benefit from compact air interfaces when adopting shared-aperture antenna concepts. Towards that, we evaluated lensed horn antennas as consolidated air interface compatible with FSO and FR3-band transmission. To simplify the remote radio head further, the joint air interface is paired with mode-selective signal coding and optical modulation to yield noise-robust radio signal transmission in the FSO mode while reaping the benefits of photonic up-conversion to the FR3 band in the RF mode. Evaluation of the hybrid FSO/FR3 transmission over shared antenna aperture yields error vector magnitudes of 5.9% and 9.4% for radio signal transmission in FSO and FR3-band modes over an end-to-end link configuration that involves a fronthauled remote radio head with a compatible optical budget of up to 19 dB, a wireless trunk link for signal relay to a distribution node and local wireless access.

title: Sub-Terahertz Wideband ASK Transceiver for 14/27/64 Gb/s 0.27-km/0.12-m/0.1-mm Interconnection in 28-nm CMOS
authors: Dawei Tang, Xiaoyue Xia, Leyang Huang, Zekun Li, Chun Yang, Zheng Yan, Si-Yuan Tang, Peigen Zhou, Rui Zhang, Liqun Lu, Wentao Zhu, Zhe Chen, Jixin Chen, Hao Gao, Wei Hong
abstract: This article presents a 28-nm CMOS sub-terahertz (sub-THz) amplitude shift-keying (ASK) transceiver achieving competitive wireless and wireline communication performance. The over-the-air (OTA) link demonstrates 14 Gb/s/0.27 km, 16Gb/s/1m, and 27 Gb/s/12 cm without equalization (EQ), while supporting 64 Gb/s on-Off keying (OOK) and 40 Gb/s pulse amplitude modulation (PAM)4 in wireline mode. In the transmitter (TX), a power-boosting technique is proposed for the oscillator to maximize output power and DC-to-RF efficiency with a compact area. An ASK modulator incorporating analog-pre-distortion (APD) compensation is proposed to support high-efficiency and high-speed modulation. A compact 90–180 GHz power amplifier (PA) is utilized to deliver 15–18 dBm output power, with an integrated broadside balanced balun for differential signal interfacing. In the receiver (RX), a 110–160 GHz low-noise amplifier (LNA) with a switchable differential and single-ended interface is proposed, and a 90–180 GHz wideband envelope detector (ED) with DC-35 GHz baseband amplifier (BBA) is designed for high-speed demodulation. A novel metamaterial-loaded modified Vivaldi antenna and a wideband ground-signal-ground (GSG) pads to waveguide transition (WGT) are proposed for wireless link. The transceiver establishes new benchmarks for silicon-based sub-THz ASK systems, demonstrating the longest reported communication distance with competitive data rates, showcasing extensive communication applications.

title: Optical Injection-Enhanced Tunable Quantum-Dot Lasers for High-Speed Direct Modulation
authors: Xiangpeng Ou, Weiping Li, Hongyan Yu, Ying Shi, Xin Yao, William He, Xiaoyi Song, Yating Wan
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: Direct modulation of quantum dot (QD) lasers is valued for its high energy efficiency and low cost. However, its modulation bandwidth is limited by finite intraband relaxation and gain compression. Here, we investigate the dynamic characteristics and demonstrate the high-speed capability of a monolithic dual-ring Vernier quantum-dot (QD) laser. The free running laser shows a 3-dB small-signal bandwidth of 5.5 GHz, which is increased beyond 9 GHz under optical injection locking (OIL) while maintaining a high side-mode suppression ratio (SMSR) and stable operation. In an isolator-free directly modulated laser and direct detection (DML-DD) link, we achieve 12 Gb/s non-return-to-zero (NRZ) transmission without OIL and 23 Gb/s NRZ under OIL, both with bit-error-rate (BER) below the hard-decision forward error correction (HD-FEC) threshold and without electronic equalization or bandwidth-optimized epitaxy. Furthermore, the device remains stable under external optical feedback (EOF) up to −10 dB. These results indicate the monolithic dual-ring Vernier QD laser as a robust, reflection tolerant transmitter platform, demonstrating that optical injection effectively overcomes its intrinsic bandwidth bottleneck to enable high-speed, isolator-free operation.

title: A Lightweight Real-Time Cars and People Detector for Consumer Unmanned Aerial Vehicles on Edge Platforms
authors: Guobiao Zuo, Shengrong Hu, Yixian Li, Kang Zhou, Qiang Wang
abstract: As unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) become increasingly prevalent in the consumer electronics market, the demand for low-power, real-time visual perception capabilities, particularly in applications such as smart security, traffic monitoring, and home services, has grown significantly. However, achieving high-precision detection of vehicles and pedestrians on resource-constrained consumer-grade UAV platforms remains a challenge due to high computational overhead and limited energy efficiency. To address this challenge, this study proposes Real-time Detection of Cars and People for UAV (RDCP-UAV), a lightweight real-time object detection model specifically designed for consumer edge devices. The proposed architecture integrates a efficient Multi-Branch Grouping and Reparameterization Aggregation Network (MBGRAN) module, which reduces model parameters and computational complexity while improving multi-scale feature extraction. Additionally, it introduces a Parallel Adaptive Channel and Spatial Self-Attention (PACSSA) mechanism to enhance target feature representation efficiently. Experimental results demonstrate that the RDCP-UAV model achieves excellent detection performance with only 5.30 M parameters and 14.0 billion floating-point operations, significantly lower than those of comparable state-of-the-art methods. Evaluation on the UAVDT dataset verified that the RDCP-UAV model has excellent generalization ability. Importantly, the model delivers real-time inference at 31.74 FPS on the NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX, a representative consumer-grade edge computing platform, demonstrating its feasibility for deployment and superior energy efficiency in practical UAV systems. This work employs algorithm-hardware co-design to presents an efficient, practical visual perception solution for next-generation intelligent consumer UAVs.

title: Accelerating the A2D ocean model with Standard Language Parallelism
authors: Bingrui Chen, Yizhong Chen, Hongyuan Guo, Jianrong Zhu
abstract: Parallel computing is crucial for enhancing the computational efficiency of ocean numerical models. Traditionally, parallelization in such models relied primarily on MPI. Subsequent developments introduced GPU-oriented computing tools, including CUDA and OpenACC, which enable accelerated computation on NVIDIA GPUs. However, each tool has its own syntax and lacks interoperability. Recent support for Standard Language Parallelism from major Fortran compiler vendors (e.g., Intel, NVIDIA) has unlocked efficient parallelization across multi-core CPUs and GPUs from a single Fortran codebase. This study employs Standard Language Parallelism to accelerate the A2D model, a two-dimensional unstructured-grid ocean numerical model, by parallelizing loops in its source code. The refactored code can be compiled for either multi-core CPU or GPU execution simply by selecting appropriate compiling flags. This approach offers advantages including high code readability, broad hardware compatibility, and reduced maintenance overhead. Using compilers such as ifort or nvfortran, the parallelized model achieves speedups exceeding 23× in both multi-core and GPU configurations compared to the original serial implementation.

title: IoT Enabled Real-Time Monitoring and Disease Prediction System for Sustainable Aquaculture
authors: P. Rohini, G. R. Kanagachidambaresan, M. Muralidhar
abstract: The global shrimp aquaculture industry is highly vulnerable to water quality fluctuations, disease outbreaks and the inefficiencies of manual monitoring. To address these challenges, this paper presents a unified Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Internet of Things (IoT) enabled framework that integrates real-time sensing, predictive analytics and automated pH sensor calibration for precision aquaculture management. A custom-designed aqua buoy system continuously measures the critical parameters including pH, Dissolved Oxygen (DO) and Oxidation Reduction Potential (ORP) using a distributed IoT sensor network with cloud connectivity. A responsive web dashboard and mobile application deliver real-time visualization, farmer advisories and automated alert notifications, facilitating timely interventions. Machine learning models are employed in the proposed model for disease classification and intelligent decision-making: Random Forest achieves 98.35% accuracy in disease prediction, while Random Forest regression provides highly reliable pH voltage prediction by minimizing manual calibration requirements. Furthermore, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks are utilized for time-series forecasting, achieving up to 94% accuracy in predicting pH, DO and ORP trends, thereby enabling early corrective actions. The proposed method enhances monitoring efficiency, reduces shrimp mortality risk and supports environmentally sustainable farming practices. This integrated approach demonstrates the potential of combining IoT, disease classification & prediction and automated calibration techniques to transform aquaculture into a data-driven, precision-managed and resource-efficient industry.

title: Prompt Tuning Guided Dual Distribution Alignment for Unsupervised 2D-3D Cross-Modal Retrieval
authors: Yaqian Zhou, Ruiqiang Guo, Dan Song, Jiayu Li, An-An Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: 2D-3D cross-modal retrieval (2D-3DCMR) aims at retrieving the most matching 3D models by leveraging 2D query images. However, the inherent modal discrepancy makes the 2D-3DCMR task still largely challenging. Besides, the scarcity of 3D labels in real-world applications severely hinders learning discriminative representations. To address these limitations, we present a prompt tuning guided dual distribution alignment (PTG-DDA) framework based on the CLIP model for the 2D-3DCMR task. Specifically, we design a learnable multi-view adaptive representation learning (MARL) module that adaptively integrates 3D features to merge complementary information and filter out redundant information across views, thereby improving the representation capability of 3D models. To mitigate the feature distribution shift between the 2D and 3D data, we design an attention-guided heterogeneous feature alignment (AHFA) module to guide the 2D and 3D inputs attend to feature banks by adopting the attention mechanism, thereby achieving heterogeneous feature alignment. Furthermore, to learn discriminative 3D features, we employ a multi-modal semantic prompt synergy (MSPS) module, which integrates class-related representations into learnable prompts to progressively learn the cross-modal synergy via a prompt synergy adapter, thereby achieving semantic feature alignment. Comprehensive experimental results on popular 2D-3DCMR benchmarks, i.e., MI3DOR and MI3DOR-2, demonstrate the superiority and effectiveness of PTG-DDA.

title: LPG Humidity Sensor with Carbonyl-Rich Graphene Oxide Coating
authors: João V. De Castro, Anne Caroline Fernandes, Nayton Claudinei Vicentini, Giovanni Romeu Carvalho, Cristiano Legnani, Alessandro Henrique De Lima, Renato L. Faraco, Felipe O. Barino, Marcus Vinicius P. De Souza, Jonas H. Osorio, Jefferson E. Tsuchida, Ulysses R. C. Vitor, Diogo V. N. Coelho, Welber G. Quirino, Alexandre Bessa Dos Santos

title: Balancing Accuracy and Efficiency in Wind Power Prediction: A Novel IFE-MLP Approach for Resource-Constrained Environments
authors: Serdal Atiç, Ammar Aslan, Ercan Izgi
abstract: Achieving sustainable development goals and mitigating the effects of climate change necessitates meeting global energy demand from clean sources. In this context, wind energy emerges as one of the most promising sources. Due to the variable and intermittent nature of wind, accurately predicting the power generated from wind energy systems is crucial for integrating these sources into the grid. While current advanced prediction models can provide this accuracy, disadvantages such as high computational complexity, large memory footprint, and low inference speeds seriously limit the practical applicability of these models in real-time and resource-constrained environments. This study proposes a new and lightweight architecture, the Intelligent Feature-Engineered Minimal Multi Layer Perceptron (IFE-MLP) model, as a feature engineering-based alternative to complex transfer learning approaches. Unlike classical transfer learning paradigms that rely on parameter transfer or domain adaptation, the proposed model achieves competitive prediction accuracy through intelligent feature construction while maintaining high computational efficiency. The performance of the proposed IFE-MLP model has been comprehensively compared with two transfer learning approaches: Fine-Tuning-Based Transfer Learning (FTTL) and Feature-Based Transfer Learning (FBTL). Data obtained from a wind power plant in Bursa, Turkey, was used for performance evaluation. The prediction results revealed that the proposed model exhibited only slightly lower prediction accuracy compared to the FTTL model. However, the main advantage of the proposed IFE-MLP model is clearly evident in terms of computational efficiency and resource utilization. The proposed model achieved its prediction performance using 450 times less memory compared to the FTTL model and 2,050 times less memory compared to the FBTL model. Furthermore, it reduced the training time by 2.2 times by requiring 546 times fewer training Floating Point Operations (FLOPs) and 435 times fewer inference FLOPs, respectively. Inference latency, a critical metric for real-time applications, was measured at 0.05-0.08 ms, corresponding to a 16-100x improvement over the compared models. The conducted decomposition study confirmed that the intelligent feature engineering components in the model architecture provided incremental and meaningful contributions to performance. The findings indicate that the proposed IFE-MLP architecture offers comparable prediction accuracy alongside a significant improvement in resource usage. These characteristics suggest that the model could be a suitable alternative for resource-constrained environments and real-time applications.

title: Emerging Efficiency Enhancement Strategies for Electric Motor Drives in Electric Vehicles: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Sadeq Ali Qasem Mohammed, Mohammad AlMuhaini, Mohammad A. Abido
abstract: The global push toward sustainable and eco-friendly electrified transportation has prompted significant advancements in electric motor drives, which play a pivotal role in electric vehicles (EVs) and industrial machinery. This paper offers a holistic and in-depth review of innovative efficiency enhancement techniques for electric motor drives applied to EVs alongside the operational challenges. Key areas of focus include optimizing motor design and adopting advanced control algorithms to achieve superior energy efficiency and improved control performance. Motor design improvements leverage advanced permanent magnet materials and innovative slot-pole configurations to minimize cogging torque and enhance thermal management. On the control front, the integration of outstanding robust adaptive control strategies and AI-driven control enables dynamic adjustment of control parameters, ensuring optimal performance under critically varying load and speed conditions. On top of that, the utilization of wide-bandgap semiconductors, e.g., silicon carbide (SiC) and gallium nitride (GaN), contributes to elevating overall system efficiency. These materials offer substantial reductions in switching losses, improved thermal conductivity, and the capability to operate at higher frequencies, thereby delivering enhanced performance and reliability. This comprehensive approach to motor drive optimization aims at setting new benchmarks in energy efficiency and sustainability, aligning with the global transition toward greener transportation solutions.

title: Stationary Human Detection in Indoor Complex Environments via Millimeter-Wave Radar
authors: Qunfeng Lu, Yijie Deng, Qin Chen, Zongjie Cao
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar has emerged as a critical technology for non-contact human sensing in security and healthcare applications. While traditional Doppler radar is limited to moving targets, micro-Doppler signatures enable the detection of stationary humans by capturing physiological signals such as respiration. However, in complex indoor environments, dynamic clutter sources (e.g., oscillating fans and swaying plants) generate periodic micro-Doppler signatures resembling human breathing, leading to frequent false alarms. To address this challenge, this article proposes a robust stationary human detection method based on sparse dictionary reconstruction. A Fourier transform-based dictionary is constructed to facilitate the sparse representation of micro-Doppler features. Leveraging the distinct sparsity characteristics of human respiration compared to environmental interference within this dictionary, the proposed method employs a Compressed Sensing (CS) framework. Specifically, the Orthogonal Matching Pursuit (OMP) algorithm is utilized to reconstruct signal components under strict sparsity constraints and physiological frequency priors. Target classification is then achieved by analyzing the reconstruction error, effectively isolating human signals from interference. Experimental results from real-world scenarios demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an interference suppression rate of 97% and a stationary human detection rate of 94%. Furthermore, in complex scenarios containing both targets and interference, the detection accuracy exceeds 86%. These results confirm that the proposed approach offers superior accuracy and robustness compared to existing methods.

title: Optimizing Charging Direction Set for Directional Mobile Chargers With Multi-Sectorial Energy Beams_supp1-3668043.pdf
authors: Zhenguo Gao
abstract: Leveraging breakthroughs in wireless power transfer technology, deploying Mobile Chargers (MCs) to charge sensor nodes can significantly prolong the operational lifetime of wireless sensor networks. Directional Mobile Chargers (DMCs), which integrate directional antennas, can focus radio frequency energy along designated directions, thereby boosting charging efficiency. Most existing works assume that DMCs emit a single energy beam; however, in practice, some DMC platforms can generate multiple sectorial- like energy beams, enabling a novel Multi-Beam DMC (MB-DMC)-based charging paradigm for Wireless Rechargeable Sensor Networks (WRSNs). This paradigm introduces a critical challenge: determining a minimum-size antenna direction set for the DMC while assuring that it is functionally equivalent to the infinite <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$[0,2\pi )$</tex-math></inline-formula> direction space in maintaining charging schedules with minimal energy loss. This paper systematically investigates the Multi-Sectorial-Beam DMC Charging Scheduling (MSBDCS) problem in MB-DMC-enhanced WRSNs (MB-WRSNs) and proves its NP-hardness. Furthermore, to address this challenge, we propose a Multi-Sectorial Beam Antenna Direction Selection (MSBADS) algorithm by efficiently exploiting geometric properties of local node set distribution, and theoretically prove its optimality in generating a minimum-size charging direction set that is functionally equivalent to the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$[0,2\pi )$</tex-math></inline-formula> space. By integrating MSBADS into a four-step charging scheduling framework, we develop our MSBADS-based four-step Charging Scheduling (MSB-4S) algorithm to address the MSBDCS problem in MB-WRSNs. Extensive simulations and testbed experiments demonstrate that MSB-4S outperforms state-of-the-art baseline schemes by over 22% in energy efficiency improvement and more than 14% in scheduling time reduction.

title: Modified Physics-Informed Neural Network for Predicting Earthquake-Induced Landslide Susceptibility
authors: Loreniel E. Anonuevo, Vince Jebryl G. Montero, Paolo L. Pacaldo, Jay Melvin B. Segales, Ken Adrian C. Villarias, Jayrold P. Arcede
abstract: Landslides represent a critical natural hazard, often triggered by environmental factors such as rainfall, seismic activity, and terrain instability. Traditional landslide prediction methods-including purely data-driven machine learning models and physics-based simulations-face limitations in scalability, generalization, and physical interpretability. This study proposes a novel approach that integrates seismic information with ground-based geotechnical information into a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework to improve landslide susceptibility modeling and prediction. By embedding physical laws governing slope stability, the model ensures geophysical consistency. The integration of seismic features-such as peak ground acceleration and distance-to-fault-with geotechnical parameters like friction angle and slope geometry allows for more comprehensive and dynamic predictions. This research aims to enhance the applicability of landslide prediction systems, improve early warning capabilities, and support disaster risk reduction efforts in vulnerable regions. Through this physics-informed, data-integrated approach, the study offers a scalable and interpretable solution for advancing environmental hazard modeling.

title: Multimodal Hate Speech Detection and Classification in Malayalam
authors: Raees A Ahmed, Aakash Sudhi, P.S Sreeja
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: As the use of internet and social media increases, hate speech has emerged as a significant issue. Hateful remarks are made anonymously by most users, and the incidence of harmful conversations, particularly on controversial subjects, increases. These kinds of content make readers feel upset and help create an unhealthy online environment. This study aims to address this problem by exploring the use of transformer models in hate speech identification and moderation of Malayalam social media posts. Based on a dataset from the DravidianLangTech competition, we train and test various transformer models for hate speech classification. This study aims to improve automated hate speech detection and removal tools for social media to provide a cleaner digital environment.

title: Phase-free Permittivity Measurement Based on Siamese Network
authors: Zijing Wang, Yuqi Chen, Shilie Zheng, Xiaonan Hui, Xianbin Yu, Xianmin Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: A phase-free permittivity measurement method using a Siamese network is proposed. The method estimates permittivity by comparing measured reflection amplitude curves with theoretical ones derived from the Nicolson–Ross–Weir(NRW) model. Instead of relying on absolute amplitude or phase values, it determines the permittivity through similarity evaluation across frequencies. This approach removes the need for phase measurement while maintaining high accuracy. Experiments in the X-band show that the proposed method achieves errors below 2% for multiple length materials, demonstrating strong generalization and low-cost implementation potential. Moreover, fine-tuning with a few experimental samples further enhances accuracy and adaptability.

title: A Cost-Effective Sizing Method for Hybrid Energy Storage Systems in Electric Railways
authors: Hammad Alnuman, Salman Aatif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Energy storage systems (ESSs) are an effective solution to capture regenerative braking energy in electric railways, reducing overall energy consumption and improving grid interaction. This study investigates the optimal sizing of a hybrid energy storage system (HESS) composed of lithium-ion batteries and supercapacitors, applied to a DC electric railway. Multiple control strategies are applied by varying key parameters including battery capacity, C-rate, number of supercapacitor banks, and the number of DC/DC converters. A multi-objective evaluation is conducted using the Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution (TOPSIS), with criteria including total system cost, annual battery cycles, and total energy savings. Simulation results based on a realistic railway model reveal that the optimal configuration consists of a 100 Ah battery, 0.5 C-rate, two parallel supercapacitor banks, and seven DC/DC converters. This configuration achieves a total cost of 154.5 k$, 252 annual battery cycles, and 14.06% energy savings, demonstrating the value of combining technical performance with economic assessment in HESS design.

title: Recommendations for Designing a Distributed Generation Inverter Transformer
authors: Oleg Kimstach, Mykola Chumak
