publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The paper analyses the problem of choosing the number of slots for a distributed generation inverter using a transformer type. The construction and operational aspects of DC-AC converters for distributed generation were examined and the arguments for the advantages of transformer-type inverters were presented. The rotating magnetic field transformer is proposed as the main component of this type of inverter, with a defined model of MMF distribution. The influence of slot numbers on the amplitudes of high harmonics of total MMF was analysed using two indirect estimates: relative maximum instantaneous deviation and relative average integral deviation. The research has defined that slot numbers such as 12 and 28 are more recommended for rotating magnetic field transformers with differential protection against the short circuit between winding turns. If the transformer is not intended to be equipped with the specified protection, then the recommended set of slot quantities consists of the following values: 10, 14, 18, 22, 28, 34, and 38.

title: CerebrAI: Smartphone-Based Facial Recognition for Classroom Attendance
authors: Khairil Haziq Haji Khairul Rijal, Worarat Krathu, Arif Bramantoro
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Attendance tracking is a critical process in many places like educational institutions and professional workplaces, but traditional methods like paper-based records and RFID systems are still being used, which are vulnerable to manipulation, errors and inefficiencies. This paper presents CerebrAI, a mobilebased facial recognition system to automate attendance recording in real time, which was developed in collaboration with King Mongkut’s University of Technology Thonburi (KMUTT). The system is designed to be cost-effective, accurate and intuitive, allowing lecturers to record student attendance without the need for expensive external hardware. Implemented using Flutter, Dart, face-recognition library, OpenCV library and a MySQL database, CerebrAI detects and verifies faces, stores attendances and provides administrators with backup functionalities. To ensure data integrity and privacy, students are unable to modify the records. By eliminating manual processes and reducing reliance on costly biometric devices, this solution showcases the feasibility of deploying AI-based attendance systems using affordable devices such as smartphones, with potential applications in broader institutional contexts.

title: Service Innovation in the Hospitality Sector: The Role of Digital Leadership, Capability of Digital Information Systems, and Digital Ecosystem
authors: Dicky Hida Syahchari, Nila Astiti, Arif. Zulkarnain, Silverius Constantino Johanes M Lake, Abdul Rauf Ridzuan, Dino Gustaf Leonandri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This research investigates Digital Leadership, Information System Capability (ISC), and the Digital Ecosystem’s influence on Service Innovation (SI) in the hotel industry. A quantitative method was used in the study, employing a structured Likert-scale questionnaire to obtain data from 258 hotel employees in Greater Jakarta, Indonesia, which were analysed with SmartPLS 4.0. Results show that Digital Leadership, ISC, and Digital Ecosystem have a significant positive impact on service innovation. The originality of the present study lies in its theoretical contribution, as it combines these three key factors into a unified model that addresses this gap. To practitioners, the results present a strategic blueprint, underscoring that investments in tech-savvy leaders and state-of-the-art information systems are essential to leverage service innovation capabilities and gain/boost competitive advantage in the digital age.

title: Image Generation Based on Large Models
authors: Mingyu Chen, Zixuan Pei, Wanmin Wu, Ruifang Liu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Translating abstract concepts from text into visual imagery presents a significant challenge for generative AI. Although text-to-image models have made remarkable progress, they often struggle to capture the profound cultural nuances, emotional depth, and era-specific details inherent in classical poetry. To address this, we propose a novel closed-loop framework that synergistically integrates Large Language Models (LLMs), Diffusion Models, and a Multi-modal Large Language Model (MLLM) to provide evaluative feedback. Our system is built around a core pipeline: "Text Semantic Parsing → Structured Prompt Construction → Image Generation → Image-Text Alignment Analysis → Multi-round Optimization." Specifically, we employ Baidu's ERNIE model for in-depth semantic parsing of the text, construct culturally-aware structured prompts for diffusion models (such as SDXL, Playground v2, and Kandinsky), and introduce a tailored evaluation system using the MoE-LLaVA model to assess the generated images. Automatic multi-round optimization is then triggered based on this assessment to refine the outputs. Experimental results show that our method significantly improves both the semantic alignment (CLIP Score) and human preference score (HPS) of the generated images compared to baseline approaches, with the highest improvements reaching 34.44% and 28.87%, respectively. This work provides a robust technical pathway for the digital preservation and innovative interpretation of classical poetry.

title: Study of Sliding Mode Control System with Unsophisticated Modeling of Dry Friction
authors: Oleksandr Derets, Yuliia Sokhina, Anatoliy Pasichnyk, Serhiy Derets
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: Models of dry friction forces have discontinuous or sharply variable characteristics, which causes self-oscillations of control systems when studying their dynamics. This problem is aggravated by the use of relay controllers, the switching conditions of which are close to the conditions for breaking the friction characteristics. The solution proposed in this work for the study of relay control systems for electric drives is to use a model of friction forces in the form of a continuous analytical function, the inflection points of which are associated with the amplitude of velocity pulsations in the sliding mode. The justified form and coordination of the nonlinearity profile with the parameters of the relay controllers make it possible to eliminate false self-oscillations of the model and reliably reproduce the static and dynamic modes of the electric drive.

title: An Improved Research on UAV Detection Based on Dynamic Feature Fusion
authors: Yuan Chai, Sining Cheng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: With the increasing security threats caused by the misuse of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), the development of high-precision and low-latency UAV monitoring systems has become an urgent need in the field of public and aviation security. However, the small physical size of UAVs, their rapid motion, and their susceptibility to environmental interference make accurate and real-time detection highly challenging. At present, the deep learning-based UAV detection method still has some shortcomings in small target detection and feature extraction in dynamic fuzzy scenes. Moreover, due to the high complexity of the model, it is difficult to realize real-time deployment on edge devices. In addition, the utilization of multimodal data is not sufficient, the robustness in complex environments is poor, and there is a lack of effective defense mechanisms against attacks and dynamic environment adaptability. To solve the above problems, this paper proposes a UAV detection method combining dynamic feature enhancement and lightweight optimization. A Dynamic Feature Fusion Module (DFFM), integrating a cross-layer feature pyramid and an attention mechanism, is designed to enhance UAV feature representation across multiple scales. In addition, a lightweight U-Net-based Deblurring Subnetwork is introduced to restore motion-blurred images. This study introduces deep separable convolution and structured pruning techniques into the MobileNet V3-Large backbone network, effectively reducing computational complexity while enhancing real-time performance. By leveraging an adaptive learning mechanism, the model parameters are dynamically adjusted according to environmental conditions such as lighting and weather, thereby improving environmental adaptability. Experimental results show that the proposed method has achieved better performance on several UAV detection data sets, especially in small target detection and motion blur scene, and has good practical value.

title: Two-Stage Converter for Small Wind Turbines: Full-Bridge DC-DC Plus Three-Leg Inverter Using Arduino Mega 2560
authors: Jonathan Wijaya, Leonardus Heru Pratomo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Wind turbines are an example of renewable energy that replaces fossil fuel electricity. Previous methods used a step-up transformer for alternating current. However, DC-side step-up power conversion using a DC-DC step-up converter was built due to efficiency, space, and other constraints. A full-bridge DC-DC converter was used to boost input voltage. A power-producing system needs a step-up converter and inverter to distribute power to the grid. conventional inverters have two legs and no current correction, limiting power conversion output. The semiconductor-ferrite transformer on full-bridge converter is a step-up DC-DC converter. The Arduino mega 2560 controls the two current circuits according to the control strategy. Both circuits use power simulation data for control. PMSG equipment replaces wind turbines. A full-bridge DC-DC converter will be boosting the voltage. A three-leg inverter will transform the full-bridge DC-DC converter’s output to AC. Three-leg inverter improve conventional inverters, while full-bridge DC-DC converters convert power more efficiently than transformers and conventional converter. Thus, wind power plants can include full-bridge DC-DC converters and three-leg inverters.

title: BMSNet: A Colorectal Polyp Segmentation Network Based on Multi-Scale and Boundary-Focused Attention
authors: Xinyu Han, Yunpeng Ma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Colorectal polyps are the primary precancerous lesions of colorectal cancer, and their early detection and precise segmentation are of significant clinical importance for reducing the risk of cancer. However, segmenting polyps under conditions of small size, diverse morphology, and complex backgrounds remains a considerable challenge. To address this, this paper proposes a Boundary-sensitive and Multi-Scale Network (BMSNet) for colorectal polyp segmentation. This method introduces a Multi-Scale Spatial and Channel Attention module (MSAA) within the classic encoder-decoder framework, achieving multi-scale feature fusion through spatial refinement and channel aggregation. Simultaneously, a Boundary-Focused Attention module (BFA) is proposed, which combines a cross-attention mechanism between boundary regions and internal regions, effectively enhancing boundary localization capability and global semantic modeling. Extensive experiments on four public colorectal polyp datasets demonstrate that BMSNet outperforms five mainstream segmentation models across multiple metrics, including Dice, mIoU, and Precision, showing particularly significant advantages in small polyp segmentation and boundary refinement. This research not only provides a robust solution for colorectal polyp segmentation in complex scenarios but also offers potential support for clinical auxiliary diagnosis.

title: Simulating Shor’s Algorithm for RSA Vulnerability Assessment: A Proof of Concept for Quantum-Resistant Security
authors: Aanisha Selavisi, Henry Feild, Yeo Sy Mey, Serina Hj Mohd Ali
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: This paper investigates the vulnerabilities of RSA encryption in the advent of quantum computing, particularly under Shor’s algorithm. A Proof of Concept (PoC) demonstrates RSA key generation, encryption, and decryption, along with an approximation of quantum attacks on classical systems. Additionally, an API was developed to evaluate RSA performance across key sizes (1024,2048, and 4096 bits) and assess security implications. The study proposes a conceptual hybrid model integrating post-quantum techniques to enhance resilience. Findings indicate that while RSA remains secure against classical attacks, its longevity is uncertain in a quantum-enabled future, highlighting the urgent need for quantum-resistant cryptographic standards.

title: Analysis on the Influence Mechanism of Different Information Push Methods on Situation Awareness
authors: Wei Li, Jun Ma, Pu Chen, Xiaoyan Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Regarding the issue of how different information push methods affect workload, this study conducted a simulated radar target search experiment. 30 healthy college students were recruited as subjects, and multi-dimensional indicators such as eye movement data, task performance, and subjective workload were collected under variables including different noise environments (45dB and 80dB) and information push methods (combinations of graphics, text, and voice). The results show that the introduction of the auditory channel adds a new channel stimulus to traditional search tasks. In high-noise environments, the effect of traditional graphic push is relatively poor, while for multi-channel information push, such as graphic-voice push and graphic-voice-text push, both push methods have good performance in improving subjects' situational awareness ability.

title: Risk Analysis and Optimization Measures for Overpressure Protection of Valve Groups Under Non-Standard Operating Conditions in Hybrid Multi-Terminal UHVDC Project
authors: Zhilong Su, Qingshi Yang, Chen Zhao, Junren Shao, Xiang Ju, Zhuoyang Li
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The Kunliulong HVDC Project is the world's first ultra-high voltage (UHV) hybrid multi-terminal HVDC project. It adopts a hybrid design combining conventional HVDC at the sending end and voltage-sourced converter (VSC)-based HVDC at the receiving end, offering flexible operation modes. However, this design significantly increases the complexity of DC control and protection systems. This paper examines a valve group overvoltage protection operation fault that occurred during the transition from hot standby to AC-side hot standby under unconventional operating conditions in a three-terminal bipolar four-converter configuration of the Kunliulong multi-terminal hybrid HVDC system. By analyzing the differences in system structure and operational characteristics between hybrid and conventional HVDC systems, it identifies risks associated with deviations in reverse transition operations to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A C}$</tex>-side hot standby and potential maloperations. Given that existing definitions of DC operating conditions struggle to accommodate the inherent characteristics of VSC-HVDC, this study conducts standardized research on converter station state transitions. It enhances the identification and prevention of operational risks under special operating conditions in the Kunliulong HVDC system, providing valuable insights and guidance for future multi-terminal hybrid HVDC transition operations and related functional designs.

title: "World Café" as an Innovative Method of Teaching Social and Humanitarian Disciplines for the Formation of Competence "Ability to Work in a Team"
authors: Svitlana Fedorenko, Olga Litvinova, Volodymyr Maslak, Oxana Dobryden, Larysa Butko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: In the article, in the context of modern requirements for the training of graduates of higher educational institutions as highly qualified specialists with an existing set of soft skills tools, an analysis of the formation possibilities of first (bachelor) level higher education graduates in specialty 141 "Electrical Energetics, Electrical engineering and Electromechanics" was carried out competence "the ability to work in a team" in the process of teaching the educational components of the socio-humanitarian block and the application of innovative teaching methods. The analysis of educational and professional programs and information on their self-evaluation of five institutions of higher education of Ukraine made it possible to establish insufficient use of the potential of innovative methods of teaching educational components of the socio-humanitarian block in the formation of general competencies, in particular the competency "ability to work in a team". To emphasize the wide possibilities of modern methods in the formation of general competences, an algorithm for adapting the "World Café" method for teaching social and humanitarian disciplines is proposed.

title: Development of a Scaling Model for Characteristic Analysis of UHVDC Converter Transformer
authors: Yao Zhang, Youliang Sun, Yongwei Xu, Wei Ma
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: SLCC (Statcom and Line commutation converter) technology suppresses harmonic currents from entering the converter transformer, leading to a reduction in its losses, temperature rise, and vibration levels. However, the high cost and complexity of constructing full-scale model make it challenging to accurately assess these benefits. To address this, this paper developes a scaled model using similarity theory, and proposes a hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization Backpropagation (PSO-BP) neural network to improve the expand the universality of the model. Additionally, the design and experimental procedures for a 10 kV scaled-down prototype are presented, providing a cost-effective and practical framework for analyzing transformer characteristic under harmonic excitation

title: Evaluation of Transformer and Convolutional Neural Networks for Autoimmune Skin Diseases
authors: Laourari Roumeissa, Sabba Sara, Zerabi Soumeya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Autoimmune skin diseases are less studied compared to common skin diseases, which greatly limits early diagnosis and treatment. Moreover, the availability of public datasets on non-autoimmune skin diseases makes research lean towards them, but few, if any, studies are conducted on autoimmune diseases due to the lack of a proper, specialized dataset. Various studies have proposed Deep Learning (DL)-based approaches for diagnosing autoimmune skin diseases, often using diverse and scattered datasets. However, these efforts remain restricted by inconsistent data and insufficient comparative evaluation on advanced architectures. More recently, transformer-based architectures have been applied to image classification due to their notable performance, yet they have been used on a very limited number of autoimmune skin diseases. In this study, we collected and combined various publicly available datasets from Kaggle, selecting only images corresponding to autoimmune conditions. After preprocessing, we applied two pre-trained Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and one Transformer-based model. Comparative evaluation showed that the Transformer outperformed CNN models, achieving 94% validation accuracy compared to 89% and 86%, confirming the potential of Transformer-based architectures in improving autoimmune skin disease diagnosis.

title: Integrating the Prophet Method to Enhance SARIMA Performance in Shallot Price Forecasting
authors: Muhammad Pandu Wirakusuma, Inung Wijayanto, Helni Mutiarsih Jumhur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Price volatility in agricultural commodities, particularly shallots (Allium ascalonicum L.), is the significant risk for market stability in Indonesia, and accurate price forecasting is crucial to determine reference price and policy. While traditional models like the Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (SARIMA) are extremely effective at modeling seasonality, they are often ineffective at modeling non-linear trends. In contrast, the Prophet model has the ability to capture long-term trends and holiday effects but may be poorer at capturing short-term dynamics. In this study, a Weighted Ensemble approach is proposed to integrate SARIMA and Prophet for better forecasting performance. The ensemble weights the models by the inverse Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the validation period in a way that the more precise model has greater influence. Using daily producer price data for May 2025, the performance of the individual and hybrid models was evaluated against MAE, MAPE, and MASE scores. As observed, the Weighted Ensemble model performs much better than the two individual models, with the lowest error rates and smoother and more stable predictions. The finding confirms that the combination of Prophet’s decompositional flexibility and SARIMA’s ability in capturing short-term correlations results in a better forecasting system. The suggested hybrid model provides a useful means of ascertaining shallot producer prices in the central markets of Indonesia, thus leading to better decision-making as well as market stability.

title: Intelligent Load Optimisation Simulation System for Diesel Generators
authors: Baili Guo, Jie Yu, Yifeng Yong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Against the backdrop of the global energy transition towards low-carbon solutions and increasingly stringent carbon emission policies, the importance of energy-saving operation techniques for diesel generator sets is becoming increasingly prominent. Traditional fixed-speed operation exhibits poor fuel economy under partial load conditions, resulting in energy wastage. To address this, this paper proposes a high-precision diesel engine simulation model based on a GWPSO hybrid optimisation method, enabling dynamic speed optimisation across all operating conditions. The simulation model precisely maps the engine's fuel consumption characteristics across different load ranges through modules including load-dependent thermal efficiency calculation, dynamic power limitation management, and composite fuel consumption rate assessment. Experimental results reveal a distinctive pattern where ‘increasing rotational speed reduces fuel consumption rate’ under low-load conditions. The proposed method proves effective across the entire operating range, delivering particularly significant fuel savings in the lowload interval. This provides both theoretical support and a technical pathway for achieving efficient, low-carbon operation of diesel generator sets.

title: ISO-Oriented AI Reliability Assessment and Certification: An Integrated AIRI-RPN Framework
authors: Xiaoyu Wang, Yueze Li, Xiujie Shi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: We present a standardized, auditable pathway for AI reliability that unifies governance quantification, composite scoring, and certification. Governance items (privacy, transparency, maintainability) are quantified by a weighted checklist <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{S}_{\boldsymbol{k}}$</tex> and used as normalized inputs to a five-indicator composite index (AIRI). Certification follows a single master rule that requires both a product threshold <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{AIRI}(M) \geq \theta$</tex> and a process risk cap <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\max 1_{k R P N_{k}} \leq \rho$</tex>. A lightweight demonstration reports <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex> Wilson confidence intervals, a robustness AUC (RAUC) summarizing adversarial behavior, and one-way weight sensitivity. The framework clarifies how checklist-based governance, capability metrics, and surveillance audits fit together, enabling reproducible scoring and replaceable datasets while remaining compatible with ISO/IEC-style requirements.

title: Design and Performance Validation of Fire- and Explosion-Proof Insulation Barrier for Transformers
authors: Haibin Zhou, Zhicheng Pan, Zhicheng Xie, Jun Deng
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and validation of a fire- and explosion-proof insulation barrier for transformers, aimed at mitigating tank ignition and explosion risks caused by on-load tap-changer failures. The stress distribution and its influencing factors on the barrier under high-energy arc impact were investigated through a coupled thermal-fluid finite element simulation. The numerical model was validated by equivalent experimental tests under fault arc conditions. Results demonstrate a non-uniform stress distribution on the barrier, with significant concentrations identified at the edges, around holes, and near connection points with other components. These stress patterns are critically influenced by the barrier's structural geometry, material properties, and impact parameters. Structural features such as rounded corners and material reinforcement should be incorporated into the design of insulating barriers to address stress concentrations at edges and holes. This study can provide a basis and reference for the optimized design and engineering application of explosion-proof insulating barriers for transformers, reducing the probability of transformer faults and fires, and minimizing equipment damage as well as power outage losses.

title: Grain Truck Recognition and Random Sampling Using an Attention-Enhanced YOLOv5s Network
authors: Zhicheng Sun, Yinhu Qiao, Chunyan Zhang, Yunlei Bai
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: With the growth of our country's population, the demand for food and food consumption will increase increasingly, and artificial intelligence will gradually replace manual and traditional operation methods to become the backbone of food collection and storage work. The image recognition technology based on deep learning was used to realize the identification and classification of grain delivery vehicles, location of post samples and random sampling. The attention mechanism is introduced to improve the network model of YOLOv5s to improve the recognition accuracy and operation speed. By testing the network models of channel attention, spatial attention and mixed attention mechanisms introduced, it is found that the average F1 value of the three attention mechanisms introduced in SE, SAM and CBAM is increased by 5%, 9% and 11.6% respectively, and the mAP index is increased by 5.2%, 8.4% and 11.8% respectively compared with the original network. The method proposed in this paper has high efficiency, high intelligence, and can be effectively applied to the field of grain sampling.

title: Adoption of Technology and Digital Tools Amongst Student in Brunei Darussalam
authors: Hazimah Marali, Azhan Ahmad, Deenina Salleh, Lee Kok Yueh
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: This study investigates the adoption of technology and digital tools in Brunei Darussalam’s education system by examining key factors that influence student access, usage, and engagement. Guided by the national vision of Wawasan 2035 and ongoing digital transformation initiatives, the research evaluates three main hypotheses: the influence of socioeconomic factors, the variation in barriers across educational levels, and the role of institutional support. Using a mixedmethod survey approach, data were collected from $\mathbf{5 8 5}$ respondents, including students, parents, and educators across primary, secondary, tertiary, and special education institutions. Statistical analyses, including regression and ANOVA, revealed that access to digital devices and reliable internet are more critical than income or parental education in determining adoption. Secondary-level students reported the fewest barriers, while primary and special education students faced greater challenges. Institutional support-through training, infrastructure, and leadership-was identified as the strongest predictor of successful technology integration. The findings provide valuable insights for educators and policymakers to enhance inclusive digital learning strategies and bridge existing digital divides.

title: Class-Specific Cross Attention for Fine Grained Paddy Disease Classification
authors: Wallace F L Chin, Wida S Suhaili
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Accurate and timely diagnosis of paddy diseases is crucial for mitigating yield loss and safeguarding global food production. In recent years, deep learning and computer vision have emerged as powerful tools for automated plant disease detection. While numerous works exist in this area, most focus on leaf-level classification under controlled environments and fixed backgrounds, limiting generalizability and real-world field utility. This work investigates the application of fine-grained image classification (FGIC) techniques to paddy disease detection, demonstrating how such methods can improve discrimination between visually similar disease classes. Additionally, we propose a Vision Transformer (ViT)-based model that integrates class-specific cross-attention with Multi-Instance Attention Pooling (MIAP) to extract class-relevant features at both global and local levels. To evaluate generalization, the study leverages publicly available online paddy disease datasets for training and assesses performance on a locally collected field dataset, exposing the challenges of distribution shifts and real-world variability. The proposed method achieved a validation accuracy of $92.5 \%$ and a testing accuracy of $62.3 \%$, outperforming other FGIC approaches under the same conditions. Model interpretability is emphasized through Grad-CAM++ visualizations, which reveal that the attention maps align with disease-relevant regions and provide insight into the decision-making process. These findings highlight the potential of FGIC methods for paddy disease detection in realistic field conditions and underscore the importance of addressing generalization to unseen datasets. By combining fine-grained attention mechanisms with crossdataset evaluation, this work offers a pathway toward robust and interpretable automated disease diagnosis for practical agricultural deployment.

title: Enhancing Quarry Management Using RTK-UAV LiDAR
authors: Jieling Wu
abstract: Recent advances in RTK-equipped Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (RTK-UAV) and LiDAR sensors have provided new opportunities for efficient, high-resolution surveying in complex environments such as quarries. While previous studies have focused on monitoring slope displacement, this study explores the broader application of RTK-UAV LiDAR technology in quarry management. Through monthly UAV-based surveys at an active quarry site in Japan, a variety of spatial data were collected and processed to support tasks such as updating site maps, estimating stockpile volumes, and monitoring haul road conditions. The study highlights improvements in efficiency and accuracy compared to conventional ground-based methods. Additionally, specific strategies were developed to process complex point cloud datasets—particularly in vegetated or irregular terrain—demonstrating the feasibility of routine UAV deployment in quarry operations. The findings suggest that integrating UAV-based 3D data can enhance site management, inventory control, and operational safety.

title: Modeling Time Series Dynamics with Fourier Ordinary Differential Equations
authors: Muhao Guo, Yang Weng
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Neural ODEs (NODEs) have emerged as powerful tools for modeling time series data, offering the flexibility to adapt to varying input scales and capture complex dynamics. However, they face significant challenges: first, their reliance on timedomain representations often limits their ability to capture longterm dependencies and periodic structures; second, the inherent mismatch between their continuous-time formulation and the discrete nature of real-world data can lead to loss of granularity and predictive accuracy. To address these limitations, we propose Fourier Ordinary Differential Equations (FODEs), an approach that embeds the dynamics in the Fourier domain. By transforming time-series data into the frequency domain using the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), FODEs uncover global patterns and periodic behaviors that remain elusive in the time domain. Additionally, we introduce a learnable element-wise filtering mechanism that aligns continuous model outputs with discrete observations, preserving granularity and enhancing accuracy. Experiments on various time series datasets demonstrate that FODEs outperform existing methods in terms of both accuracy and efficiency. By effectively capturing both long- and short-term patterns, FODEs provide a robust framework for modeling time series dynamics.

title: Efficient Text-Video Aligner Method for Text-Video Retrieval
authors: Huaiqi Li, Chunxiao Fan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: To address the challenges of global alignment methods in text-video retrieval, such as the lack of fine-grained semantic matching and the cross-modal semantic gap leading to difficulties in feature alignment, and considering that global and local information has not been fully utilized, we propose an Efficient Text-video Aligner (ETVA) method. The model consists of a text encoder, a video encoder, a text-video global alignment module, and a text-video fine-grained local alignment module. The text encoder employs the ALBERT model, which accurately extracts text features through its bidirectional encoding capability, enhancing the temporal consistency and semantic correlation of cross-modal features. The video encoder utilizes a multi-expert module strategy to comprehensively capture video information from multi-modal and multi-feature perspectives. The global alignment module effectively achieves global semantic alignment by aggregating and transforming features. The fine-grained local alignment module, which is based on a shared clustering center mechanism, deeply explores the semantic association of local details in text and video. Experiments are conducted on the MSRVTT, ActivityNet Captions, and LSMDC datasets, using Recall@K and Median Rank as evaluation metrics, and results demonstrate that ETVA performs well across different datasets, with improved retrieval accuracy compared to other methods.

title: Research on high-precision target location algorithm based on Loran-C system
authors: Youming Zhao, Baixiao Chen, Gan Li, Jintao Yan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: The use of Loran-C system as a wartime backup to GNSS has become a research hotspot. This paper investigates a sea surface target localization method using Time Difference of Arrival (TDOA) information based on the South China Sea chain of China's Long-Range Navigation (Loran) System. A multi-station collaborative TDOA localization model is established. An algorithm combining Taylor series expansion with the weighted least squares method is employed to estimate the target position. Simulation results demonstrate that the adopted algorithm yields high positioning accuracy and rapid convergence. It performs well across different station combinations, verifying its feasibility and stability as a practical backup positioning solution to GNSS.

title: LiTANet: Lightweight Semantic Segmentation Network with Triple Attention and Depthwise Separable Convolution for Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Suye Chen, Xv Chen, Taiyao Pan, Yuxia Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: This paper presents LiTANet, a lightweight semantic segmentation network designed for high-resolution remote sensing imagery. LiTANet integrates depthwise separable convolution with a triple attention mechanism to enhance feature representation while significantly reducing computational cost. A robust encoder–decoder architecture with H-Swish activation further strengthens non-linear fitting ability and improves feature extraction efficiency. Extensive experiments conducted on the ISPRS Potsdam and Vaihingen datasets demonstrate that LiTANet achieves competitive segmentation accuracy, with Avg.F1 scores of 89.2% and 88.4%, respectively, while using only 10.5M parameters, substantially fewer than traditional deep models. Additional ablation experiments verify the effectiveness of each key module. The results show that LiTANet achieves an excellent balance between accuracy, generalization capability, and model efficiency, providing a new option for resource-constrained remote sensing applications.

title: Controllable Medical Image Generation Using Text Prompts on Multi-Phase Contrast CT Data
authors: Nguyen Duong Nguyen Nhat, Thanh Huyen Dang Thi, Viet Dung Nguyen LTT Dao

title: Few-Shot Named Entity Recognition Method Based on Attention Mechanism and Prototypical Network
authors: Kangrui Xie, Yanhua Chu, Liying Wang, Yueming Wang, Jinling Yu, Haili Luo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In few-shot named entity recognition tasks, traditional prototype construction methods struggle to accurately represent the semantic features of the text, leading to poor prototype representativeness. To address this issue, a method based on attention mechanisms and prototype networks is proposed for entity recognition in few-shot scenarios. The approach leverages multi-head attention mechanisms to enhance the model's feature extraction capability and employs the Mini Batch K-Means clustering algorithm to refine entity prototypes. Additionally, a distance-weighted module is introduced to dynamically adjust prototype weights, thereby improving the semantic representational power of the prototypes. Experimental results on datasets such as Few-NERD show that the proposed method effectively improves entity recognition accuracy, with F1 scores surpassing those of baseline models, validating its effectiveness in few-shot scenarios.

title: Decision letter for "Descriptor: Digital Dataset of Animal Tissues for Macroscopic Pathology in Cancer: Image and Ultrasound Data (DATP-IU)"
authors: N/A

title: Velocity Disambiguation for Video Frame Interpolation_supp2-3667437.mp4
authors: Sizhuo Ma
abstract: Existing video frame interpolation (VFI) methods blindly predict where each object is at a specific timestep $t$ (“time indexing”), which struggles to predict precise object movements. Given two images of a baseball, there are infinitely many possible trajectories: accelerating or decelerating, straight or curved. This often results in blurry frames as the method averages out these possibilities. Instead of forcing the network to learn this complicated time-to-location mapping implicitly together with predicting the frames, we provide the network with an explicit hint on how far the object has traveled between start and end frames, a novel approach termed “distance indexing”. This method offers a clearer learning goal for models, reducing the uncertainty tied to object speeds. We further observed that, even with this extra guidance, objects can still be blurry especially when they are equally far from both input frames (i.e., halfway in-between), due to the directional ambiguity in long-range motion. To solve this, we propose an iterative reference-based estimation strategy that breaks down a long-range prediction into several short-range steps. When integrating our plug-and-play strategies into state-of-the-art learning-based models, they exhibit markedly sharper outputs and superior perceptual quality in arbitrary time interpolations, using a uniform distance indexing map in the same format as time indexing without requiring extra computation. Furthermore, we demonstrate that if additional latency is acceptable, a continuous map estimator can be employed to compute a pixel-wise dense distance indexing using multiple nearby frames. Combined with efficient multi-frame refinement, this extension can further disambiguate complex motion, thus enhancing performance both qualitatively and quantitatively. Additionally, the ability to manually specify distance indexing allows for independent temporal manipulation of each object, providing a novel tool for video editing tasks such as re-timing. The code is available at https://zzh-tech.github.io/InterpAny-Clearer/.

title: Machine Learning-Driven Intelligent Memory System Design: From the On-Chip Caches to the Storage
authors: Rahul Bera, Rakesh Nadig, Onur Mutlu
abstract:
Despite the data-rich environment in which memory systems of modern computing platforms operate, many state-of-the-art architectural policies employed in memory system rely on static, human-designed heuristics that fail to adapt dynamically to workload and system behavior. In this article, we propose a fundamentally different design approach: using lightweight and practical machine learning (ML) methods to enable adaptive, data-driven control throughout the memory hierarchy.

We present three ML-guided architectural policies: (1) Pythia, a reinforcement learning-based data prefetcher for on-chip caches, (2) Hermes, a perceptron learning-based off-chip predictor for multi-level cache hierarchies, and (3) Sibyl, a reinforcement learning-based data placement policy for hybrid storage system Our evaluation shows that Pythia, Hermes, and Sibyl significantly outperform the best-prior human-designed policies, while incurring modest hardware overheads. Collectively, this article demonstrates that integrating adaptive learning into memory subsystems can lead to intelligent, self-optimizing architectures that unlock performance and efficiency gains beyond what is possible with traditional human-designed approaches.

title: Estimating Leaf Area Index and Leaf Nitrogen Content for Individual Populus Tree Using UAV Hyperspectral Data with Dual-Branch Deep Learning Architecture
authors: Zhulin Chen, Huimin Zou, Xuefeng Wang, Naijing Zhang, Guofeng Tao, Jie Li, Shijiao Qiao, Sheng Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) hyperspectral remote sensing provides a flexible approach for quantifying leaf area index (LAI) and leaf nitrogen content (LNC) of individual Populus tree. However, current methods neglect the influence of spatial scale, feature form and model structure, resulting in unclear scale determination and limited spectral–spatial feature utilization. This study proposed a dual-branch deep learning architecture to fully exploit spatial and spectral features across multiple scales and feature forms. Hyperspectral image cubes of individual trees were generated at four scales. Three feature forms were evaluated, including all bands, selected bands, and vegetation indices (VIs). The dual-branch comprises parallel spectral and spatial branches, where spectral branch processes the averaged canopy reflectance, and the spatial branch uses the canopy image as input. The spectral branch integrates one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1-D CNN), attention mechanism (AM), and long short-term memory modules, while the spatial branch employs 2-D CNN, residual learning, and AM modules. In each branch, the optimal feature forms and spatial scales were determined and then used for develop the dual-branch models. The results showed that the optimal spatial resolutions for LAI and LNC estimation were 0.10 m and 0.05 m, respectively. Both traits achieved optimal accuracy with selected bands used in the spectral branch and VIs used in the spatial branch. The proposed dual-branch models outperformed conventional DL models, with R2 of 0.861±0.006 for LAI and 0.842±0.009 for LNC. This method provides an accurate high-throughput approach for monitoring of Populus germplasm, facilitating precision breeding and phenotyping applications.

title: ICISIT 2025 List Reviewer Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)

title: Multi-view Manifold-Adaptive Kernel Regression for Speech Classification from EEG Signals
authors: Xie He, Qi Cui, Chang Wu, Yong Peng, Wanzeng Kong
abstract: Decoding speech intentions from electroencephalogram (EEG) data is the primary task in speech brain-computer interface (BCI) systems, which remains challenging due to the unclear discriminative task-aware features, and underlying nonlinear properties besides the well-known low signal-to-noise ratio of EEG data. Existing approaches typically rely either on single-domain features or performing feature learning by deep neural networks; therefore, they either fail to capture comprehensive signal patterns, or typically require large-sized EEG data to fit the parameter spaces and often have limited interpretability. To address these limitations, we propose a Multi-view Manifold-Adaptive Kernel Regression (MMKR) model for speech recognition from EEG signals in this paper. By treating temporal, spectral, and statistical EEG representations as complementary feature views, view-specific manifold-adaptive kernels are constructed in MMKR to incorporate local graph structure into kernel similarity; besides, a data-driven adaptive view weighting mechanism is used to characterize their contributions. We evaluate MMKR on both overt and imagined speech EEG datasets and the results demonstrate that MMKR achieves superior classification accuracy and robustness compared to some representative single-view, multi-view, and kernel-based baselines. Moreover, analysis on the local manifold-modulated kernel matrix and the learned view contributions are provided.

title: Leadless Pacemakers: Past, Present, and Future – A Review of Antennas and Wireless Power and Data Telemetry
authors: Deepti Sharma, Toni Björninen, Rakesh Nath Tiwari
abstract: The leadless cardiac pacemaker is a revolutionary device for bradycardia patients. It is a single-component, capsule-type implantable medical device that corrects the heart's electrical function when the heart rate falls below the standard value of 60 beats per minute. The remote monitoring feature enables the leadless pacemaker to monitor bradycardia patients’ heart health, and it is only possible due to the ultra-compact antenna of the implantable leadless pacemaker's biotelemetry system. This article reviews the leadless pacemaker's antennas for biotelemetry and power telemetry systems, the importance of intra-cardiac communication for dual-chamber leadless pacemakers, and future requirements of leadless pacemaker antennas. Considering the real-time device architecture, some crucial aspects of antenna design in the simulator are given by briefing the research conducted to date. Furthermore, experimental verification of the antenna's functioning in the cardiac environment is also provided by summarizing different approaches used by previous studies. Dual-chamber leadless pacing communication and the requirement of leadless pacemaker antennas in 5G/6G IoT healthcare environment are also briefly discussed.

title: Safety-Guaranteed Adaptive Control of Teleoperation Systems Under Event-Triggered Mechanisms
authors: Yuling Li, Rong Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This brief addresses the problem of safety-critical control for teleoperation systems with unknown dynamics, time delays, and event-triggered mechanisms. The goal is to ensure that the slave robot stays safe in an unknown environment while guaranteeing master–slave synchronization. We first define virtual reference systems that serve as generators of the desired trajectories and then propose control barrier functions (CBFs) to ensure safety requirements for the virtual reference system. The safety-critical control problem for the slave is formulated as a quadratic program (QP). To save computational resources and improve efficiency, we propose event-triggered mechanisms to determine when to solve the QP and update the controllers. Stability analysis shows that the closed-loop system is stable while achieving master–slave synchronization. It is also proven that the proposed framework ensures the safety of the system. Simulation and experimental results are provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the method.

title: NAVIgator under Fire: Characterizing Voltage Limits and Soft Error Resilience of AMD NAVI GPUs
authors: Maria Trakosa, Odysseas Chatzopoulos, Dimitris Gizopoulos
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: As semiconductor fabrication scales to smaller technology nodes, process variation presents significant challenges, affecting power consumption, thermal behavior, and voltage stability in silicon compute devices. Conservative voltage guardbands are traditionally used to ensure reliable operation under worst-case process, voltage, and temperature (PVT) variations, but they lead to excessive power consumption. Reducing the supply voltage is a promising technique for improving energy efficiency without sacrificing computational correctness and performance. While extensive research has been conducted on reducing the voltage levels in CPUs and NVIDIA GPUs, the AMD GPU side remains relatively unexplored. Process variation, inherent in semiconductor manufacturing, results in differences in power efficiency, thermal characteristics, and voltage stability even among identical GPUs from the same production batch. In this paper, we present an extensive study on voltage scaling beyond nominal conditions for three modern AMD NAVI GPUs (i.e., RX 7600 XT, 7700 XT, and 7800 XT) executing both conventional benchmarks and PyTorch-based machine learning workloads. We evaluate and present power savings and execution stability by combining undervolting characterization with neutron irradiation physical experiments, contributing to a deeper understanding of undervolting and resilience of AMD GPUs.

title: Investigation of Sub-cycle overvoltages experienced by Inverter Based Resource (IBR) Plants due to System Disturbances
authors: Mobolaji Bello, Manish Patel, Ali Bidram, Robert Arritt
abstract: IEEE Std 2800-2022 marks a major step forward in defining minimum technical requirements for connecting inverter-based resources (IBRs) to the Bulk Power System (BPS). However, as the standard gains wider adoption and real-world implementation progresses, stakeholders (including IBR owners, grid operators, Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs), and technical working groups) have noted key observations and limitations related to its transient overvoltage (TrOV) ride-through requirements in clause 7.2.3. Under the current specification, the IEEE Std 2800-2022 TrOV ride-through requirements specifies that IBR plants conform at the point of measurement (POM). Typically, there is no transient overvoltage protection applied at the POM. The transient overvoltage protection is generally applied at the IBR unit terminals. If the voltage transients at the POC is expected to be more than the transient overvoltage capability of the IBR unit, then proper application of surge protection within the plant may be necessary. This creates challenges in demonstrating conformance to TrOV ride-through requirements. To show conformance to TrOV ride-through requirements, it is important to understand how TrOV manifests at the IBR plant POM and at individual IBR unit terminals (POC) during various transmission grid disturbances or system events. This work examines how TrOV manifests at the plant point of measurement and at individual IBR unit terminals. Both non-aggregated and aggregated IBR plant models are considered, with key learnings guiding the choice of model for analysis. Additionally, it introduces several assessment approaches for TrOV conditions, leveraging electromagnetic transient (EMT) simulations to validate findings. Lastly, it highlights some of the gaps hindering the conformance to the TrOV ride-through requirements.

title: KeyNode-Driven Geometry Coding for Real-World Scanned Human Dynamic Mesh Compression
authors: Huong Hoang, Truong Nguyen, Pamela Cosman
abstract: The compression of real-world scanned 3D human dynamic meshes is an emerging research area, driven by applications such as telepresence, virtual reality, and 3D digital streaming. Unlike synthesized dynamic meshes with fixed topology, scanned dynamic meshes often not only have varying topology across frames but also scan defects such as holes and outliers, increasing the complexity of prediction and compression. Additionally, human meshes often combine rigid and non-rigid motions, making accurate prediction and encoding significantly more difficult compared to objects that exhibit purely rigid motion. To address these challenges, we propose a compression method designed for real-world scanned human dynamic meshes, leveraging embedded key nodes. The temporal motion of each vertex is formulated as a distance-weighted combination of transformations from neighboring key nodes, requiring the transmission of solely the key nodes' transformations. To enhance the quality of the KeyNode-driven prediction, we introduce an octree-based residual coding scheme and a Dual-direction prediction mode, which uses I-frames from both directions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves significant improvements over the state-of-the-art, with an average bitrate savings of 58.43% across the evaluated sequences, particularly excelling at low bitrates.

title: Co-Rotational/Auto-Resistance Enhanced Single-End Segmented Rotor Induction Motor for Torpedo-Like Propulsion: High Starting Performance
authors: Mengxia Cai, Chaohao Kan, Haihong Huang, Yingang Yu
abstract: Addressing the critical requirements for torpedo-like devices, specifically the need for high starting torque to overcome seabed sludge adhesion after prolonged underwater dormancy, enhanced operational reliability, and rapid transient response, this paper proposes a single-end segmented rotor induction motor based on the Co-Rotational/Auto-Resistance Enhancement Theory. The motor employs the magnetic-field complementarity principle in its stator winding design combined with an single-end segmented rotor structure. Its core operational principle enables the equivalent parallel operation of multiple motors with identical rotational directions but different pole pairs during startup while effectively increasing the referred rotor resistance to the stator side, thereby significantly improving the starting performance. Through an in-depth analysis of the working principles of the motor, equivalent circuits for their operational states were established and simulation models were developed. Comprehensive simulations and experimental studies have systematically validated key parameters, including the air-gap flux density, stator tooth flux density, current characteristics, and torque performance. The results demonstrate that the proposed design substantially improves the starting performance, structural reliability, and millisecond-level transient response while maintaining a steady-state performance comparable to that of conventional squirrel-cage induction motors of the same frame size, confirming its significant engineering application value.

title: Enhancing Thyroid Disease Diagnosis with Machine Learning and Counterfactual Explainable AI
authors: Mohammad Zahidul Alam, Rukshanda Rahman, Hasan Mahmud Sozib, Halima Ahmed, Adib Hossain, Anseena Anees Sabeena, Afia Fairooz Tasnim, Fahad Ahmed, Md Imran Sarkar, Timotei István Erdei
abstract: Thyroid disease affects millions worldwide and is often underdiagnosed due to complex symptom overlaps and reliance on laboratory interpretation. Machine learning (ML) offers promise for automated diagnosis, but model transparency remains a barrier to clinical adoption. In this study, we develop an interpretable ML framework for thyroid disease classification using a real-world dataset from the UCI repository. We evaluate five classifiers—including Random Forest, CatBoost, and Support Vector Classifier—under a rigorous nested cross-validation scheme to ensure unbiased performance estimation. Our best models achieve near-perfect accuracy (up to 99.7%) and AUC (1.000) on a reduced set of ten consensus physiological features, demonstrating that high performance can be maintained with parsimonious inputs. To enhance trust and clinical utility, we integrate dual explainable AI (XAI) techniques: SHAP for local feature attribution and counterfactual analysis for actionable "what-if" reasoning. These explanations align closely with known endocrine physiology (e.g., TSH and FTI as primary drivers), supporting their face validity. While limited by class imbalance and lack of external validation, our approach combines strong predictive power with transparent decision-making, offering a foundation for trustworthy clinical decision support systems in thyroid care.

title: Adapting CLIP for 3D Human Pose Estimation
authors: Lu Zhou, Yingying Chen, Jinqiao Wang
abstract: Two-stage 3D human pose estimation has garnered remarkable progress, whereas ill-posed issue still poses great deterioration to the performance. During the 2D-3D projection, comprehension of the human topology serves as a significant cue to remove the ambiguities. However, limited feature representation restricts the grasp of human knowledge. In this light, we advocate for enriching the feature representation via integrating information from different sources. Contrastive imagetext pretrained models like CLIP demonstrate the capability of generalized representation. Exploiting the strong representation power of these models improves the performance of massive downstream tasks. Inspired by this, we resort to CLIP and delve into the integration of multi-source information with the distillation technique to reduce the location error of the regression task. Firstly, we endeavor to adapt the relation knowledge from CLIP text encoder to the transformer block of joint relation modeling module. Embedding of external language knowledge enriches the feature with the tailored adjuster and conveys the relation information from a quite different aspect. Secondly, we employ the CLIP visual encoder as another source to attain the knowledge from the vision aspect.We transfer the discrete human joints into the sketch image where human skeleton is depicted on a white board. Afterwards, the sketch drawing serves as the input of CLIP visual encoder, leading to a full understanding of human anatomy. We inject this representation into 3D human pose estimation network with the distillation technique where a dual-path distillation module is advanced to retain multimodal information. Extensive experiments on Human3.6M and MPIINF- 3DHP showcase that proposed method achieves competitive results over the previous approaches and improves the location precision, revealing the efficacy of our approach.

title: An Efficient Edge-Cloud Collaboration System with Foundational Models for Open-Set IoT Applications
authors: Bufang Yang, Wenrui Lu, Lixing He, Neiwen Ling, Zhenyu Yan, Guoliang Xing, Xian Shuai, Xiaozhe Ren, Xin Jiang
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) models have been widely deployed on edge devices, enabling various IoT applications. However, lightweight on-device AI models on resource-limited edge devices hinder their adaptability to dynamic environments and tasks. Despite the superior generalization capabilities of recently developed Foundation Models (FMs), utilizing their extensive knowledge on the resource-constrained edge platforms remains unexplored. In this work, we introduce DeepEdgeFM, an edge-cloud collaborative system with FMs that enables open-set learning, simultaneously achieving generalizability and efficiency for IoT applications. DeepEdgeFM employs a spatiotemporalaware semantic customization approach that leverages spatial, temporal, and domain-specific knowledge from FMs to continuously customize edge models using unlabeled sensor data in emerging IoT environments. Meanwhile, DeepEdgeFM utilizes a dynamic model switching strategy to selectively query the knowledge of FMs based on sensor-data uncertainty and real-time network fluctuations. We implement DeepEdgeFM on five FMs and multi-modal large language models (MLLMs), covering four types of sensor data modalities. We evaluate DeepEdgeFM on two edge platforms, five public datasets, and two self-collected datasets covering both indoor and outdoor real-world environments. The results show that DeepEdgeFM outperforms state-ofthe- art baselines, achieving up to an 18.6% accuracy gain and a 38.6

title: Learning-Based Real-Time Shape and Force Estimation for Continuum Robots Using FBG-Embedded Actuation Cables
authors: Jingyuan Xia, Zecai Lin, Zheng Xu, Yun Zou, Anzhu Gao
abstract: Continuum robots are widely used in endoluminal intervention and treatment due to their dexterity and compliance. However, it is challenging to achieve simultaneous shape and force estimation of continuum robots under the requirement of robot miniaturization. To address it, this article proposes a learning-based real-time tip force and shape estimation for continuum robots using distributed tensions and proximal displacements of actuation fibers. It only needs the tension-sensing fibers for the actuation and sensing without using additional sensors. First, each utilized actuation cable is an optical fiber with inscribed distributed fiber Bragg grating sensors on it to provide distributed tension sensing. Finally, a neural network-based method is proposed to estimate the external force at the tip using the wavelength shifts of the fiber Bragg grating sensors on the actuating fibers and cable length changes as inputs, also the kinetostatic model is established to reconstruct the robot’s shape in real time. Then, experimental results show that the average static force estimation error is 6.2 mN and the average tip position error from shape estimation is 3 mm. Furthermore, an augmented reality platform was developed to overlay the real-time estimated external forces and robot shape onto two-dimensional video images from an external monocular camera. This work would provide a potential to promote the embodied intelligence of continuum robots with miniaturization and integration.
