title: A Synthetic Oversampling Method Based on Local Density and Minority Class Complexity Measurement
authors: Qiangkui Leng, Jinxin Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Class imbalance is a core challenge in supervised learning. Traditional classifiers, biased by data distribution, favor majority classes and fail to learn minority class decision boundaries. Most oversampling techniques ignore data distribution complexity, thereby causing inter-class overlap and minority intraclass imbalance, which limits model generalization. To address these issues, this paper proposes an Adaptive Oversampling algorithm integrating Local density and hypersphere constraints (AOLM). AOLM adopts a three-stage framework: First, it calculates the local density of minority samples via density estimation, filtering noise samples from the majority class that overlap with the minority class to reduce interference. Second, it dynamically constructs a hypersphere space to enclose minority samples by fusing the imbalance ratio, distance to the nearest heterogeneous neighbor, and local density, thus isolating inter-class overlap spatially. Third, it designs an adaptive oversampling strategy: the sampling quantity is allocated based on the hypersphere radius, internal sample number, and minority sample variance. Hyperspheres with smaller radii, fewer instances, and larger variances receive higher weights. Random oversampling replaces SMOTE to avoid overfitting. Experimental results show that AOLM performs well on KEEL and both simulated and real imbalanced datasets. It solves inter-class overlap via hypersphere constraints and adaptively balances the minority intra-class distribution, thereby outperforming mainstream oversampling methods significantly.

title: Ground Fault and Protection of VSC-HVDC System Sent by Long Submarine Cable
authors: Jiacheng Wang, Ruhai Huang, Zhiqiang Yang, Wei Li, Tianlun Wang, Chongxue Jiang
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Due to the substantial ground capacitance of long submarine cables, the fault characteristics of offshore wind voltage source converter based high-voltage direct current (VSC-HVDC) systems during ground faults differ significantly from those of onshore systems using overhead lines. To address the limitations in existing research, this paper is dedicated to developing systematic grounding fault protection strategies for such long-cable VSC-HVDC systems to enhance operational reliability. This paper analyzes the fault characteristics of VSC-HVDC systems employing long-distance cables during both fault operation and fault blocking following AC valve-side grounding faults and submarine cable grounding faults. The study reveals that fault currents persist during the blocking phase after a single-phase-to-ground fault occurs on the valve-side of the converter transformer, which cannot be neglected. Based on the fault characteristic analysis results, an adaptive protection configuration scheme has been proposed. Finally, a hardware-in-the-loop real-time simulation platform, built with industrial control and protection devices, is developed to validate the effectiveness of the proposed protection scheme.

title: Multi-Objective Low-Carbon Dispatch Optimization for Power Systems With Pumped Storage
authors: Weihua Huang, Zhiming Chen, Dongsen Wei, Hua Li, Guozhong Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: To achieve “dual carbon” targets and promote the large-scale accommodation of renewable energy, this paper proposes an efficient and low-carbon dispatch model for power systems integrated with pumped storage energy. A multi-objective optimization model is developed to cooperatively minimize system operating costs, renewable energy curtailment, the output fluctuation of combined renewable energy sources, and total system carbon emissions. The model innovatively incorporates a dynamic carbon emission coefficient based on the output levels of thermal power units to improve the accuracy of carbon accounting. A case study based on a large-scale regional power grid demonstrates that the model can effectively coordinate various power sources. Results show that appropriately relaxing the operational boundary conditions of pumped storage can significantly reduce system operating costs and carbon emissions. Finally, the paper analyzes optimal pumped storage strategies under various weather scenarios and recommends a centralized control strategy for pumped storage clusters to maximize their overall value in future power systems.

title: Navigation Error Analysis and Improvement Methods in Air Pilot System
authors: Yihan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The air navigation system faces the problem of navigation error caused by high dynamic environment and electromagnetic interference, which restricts the accuracy and reliability of the system. In this paper, a multi-source data fusion architecture based on federal Kalman filter is proposed, a temperature sensitivity compensation model and an adaptive factor adjustment mechanism are designed, and a dynamic weight allocation strategy and a multi-level state identifier are developed. The improved method effectively solves the problems of filter dispersion of traditional navigation system in high manoeuvrability scenarios and positioning accuracy in GPS weak signal environments, significantly improves the stability and robustness of the air navigation system in complex environments, and provides a new idea for the design of high-precision navigation system.

title: MSViT: A Multi-Scale Attention Fusion Model for Sugarcane Disease Classification in Complex Backgrounds
authors: Yuxiang Cai, Ying Zhu, Cuimin Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Automatic sugarcane disease recognition in complex field backgrounds is crucial for precision agriculture. To address challenges such as background interference, variable lesion scales, and confusion between similar diseases, this paper proposes a multi-scale attention fusion model named MSViT. The model uses ResNet-18 as its backbone, enhances key features by embedding Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) channel attention modules into residual blocks, and incorporates an innovative multi-scale Vision Transformer (ViT) module at the network's later stage. This ViT module leverages parallel multi-scale convolutions and a self-attention mechanism to fuse local details with global context. Experiments on a self-built sugarcane disease dataset with complex backgrounds show that MSViT achieves a classification accuracy of 90.15%, outperforming various comparative models and validating its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Experimental study of Li-ion Batteries Performance Under Real-time Dynamic Load
authors: Efat Mohammadi, Alexander Headley
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: The lifecycle of batteries is greatly affected by the loading conditions in real-world applications. Previous experimental studies, focusing on simplified idealized lab conditions (steady-state constant current, constant voltage (CCCV) profiles) could be potentially misleading, obscuring important information about dynamic conditions. Our work aims to overcome the limitations of idealized studies using a Power-Hardware-in-the-Loop (PHIL) system. Our developed PHIL model integrates real and simulated components in a controlled environment, while being able to perform real dynamic charge and discharge cycling tests of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs). We assess the performance of LIBs by subjecting a Lithium Cobalt Oxide (ICR) cell to an EV drive cycle test while monitoring changes in capacity, resistance, and electrochemical properties. Our analysis determines the effects of real-world dynamic loads on degradation rates such that we can eventually develop more durable systems and system controls.

title: Regenerative Brake Control using PID for a Small Electric Vehicle with In-Wheel Motors
authors: Mohamad Heerwan Bin Peeie, Rinasa Agistya Anugrah
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: Small electric vehicles (EVs) equipped with inwheel motors (IWMs) offer several advantages, including rapid torque response and the ability to control regenerative braking force independently and directly. However, under hard brake conditions, the wheels may slip, causing the vehicle to skid if regenerative brake control is not employed. This study aims to enhance the braking performance of small EVs by implementing PID (Proportional-Integral-Derivative) control in the regenerative braking system. Simulations were conducted under both dry and wet road conditions, comparing scenarios with and without PID control activation. In the simulation, the initial speed is set to 30 km/h and for the PID activation, the desired slip ratio is set to 0.1 and 0.15 for dry and wet road surfaces, respectively. The results show that PID control significantly enhances braking performance on both dry and wet surfaces. On dry roads, stopping time is reduced from 4.4 seconds to 2.4 seconds. For wet roads, the stopping time decreases by 2 seconds, going from 4.5 seconds without PID control to 2.5 seconds with it. Additionally, on wet surfaces, PID control helps to prevent wheel skidding. Consequently, this leads to enhanced stability and safety for the vehicle.

title: Query-based Collaborative Multimodal Token Pruning for Audio-Visual Question Answering
authors: Xilin Qin, Dexiang Hong, Weidong Chen, Cheng Ye, Xinyan Liu, Peipei Song, Lei Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Audio-Visual Question Answering (AVQA) requires complex reasoning across auditory and visual modalities. While recent advancements leverage sophisticated spatio-temporal representations, cross-modal fusion, and question-aware mechanisms to boost performance, they frequently neglect the computational burden and significant information redundancy inherent in processing long, dense video sequences. This inefficiency stems from frame-by-frame processing where critical information is often sparse. To bridge this efficiency gap, this paper introduces a novel Query-based Collaborative Multimodal Token Pruning strategy. Our method exploits the coupling relationship across modalities between layers to collaboratively assesses multimodal token importance required for the current state and update the query accordingly by the proposed Adaptive Multimodal Token Pruning. This query-based collaboration enables highly efficient, focused reasoning. Experiments on the MUSIC-AVQA dataset demonstrate that our method prunes up to 80% of multimodal tokens without accuracy degradation, validating our query-based collaborative multimodal token pruning as a highly effective strategy for building more efficient and robust AVQA models.

title: Obstacle Recognition System For Lawn Mower Based On Machine Vision
authors: Yingdao Li, Han Wu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of weak environmental perception ability, low obstacle avoidance efficiency, and redundant path planning of traditional lawn mowers in complex courtyard environments, an embedded machine vision solution based on YOLOv8 object detection algorithm is proposed. The hardware of the system consists of camera, acquisition card, and Raspberry Pi. The software system optimizes model training based on the PyTorch framework and fine tunes network parameters through a self-built dataset. After 500 rounds of training, it can achieve 90% accuracy mAP@0.5 Detection accuracy, inference delay controlled within 1000ms. Experiments have shown that the system can achieve a processing speed of 30fps at a resolution of 640 × 480, with a false detection rate of less than 4.2%, providing a low-cost and highly robust environmental perception solution for intelligent lawn mowers. Promoted the transformation of home service robots from laboratory validation to large-scale application.

title: Research on Simulation Method of Aerodynamic Parameters of Ice-Covered Conductors
authors: Xuetong Zhu, Gang Qiu, Nailong Zhang, Wenxuan Zhou, Fanghui Yin
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The galloping of conductors caused by icing on transmission lines seriously threatens the safe and stable operation of the power grid. In view of the problems such as high cost and long cycle that the current research on the aerodynamic characteristics of ice-covered conductors mainly relies on wind tunnel tests, as well as the lack of universal simulation models in numerical simulation methods, this paper systematically conducts research on the numerical simulation methods of aerodynamic parameters for ice-covered conductors. Based on the vertical gallop mechanism of Den Hartog and the torsional gallop mechanism of Nigol, taking the typical crescent-shaped ice-covered conductor as the object, three flow field domain models of circular, rectangular and mixed shapes were established. The influence of different flow field Settings on the simulation results of aerodynamic parameters was compared and analyzed. The research results show that the aerodynamic parameters obtained by numerical simulation have an error of less than 5% compared with the wind tunnel test data, meeting the requirements of engineering applications. Circular and mixed-shaped flow fields have higher modeling and computational efficiency in parametric studies. This study provides an efficient and reliable numerical simulation method for the analysis of aerodynamic characteristics of ice-covered conductors, which can offer a theoretical basis for the prevention and control of gallop in transmission lines.

title: Dialogue System Using Emotion Analysis
authors: Ryusei Maeda, Kenta Morita, Haruhiko Takase
abstract: This study aims to develop a voice-based dialogue system that can listen to users' complaints in a more human-like and friendly way. Existing dialogue systems are not designed specifically for listening to complaints, and previous systems often lacked emotional connection. To solve this problem, we propose a new method using an emotion analysis AI based on Plutchik's Wheel of Emotions. The system analyzes the speaker's emotions and displays a corresponding facial image to visually convey empathy. A user study showed that the image-based emotion feedback was positively received and helped users feel more understood. However, the verbal responses of the system remain an area for improvement.

title: Test Data Generation Based on Artificial Bee Colonyoptimized Genetic Algorithm
authors: ZhiWei Liu, QiuLian Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the growing complexity of software systems, automatic test data generation - a critical step in software testing - directly dictates the cost of testing efforts and the overall software quality. To mitigate the issues of premature convergence and inadequate local search capability in traditional genetic algorithms (GAS) for test data generation, this paper proposes an automated test data generation method based on an improved artificial bee colony (ABC)-optimized genetic algorithm. It sustains population diversity via a phased search strategy of the bee colony and leverages genetic operations to strengthen local search performance. Experimental results indicate that compared with conventional GAS, the proposed method yields substantial enhancements in key metrics including path coverage, convergence generation, and execution time, particularly exhibiting superior coverage for complex path conditions.

title: A Novel Initialization Strategy for the Control System of VSC-HVDC
authors: Wenwen Mei, Zhou Li, Xiao-Ping Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: VSC-HVDC systems are widely used in modern power grids, supporting various control modes such as PQ decoupling and amplitude-phase control. These control strategies typically rely on Proportional-Integral (PI) controllers, which regulate system behavior by tracking deviations in system status. However, it is found that the traditional initialization methods for PI controllers would cause mismatch between the control output and system status. Subsequently, the “control disturbance” will be introduced to power systems, which may lead to system oscillations and even affect system stability. To address this issue, this paper theoretically analyzes the mechanism of “control disturbance” and proposes a novel initialization strategy for the control system of VSC-HVDC. By calculating the initial integrator value based on the functional relationship between actual system status and integrator output, the proposed strategy effectively eliminates control disturbances and achieves fast and accurate matching of actual system status.

title: Research on Mechanism and Coordinated Suppression of Transient Overvoltage in HVDC Systems with High Penetration of Renewable Energy Sources
authors: Peiran Zhang, Hang Liu, Fangqun Liao, Yanbo Hao, Shuyu Liang, Pu Yan
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: As the integration scale of high-proportion renewable energy into the power grid expands, the challenge of transient overvoltage becomes increasingly critical during transmission via High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) systems. This issue is particularly acute at the sending-end grid, where it poses risks to safety and stability. Utilizing the PSCAD simulation environment, this study constructs a comprehensive electromagnetic transient model of an HVDC sending system that incorporates wind, solar, and thermal power generation units. Building on this model, the paper conducts an in-depth examination of the generation mechanism and the impact patterns of high renewable energy penetration on sending-end transient overvoltage during commutation failure scenarios. Simulation outcomes demonstrate a distinct trend: higher renewable energy penetration rates correlate with elevated transient overvoltage levels at the sending end. To mitigate this issue effectively, this study introduces a novel coordinated control strategy involving synchronous condensers and converter station AC filters. This approach is founded on analyzing the performance characteristics of AC filters and synchronous condensers within a high-voltage DC sending-end system equipped with synchronous condensers. The results indicate that the proposed strategy successfully smooths sending-end transient overvoltage across various renewable penetration levels. When compared to conventional compensation techniques, this method offers superior suppression capabilities and significant value for engineering applications.

title: User Portrait Construction Based on Discrete Cuckoo Algorithm and Swarm Intelligence Perception Task Allocation Algorithm
authors: Lvzhe Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In response to the problem of low efficiency in task allocation for crowdsensing, which can only match tasks and users roughly and ignore the multidimensional characteristics of users and the actual needs in complex scenarios, a user portrait construction crowdsensing task allocation algorithm based on discrete cuckoo algorithm is proposed. Based on the characteristics of mobile crowdsensing tasks, a multi-objective optimization model is constructed by clarifying task state update rules. Based on user behavior, device functionality, and other attributes, the basic attributes, behavioral characteristics, and demand characteristics are subdivided into three main tags to construct a user profile. Using the discrete cuckoo algorithm, the optimal allocation scheme for swarm intelligence perception tasks is generated through discretization processing and parameter optimization (introducing population entropy and adaptive step size control factors) to achieve allocation. The experimental results show that as the number of evolutionary generations increases, this research method causes the optimal solution curve to quickly rise and steadily approach a better solution; Under different combinations of tasks and user numbers, the average completion rate of tasks has increased by about 15% - 20%, and the load balancing rate of user tasks has become more stable and reasonable, improving the efficiency of task allocation in crowdsourcing perception.

title: Stackelberg Game-Based Optimization Method for Carbon Emission Reduction in Power Procurement
authors: Sheng Zhou, Xinzhe Zhao, Bo Zou, Ke Chen, Jiaxiang Yan, Yuanqian Ma, Zhenzhi Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the advancement of global carbon neutrality goals, carbon emission reduction in the power system has become a key issue. Power procurement involves customer enterprises, thermal power plants, and renewable energy power plants, but their interest disparities impede coordination between carbon reduction goals and economic efficiency. In this paper, a Stackelberg game-based carbon reduction optimization method is proposed: a refined power procurement contract framework is designed, carbon flow calculation models for day-ahead basic scenarios and intraday deviations are constructed, and a multi-agent game optimization model is established. Case studies on a 4-bus system simplified based on actual situations are performed for verifying. The simulation results show that this method enables coordinated optimization of power procurement economics and carbon reduction objectives, with balanced multi-party interests, additionally, providing decision-making support for low-carbon power market operation.

title: Research on Coordinated Trading Strategy of Multi-Energy Storage Systems in Electricity Market Environment Based on PIMO
authors: Pengxiang Gao, Tong Xu, Longxiang Yan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the increasing integration of high-penetration renewable energy into power systems, energy storage has become a key technology for supporting the stability of new power systems due to its flexible regulation capability. However, in electricity market environments, how to coordinate multiple heterogeneous energy storage technologies-such as energy-type and power-type storage-to cope with complex market constraints and maximize overall revenue remains a critical challenge. To address this issue, this paper proposes a coordinated trading strategy for multienergy storage systems based on a projection-iterative-methodsbased optimizer (PIMO). The strategy establishes an integrated model that simultaneously considers energy, frequency regulation, and reserve markets. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy can effectively leverage the respective technical advantages of pumped hydro storage, lithium-ion batteries, flywheel energy storage, and compressed air energy storage, achieving synergistic complementary. Compared with other algorithms, the projection-based optimization strategy demonstrates superior performance in both convergence speed and global search capability, validating the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed model and algorithm.

title: Analysis and Localization of Mis-Triggering Faults for HVDC Transmission Converter Valves
authors: Cong Huang, Yunze Deng, Wanli Shi, Guangbin Qin, Boyi Fang, Hang Li, Kunlun Han, Yang Song
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Commutation failure is one of the common faults in HVDC transmission systems, particularly in converter stations connected to weak AC systems, where its occurrence is highly frequent. However, transient abnormalities in the converter valve triggering system exhibit fault characteristics very similar to those of commutation failure, which can easily lead to misjudgment by maintenance personnel, hindering timely fault localization and equipment anomaly tracking. Although transient abnormalities in the triggering system do not cause the control and protection system to block the HVDC system immediately, but such anomalies may evolve into potential hazards, eventually resulting in DC blocking fault. Therefore, this paper analyzes the principle of mist-riggering in converter valves, clarifies the fundamental differences between mis-triggering and commutation failure, and facilitates early detection of triggering system abnormalities by maintenance personnel. Furthermore, based on the structure of the converter valve triggering system, the paper elaborates on how to locate fault points based on mis-triggering phenomena, enabling targeted maintenance during HVDC system outages to eliminate triggering system anomalies. The proposed analysis and localization methods for converter valve mis-triggering can effectively enhance the risk identification and resilience of the triggering system, ensuring the safe and stable operation of HVDC transmission systems.

title: Dynamic gesture recognition based on multi-feature combination and feature enhancement
authors: Wang Deguo, Li Zejiang, Yan He
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: To address the poor adaptability of traditional dynamic hand gesture recognition (HGR) methods in handling inter-subject variations and their limited capacity to capture diverse hand shapes and motion patterns across users, this paper proposes a feature-enhanced multimodal fusion network (MGD-Net). The network adopts a feature-level fusion strategy to fully leverage the complementary advantages of depth images and 3D hand skeleton data, thereby improving the model’s ability to represent gestures under individual differences. Specifically, MGD-Net takes feature-enhanced grayscale depth images as input to a convolutional neural network (CNN) for spatial feature extraction, employs a bidirectional gated recurrent unit (BiGRU) to model temporal dependencies, and introduces a DeepGRU(D-GRU) module to effectively capture the dynamic variations of Three-Dimensional(3D) skeletal features. This architecture enables efficient multimodal feature fusion and precise gesture recognition. Experimental evaluations on two public datasets, DHG-14/28 and SHREC-2017, demonstrate that MGD-Net achieves competitive accuracy rates of 91.24% and 89.88% for 14 and 28 gesture classes on DHG-14/28, and 94.28% and 90.38% on SHREC-2017, respectively, showcasing its robust performance and generalization capability, particularly in scenarios involving significant user-specific morphological variations. This work contributes insights to the field of multimodal perception, with implications for the upcoming 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence-Powered Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT2025).

title: Nonlinear Dynamics of Electromechanical Tracking Systems With Distributed Parameters of Plant
authors: Borys Kuznetsov, Ihor Bovdui, Tatyana Nikitina, Valerij Kolomiets, Borys Kobylianskyi, Olena Voloshko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: Aim. Accuracy improving and sensitivity reducing to plant parameters changes for nonlinear electromechanical tracking systems (NEMTS) with plant distributed parameters (PDP) based on nonlinear robust control (NRC). Multi objective design method (MODM) for NRC by NEMTS taking into account dry friction moments on drive motor and working element shafts and elastic elements and with plant parametric uncertainty (PPU) based on MODM developed. NRC target vector calculated by solving the corresponding multi-criteria nonlinear programming problem (MCNPP) with nonlinear objective function vectors (NOFV) calculated algorithmic and associated with NRC design and NEMTS modeling for operation various modes with different input signals and for various PDP values. NRC and non-linear robust observers (NRO) design reduced to Hamilton-Jacobi-Isaacs equations (HJIE) solution. Comparison of designed robust NEMTS with PDP dynamic characteristics showed designed NRC used allowed accuracy improving and sensitivity reducing to PDP changes with existing system comparison.

title: Analysis of the Process of Learning Information Acquisition Based on the Lotka-Volterra Model
authors: Oleksii Chornyi, Larysa Herasymenko, Valerii Tytiuk, Victor Busher
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The paper is devoted to the actual problem of modeling and analyzing the process of students' learning information acquisition. The Lotka-Volterra model is used to describe the dynamics of learning information acquisition. The results of the analysis allow us to identify problematic issues caused by a drop in attention, memory impairment, lack of motivation and cognitive interest of students, as well as external spontaneous factors related to interruptions in classes by alarms, lack of power supply, etc. It has been established that external and internal influences controlled by the teacher can activate students' work. The obtained data are the basis for the development of a methodology for teaching professional disciplines in electrical engineering, which involves comprehensive work on the organization of active learning in lectures, practical, laboratory classes in a blended learning environment.

title: Methodology to Identify Stressor Impacts on Energy Storage System Degradation
authors: Jennifer Farler, Alexander Headley
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy and electrification has introduced new challenges for grid reliability, with energy storage systems (ESS) playing a key role in maintaining stability. Lithium-ion batteries are widely used but their long-term effectiveness is limited by degradation. Conventional battery management systems (BMS) estimate state of health (SOH) but cannot directly link operational stressors such as temperature, ramp rate, and state of charge (SOC) to degradation. Here, we introduce an EKF-based method for realtime SOH estimation that simultaneously adapts a stressorinformed degradation model, directly connecting operational conditions to observed capacity loss. The approach is trained using cycling test results from a lithium iron phosphate (LFP) cell over four years. Results show that the EKF provides nearly stable SOH estimates under intermittent measurements, with a degradation model correlated to experimental stressors. These results lay the groundwork for future algorithms that can extract degradation models from dynamic and uncertain data, leading to BMS designs that can leverage this information over the life of the system.

title: GIICS: A dataset for gaze-direction-based intention inference in complex visual scenes
authors: Qianxi Yu, Chen Ling, Shitao Feng, Jiayi Hu, Yongtong Lu, Nai Ding
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: To precisely recognize a visual object embedded in a complex visual scene, a person often has to adjust his or her gaze to fixate on the object, i.e., placing the object into the center of the visual field. Therefore, correctly understanding gaze direction and, more importantly, which object is being fixated on, is necessary to understand the intention of a person. To test how well vision large language models (VLLMs) can understand gaze direction and which object is being fixated on in a complex visual scene, the current study presents a novel dataset, referred to as the Gaze-based Intention Inference in Complex Scenes (GIICS), that comprises over 2800 pictures taken in 6 indoor and 14 outdoor backgrounds. Each picture contains two visual objects embedded in the visual background, with a viewer fixating on one of them. To verify if VLLMs can directly understand gaze direction instead of inferring gaze direction based on body posture or head direction, we constructed the GIICS-BodyPosture subset of images featuring uncommon combinations of body and head directions. Moreover, we constructed the GIICS-ObjectAngle subset, where we manipulated the visual angles between competing objects (e.g., objects placed to the left or right of the viewer) to investigate how angular intervals affect gaze target detection. We paired each picture with a visual question answering (VQA) question (e.g., "what is the person looking atƒ (A) the yellow object; (B) the blue object; (C) neither.") and evaluated how well nine popular VLLMs, e.g., Llama and Qwen2.5-VL, could answer the questions in both Chinese and English. The median VQA accuracy across models was 37.1% (VQA in Chinese) and 40.6% (VQA in English) and the highest performance was 57.1% (VQA in Chinese) and 58.8% (VQA in English). These results reveal significant deficits of current VLLMs when inferring human intention based on gaze direction in complex visual scenes.

title: High-Variance Graph Framelets for Heterophilous Graph Learning
authors: Ruigang Zheng, Ming Li, Xiaosheng Zhuang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Heterophilous graphs are characterized by connections predominantly occurring between nodes of differing classes, resulting in highly non-smooth or “highly varying” label distributions across the graph. This structural property challenges conventional graph learning methods, which often rely on the assumption of label and feature smoothness. Motivated by the need to better align with the intrinsic heterophily in such graphs, we propose a general parameterized system of high-variance graph framelets. These framelets are designed to generate feature representations that are themselves highly varying, thereby enhancing the expressiveness and discriminative power of node features in heterophilous settings. The proposed high-variance framelets can be flexibly constructed without requiring data leakage or task-specific training, making them a lightweight yet effective addition to existing models. Experimental results on two representative heterophilous graph datasets demonstrate that our method consistently improves node classification accuracy, highlighting the potential of high-variance representations for addressing the challenges of heterophilous graph learning. This work opens up promising avenues for developing more adaptive and theoretically grounded spectral methods, particularly in settings where smoothness assumptions fail to hold.

title: SepAMP: A Scalable and Efficient Framework for Forward-Only Test-Time Adaptation
authors: Jiahao Wu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: Deep learning models often suffer substantial degradation when deployed under distribution shifts induced by natural corruptions, domain changes, or reduced numerical precision. Test-time adaptation (TTA) can mitigate these failures, yet most effective methods rely on backpropagation and therefore remain impractical for quantized or resource-constrained environments. Forward-only approaches remove the need for gradients but still incur significant overhead due to high-dimensional prompt search and repeated full-precision forward evaluations. To address these problems, We propose SepAMP, a fast and scalable forward-only TTA framework to reduce optimization and inference cost substantially while preserving adaptation quality. Experiments on full-precision and quantized ViT models demonstrate that Sep-AMP achieves up to 69.87% latency reduction while maintaining competitive or superior robustness across multiple distribution-shift benchmarks, making it practical for real-world deployment.

title: Research on Vehicle Appearance Defect Detection Based on Machine Vision
authors: Han Jiechu, Zhang Yu, Ma Jialei, Sheng Yiqi, Zhao Guoan
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: The accurate detection of minute scratches and dents remains a significant challenge in automated vehicle appearance inspection. While the YOLO series, renowned for its real-time performance, is widely applied in this field, existing models often suffer from the loss of shallow features and insufficient fusion mechanisms when dealing with small, low-contrast defects, leading to high miss rates. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an enhanced YOLOv8-based model, termed YOLO-CBS. Different from conventional uniform feature extraction, we introduce a differentiated stacking strategy of CBS modules specifically targeting the high-resolution shallow pathways (P2/P3). This design effectively compensates for the information loss caused by down-sampling and bridges the semantic gap between shallow texture details and deep semantic features. Experimental validation was conducted on a dedicated dataset of $\mathbf{5, 0 0 0}$ vehicle images. Results indicate that YOLO-CBS achieves a mAP@0.5 of 88.53% and mAP@0.5:0.95 of 63.89%, outperforming the baseline YOLOv8n by 3.06% and 2.52%, respectively. This study provides a high-precision, robust solution for automated inspection in automotive manufacturing, significantly reducing the missed detection rate of micro-defects.

title: Evaluating Knowledge Graph-Enhanced Context for Multiple-Choice Question Answering
authors: Maryam Ghanbari, Renata Dividino
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Despite their impressive capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) struggle to access knowledge not encoded during pre-training. In-context learning (ICL) addresses this limitation by embedding relevant information directly in the prompt, enabling LLMs to use external knowledge without updating their parameters. Recent research has explored integrating knowledge from knowledge graphs (KGs) into prompts, as KGs offer structured and factual representations of concepts and their relationships. A common strategy involves identifying key concepts in the task, grounding them in the KG, extracting subgraphs that connect question and answer concepts, and incorporating the corresponding statements into the prompts. However, a major challenge in ICL is selecting appropriate knowledge, that is, information that supports the model's reasoning and improves performance, while minimizing irrelevant or noisy content that can reduce accuracy. This study investigates how the representation of knowledge in prompts and the relevance and scope of task-grounded KG knowledge affect LLM performance on multiple-choice question answering (MCQA) tasks. We compare triple-based versus path-based representations, assess relevance filtering strategies, and evaluate different knowledge processing approaches. Our findings show that path-based representations outperform triple-based approaches but are more sensitive to noise. While KG-based knowledge can enhance LLM reasoning, imprecise selection of relevant knowledge can degrade performance below the zero-shot baseline (where no additional context is provided), highlighting the challenge of integrating KG knowledge without task-specific selection strategies. Expanding the scope of extracted subgraphs increases recall (retrieving more relevant information) but reduces precision (as more noise is also included). These findings underscore the critical importance of balancing informativeness with noise reduction in KG-enhanced LLM systems. Source code is publicly available at https://github.com/maryam-ghanbari/KGSweetSpot.

title: Graph-Based Identification of Influential Groups in Cross-Platform Narrative Dissemination
authors: Ridwan Amure, Nitin Agarwal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: In this study, we examine how political discourse spreads across social media by focusing on influential groups of users—referred to as focal structures. We constructed a crossplatform multiplex network that maps the relationships between users and narratives and apply the Contextual Focal Structures Analysis (CFSA) algorithm to identify context-specific influential groups. Our analysis focuses on social media narratives for two events, viz., Taiwan's 2024 presidential election and the 2024 U.S. presidential election. Our research presents a method for modeling narrative-based influence across platforms, identifying the influential groups of users driving these narratives, and evaluating the approach on 4,817 Instagram, 2,560 TikTok, 11,134 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X$</tex> and 7,327 YouTube posts for the Taiwan election, and 2,431 Instagram, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$46,520 \mathrm{X}$</tex>, and 8,462 YouTube for the U.S. election. Influence, we find, is not always tied to individual actors but often emerges from coordinated or organically aligned groups. Understanding these dynamics is essential in a media landscape where cross-platform flows shape perception and political outcomes.

title: Research on On-Site Automatic Inspection Technology for Secondary Equipment Based on New-Generation Substations
authors: Huihai Liu, Ye Xia, Xiaoli Zhang, Wen Shi, Xuecheng Dong, Jiashu Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Rapid and efficient on-site inspection of relay protection is a crucial step in ensuring the safe operation of secondary equipment. Traditional manual inspection methods are time-consuming, difficult to control quality, and challenging to digitize, thus no longer meeting the needs of onsite inspection for the new generation of substation secondary equipment. This paper proposes a design scheme for automatic on-site inspection of the new generation of substation secondary equipment, exploring new technologies such as intelligent configuration based on the entire substation system configuration file, intelligent generation of test cases, automatic verification of ICD models, identification of setting files, and standardized information exchange. This paper has developed the PMS3.0 automatic testing system, which utilizes mobile operation and maintenance terminals and intelligent testers as carriers. By adopting innovative ideas such as “paperless”, “automated”, and “closed-loop process online”, it revolutionizes the inspection mode of secondary equipment, addressing challenges such as device differences, complex verification files, and information exchange. It achieves functions such as automatic generation of operation instructions, automatic inspection throughout the entire process, and data sharing, supporting on-site inspection and commissioning of all types of secondary equipment across all voltage levels, and ensuring the safe and stable operation of the equipment.

title: Low Ripple Operation Strategy for High Power and Low Cost Hybrid Hydrogen Production Converters
authors: Hongyu Jiang, Zhuoning Gu, Jianzhong Xu, Chengyong Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: In high-power electrolytic hydrogen production systems, balancing cost-effectiveness with power quality presents a significant challenge. While traditional thyristor rectifiers are economically viable, they typically exhibit substantial output current ripple. Conversely, solutions based entirely on full-control devices offer superior performance but come at a prohibitive cost. To resolve this trade-off, this paper proposes a hybrid rectifier topology that combines a thyristor unit with a parallel auxiliary converter. The system utilizes a 12pulse thyristor rectifier for bulk power transmission, while an active auxiliary branch-comprising a cascaded voltage source rectifier (VSR) and a dual active bridge (DAB)-is employed for ripple compensation. Furthermore, a composite control strategy integrating repetitive control (RC) is designed to ensure the auxiliary circuit accurately tracks and cancels low-frequency harmonic components. Simulation results confirm that the proposed scheme effectively suppresses the DC current ripple and enhances system performance without significantly increasing implementation costs.

title: Research on the Commercial Cryptography Protection Framework for the Power Sector: A Roadmap from Legacy Cryptography to Post-Quantum Migration
authors: Kaitian Huang, Yuan Pan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: As the power industry's digital transformation accelerates, commercial cryptography as a core technical means to ensure the safe and stable operation of power systems, is facing unprecedented opportunities and challenges. This study focuses on the cryptography application scenarios across the generation, transmission, transformation, distribution, and consumption links of the power industry. It systematically analyzes the technical path for migrating from traditional cryptography applications to post-quantum computing-resistant cryptography applications, explores the implementation scheme of quantum key distribution (QKD) technology in internal communication networks, and discusses the application prospects of privacy-preserving computation technology for data services. The research indicates that the power industry has established a relatively sound commercial cryptography application system. However, against the backdrop of the looming threat of quantum computing, the security of traditional cryptographic algorithms will face severe challenges. By constructing a ‘traditional cryptography-post-quantum computing-resistant cryptography hybrid architecture’, deploying quantum key distribution (QKD) networks, and applying privacy-preserving computation technology for data services, the cryptographic security protection capability of power systems can be significantly enhanced. This study provides theoretical guidance and technical support for the upgrading and transformation of the commercial cryptography protection technology system in the power industry.

title: Data-Centric AI for MOOC Sentiment Analysis: Transformer-based Hybrid Target Augmentation
authors: Wafa Ghemmaz, Mohamed Madassi, Ferhat Boulahia, Imed Eddine Talhi, Maroua Bouzid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Sentiment analysis in Massive Online Courses (MOOCs) plays a crucial role in understanding the emotions of learners and assessing the courses quality. Furthermore, it enables instructors and platform providers to identify dissatisfaction and enhance learning experiences. However, it presents a major challenge, especially when the distribution of emotions is highly imbalanced with negative opinions being significantly underrepresented. While existing studies are mainly model-centric AI, this work adopts a data-centric perspective and focuses on improving the data quality. Thus, we propose Hybrid Target Augmentation (HTA), a novel data augmentation method that combines Back-Translation and Masked Language Modeling to leverage class imbalance problem by generating negative samples while preserving contextual coherence. The Experiments conducted on large-scale Udemy reviews dataset demonstrate that HTA significantly improves performance across multiple Transformer-based models. In particular, XLM-Roberta finetuned with HTA achieved up to 92% accuracy. Moreover, the class-level performance for the negative class is improved, Recall increased from 88.40% to 89.47%, and F1-score rose from 87.94% to 88.13%. The results confirm the effectiveness of HTA and demonstrate its potential for improving sentiment analysis in MOOCs.

title: We Really Need the Help of AI: A Case Study of AI Adoption in Cybersecurity
authors: Stefani Slavova, Yury Zhauniarovich
abstract: None

title: Seek and You Shall SOC: Blending Human Expertise with Multimodal Generative AI for Scalable Threat Prevention
authors: Dan Xu, Iqbal Gondal, Xun Yi, Teo Susnjak, Timothy McIntosh
abstract: None

title: Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Distributed Task Assignment in Multi-UAV Cooperative Edge Computing
authors: Wendong Zuo, Siteng Liao, Yangguang Cui, Tong Liu, Hui Cai, Shaohua Wan
abstract: Leveraging flexible deployment and wide-area coverage, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) assisted edge computing (EC) extends computation capability to the network edge and has become a key paradigm for improving the quality of service in the Internet of Things. However, constrained by onboard resources, UAVs struggle to independently process massive heterogeneous tasks in real time. Existing studies mainly focus on cooperation between UAVs and ground devices or the cloud, while the coupled cooperative relationships among UAVs and the performance scalability in large-scale UAV networks remain insufficiently characterized. To this end, we propose a distributed task assignment framework for a multi-UAV cooperative EC system, where each UAV explicitly accounts for cooperation with other UAVs and, leveraging controllable mobility, assigns a portion of local tasks to other UAVs or base stations to minimize the long-term system-wide total latency. To address the resulting joint trajectory control and task assignment optimization problem, we first formulate it as a Markov decision process. We then propose a Multi-head self-Attention critic-assisted MADDPG (MA <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> DDPG) algorithm to train local online decision models for UAVs. Under the fully cooperative setting, we employ a centralized Critic to exploit global information and reduce parameter redundancy; concurrently, a multi-head self-attention mechanism is incorporated into the Critic to aggregate multi-UAV interaction information, delineate implicit cooperative dependencies, and alleviate the network input dimensionality curse arising from increasing UAV scale. Finally, extensive simulation experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Formation Control for AUSVs With Input Saturation Under Event-Triggered Framework
authors: Tao Li, Linran Tian, Jiajun Zhao
abstract: This work investigates distributed formation control for multiple autonomous underactuated surface vehicles (AUSVs) with input saturation under event-triggered framework. Within the leader–follower framework, the original AUSVs model is transformed into a minimum-phase nonlinear cascaded form to obtain a dynamic tracking error system. Based on this, a distributed formation control strategy is developed under an event-triggered framework by integrating backstepping and relay-switching control techniques. Stability analysis shows that the proposed formation controller enforces input saturation and renders the leader–follower tracking error uniformly ultimately bounded, with exponential decay toward a small residual set. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
authors: N/A

title: Gap-Filling of Highly Incomplete Daily Chlorophyll-a Remote Sensing Time Series Data over the Eastern China Seas via Spatiotemporal-Periodicity Aware Tensor Completion
authors: Gaoxiang Zhou, Chao Ding, Zeifei Ming, Ming Xu, Ming Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The application of daily Chlorophyll-a (Chla) concentration remote sensing data is inevitably affected by large gaps caused by clouds, sunlight, and other undesirable conditions. Existing methods face challenges in addressing large data gaps due to their limited ability to leverage spatiotemporal correlation and periodic features. This study presents a spatiotemporal–periodicity aware tensor completion method for gap-filling highly incomplete daily Chla datasets over the eastern China seas. The method first performs periodic-aware tensor restructuring to align interannual seasonal patterns, then embeds spatial and temporal features through graph Laplacian, fractional temporal differencing, and periodic circulant transforms, followed by adaptive low-rank tensor completion with mode-wise weight optimization. Experiments using 10 years of MODIS-Aqua daily Chla data over the eastern China seas, with an average missing rate of 87%, demonstrate that the proposed method consistently achieves high reconstruction accuracy across multiple years, with correlation coefficients above 0.97, RMSDs below 0.43 mg/m3, and MAPDs under 15.2%. The method remains robust even under missing rates up to 95%. Compared with linear interpolation, DINEOF, DINCAE, CP, and Tucker, the proposed method achieves the highest overall accuracy and superior spatial-temporal consistency, particularly under complex missing patterns. Additionally, ablation results confirm the complementary contributions of the proposed spatial, temporal, and periodic transform operations. The findings indicate that the proposed method provides a promising approach for filling large data gaps in daily Chla remote sensing dataset, which could enhance the application of Chla data in marine ecosystem-related studies.

title: Adaptive Consensus Multi-Teacher Distillation for Generalizable Face Forgery Detection
authors: Jiuyao Jing, Feiyang Sun, Chunlei Peng, Decheng Liu, Nannan Wang, Xinbo Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Recent advancements in deep learning have significantly lowered the cost of generating and processing facial images, but this progress has also presented a growing challenge for face forgery detection. Existing detection methods, however, often struggle to generalize effectively to forged samples generated by previously unseen forgery techniques. Additionally, transferring knowledge through knowledge distillation to enhance generalization remains a persistent challenge. To address these issues, this paper proposes Adaptive Consensus Multi-teacher Knowledge Distillation (ACMD), a novel framework aimed at improving the generalization capabilities of face forgery detection models. ACMD leverages multiple teacher models with varying network architectures and introduces a consensus mechanism to resolve knowledge conflicts arising from the diversity of teacher models. It further adapts the assignment of sample-specific distillation weights, based on each teacher model’s prediction confidence. By combining this with feature-based knowledge distillation, the student model gains a deeper understanding of the teacher’s knowledge. Extensive experiments on the DeepfakeBench benchmark demonstrate that ACMD not only outperforms a single teacher model but also achieves state-of-the-art performance in dataset evaluations. Specifically, in a cross-dataset evaluation from FaceForensics++ to CDFv2 and DFDC, ACMD achieves frame-level AUCs of 84.22% and 74.43%, respectively, surpassing all baseline models. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each component and highlight their complementary contributions to the overall detection performance.

title: Superconducting Stator Winding for Axial Flux Electrical Machines Applications
authors: Giuseppe Messina, Gianluca De Marzi, Ferruccio Maierna, Luigi Muzzi
abstract: Axial flux permanent magnet (AFPM) electrical machines, characterized by disc-shaped rotor and stator structures, offer a high power-to-weight ratio. The replacement of traditional copper stator windings with high-temperature superconducting (HTS) windings has led to the development of lighter, more compact, and higher-performing machines, despite the technological challenges posed by HTS windings. Notably, HTS tapes experience AC power loss when subjected to alternating currents or when interacting with time-varying magnetic fields. Research at the Superconductivity Group in ENEA Frascati, in partnership with ICAS Company, focuses on the electromagnetic characteristics of second-generation (2G) HTS materials, particularly concerning AC loss in HTS coils. This paper presents the experimental results of two HTS coils wound using distinct HTS tape structures and tested within the liquid nitrogen (LN2) temperature range. Building on the geometric data from these coils, the electromagnetic behaviour of superconducting stator winding (SSW) topologies made from them was investigated. Finite element method (FEM) models of two AFPM prototypes, associated with two SSW topologies, were created, analyzed, and compared. By combining experimental data from the HTS coils with simulation results, a preliminary analytical design for AFPM superconducting synchronous prototypes is developed, enabling predictions of their performance across a range of operating conditions and rotor speeds.

title: A Superconducting DC Traction Substation kW-scale Prototype
authors: L. Ferreira, Y. Baazizi, J. M. Delgado Quintero, S. Meunier, T. Phulpin, F. Trillaud, L. Quéval
abstract: This work investigates the feasibility of a superconducting DC railway traction substation in which the conventional transformer, diode rectifier, and copper cables are replaced by a partially superconducting transformer, a cryogenic diode rectifier, and superconducting cables. A 3 kW prototype DC traction substation was designed, assembled and experimentally characterized. System performance, encompassing electrical efficiency (i.e., not including the cooling penalty), was evaluated, providing insights into the behavior of each superconducting component compared with its conventional counterpart. The transformer employs a copper primary winding at room temperature and a high temperature superconducting secondary winding operating at 77 K, eliminating the need for bulky terminations. The proposed design successfully operated and exhibited higher efficiency compared to its conventional equivalent. The cryogenic diode rectifier, based on selected commercial diodes, demonstrated higher efficiency than the room-temperature version on a large operating range. The high temperature superconducting cables operated without any measurable losses under rectified load current conditions. Overall, the results confirm that the superconducting system operated as intended, suggesting possible gains in compactness and electrical efficiency compared to conventional DC traction substations.

title: An Enhanced Spherical Vector Wave Approach for High-Fidelity Channel Emulation in MPAC
authors: Jianchuan Wei, Zexin Dang, Zhanying Peng, Xiaoming Chen
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The plane wave synthesis (PWS) method is conventionally considered superior to the spherical vector wave (SVW) technique for two-dimensional (2-D) multiple-input multiple-output over-the-air (OTA) testing. This letter consummates this perception by identifying that the degradation of the conventional SVW stems primarily from sensitivity to the mode truncation number. To address this, we propose a robust enhanced SVW scheme formulated directly in the electromagnetic field domain. By exploiting the natural low-pass filtering property of the spherical sampling matrix, the proposed method inherently avoids the overfitting issue of high-order modes, thereby eliminating the truncation sensitivity. Our simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme ensures numerical stability and consistently outperforms the classic PWS in terms of reconstruction accuracy, rotational invariance, and test zone size. This work effectively revitalizes the SVW technique for high-precision 2-D OTA testing.

title: AFed-CHKD: an Adversarial Federated Learning based Cluster Head Selection and Knowledge Distillation in Vehicular Sensor Network_supp2-3665994.pdf
authors: Shibao Sun
abstract: Federated learning (FL) supports collaborative modeling among clients in vehicular sensor network (VSN). However, the frequent topology changes of VSN lead to high concurrency and poor reliability in the interaction of model parameters between vehicle clients. It leads to reduced timeliness of federal collaborative decision-making and increased vulner-ability to gradient inversion attacks (GIA). To address the above issues, we propose an adversarial FL based cluster head selection and knowledge distillation(KD). We design an adaptive cluster head selection mechanism based on multi-dimensional attributes of vehicle. The cluster head (teacher model) transmits the distilled knowledge (soft label) to the member vehicles within the cluster (student model), compressing interaction parameters. To defend against GIA, the cluster head acts as a generator to construct adversarial examples, and members within the cluster act as discriminators. Based on our designed and configured intelligent vehicle platform, AFed-CHKD is compared with several baseline methods. The results show that the accuracy standard deviation of the model is 1.5%, and the convergence rate of the model is improved by an average of 1.54 times. The reconstructed images by the attacker have blurred boundaries, which proves that AFed-CHKD is resistant to GIA.

title: Self-heating in SIS Mixers: Experimental Evidence and Theoretical Modeling
authors: Wenlei Shan, Shohei Ezaki
abstract: This work investigates the relationship between self-heating and the characteristic features observed in the current–voltage characteristics (IVCs) of superconductor–insulator–superconductor (SIS) junctions. Finite-element analysis is employed to evaluate the steady-state temperature distribution around SIS junctions, explicitly accounting for the temperature dependence of the thermal conductivities of the constituent materials. This approach enables flexible estimation of self-heating under various practical conditions, such as different substrate materials, interfacial thermal resistances, and geometric layouts. A heating coefficient is extracted from the simulations and used as an input parameter for IVC modeling. Incorporating self-heating through temperature-dependent gap energy and gap broadening, the simulated IVCs reproduce bending features near the energy gap that agree with measured characteristics. Furthermore, when an unintended weak link, such as a fabrication-induced defect near a via hole, is present near an SIS junction, its critical current can be significantly reduced by junction heating, producing unexpected bends at the linear branch of measured IVCs. Conversely, such bends may serve as indicators that the junction temperature approaches the superconducting transition temperature.

title: Bluetooth Extended Advertising: Analysis and Optimization of Scanning Performance Degradation
authors: Lingao Meng, Bing Lin
abstract: This study conducts a comprehensive empirical analysis of the extended advertising feature of Bluetooth Low Energy introduced in the Bluetooth 5.0 Core Specification. We first deconstruct its architectural transformation compared to legacy advertising, focusing on the implementation principles and key parameters of extended advertising and extended scanning. Subsequently, through in-depth testing of the protocol stacks of four major chip suppliers (Nordic, Silicon Labs, Realtek, and Telink), we quantified the differences in key timing parameters, such as auxiliary offset and state switching time, and showed how these differences directly result in significant scanning performance gaps. These gaps are observed under scenarios including interference-free conditions, coexistence of advertising and scanning, interference from surrounding extended advertising, and Wi-Fi interference. Our analysis further highlights protocol-level challenges, such as an undefined frequency hopping method and reduced scanning capabilities under interference. Based on this analysis, we propose an engineering guideline for parameter optimization, anti-interference frequency-hopping mechanisms, and efficient Link Layer filtering strategies. This work provides engineers and researchers with insight to navigate the complexities of extended advertising and to develop reliable, high-performance connectionless applications.

title: Mitigating the Process Variations in Negative Capacitance Junctionless Device and Circuit
authors: S R Ruma, Manish Gupta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: Through well-calibrated simulations, this work presents a comprehensive analysis of process-induced variations in on-current (ION), off-current (IOFF), and threshold voltage (Vth) for negative capacitance (NC) junctionless (JL) transistors designed on bulk substrate. The performance and variability metrics are benchmarked against SOI-based NCJL transistors. The results reveal a significant reduction in (ION), (IOFF), and (Vth) due to the variation in geometrical device parameters in bulk NCJL devices. The outperformance is primarily due to the shift in the conduction channel towards the front surface, which enhances the effectiveness of the negative capacitance effect in bulk NCJL device. In addition, the lower variability observed in bulk NCJL devices translates into improved noise margin (NM) and reduced process-induced NM fluctuations in inverter circuits. Results reported for the first time demonstrate the enhanced device- and circuit-level performance of bulk NCJL transistors in comparison to their SOI counterparts.

title: A Bus-Voltage-Adaptive Dual-Mode Stacked Half-Bridge LCLC Converter for Electric Vehicles Energy Router Applications
authors: Pengcheng Wang, Qiuye Sun, Jun Yang, Rui Wang, Rongda Xing, Chenghao Sun
abstract: High-voltage Electric Vehicle (EV) battery platforms challenge fast charging compatibility. High-voltage electric vehicle (EV) battery platforms face challenges in fast-charging compatibility. A dual-battery pack scheme utilizing a double-pole double-throw (DPDT) switch improves charging compatibility, but results in an excessively wide bus voltage. Conventional auxiliary power modules (APMs) become unsuitable under such conditions. This paper therefore proposes an adaptive dual-mode buck/boost converter for HV/LV platforms. Firstly, by altering its connection configuration, the proposed converter is better suited for dual-battery-pack systems utilizing DPDT switches. It can accommodate a wide bus voltage range from 250 V to 1000 V (or even higher), while the maximum voltage stress on its MOSFET is only 500 V. Secondly, in boost mode at both platforms, the converter is fundamentally reconfigured into an LLC converter with a parallel LC branch on the primary side (LCLC converter). The magnetizing inductance of this converter increases with the switching frequency, enabling the required voltage gain while simultaneously suppressing circulating current losses. Thirdly, in buck mode at both platforms, it operates as an LLC converter with a larger resonant inductance and higher quality factor, thereby enhancing step-down capability within a narrower frequency variation range. Finally, A 2.4kW prototype with 96% peak efficiency (250-1000V input, 48V output) validates the design performance.

title: Strategic Shaping of Human Prosociality: A Latent-State POMDP Framework_supp1-3668141.mp4
authors: Kumar Akash
abstract: We propose a decision-theoretic framework in which a robot strategically can shape inferred human's prosocial state during repeated interactions. Modeling the human's prosociality as a latent state that evolves over time, the robot learns to infer and influence this state through its own actions, including helping and signaling. We formalize this as a latent-state POMDP with limited observations and learn the transition and observation dynamics using expectation maximization. The resulting belief-based policy balances task and social objectives, selecting actions that maximize long-term cooperative outcomes. We evaluate the model using data from user studies and show that the learned policy outperforms baseline strategies in both team performance and increasing observed human cooperative behavior.

title: TC-EEGNet: A Temporal Convolution Enhanced EEGNet for EEG Based Alcohol Addiction Classification
authors: Yuanchen Li, Jundi Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Alcohol addiction is a chronic disorder that severely affects brain function and remains a major global public health concern. Accurate and efficient detection of alcohol addiction based on electroencephalography (EEG) signals is essential for early diagnosis. However, traditional machine learning (ML) approaches rely on manual feature extraction and multiple classifier evaluations, while deep learning (DL) models still suffer from limited classification accuracy and generalization capability. To address these limitations, this study proposes TC-EEGNet, a temporal-convolution enhanced EEGNet model that integrates depthwise spatial, separable, and temporal convolutional layers to effectively capture both spatial and temporal dependencies in EEG signals. The proposed method was evaluated on a public EEG dataset using 10-fold cross-validation, achieving a validation accuracy of 94.34%, an F1-score of 93.70%, and an AUC of 92.90%. Comparative experiments with existing approaches further confirm the superior performance and robustness of TC-EEGNet. The results indicate that the proposed framework provides a promising direction for EEG-based alcohol addiction detection and can be extended to other EEG-related neurological disorder analyses.

title: Self-Disentangling Domain-Specific and Domain-Agnostic Representations Across Multiple Sources for Data-To-Text Generation
authors: Mingxuan Du, Jingbo Zhou, Fuzhen Zhuang, Yuhong Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Recent years have witnessed the increasing research attention on the topic of data-to-text generation(it is also called table-to-text generation), due to its wide range of applications, such as generating textual summaries from structured knowledge graphs. Existing methods usually require a large amount of labeled data to achieve satisfying performance. However, it is very expensive and time consuming to collect enough labeled data in a specific domain to train a model. Though it is possible to pre-train a general model through multiple source domain data and then use the target domain data to fine-tune it, there may exist the gap between each other. To this end, we propose a novel data-to-text generation model, named SMSTL, with utilizing multiple sources in a transfer learning setting. The core of SMSTL is a special designed self-disentangling mechanism to disentangle the domain-specific representations and domainagnostic representations tailored for data-to-text generation. We conduct extensive experimental evaluation to demonstrate that our method can significantly outperform the state-of-the-art methods.

title: Multi-Dimensional Feature Fusion-Based Intelligent Early Warning System for Internal Topological Faults in Electric Energy Meters
authors: Kaiji Liao, Wentao Chang, Ruichao Li, Xiaojun Li, Jie Shen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: In recent years, with the rapid expansion of China's power grid and significant improvements in intelligent capabilities, smart electricity meters have been widely deployed across the grid. However, owing to the lack of reliable fault monitoring and early warning mechanisms, a large number of such meters experience internal topological faults annually. To address this issue, we propose a machine learning–based monitoring and early warning model using multidimensional feature fusion. By integrating three categories of key features—attribute features, operational load features, and derived composite features (totaling over ten types) — an XGBoost classification algorithm framework is constructed to implement risk early warning for internal topological faults. Validated with data from millions of users in a southwestern Chinese province, the proposed model achieves an accuracy of approximately 90%, reduces annual power outage duration by over 20,000 h, and significantly enhances the grid’s safety and operational stability.

title: Prostate Cancer Lesion Detection Based on Pseudo-Normal Image Generation Using Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Yang Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: The deep integration of artificial intelligence technology into medical image analysis has positioned the early and accurate diagnosis of prostate cancer as a critical challenge in clinical research. To address issues such as ambiguous lesion boundaries and strong heterogeneity in multi-modal MRI data for prostate cancer, this paper proposes a lesion detection and segmentation method based on a generative adversarial network (GAN) that synthesizes pseudo-normal images. This method utilizes the GAN to generate pseudo-normal images, thereby accentuating the differences between lesions and normal tissue. Combined with a difference-aware weighting mechanism that dynamically adjusts loss function weights, the approach significantly enhances segmentation accuracy and model generalization. Experiments conducted on the public PICAI2022 dataset and a private dataset demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing mainstream models across multiple metrics, including the Dice coefficient, sensitivity, and false positive rate (FPR), achieving a reduction in FPR by 0.3 per case. This provides an effective solution for computer-aided diagnosis in prostate cancer imaging.

title: Advanced Object Detection and Grasping with Faster R-CNN in Robotics
authors: Bushra Ziafat, Jing Zhang, Sadeed Anwar, Hongliang Guo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Robot grasping is an approach that enables robots to identify, grasp, and retain things through robotic arms. It has significant value in terms of enabling tasks to be automated like sorting, building, and relocating things. Grasping things in real applications is not easy because things have varying shape, size, form, and location. Conventional methods and previous deep learning networks tend to fail to produce precise results due to these problems. In this research, we propose a new method utilizing Faster R-CNN with a ResNet50 backbone for enhancing object grasp detection. The approach integrates object detection and grasp prediction into a single framework with RGB-D images. Faster R CNN's region proposal network assists in providing highquality regions of grasp, enhancing accuracy and efficiency. The network achieves an accuracy of 98%, mean IoU of 52.28, precision of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 8. 3 1 \%}$</tex>, and recall of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3. 3 7 \%}$</tex>.

title: Comparative Analysis of MPLS LDP and Segment Routing on Nokia 7750 Using BGP Auto-Discovery
authors: Christ Geofany, Imelda Uli Vistalina Simanjuntak, Lukman Medriavin Silalahi, Dwi Harinitha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: The rising need for fast and stable data communication in enterprise environments poses significant network management challenges, particularly in scaling and configuring full-mesh Virtual Private Network (VPN) services. This research investigates a more efficient solution: leveraging Multi-Protocol Label Switching (MPLS) technology integrated with Segment Routing (SR) and BGP Auto-Discovery (BGP A-D) protocols. The core objective is to demonstrate how this architecture can simplify configuration while simultaneously enhancing scalability and dynamic path management in complex VPN setups. The study utilized a controlled network simulation environment, deploying six virtual Nokia 7750 routers configured using OSPF and BGP dynamic routing. Performance was rigorously assessed by testing the network under heavy 10 MB and 20 MB traffic loads, along with multiple ICMP packet transmissions. The evaluation focused on critical Quality of Service (QoS) metrics, including throughput, delay, jitter, and RTT, analyzed meticulously using GNS3 and Wireshark. The test results confirm that the MPLS network utilizing Segment Routing (SR) generally outperforms LDP. Crucially, SR achieved a lower average delay of 2.57 ms, compared to LDP’s 2.7 ms. These findings strongly support the conclusion that implementing SR with BGP A-D is a superior approach for enhancing the efficiency and dynamic performance of modern MPLS-based VPN services.

title: Current-Limiting Function Integration of Direct Current Circuit Breaker and Its Operating Characteristic
authors: Yumin Zhang, Jing Xu, Xiaoguang Xi, He Yang, Zhicheng Zhang, Zhihao Zhu, Weijie Wen
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: With advantages of high efficiency and flexible controllability, low voltage DC power supply system is considered an ideal solution to support the rapid growth of renewable energy and new types of loads. However, due to high-power electronics penetration, low inertia, and weak damping characteristics, short circuit faults lead to a rapid rise in current with no natural zero-crossing point, making it difficult for conventional circuit breakers to interrupt the current quickly and effectively, which can easily cause widespread power outages. Existing hybrid DC circuit breakers and current-limiting devices are not suitable for the low voltage scenarios. The main contributions of this paper are as follows: current-limiting function is integrated to existing direct current circuit breaker (CL-DCB), which can achieve fast limiting of surge current and interruption of fault current. The structure and working principle of the CL-DCB are analyzed in detail, and its operational characteristics are verified through simulation, demonstrating its potential to enhance system reliability.

title: Blockchain-Secured Federated Learning for Energy Forecasting in Steel Manufacturing
authors: Nazia Sultana Plabon, Ashok Pal Emon, Mohammed Saifuddin Munna, Joy Dey, Indra Jit Barua
