abstract: Energy forecasting in steel manufacturing faces critical challenges: centralized methods compromise data privacy, while decentralized approaches lack robust security against adversarial attacks. This paper presents BlockchainSecured Federated Learning (BSFL), a novel framework that combines federated learning with blockchain technology to enable accurate, privacy-preserving, and tamper-proof energy predictions. The system employs a hybrid LSTM-Transformer model for local training at steel plants, Hyperledger Fabric for secure global aggregation, and differential privacy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon=0.5$</tex>) to protect sensitive data patterns. Tests on real-world steel industry data (35,040 entries) demonstrated BSFL's superiority: it achieved a 0.042 kWh MAE and 0.058 kWh RMSE, outperforming centralized Transformer baselines by 12% and 15%, respectively. The blockchain component detected 98.5% of simulated model poisoning attacks, while differential privacy reduced inference attack success to 1.5%. Communication overhead was reduced by 65% compared to conventional federated learning. BSFL's key contribution is a scalable industrial solution that balances accuracy, privacy, and security without data centralization. This work advances secure AI applications in energy-intensive sectors where data governance is paramount.

title: Low-Noise Sampling and Processing of Signals in Grating Interferometry
authors: Lei Wang, Qingyang Zeng, Linbin Luo, Xinghui Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Precision displacement measurement is a core technology in high-end equipment manufacturing. Grating interferometry, particularly homodyne interferometry, represents a significant solution for precision displacement measurement. However, the practical accuracy of homodyne interferometry systems is largely limited by the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR). Addressing the issue of low-noise sampling and processing for homodyne grating interference signals, this paper proposes and implements a collaborative software-hardware design scheme. In terms of hardware, a precision Analog Front-End (AFE) sampling circuit is designed, adopting a five-fold noise reduction strategy to suppress noise introduction from the physical source as much as possible. In terms of software, a high-order Infinite Impulse Response (IIR) digital filter is introduced. This filter achieves steep frequency roll-off with extremely low computational latency and resource consumption, and allows for dynamic adjustment of the cutoff bandwidth to meet optimal SNR requirements under different measurement speeds. Finally, the scheme is validated through a grating measurement experimental platform. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed collaborative software-hardware low-noise processing scheme effectively guarantees signal integrity and high SNR, providing a reliable engineering approach for achieving nanometer-level precision measurement.

title: DMF:A plugin based on dynamic multi-scale fusion for multimodal sentiment analysis
authors: Jin Wu, Wentian Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Current multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) approaches based on large language models (LLMs) still face two major challenges: 1) fine-tuning LLMs for MSA tasks often undermines their inherent general-purpose capabilities, and 2) the large number of trainable parameters leads to excessive computational costs, making them impractical for resource-constrained environments. To address these issues, we propose DMF, a lightweight and pluggable adapter tailored for efficient multimodal sentiment analysis. Specifically, the proposed DMF introduces a Text-Guide-Mixer (TGM) that establishes explicit cross-modal interactions between textual and non-textual modalities via element-wise (Hadamard) operations, enabling better semantic alignment across modalities. Furthermore, a Mixture of Experts Fusion (MOEF) module is developed to dynamically integrate multi-scale features through adaptive gating mechanisms, allowing the model to flexibly capture complex cross-modal dependencies. The fused features are projected into pseudo-tokens and fed into a frozen LLM for sentiment prediction. Extensive experiments on both English (CMU-MOSEI) and Chinese (SIMS-v2) datasets demonstrate that DMF achieves competitive performance with minimal computational overhead, with only a small number of additional trainable parameters introduced.

title: Fault Ride-Through Strategy for DC Line Fault of HVDC Based on Reactive Current Control
authors: Yanbei Lyu, Dongbin Lu, Zhiling Huang, Xiang Yu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: When a DC line fault occurs in a high-voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission system, the excessive reactive power in the station during retard and deionization can easily cause overvoltage in the AC system. This article proposes a DC line fault ride-through strategy based on reactive current control. When a DC line fault is detected, both stations implement reactive current control, which can control the current flowing through the fault point to zero while keeping the AC voltage constant. For operation conditions that require blocking without subsequent restart, the reactive current command can be updated in realtime according to the operation status of the filters, and the HVDC system can be blocked after all filters have been cut off. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy was verified through simulation experiments.

title: DAGP2-YOLO: A Small Target Detection Algorithm for Moving Tennis Balls
authors: Jinfu Zhang, Kaizhe Zhan, Haowei Chen, Zhen Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: To achieve high-precision real-time moving tennis detection in tennis training facility scenarios, a high-accuracy tennis detection algorithm named DAGP2-YOLO is proposed. The method introduces a Deformable Attention Ghost (DAG) module that integrates deformable attention mechanisms with Ghost residual blocks, constructing a lightweight backbone network. Additionally, a small-object detection head (P2) is incorporated to enhance detection accuracy for moving tennis balls. Experimental results on the dataset demonstrate that compared to the baseline, DAGP2-YOLO shows improvements of 4.4% in precision and 11.3% in recall, exhibiting performance advantages over other algorithms.

title: Synthesis of Planar Sparse Arrays Based on Improved Dung Beetle Optimization Algorithm
authors: Yuxiao Li, Weizheng Ren
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In this letter, a method of combining the improved dung beetle optimization algorithm (DBO) with matrix mapping is proposed to optimize the sidelobe level (SLL) and directivity of sparse planar phased arrays. The algorithm is developed and improved based on the DBO by integrating multiple strategies, which enhances the diversity of the population and improves the algorithm's optimization seeking ability by improving the traditional algorithm. By combining matrix mapping with the improved algorithm, the difficulty and cost of array synthesis are reduced. The method can effectively synthesize wide-angle scanning sparse planar phased arrays with low PSLL and no significant directional degradation. The results are compared with other methods on two examples, the PSLL is reduced by about 0.65 dB and the directivity is improved by about 1.1 dBi, which verifies the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Research on Fault Diagnosis Method of Airborne IEEE 1394B Bus based on LSTM
authors: Jian Wu, Wei Niu, Hongyong An
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Traditional fault diagnosis methods relying on manual thresholding and time/frequency domain analysis have limitations in addressing nonlinear, time-varying, and gradual faults occurring in airborne IEEE 1394B buses under complex operating conditions, failing to meet the real-time and precision requirements of airborne systems. This paper proposes a fault diagnosis method based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. A multi-dimensional time-series data acquisition and preprocessing module is constructed to preprocess bus voltage signals, error frame counts, and other data, generating input tensors with integrated features through sliding time windows. The network architecture incorporates two LSTM hidden layers, combines dropout layers to prevent overfitting, introduces focal loss to address imbalanced fault samples, and employs Adam optimizer for model training. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 94.6% diagnostic accuracy, showing 5.7%-12.3% higher accuracy compared to SVM, BP neural networks, and RNNs, while achieving over 15% improvement in recall rate for gradual faults. These validations confirm the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: A Credible Beacon Based Trust Evaluation Model for Improving Communication Reliability of VANETs
authors: Changwang Cai, Li Liang, Xiao Liu, Jining Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In vehicular ad hoc networks (VANETs), the trust value of a vehicle is usually used to determine whether it serves as a communication node, which leads to a significant impact of trust evaluation on communication. In this paper, to improve the accuracy of trust evaluation, a credible beacon based trust evaluation model (CBTE) is proposed, which carefully filters the opinions of neighboring vehicle. Firstly, to comprehensively select beacons for trust evaluation, three factors of the beacon message are considered, which are expectation, risk, and confidence. Then, a beacon reliability evaluation algorithm is designed to analyze whether the beacon is trustworthy and to determine whether to adopt the opinion of the beacon. Due to the strict recommendation information screening, the accuracy of the proposed trust evaluation algorithm is high, which is beneficial for improving the success rate of communication in VANETs. Finally, experimental results verify the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: Trace-Aware LSTM Model Checking for Safety-Critical Systems
authors: Chaker Nasri, Ahlem Ben Younes, Leila Ben Ayed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid verification methodology integrating NuSMV symbolic model checking with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to alleviate scalability limitations in safety-critical systems. Temporal execution traces from NuSMV are leveraged to train LSTMs for intelligent filtering and prioritization of system paths, reducing verification workload by nearly half.A case study on an aircraft landing gear controller evaluates the CTL property AG (gear_down → AF gear_locked). The dataset, derived from correct and faulty SMV models, trains and validates the LSTM, achieving high predictive accuracy (>98%) and a significant reduction in verification effort.Our pipeline contributes three key aspects: (i) explainable predictions for fault localization, (ii) automated patching for model correction, and (iii) visualization for interpretable verification outcomes. Limitations and future directions, including transformer-based models and additional safety-critical benchmarks, are discussed.

title: An Edge-AI Adaptive Color Recognition System for Color Vision Deficiency: Simulation, Feedback, and Lightweight Deep Learning
authors: Sellam Aya, Bendjillali.Ridha.Ilyas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Color Vision Deficiency (CVD) affects a significant portion of the global population, limiting the ability to accurately distinguish between specific color channels. Existing assistive tools often lack adaptability, require expensive hardware, or rely on static thresholding. In this work, we propose an adaptive, simulation-based color recognition system designed for individuals with CVD. The system integrates perceptual distortion modeling, lightweight deep learning classifiers (MobileNetV2, CNN, TinyViT), and a real-time feedback loop that enables incremental personalization. A color-distorted dataset is generated using LMS-based simulation for protanopia, deuteranopia, and tritanopia. Classification performance is evaluated across multiple metrics, including F1-score, MCC, and AUROC, along with latency and energy profiling on Raspberry Pi and Jetson Nano. Results demonstrate high accuracy (≥95%) and enhanced personalization through user calibration. The proposed approach is scalable, user-centric, and optimized for real-time deployment in low-power assistive devices..

title: CAD Question Answering using Tool-Augmented Large Language Models
authors: Lufeng Wang, Ke Ke, Zhuo Song, Dehui Lu, Guozhong Cheng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Computer-Aided Design (CAD) is widely applied in both industrial and architectural design. Extracting information from CAD drawings is a fundamental upstream task; however, current practices mainly rely on manual visual inspection, where individuals search through dense textual and numerical data based on their own knowledge, resulting in low efficiency. To address this issue, this study proposes a CAD question-answering method based on large language models through prompt engineering and function calling. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves an answer accuracy of approximately 95%.

title: State Assessment and Maintenance Strategy Optimization for DC Grounding Electrode Systems Based on Multi-Parameter Monitoring and Analysis
authors: Yangzhi Wei, Guogen Li, Cheng Liu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The DC grounding electrode is a critical component for the safe and stable operation of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) transmission systems, with its condition directly impacting the safety of surrounding environments and equipment. Based on long-term operational maintenance practices for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 800 \text{kV}$</tex> HVDC project's grounding electrode system, this paper proposes an integrated methodology for state assessment and maintenance optimization. This method consolidates multi-source monitoring data, including electrode line impedance, soil characteristics of the electrode site, step voltage distribution, dynamic ground-return current, and groundwater level around the electrode ring. By establishing an early warning mechanism centered on step voltage safety thresholds, and integrating the principle of electrode overflow effects with seasonal hydrological patterns, in-depth analysis is conducted on insulation foundation performance degradation and electrode ring corrosion risk. Engineering practice demonstrates that the proposed method can effectively identify potential hazards, guide preventive maintenance, and shift the maintenance paradigm from reactive handling to proactive warning, significantly enhancing the operational reliability and safety of the grounding electrode system. It provides a valuable reference for the refined maintenance of similar projects.

title: A Single-Core Parallel Neuromorphic Accelerator for Event-Driven Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Chengxi Xiong, Liwei Meng, Pujun Zhou, Shaogang Hu, Guanchao Qiao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) offer a promising energy-efficient alternative to traditional Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), particularly for resource-constrained edge computing applications. However, as SNNs increase in scale and complexity, the demand for higher computational throughput presents a significant hardware design challenge, especially when computing more complex Spiking Convolutional Neural Networks(SCNNs). Existing parallel architectures, such as multi-core designs and systolic arrays, are often ill-suited for the sparse, event-driven nature of SNNs, leading to wasted computation and excessive power consumption from processing invalid data. To address this, we propose the SCNNs accelerator, a novel framework that implements a highly efficient ’single-core parallel’ strategy. Inspired by the functional modularity of the cerebral cortex, our architecture parallelizes only the computational logic while sharing a unified memory subsystem. This approach avoids the complex, power-hungry inter-core communication fabrics found in multi-core systems and the inherent inefficiency of systolic arrays when handling sparse data. By processing information only when triggered by input spike events, our design achieves a high throughput of 4.877 GSOP/s@100MHz and an energy efficiency of 2.358 pJ/SOP, creating a streamlined hardware solution that effectively balances parallelism with the asynchronous nature of SNNs.

title: Coverage and Rate Analysis of Follower-Based LEO Satellite Networks: A Stochastic Geometry Approach
authors: Juanjuan Ru, Ruibo Wang, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
abstract: To mitigate inter-satellite interference and payload limits in LEO mega-constellations, satellite clusters, groups of small cooperative satellites have been proposed to improve performance and reduce interference. The typical configuration divides the cluster into a leader satellite with full processing and control capabilities and multiple simpler follower satellites that assist with coverage and throughput. These clusters enhance coverage and throughput, prompting interest in their performance gains and optimal deployment. Given that the spherical stochastic geometry (SG) model has been proven effective for modeling such structures, we establish a performance evaluation framework based on the SG approach for the leader-follower satellite architecture, enabling an assessment of communication performance under different deployment configurations quantitatively. We derive analytical expressions for the outage probability and average data rate to evaluate the communication performance of the satellite system, along with low-complexity approximations. Numerical results demonstrate the performance advantages of the leader-follower architecture over a single leader satellite and explore optimal deployment configurations for the follower satellites.

title: Coupled-Interference Modeled FTN Signaling Over Doubly Selective Fading Channels: Joint Subpath Recovery and Iterative Detection
authors: Qiang Li, Yan Wang, Liping Li, Yingsong Li, Xingwang Li, Kai-Kit Wong, Chau Yuen
abstract: Faster-than-Nyquist (FTN) technique promises higher capacity and spectral efficiency for wireless communications. However, existing FTN studies over doubly-selective fading (DSF) channels separate channel-induced inter-symbol interference (channel-ISI) and FTN-induced ISI (FTN-ISI) to simplify cancellation. In practical DSF scenarios, the inherent coupling between FTN-ISI and channel-ISI causes significant performance degradation in conventional detection algorithms. To address this limitation, we first derive a practical FTN transmission model over DSF channels and construct the corresponding coupled interference matrix. Considering that data detection relies on efficient channel estimation, we propose a channel estimation algorithm with joint recovery of resolvable subpath parameters. This algorithm decomposes propagation paths into resolvable subpaths with independent delay-Doppler characteristics, achieving enhanced estimation accuracy through joint gain-phase optimization. Finally, building on the derived transceiver model and coupled interference matrix, we propose a whitening-enhanced orthogonal approximate message passing (WE-OAMP) algorithm that suppresses coupled interference through iterative linear-nonlinear estimation while maintaining spectral compactness. This algorithm constructs a whitening matrix using the FTN-ISI matrix to suppress noise correlation, and then performs detection through iterative linear and nonlinear estimation. Simulation results validate that the WE-OAMP algorithm outperforms benchmark algorithms in terms of bit error rate performance, especially in coded systems. Furthermore, we derive the achievable capacity of FTN signaling with WE-OAMP detection, demonstrating capacity improvement compared to Nyquist systems.

title: LMa-BMN: Local Motion-Aware Boundary Matching Network for Macro- and Micro-Expression Spotting
authors: Jiateng Liu, Hengcan Shi, Xiaolin Xu, Wen Liu, Feifan Wang, Yuyun Liu, Changyou Mu, Yaonan Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Facial expression spotting aims to locate the intervals at which expressions occur in long videos, which is a challenging issue that has attracted the attention of many researchers in recent years. Unlike temporal action locations, the movements of macro and micro expressions are more subtle, which makes it extremely difficult to spot them accurately. However, existing spotting methods usually focus on introducing expression-related cues like AUs to help with spotting, but are unable to generate reliable starting and end scores to represent the whole occurrence of expressions. To handle the crucial facial expression spotting issue, in this paper, we propose a novel Local Motion-Aware Boundary Matching Network (LMa-BMN) by utilizing a boundary matching network and focusing on the subtle motion information in the long video of the face. We first aggregate the local facial motion information through a rank-pooling approach by a local motion representation module, then the BMN generates starting and ending probability scores for the local intervals as well as boundary-matching maps for the global sequence. The proposed framework is evaluated on the MEGC2022 Test Set, and it outperforms the baseline method significantly, which demonstrates that it can address the crucial macro- and micro-expression spotting issues more efficiently.

title: Complex Regional Power Grid Voltage and Reactive Power Automatic Control Technology Based on Edge Computing
authors: Yifan Yang, Bin Zhao, Yi Li, Jia Wang, Hancong Ma, Zhenyu Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: High penetration of distributed energy and complex loads cause grid voltage fluctuations, low reactive power efficiency and equipment overload problems. This paper proposes a three-level collaborative control architecture based on edge computing. By deploying edge units at key nodes, data collection and local optimization are realized; the regional layer integrates multi-node data to build a digital twin model, and uses federated learning to achieve cross-node reactive power collaboration; the master station layer dynamically adjusts the strategy weight. Experiments show that the voltage qualification rate of this method reaches 98.5% in load mutation scenarios, the reactive power compensation efficiency is stable at 92.8% in N-1 line fault scenarios, and the transformer load peak is reduced. This verifies its high efficiency and stability in complex power grid environments, and provides an innovative solution for the safe and stable operation of power grids under high penetration of distributed energy access.

title: Impact of Train-Test Splitting Ratios on Machine Learning Models for Daily Electricity Load Time Series Forecasting
authors: Camellia Adelsa Indranie, Ardiansyah Ramadhan, Jangkung Raharjo, Rifki Rahman Nur Ikhsan, Muhammad Faiz Rohadi, Dzikry Ilham Anandaputra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: Electricity load forecasting is crucial for energy management because it ensures grid stability, reduces costs, and optimizes resources. Forecast accuracy is strongly influenced by methodological choices, particularly the train-test split ratio that balances training adequacy and evaluation reliability. This study investigates the effect of different split ratios on daily electricity load forecasting using several machine learning models, namely Linear Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM. Load data were aggregated at one, three, six, twelve, and twenty-four-hour levels. Feature engineering included temporal indicators, lag features with multiple steps, and rolling means. Three train-test ratios of 70:30, 80:20, and 90:10 were compared. Ensemble models were optimized using Optuna, while Linear Regression was applied without tuning. Model performance was evaluated using Mean Absolute Error, Root Mean Squared Error, Mean Absolute Percentage Error, and the Coefficient of Determination. The results indicate that split ratio selection significantly affects forecasting outcomes. The best performance was achieved by XGBoost with a 70:30 split and one-hour aggregation, improving Mean Absolute Error from 204.53 to 197.47. These findings demonstrate that appropriate data partitioning combined with model tuning enhances forecasting accuracy and provides practical guidance for developing robust forecasting systems.

title: UB-PPG: An U-net and BiLSTM Paralleled Network for PPG Based Fatigue Detection
authors: Xingyu Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Fatigue degrades cognitive performance and poses severe safety risks in critical domains such as transportation and healthcare. This study presents a non-invasive fatigue-detection framework based on photoplethysmography-derived pulse rate variability (PPG-PRV) signals. To capture fatigue-related dynamics, time–frequency analysis is applied using Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) and Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) with Marr and Morlet wavelets. A hybrid deep architecture combining a U-Net convolutional encoder and a bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) sequence model jointly learns spatial–spectral patterns and temporal dependencies from PRV signals. Experiments on a dataset labeled via a Psychomotor Vigilance Task (PVT) demonstrate that STFT-based features achieve the best performance, reaching 88.2% accuracy and 88.8% Macro-F1, outperforming both CWT variants while maintaining minimal computational cost (18 ms per inference). These results confirm that the STFT’s uniform spectral resolution aligns optimally with fatigue-related frequency bands, enabling efficient and accurate monitoring. Overall, the proposed framework achieves real-time, non-invasive detection of cognitive fatigue, offering practical potential for continuous human-state monitoring in safety-critical applications.

title: Magnetic Field Distribution and Safety Protection Strategy of Bridge Arm Reactors in VSC-HVDC Converter Stations
authors: Yihui Zheng, Xiaoyang Bai, Jiexiong Xu, Kejie Huang, Haijuan Lan, Ting Hou
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The operational safety of maintenance personnel in VSC-HVDC converter stations represents a critical concern, particularly due to strong leakage magnetic fields generated by dry-type air-core bridge arm reactors. Existing magnetic shielding designs relying on finite element analysis often involve extended development periods and substantial computational costs. This study presents an efficient simulation-based framework for magnetic field assessment and safety demarcation. A simplified three-phase 3D model is developed to characterize the spatial distribution of magnetic fields, with computational analysis performed using the self-developed multi-physics platform TR-Sim V2024. Numerical simulations focus on evaluating the magnetic field intensity at a representative height of 1.8 m above ground level, corresponding to the typical standing position of personnel. Based on industry regulatory standards, a safety protection strategy is formulated, including the implementation of restricted zones bounded by peripheral fencing aligned with specified magnetic field thresholds. The proposed approach offers a practical and time-efficient solution for occupational magnetic field exposure control in converter station environments.

title: Hierarchical Line Feature Matching in Complex Scenes: From Motion Consistency Clustering to Point-Line Geometric Verification
authors: Yufan Ge, Chenyang Zhang, Jianghua Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Line feature matching is a fundamental task in computer vision, with wide applications in 3D reconstruction, image registration, and visual positioning. Traditional methods, often reliant on local feature descriptors or pre-defined geometric models, frequently struggle in the presence of large-scale scaling, non-rigid transformations, or sparse texture scenes. This paper proposes a robust line feature matching method that combines spatial clustering of point features with point-line geometric invariants. First, an improved RFM-SCAN algorithm is employed to spatially cluster initial point feature matches, effectively purifying them by enforcing motion consistency. Subsequently, for the line feature matching, the core innovation is introduced: a dual-invariant construction comprising a point-to-line distance ratio invariant and a direction vector invariant. High-accuracy line correspondences are then achieved by leveraging these dual invariants in conjunction with rigorous geometric consistency constraints. Experimental results demonstrate that our method maintains high matching accuracy and robustness across various challenging conditions—including scale and rotation changes, occlusion, illumination variation, and low-texture scenes—without relying on GPU acceleration or large-scale training data.

title: Reinforcement Learning in TrackMania
authors: Guanchen Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This project aimed to train agents in TrackMania Nations Forever, a racing game in which vehicles traverse courses and compete for the best lap time. We developed agents trained using reinforcement learning to simulate human gameplay, with the objective of completing the course in the shortest time possible. Our implementation, based on Deep Q-Learning, produced lap times that were comparable to those of human experts and demonstrated generalisation characteristics that enabled the agents to complete unfamiliar courses. Moreover, the agents exhibited performance stability that surpassed human consistency.

title: Optimization of Time Series Statistical Prediction Model Integrating Transformer Structure
authors: Yumeng Yan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This work suggests a BiLSTM- ASSAM-Transformer model to overcome the shortcomings in modeling mixed temporal patterns in long-term power load forecasting and the inefficiencies in global attention computing. By combining an Adaptive Sparse Self-Attention Mechanism (ASSAM) for dynamically choosing important long-term dependencies through top-k sparsification with a Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) layer for capturing local short-term fluctuations. The model outperforms state-of-the-art baselines with a reaction time of 16.9 seconds, achieving RMSE, MAE, and MAPE values of 826.78 MW, 635.42 MW, and 2.44%, respectively, when tested on five years of PJM grid data. The contributions of each module and the model's capacity to concentrate on important periodic patterns are validated by ablation tests and attention visualizations. This study provides a precise and effective power load forecasting method.

title: Channel large-scale separable convolution assisted underwater image enhancement
authors: Qilin Jin, Qingbang Han, Jiayu Xia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: To solve the degradation problem attributed to the scattering and absorption of light by small substances in the underwater environment, an underwater image network architecture with channel large-scale separable convolution is proposed. The paper combines the channel attention mechanism with the long-distance perception capability of large-scale separable convolutions to design a large-scale separable module. The module is integrated with feature extraction, such as the adaptive selection internal supervision module and bifocal context attention module. Meanwhile, the loss function is optimized to significantly enhance the quality of underwater images and achieve details. Maintain global consistency, especially in color fidelity. Across three public datasets, the method achieves improvements in key metrics, including PSNR, BRISQUE, and SSIM. It strikes a balance between performance and efficiency while providing technical support for object recognition and segmentation in complex underwater scenes.

title: Electromagnetic Noise Suppression in High-Speed PMSM Using D-Axis Harmonic Injection Strategy
authors: Yifan Yuan, Jun Diao, Yanan Cheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: High-speed permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) are widely used in medical devices and other fields due to their high power density. However, their inherent high frequency electromagnetic vibration and noise significantly compromise system reliability and acoustic performance. To address this issue, this paper proposes a direct d-axis harmonic injection method that eliminates the need for additional harmonic detection and extraction modules. By establishing a direct mapping between noise orders and injection frequencies, the method injects predefined harmonics into the d-axis. Leveraging coordinate transformation, it generates counter electromagnetic force waves that suppress noise at its source while effectively reducing system complexity and computational burden. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed method achieves significant suppression of specific-order electromagnetic noise on a turbo blower platform. The method provides a structurally simple and effective low noise solution, with analysis of long-term operation informing guidelines for robust implementation.

title: LINK-KG: LLM-Driven Coreference-Resolved Knowledge Graphs for Human Smuggling Networks
authors: Dipak Meher, Carlotta Domeniconi, Guadalupe Correa-Cabrera
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Human smuggling networks are complex and constantly evolving, making them difficult to analyze comprehensively. Legal case documents offer rich factual and procedural insights into these networks but are often long, unstructured, and filled with ambiguous or shifting references, posing significant challenges for automated knowledge graph (KG) construction. Existing methods either overlook coreference resolution or fail to scale beyond short text spans, leading to fragmented graphs and inconsistent entity linking. We propose LINK-KG, a modular framework that integrates a three-stage, LLM-guided coreference resolution pipeline with downstream KG extraction. At the core of our approach is a type-specific Prompt Cache, which consistently tracks and resolves references across document chunks, enabling clean and disambiguated narratives for structured knowledge graph construction from both short and long legal texts. LINK-KG reduces average node duplication by 45.21% and noisy nodes by 32.22% compared to baseline methods, resulting in cleaner and more coherent graph structures. These improvements establish LINK-KG as a strong foundation for analyzing complex criminal networks<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>Note: All example names in this paper are anonymized or fictitious to protect privacy. Full proper names and company names are replaced with initials followed by a period (e.g., “L.R.C.”)..

title: Adaptive vs Static Hyperparameter Optimization Across CNN and Transformer Models
authors: Nam-Thuan Trinh, Phi-Hung Hoang, Thi-Thu-Hong Phan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This study investigates the effectiveness of adaptive multi-fidelity hyperparameter optimization (HPO) strategies in deep learning model tuning. A comprehensive empirical comparison was conducted among four widely used HPO algorithms-Random Search (RS), Tree-structured Parzen Estimator (TPE), Hyperband, and Asynchronous Successive Halving Algorithm (ASHA)-across three modern architectures: EfficientNet-B0, DenseNet-121, and Swin-Tiny. Experimental results on a real-world dataset show that adaptive resource allocation strategies (Hyperband, ASHA) consistently outperform static and probabilistic approaches (RS, TPE) in both accuracy and computational efficiency. Specifically, Hyperband achieved the highest accuracy on Swin-Tiny (84.22%) and DenseNet- 121(83.74%), while ASHA reached 83.57% on EfficientNet-B0 with substantially lower training time. Moreover, the relative advantage of each optimizer varies across architectures, suggesting an interaction between optimization strategy and network design. These findings demonstrate that adaptive HPO methods are not only more effective overall but also provide practical insights for scalable and efficient model tuning in agricultural computer vision.

title: Air-to-Ground Channels in Tropical Archipelagos:A Systematic Review for In-Flight Connectivity over Eastern Indonesia
authors: Iswidodo, Fadli Sirait, Setiyo Budiyanto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This systematic literature review (SLR) maps and synthesizes Air-to-Ground (A2G) channel models for In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) in tropical settings typified by Eastern Indonesia, where complex topography, dense vegetation, and dynamic weather challenge classical propagation assumptions. Following PRISMA/PICOS, we screened publications (Jan-2015-Mar-2025) from IEEE Xplore, ScienceDirect, and SpringerLink; $\mathbf{8 4}$ studies were included from $\mathbf{1 0 0}$ records. The review focuses on peer-reviewed studies from major commercial databases; thus, emerging repositories (arXiv, EuCAP, ICUAS) and non-English regional datasets remain outside this scope. We classify approaches by method (empirical, deterministic/raytracing, hybrid, AI/ML), environment, and platform. Results show a persistent dominance of empirical/review studies ($\approx 45 \%$) and continued reliance on FSPL/Rician models, which underrepresent tropical phenomena (rain/humidity attenuation, ocean multipath, terrain shadowing). Research is skewed to lowaltitude UAVs ($\lt 200 \mathrm{~m}$; 53.6%), with limited evidence at commercial cruise altitudes. The paucity of open SE-Asia A2G datasets further constrains contextual AI models. We argue for a paradigm shift to context-aware, weather-informed tropical models, recommending (i) integration of high-fidelity geospatial and real-time meteorological data and (ii) hybrid validation that fuses physical ray tracing with data-driven learning. These insights provide an evidence map, highlight actionable gaps, and inform the design of more precise and adaptive A2G-IFC systems for tropical air routes. Limitations: three databases; 2015-2025 window.

title: Integrating Household Carbon Emission Accounting With Demand-Side Management: Feature Extraction and Analysis of Users in Power System
authors: Chun Wang, Yuxiong Huang, Xuanman Rong, Gengfeng Li, Binrong Zhang, Maoguang Qiu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Since the late 1990s, global warming has become a central concern worldwide, largely driven by greenhouse gas emissions from human activities, with residential energy use representing a significant share.firstly This study develops a comprehensive carbon emission accounting framework for residential electricity consumers within power systems by integrating emission factor and input-output approaches to quantify both direct and indirect emissions. Besides, to enable cross-user comparisons and enhance demand-side analysis, a max-min normalization scoring system combined with mRMRbased feature selection was applied for extracting key determinants of household carbon emissions. Finally, leveraging actual data from over 21,000 Chinese households, the study identifies six representative demand-side carbon emission profiles, highlighting the diversity of residential energy consumption patterns. These findings provide a data-driven foundation for targeted emission reduction strategies, demandside management programs, and personalized low-carbon policy design in modern power systems.

title: Fault Tolerant Control for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Formation Based on Distributed Nonlinear Model Predictive Control
authors: Jinghe Hu, Bin Xian, Peng Shao
abstract: This article proposes a new fault-tolerant obstacle avoidance and trajectory tracking strategy for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) formations. The proposed method integrates deep neural networks (DNNs) with distributed nonlinear model predictive control (DNMPC) to handle actuator faults and collision risks in complex environments. The DNNs serve as the primary component for fault-dynamics modeling and real-time compensation, which ensures the system's stability through adaptive weight updating. The DNMPC framework utilizes state information from neighbors to predict future states and compute optimal control inputs. This approach reduces computational and communication burdens and enables real-time operation. A terminal cost function and terminal set are designed to guarantee the system's stability. Comparative simulations and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed methodology improves the trajectory–tracking accuracy and formation-maintenance performance. The formation retains full tracking capability under actuator faults, which validates the approach's effectiveness and practicality in challenging environments.

title: Optimized Design and Cryogenic Characterization of Flip-Chip InP-Based Balanced UWB BPF
authors: Hongliang Tian, Haiwen Liu, Ken Ning, Shaofei Wang, Chaoyun Song, Xuehui Guan, Wenzhuo Zhang, Junfa Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: In this article, a flip-chip InP-based balanced ultrawideband (UWB) bandpass filter (BPF) is optimally designed and characterized under cryogenic conditions. The filter chip is designed using spiral inductors and metal–insulator–metal (MIM) capacitors. An electromagnetic (EM)-circuit co-optimization method is introduced to assist in the design process, and the gray wolf algorithm is adopted to iteratively refine variables during each optimization stage. The filter die is then flip-chip bonded to a microstrip-line (MSL) structure on an alumina ceramic substrate. The final filter module is experimentally characterized at 300, 70, and 5K. Measurements show minimal variations in differential-mode (DM) center frequency (15.45 GHz), bandwidth (101.9%), out-of-band suppression, and common-mode rejection ratio (CMRR) (20 dB in the range of 0–24.5 GHz) across different temperatures. Notably, the minimum DM in-band insertion loss (IL) decreases from 1.02 dB at 300 K to 0.72 dB at 70 K, with improvements of 0.3–0.8 dB across the passband. Analysis of the temperature distribution and deformation shows that the reduction in IL is primarily attributed to the increased conductivity of gold at low temperature, which significantly outweighs the effects of dielectric loss and structural deformation.

title: Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)

title: DPM-RPL: Routing-Based Distributed Dynamic Power Management for NVP IoT Networks
authors: Bardia Safaei, Mohammadali Khodabandelou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: The RPL routing protocol suffers from inefficient energy consumption, often leading to energy holes in IoT networks. Although RDC-based routing protocols try to reduce energy consumption by accounting for MAC radio duty-cycling, their gains are limited to transceiver-level optimization. These approaches neglect system-level power management, and do not push IoT devices into deep sleep state, keeping the processor, memory, and peripherals active because nodes are assumed unable to retain routing state when powered down. Achieving deeper energy savings requires preserving routing state across power cycles, which is only possible with Non-Volatile Memory (NVM). Building on this observation, this paper proposes DPM-RPL, a distributed routing-based Dynamic Power Management protocol for Non-Volatile Processor (NVP) IoT networks. By embedding system-level power management directly into the network layer, DPM-RPL enables nodes to safely enter deep sleep states while retaining essential routing and operational states. DPM-RPL revolutionizes energy savings by allowing the device’s always-on components to be fully powered down. To provide routing state retention and node synchronization across power cycles, DPM-RPL introduces a novel Deep Sleep Cycle (DSC) metric, enabling nodes to estimate the sleep and active periods of candidate parents and select those with the longest overlapping active durations. This metric improves synchronization between nodes, reducing missed channel checks, strobe-induced energy drain, retransmissions, network disconnections, and control over-head, thereby improving energy efficiency, Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR), and network stability. Additionally, when calculating DSC, DPM-RPL uses Exponential Moving Average (EMA) forecasting to estimate the time between application events, allowing it to adjust sleep and wake periods to match activity patterns. This approach minimizes energy waste due to peripheral reinitializations and excessive cold start and ramp-up phases of critical hardware components such as phase-locked loops, providing energy-efficiency, communication reliability, and reduced latency. DPM-RPL primarily targets dynamic energy reduction, while the incorporation of NVM further decreases energy consumption by minimizing leakage during deep sleep. Extensive simulations demonstrate that DPM-RPL reduces average energy consumption by up to 2.4× (140%) compared to the state of the art, while improving PDR by 37%, reducing latency by more than threefold, and decreasing retransmissions by 57%.

title: Electromagnetic Field in Multilayered Rough Medium by Generalized Reflection/Transmission Method
authors: Kai Zhao, Decheng Hong, Xiaofei Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In this work, we propose a semi-analytical method for electromagnetic (EM) fields in the multilayered media with two-dimensional (2D) rough interfaces (TDRI). Unlike previous physical models that address 2D EM fields scattered by rough interfaces from a plane wave source, the EM field to be solved here is radiated by triaxial magnetic dipoles embedded in any layer. To efficiently solve such an asymmetric three-dimensional (3D) EM field, we divided it into upgoing and downgoing waves in the spectral domain, then those upgoing/downgoing waves in adjacent layers are analytically connected by the generalized transmission and reflection coefficients crossing the rough boundary zones. Affected by rough boundaries, the transmission and reflection coefficients are determined using the surface integral equation. In the course of solving integral equations, we tackle the challenges associated with the extended boundary conditions and mathematical techniques related to the matrix equations. The numerical results compared with the finite element method (FEM) validated our formulations. The provided method can be widely used for forward modeling and inversion in geophysical prospecting.

title: Detection of Wire Lift-off in Si-IGBTs and SiC-MOSFETs Using Machine Learning on Switching Waveforms
authors: Kazune Idei, Thatree Mamee, Hauke Lutzen, Nando Kaminski, Katsuhiro Hata, Makoto Takamiya, Shin-ichi Nishizawa, Wataru Saito
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Power cycling degradation in power modules is one of the most common failure modes in power electronic systems. This study presents a method to detect wire lift-off using machine learning analysis of switching waveforms of gate voltage and emitter/source current in Si-IGBT and SiC-MOSFET power modules. Five key aspects are investigated as follows: (1) evaluation of different detection signals and their combinations, (2) segment-wise analysis to identify waveform regions that contribute most to classification, (3) impact of waveform sampling rate on detection accuracy, (4) validation using mixed datasets from multiple modules, and (5) comparison between Si-IGBT and SiC-MOSFET devices. The turn-off gate voltage waveforms provide the highest accuracy, and a high accuracy of about 99 % was achieved even with downsampling from 2.5 GS/s to 100 MS/s. The mixed datasets show no degradation in accuracy, and the optimum gate drive condition for high accuracy differs between Si-IGBTs and SiC-MOSFETs. Overall, the proposed method achieves over 99 % classification accuracy, demonstrating its potential for reliable and low-cost monitoring of power device degradation.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers

title: Augmented High-Resolution Range Profile for Recurrent Neural Network-Based People Counting Using Millimeter-Wave FMCW MIMO Radar
authors: Massala Mboyi Gilles Yowel, Yong-Il Park, Jung-Hoon Han
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This study proposes a novel framework to enhance automatic people counting using millimeter-wave frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) multiple-input–multiple-output (MIMO) radar. The approach leverages an augmented high-resolution range profile (HRRP) as input to a recurrent neural network (RNN). Conventional single-channel HRRP inputs often lead to instability during training and suboptimal performance due to radar-specific limitations, such as false alarm, missed detection, and limited resolution in distinguishing closely spaced targets, particularly in regions where moving target trajectories intersect. To address this limitation, we introduce a multichannel input representation referred to as the augmented HRRP, comprising the in-phase and quadrature (I/Q) components of the time-domain radar signal, as well as the magnitude and phase of the corresponding HRRP. The proposed model is evaluated using a hold-out validation scheme on real-world radar measurements collected across 11 scenarios involving dynamic targets, with the number of individuals varying from 0 to 10. The highest performance is achieved using a hybrid convolutional neural network-gated recurrent unit (CNN-GRU) architecture, yielding a peak accuracy of 95.07%, a macro-averaged precision of 0.9511, a macro-averaged recall of 0.9502, and a macro-averaged <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$F1$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-score of 0.9506.

title: Meta-Reinforcement Learning for Fast and Data-Efficient Spectrum Allocation in Dynamic Wireless Networks
authors: Oluwaseyi Giwa, Tobi Ebenezer Awodumila, Muhammad Ahmed Mohsin, Ahsan Bilal, Muhammad Ali Jamshed
abstract: Efficient spectrum allocation is vital for 5G/6G networks, yet traditional deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods suffer from high sample complexity and unsafe exploration that can disrupt network stability. To address these challenges, we propose a meta-learning framework that learns a robust initial policy capable of rapid and safe adaptation to changing wireless conditions. We implement three meta-learning architectures using model-agnostic techniques—model-agnostic meta-learning (MAML), recurrent neural network (RNN), and RNN with a self-attention mechanism—and compare them against a DRL baseline and classical heuristic approaches in a dynamic integrated access/ backhaul (IAB) environment. The attention-based agent achieves a peak throughput of ≈ 49 Mbps, reducing SINR and latency violations by over 60% relative to PPO, and attains 97% of the fairness level of the exhaustive-search upper bound. These results demonstrate that meta-learning enables data-efficient, reliable, and scalable spectrum management for next-generation wireless systems.

title: Dynamic State Space Models with Temporal Adaptation for Time-Varying Sequence Modeling
authors: Tongyi Liang, Han-Xiong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Real-world dynamic systems often exhibit time-varying behavior. While state space models (SSMs) have shown great potential for sequence modeling, especially in capturing long-range dependencies, most previous studies have been limited to time-invariant dynamics. To overcome this limitation, we propose a neural network architecture based on time-varying SSMs with dynamics that evolve over time, called dynamic SSMs. To enhance scalability and efficiency, several techniques are introduced, including sparsification strategy via diagonalization and fast tensor convolution with quasi-linear complexity in sequence length. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets show that the proposed model consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, the model achieves significantly lower time and space complexity compared to architectures such as Transformer and LSTM. This work advances the theoretical foundation of SSMs-based neural networks in deep learning and promotes their further development. Code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/leonty1/dssm</uri> <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/leonty1/dssm</uri>.

title: Inter-User Interference Analysis in a Point-to-Multipoint Sub-THz Wireless Communication Link
authors: J. Dittmer, A. Bhutani, C. Bremauer, F. Beuthan, J. Seidel, S. Wagner, A. Tessmann, T. Zwick, C. Koos, S. Randel
abstract: This paper presents the analysis of the inter-user interference (IUI) in a multiuser wireless link operating in the sub-THz frequency range of 270 GHz to 330 GHz. The link utilizes an optoelectronic transmitter (Tx) comprising two integrated external cavity lasers with a wide tuning range, a broadband electro-optic modulator, and a uni-traveling carrier photodiode with a WR3.4 output. A conformal lens-integrated WR3.4 frequency beam-steering antenna transmits the signal to seven users distributed in space. At the user side, an electronic sub-THz receiver is used for the wireless link. A finite portion of the Rx bandwidth (270 GHz to 330 GHz) is allocated to each user. The power ratio between the individual user channels is experimentally evaluated in three different test scenarios, operating at carrier frequencies of 290 GHz, 300 GHz, and 310 GHz, respectively. This evaluation is based on the frequency response analysis of the optoelectronic Tx, the electronic Rx, and the radiation characteristics of the frequency beam-steering antenna. To the best of our knowledge, this work presents the first IUI analysis in a sub-THz wireless link operating over a distance of 10 m, with a record high gross aggregated data rate of 140 Gbps across seven users. A worst-case power ratio of -2.5 dB between adjacent channels and an average power ratio of -11.3 dB is observed, highlighting the potential of sub-THz communication for high-data-rate multiuser networks.

title: Robust Detection of Dummy Data Attacks in Smart Grids via Transformer-Based Reinforcement Learning Under Partial Observability
authors: Zhixun Zhang, Jianquan Lu, Jianqiang Hu, Josep M. Guerrero
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Smart grids, as critical cyber-physical systems (CPSs) integrating advanced sensing, communication, and control technologies, face heightened vulnerability to sophisticated cyber threats. Among these emerging threats, dummy data attacks (DDAs) are becoming increasingly prominent due to their unique evasion capabilities. Unlike traditional false data injection attacks (FDIAs), DDAs optimize attack vectors to statistically mimic normal measurement data, thereby effectively bypassing existing data-driven detection mechanisms. To address this challenge, this article presents a unified detection framework for DDAs. First, a multiconstraint optimization model is constructed to simulate the generation process of stealthy DDAs. Second, a multidimensional feature extraction framework based on graph spectral wavelet transforms, topological data analysis, and centrality metrics is developed to capture subtle perturbations in voltage signals from multiple perspectives, including frequency domain, spatial structure, and topological geometry. Third, a partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP) is integrated with a transformer-based reinforcement learning (RL) architecture, enabling robust and adaptive detection under partial observability conditions. Furthermore, uncertainty estimation mechanisms are incorporated to quantify detection confidence, coupled with spatial consistency constraints to enhance decision reliability. Finally, simulation results on IEEE benchmark bus systems demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed detection method.

title: CF-GAT: Curvature-fused Graph Attention Network for High-Precision Unordered Facial Point Cloud Landmark Detection
authors: Juncheng Han, Yuping Ye, Xintong Yang, Juan Zhao, Zhan Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Due to the lack of large-scale, accurately annotated 3D facial datasets, most current 3D facial landmark detection algorithms rely on 2D texture assistance or non-real digital 3D faces. The performance of these algorithms is limited by the accuracy of texture mapping and the difference between digital faces and actual faces. To tackle these challenges, we built a large-scale, high-precision, and accurately annotated 3D facial database using a structured light system. Building upon this foundation, we proposed a novel point cloud sampling method and 3D facial landmark detection algorithm. This method utilizes a curvature-fused graph attention network to directly predict landmark coordinates from 3D point clouds. Firstly, we extracted a simplifying point set carrying curvature information from the original 3D facial point cloud via geometric point sampling. Then, we incorporated curvature-encoded positional information as the learning component of the attention module and employed it as a feature extractor to construct the network. We evaluated the performance of the method on BU-3DFE, FaceScape and our custom dataset. Compared to existing facial landmark detection algorithms, our model achieved higher accuracy. To facilitate future research on face related applications, we have made the database available on Github <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: Physics-Aware Multi-Feature Fusion Approach for Robust Channel Estimation
authors: Jiancheng Chen, Jiuwu Zhang, Bojun Zhang, Xiaomin Zhou, Mingli Feng, Keqiu Li
abstract: Accurate CSI feedback is crucial for Massive MIMO systems, yet it remains challenging in resource-constrained IoT scenarios due to strict pilot overhead constraints. Under such extreme data sparsity, conventional data-driven methods often fail to generalize. To address this, this paper proposes a Physics-Aware Multi-Feature fusion approach (PAMF), a deep learning framework that systematically integrates data-driven learning with wireless propagation physics. The framework includes dedicated feature extractors based on spatial, frequency-domain, statistical, and physics-based methods, along with a deep residual reconstruction network. A key innovation lies in its dual-level physical constraint mechanism, which incorporates domain knowledge at both the feature and loss levels to ensure physically plausible channel estimates. By leveraging multi-modal feature representations and physics-aware optimization, PAMF effectively recovers the complete channel matrix from sparse pilot signals, which not only improves feature discrimination, but also leads to greater robustness particularly under the dynamic conditions typical of urban mobile networks. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms existing approaches across diverse datasets including MIMO configurations of various scales, different modulation schemes, and real-world Wi-Fi CSI Specifically, on real-world Wi-Fi data, PAMF achieves an NMSE of 0.1002, approximately 62% lower than the ChannelNet baseline (0.2669). Overall, this study contributes a practical and physical-aware framework for channel estimation, paving the way for more reliability and efficiency next-generation wireless systems, with direct implications for large-scale IoT deployments.

title: High-Speed Machine Design for Electric Turbochargers Based on General Air-gap Field Modulation Theory
authors: Wenfei Yu, Jiawei Zhou, Xianyu Yan, Shangjian Dai, Wei Hua
abstract: With the development of the automotive industry towards higher efficiency and lower emissions, electric turbocharged high-speed machines (HSMs) have garnered significant attention due to their potential to enhance power performance and fuel economy. This paper investigates a 5kW, 100,000r/min high-speed permanent magnet synchronous machine (HSPMSM) for electric turbochargers, focusing on its electromagnetic and mechanical performance. Firstly, based on the general air-gap field modulation theory (GAFMT), this study proposes a theoretical analysis method for cogging torque that considers machine slot-pole combinations. It reveals the relationship between magnetic field harmonics generated by modulation behavior and torque ripple, while elucidating the significance of magnetic field modulation in cogging torque generation. The proposed method is validated by comparing the results from finite element analysis (FEA), a preliminary selection of slot-pole combinations with low cogging torque and torque ripple is conducted. Secondly, a systematic and in-depth comparative analysis is conducted on six different slot-pole combinations, covering key performance indices such as no-load back electromotive force (EMF), cogging torque, losses, torque, and torque ripple. Moreover, the rotor's mechanical stress and dynamic performance under ultra-high-speed conditions are also evaluated. The most suitable slot-pole combination for electric turbocharger applications is identified through a comprehensive evaluation of electromagnetic performance, efficiency, and manufacturability. Finally, a prototype machine is manufactured, and experimental validation confirms the accuracy of the proposed analysis and modeling methods.

title: Development of Energy Management Experimental System for PV-Storage-Charging-Hydrogen Integrated Stations Based on Low Voltage DC Technology
authors: Yufei Wang, Shiqi Liu, Yiyun Tu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: In order to achieve efficient utilization of renewable energy and multi-energy complementarity, this paper designs and develops an energy management system for the PV-storage-charging-hydrogen integrated station based on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 8 V}$</tex> low-voltage direct current (LVDC) technology. The system integrates photovoltaic power generation, battery storage, DC charging piles, and water electrolysis hydrogen production units, forming a localized DC low-voltage system that effectively reduces the energy losses associated with multi-stage power conversion in traditional AC systems. This paper provides a detailed description of the system's overall architecture, the hardware platform based on STM32F4 and industrial control computers, the software monitoring platform developed in C# and the core energy management strategies. Experimental tests are conducted under three typical operating conditions: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> smoothing, distribution grid congestion and voltage sag. The results show that the energy management system can effectively smooth photovoltaic fluctuations and alleviate grid congestion, validating the feasibility and effectiveness of the LVDC architecture in enhancing system efficiency and operational flexibility.

title: The Well-Posedness of the Converter Transformer and Its Relay Protection System
authors: Zhixin Zhao, Xinmeng Chen, Na Li
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: This paper investigates a converter transformer together with its relay protection system, where the protection scheme comprises a single primary protection and a backup protection. By utilizing the augmented variable method in conjunction with the system state transition process, the state equations describing the system are derived. Furthermore, the system is reformulated as an abstract Cauchy problem in a Banach space, and its well-posedness is established using the theory of operator semigroups. The well-posedness of the system fundamentally guarantees both the mathematical rigor and physical soundness of the system equations, thereby establishing a solid foundation for precise reliability metrics computation and practical risk assessment.

title: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning with Zero-Order Optimization for Scalable AI Models in Signal and Network Processing
authors: Junming Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: The deployment of large-scale AI models in resource-constrained environments such as edge devices for signal and network processing poses significant challenges due to substantial computational and memory requirements. While Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) methods have emerged as promising solutions, they often rely on gradient-based optimization which remains memory-intensive for ultra-large models. This paper introduces a novel Hessian-informed zeroth-order optimization framework (HiZOO) that integrates with PEFT techniques to achieve memory-efficient adaptation of massive models while maintaining competitive performance. Our approach leverages diagonal Hessian information to enhance zeroth-order optimization, addressing the challenge of heterogeneous parameter curvatures without requiring expensive backpropagation. Extensive experiments on diverse signal processing tasks demonstrate that HiZOO reduces memory usage by up to 70% compared to conventional PEFT methods while achieving accuracy improvements of up to 5.8% on challenging radio frequency signal classification and network traffic analysis tasks. The proposed method enables scalable deployment of large AI models across heterogeneous edge devices, effectively bridging the gap between model performance and resource constraints in practical signal and network processing applications.

title: Spinal Magnetic Resonance Imaging Super-Resolution Based on Improved Latent Diffusion Models
authors: Zhan Wang, Junfeng Jiang, Zhengming Chen, Jie Chen, Yanan Du, Feng Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: The inherent trade-off between scanning speed and image quality in Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) poses significant challenges for the clinical diagnosis of spinal pathologies. To address this, we propose a high-fidelity super-resolution reconstruction framework based on Latent Diffusion Models (LDMs), specifically tailored for spinal MRI. Distinct from traditional pixel-space approaches, our method transfers the generative process to a compressed latent space, achieving an optimal balance between texture preservation and computational complexity reduction. Specifically, we first design an autoencoder incorporating perceptual and adversarial losses, supplemented by a regularization strategy. Compared to standard L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> losses, this approach improves the Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) by 4 dB, increases the Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) by 0.02, and reduces the Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (LPIPS) by 0.02. Furthermore, addressing the strict spatial alignment characteristic of super-resolution tasks, we replace the conventional cross-attention mechanism with a channel concatenation-based conditioning strategy, significantly enhancing edge alignment precision. To facilitate clinical deployment, we further optimize the denoising network architecture by retaining attention mechanisms exclusively at the bottleneck. This reduces VRAM consumption and training time by approximately 65% with negligible performance degradation (only a 0.07 dB drop in PSNR). Extensive experiments on a spinal MRI dataset provided by our collaborative partner demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in SSIM and perceptual quality (LPIPS), accurately recovering intricate anatomical structures.

title: Identification of Grid Partitions and Key Transmission Lines Based on Leiden Clustering and Multi-Dimensional Evaluation
authors: Xun Wu, Zhou Li
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Aiming at the needs of modern power system partition scheduling, fault prevention and control as well as precise installation of power electronic equipment, this paper proposes a novel grid partitioning method to synchronize the multi-dimensional importance evaluation of key transmission lines. The method firstly constructs a comprehensive similarity grid topology graph integrating impedance, voltage magnitude and phase angle features, injects generator node a priori constraints through a semi-supervised label propagation algorithm, and realizes high-precision partitioning by using the Leiden algorithm; secondly, it quantifies the importance of transmission lines in four dimensions, namely, topology, electrical correlation, power transmission, and fault vulnerability to form the prioritized ordering. The IEEE 39-node system is used as a case study, and the results show that: the clustering modularity value of the proposed method is higher than that of the classical Louvain algorithm, and the partitioning results are in accordance with the logic; based on the importance ranking of the existing transmission lines, it can provide decision-making support for a series of future measures, such as grid scheduling, security prevention and control, and equipment installation.

title: Research on a Differential High-Precision Non-Invasive DC Voltage Measurement Method Using the Charge Simulation Method
authors: Zehao Li, Xiong Xiao, Zijian He, Maoheng Jing, Boyuan Gao, Pengfei Yang
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: High-precision voltage inversion is crucial for monitoring ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission systems. This paper proposes a high-precision DC voltage inversion method based on a differential charge simulation method to meet the non-invasive voltage measurement requirements of UHVDC transmission lines. The relationship between the differential electric field and the conductor voltage, together with the corresponding inversion formula, is derived from electromagnetic theory, and the optimal inversion angle is identified. Numerical simulations are performed to verify the inversion accuracy and the applicable angular range. Low-voltage experiments are then carried out to validate the inversion accuracy at different angles and to evaluate the voltage inversion resolution. Simulation results indicate an inversion error of less than 0.2 % of full scale (0.2 % FS), while measurements on a low-voltage test platform show that, at the optimal deployment angle of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-45^{\circ}$</tex>, the voltage inversion error is as low as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.011 \% \text{FS}$</tex>, with a voltage resolution better than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ V}$</tex>. These results demonstrate that the proposed method provides a feasible solution for non-invasive UHVDC transmission-line voltage monitoring.

title: Study on the Optimization of Wireless Charging Transmission Efficiency and Distance Enhancement of Intelligent Vehicles Based on Magnetic Coupling Resonance
authors: Zhenhong Luo, Xingzhi Lin, Tao Lin
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: For the issues of transmission efficiency degradation and distance limitation in wireless charging systems for smart vehicles under dynamic operating conditions, a multi-dimensional collaborative optimization method based on magnetic coupling resonance (Magnetic Coupled Resonance, MCR) is proposed. By designing an asymmetric composite resonator through three-dimensional magnetic field reconstruction, and combining particle swarm optimization algorithms to enhance coupling coefficients and offset resistance; constructing dynamic impedance matching networks and adaptive frequency tuning algorithms to achieve real-time impedance matching and frequency splitting suppression over a wide load range; building a hardware-in-theloop test platform to verify system robustness. Experiments show that the system achieves an efficiency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5. 3 \%}-\mathbf{9 4. 2 \%}$</tex> within a transmission distance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 - 3 0 ~ c m}$</tex>, with lateral offset tolerance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm \mathbf{2 5 ~ c m}$</tex>, dynamic response time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{< 5 0 ~ m s}$</tex>, and temperature rise below 15° C. The research findings provide theoretical support and innovative solutions for the engineering application of wireless charging technology for smart vehicles.

title: A Systematic Literature Review of Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Enhancing E-Commerce Recommender Systems
authors: Andy Supriyadi, Herman Dwi Surjono, Handaru Jati, Nurul Firdaus
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: Hybrid deep learning recommender systems have received significant interest from e-commerce recently, mainly because of their potential for improving recommendation quality. However, core issues such as recommendation diversity, scalability, interpretability and real-time adaptability remain largely unexplored in previous works. Also, the trustworthiness of reviews using the ABSA for better product recommendations is not well exploited. This paper systematically reviews hybrid DL models in e-commerce recommendation systems, focusing on the advancement of the models, challenges they face, and ABSA integration. Based on the PRISMA process, SLR is carried out with a total of 691 papers being screened, from which twenty-five were chosen to be reviewed in more detail. The review proves that to combine sentiment analysis with ABSA can highly enhance the performance of score-valued product recommending system both in terms of accuracy and diversity. These hybrid systems resolve some of the most well known problems like cold-start, data sparsity and over-specialization and promotes increased personalization of user experience. Unlike prior work, this overview takes a direct look at the role of ABSA in enhancing product recommendations and provides a different insight on its use. This paper also discusses unaddressed issues pertaining to scalability, interpretability, and online adaptation. In conclusion, this research emphasizes not only the significant advantages but also the existing headwinds of hybrid models in e-commerce and suggests future areas for academic research..

title: Application of Phase-Resolved Partial Discharge Patterns for Detecting Potentially Hazardous Partial Discharges within Insulation Systems
authors: Yevhenii Trotsenko, Olexandr Protsenko, Dmytro Myronov, Vadim Chumack, Yaroslav Haran, Julia Peretyatko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: Deviations from the sinusoidal voltage waveform and various transient processes significantly impact the longevity of electrical equipment such as induction motors and power transformers. These deviations cause excessive heating, increased electrical stress, and mechanical vibrations, leading to premature aging and equipment failure. Diagnosing the insulation condition is crucial for maintaining the reliability and efficiency of electrical systems. Partial discharges within insulation are particularly harmful, gradually eroding the material and leading to insulation breakdown and equipment failure. This paper describes the practical application of phase-resolved partial discharge patterns for detecting hazardous partial discharges within insulation systems. By analyzing the temporal and amplitude characteristics of partial discharges across different phases, this study establishes a reliable method for identifying and assessing the severity of insulation defects. Experimental results from partial discharge measurements on electrical insulating cardboard samples are presented, demonstrating the effectiveness of phase-resolved analysis.
