The developed software tool processes partial discharge data to correlate time-resolved partial discharge events with the phase angle of the applied alternating voltage, resulting in detailed phase-resolved partial discharge patterns. These patterns provide insights into the nature and characteristics of partial discharges, aiding in early detection of insulation degradation. Additionally, the results can be used as input data for training and testing artificial neural networks, improving the accuracy of partial discharge classification models and insulation diagnosis techniques. This approach supports the development of condition-based maintenance strategies, optimizing equipment operation, extending service life, and enhancing overall system reliability and safety.

title: Utilizing Arithmetic Meta-Learning Method for Unsupervised Learning Purposes
authors: Erfan Arzhmand, Fatemeh Hosseini, Hossein Rashid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: In domains where structured data is abundant yet labels are scarce, unsupervised representation learning has become a pivotal approach for building scalable intelligent systems. This paper introduces a domain-general meta-learning framework, Arithmetic Meta-Learning (AML), designed to enhance unsupervised learning by creating and solving analogical pseudo-tasks without requiring annotated data. While Arithmetic Meta-learning for Unsupervised Purpose Modeling (AML-UPM) is general-purpose, we validate its effectiveness through implementation on a music playlist recommendation benchmark, demonstrating its ability to learn compositional and semantically rich representations. We evaluate AML on the Spotify Million Playlist Dataset, which presents a challenging testbed with diverse user-generated playlists and no explicit labels. Our framework incorporates dynamic fusion of contrastive, reconstruction, and stability objectives, weighted adaptively during training based on metalevel feedback. Empirical results show that AML consistently outperforms baseline models that use static objectives or handcrafted similarity measures. Most notably, it captures latent semantic patterns across playlists and demonstrates meaningful extrapolation capabilities on unseen data clusters. Though our experiments focus on music playlist recommendation, the principles underlying AML are broadly applicable. AML also opens new research avenues in self-supervised adaptation, analogical reasoning, and embedding generalization across domains.

title: An Adaptive Mpr Selection Routing Algorithm for Flying Ad Hoc Networks Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wenbin Jiang, Yuwang Yang, Bo Song, Yaqi Ke
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the rapid development of UAV technology, Flight Ad hoc Networks (FANETs) have become a significant research hotspot. However, their highly dynamic topology and frequent link changes pose significant challenges to the stability and reliability of routing. Traditional routing protocols, such as Optimized Link-State Routing (OLSR), rely on static multipoint relay (MPR) selection using greedy heuristics, which often fails under rapidly changing topologies. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes an adaptive routing algorithm based on deep reinforcement learning, named DAM-OLSR. This algorithm models the MPR selection problem as a Markov decision process and employs an adaptive optimization mechanism based on Qlearning, combined with the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm to cope with high-dimensional dynamic environments. Simulation results show that, compared with the traditional OLSR and greedy algorithms, DAM-OLSR selects relatively fewer MPR nodes, although slightly more than the optimal algorithm, and achieves a significant improvement in throughput compared with all three algorithms.

title: Minimizing the Magnetic Signature of Uncertain Extended Technical Objects based on Predictive Meta-Model
authors: Borys Kuznetsov, Ihor Bovdui, Tatyana Nikitina, Kostiantyn Chunikhin, Borys Kobylianskyi, Olena Voloshko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: Minimizing the magnetic field signature (MFS) of uncertain energy-saturated extended technical objects (UESETO) based on multy-spheroidal meta-model (MSMM) of MFS to compensate of external magnetic field spheroidal harmonies (EMFSH) of UESETO using compensating magnetic dipoles (CMD). Geometric location coordinates and magnitudes of spherical harmonics of CMD calculated as magneto-static geometric inverse problems (MSGIP) solution in nonlinear minimax optimization problem (NMMOP) form based on MSMM of MFS for UESETO. Nonlinear objective function (NOF) calculated as weighted sum of squared of resulting MFS by COMSOL Multiphysics software package used. NMMOP solutions calculated based on nonlinear particle swarm optimization (NPSO) algorithms. The results of minimizing of initial MFS of UESETO based on MSMM of initial MFS in spatial prolate spheroidal harmonics (SPSH) form in prolate spheroidal coordinate system (PSCS) using CMD and taking into account magnetic characteristics uncertainty (MCU) of UESETO are given.

title: An Improved Artificial Lemming Algorithm for Feature Selection in Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Anlei Wei, Guang Cheng, Weimin Zeng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: High-dimensional redundant features, along with the limitations of the Artificial Lemming Algorithm (ALA)—including the imbalance between exploration and exploitation, low search efficiency in high-dimensional spaces, and poor adaptability to discrete features—can result in low detection accuracy and high computational overhead in intrusion detection systems (IDS). To address these challenges, this paper proposes an Improved Artificial Lemming Algorithm (IALA) for IDS feature selection. Experiments on the IDS2018, NSL-KDD, and UNSW-NB15 datasets using support vector machine (SVM) and decision tree classifiers demonstrate that the proposed IALA effectively extracts optimal feature subsets with high efficiency. It achieves a balanced trade-off among the IDS objectives, including low false negatives, low false positives, and high reliability, thereby offering a practical and efficient feature selection approach for intrusion detection.

title: Beam-Switching-Based Joint DOA and TOA Acquisition and Tracking Using Commercial 5G NR Signals for Outdoor Positioning
authors: Wenxin Dong, Zhanghai Ju, Hongjian Jiao, Zhaoliang Liu, Ruizhi Chen, Liang Chen
abstract: The proliferation of 5G wireless devices creates a pressing need for high-precision positioning services. This paper presents an integrated acquisition–and–tracking framework that leverages physical broadcast channel (PBCH) transmissions from commercial 5G New Radio (NR) base stations (BSs). To select the strongest downlink beam without prior knowledge of the BS radiation pattern, we use an adaptive beam-switching strategy based on reference signal received power (RSRP), reference signal received quality (RSRQ), and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). For coarse acquisition, direction-of-arrival (DOA) and time-of-arrival (TOA) estimates are produced by a two-stage procedure that combines three-dimensional sparse Bayesian learning (SBL) with gradient-ascent off-grid refinement. A closed-loop tracker then continuously refines these estimates through azimuth-locked loop (ALL), elevation-locked loop (ELL), and delay-locked loop (DLL) modules. We further derive information-theoretic limits that bound acquisition and tracking and elucidate the key factors shaping these limits. The complete framework is implemented on a software-defined radio (SDR) 5G receiver and validated in outdoor field trials. Results indicate robust performance, with an average TOA root-mean-square error (RMSE) of 3.58 m, an azimuth RMSE of 5.77°, and an elevation RMSE of 2.74°, while requiring only 2.64 switching events per minute.

title: Research on Fault Detection Analysis and Optimization Measures in Bipolar Region of UHV Multi-Terminal Flexible DC Transmission System
authors: Hangtian Dong, Wanli Shi, Yukun Xiao, Chang Ruan, Sai Ding, Peng Zhang, Xiaoxia Li, Jianhua Lu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Taking Kunliulong DC transmission project as an example, this paper introduces the detection method of bipolar fault in multi-terminal DC system under different operation modes, aiming at the problem that the fault detection of bipolar region is insensitive during the bipolar equilibrium operation of multiterminal DC engineering, and compares the response of the method when different types of faults are set in different positions in the bipolar region through RTDS simulation, and proposes an optimization method for detecting bipolar faults, which has certain reference value for on-site operation and maintenance personnel and subsequent multi-terminal DC engineering construction

title: Reduced-Order Search Method for Coupling Matrix in Wireless Cable Testing
authors: Feilong Wang, Shangbing Qiao, Yuxin Ren, Yuhang Guo, Wanji Hou, Xiaolong Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: Driven by the explosive growth of mobile data traffic and ultra-broadband services, fifth-generation (5G) mobile communication systems have been commercially deployed worldwide. To ensure consistent and superior user experience, terminal devices must be evaluated under repeatable and controllable channel conditions. Although cable-based setups have long been the mainstream solution for multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) terminal testing, the wireless cable method—capable of emulating conducted connectivity without physical RF cables—has emerged as a compelling alternative. However, the performance of wireless cable systems heavily depends on the accuracy and robustness of the coupling matrix. Existing search algorithms for coupling matrix estimation suffer from high computational complexity, limiting their practicality. To address this issue, this paper proposes a reduced-order search (ROS) method that decreases the computational complexity from exponential to linear order. Finally, a 4×4 MIMO terminal is tested in a full anechoic chamber, and the results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces complexity while maintaining high isolation performance.

title: Continuous Sign Language Pose Generation via Text-driven Diffusion Model
authors: Jiayi He, Guangzong Zhang, Xu Wang, Ruobei Zhang, Ao Liu, Jiabao Guo
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Sign language generation aims to generate semantically aligned sign pose sequences from text. Diffusion models have shown great potential in generation tasks and provided new ideas for high-quality sequence generation. To this end, we propose a Text-driven Diffusion Model (TDM) for sign language generation. During the training phase, TDM encodes text and incorporates it as conditional input into the diffusion model to generate sign pose sequences. To ensure the high quality and accuracy of the generated pose sequences, we use two loss functions. The joint loss ℒ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">joint</inf> is used to precisely measure and minimize the differences between the joint positions of the generated pose sequences and those of the ground truth. Similarly, the bone orientation loss ℒ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">bone</inf> is instrumental in ensuring that the orientation of the bones in the generated poses aligns with the actual and correct orientations. In the inference stage, TDM starts from a noise sequence under the strict constraints of text conditions and gradually refines and generates a semantically consistent sign pose sequence. Extensive experiments on the PHOENIX14T datasets validate the effectiveness of our method.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Self-Disturbance-Resistant Control Strategy for LLC Resonant Converters
authors: Sida Chen, Yongzhi Zhao, Dengwen Dong, Rui Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address the problems of weak anti-disturbance capability, slow dynamic response and difficult parameter tuning of traditional control methods for LLC resonant converters, this paper proposes a DDPG-LADRC control strategy (Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient-Optimized Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control). First, a small-signal model of the LLC resonant converter is established based on the extended describing function method, and a second-order Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Controller (LADRC) is designed to realize closed-loop voltage control. Then, the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm is introduced, which takes the voltage error and its rate of change as the state, and the key parameters of LADRC as the action. Through the Actor-Critic network and experience replay mechanism, online adaptive tuning of the controller parameters is achieved. Simulations in Matlab/Simulink show that this strategy enables the converter to have a steady-state overshoot of less than 3%. Under 20% input voltage fluctuation or 20 % load disturbance, the settling time is reduced by approximately 30 % compared with the traditional LADRC, and the anti-disturbance performance is significantly improved. This strategy provides a practical solution for the control of LLC resonant converters.

title: A Survey of Generalization of Graph Anomaly Detection: From Transfer Learning to Foundation Models
authors: Junjun Pan, Yu Zheng, Yue Tan, Yixin Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)

title: Analysis of the Application Effectiveness of Intelligent Inspection Robots in UHVDC Converter Stations
authors: Sen He, Weilong Lin, Yaohui Xiao, Zhenzhen Zhou, Yunhai Song, Huailin Huang
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: This paper focuses on the application of intelligent inspection robots in ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) converter stations, with an emphasis on analyzing the operational efficiency and engineering practical value of these robots in such specific high-risk scenarios. The paper systematically elaborates on the research approach tailored to the special requirements of converter stations and the integrated “Sensing-Analysis-Supervision” architecture. It delves into the key technologies and algorithm optimization schemes suitable for environments with strong electromagnetic interference and complex spatial structures. Through actual deployment and comparative testing at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 800 \text{kV}$</tex> UHVDC converter station, the paper details the robot inspection process and analyzes massive monitoring data. The results show that the application of intelligent inspection robots in UHVDC converter stations can improve inspection efficiency by approximately 60%, reduce the average response time for fault early warnings in key equipment areas such as valve halls and converter transformers by over 50%, and increase the accuracy of defect identification to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 5 \%}$</tex>. This significantly reduces the probability of maintenance personnel being exposed to extremely strong electromagnetic fields and high-risk environments, providing strong technical support for ensuring the safe and stable operation of the national “West-East Power Transmission” strategic backbone grid.

title: Dataset Fusion and Multi-Task Learning for Robust Wheat Grain Storage Monitoring: A Survey
authors: Djakhdjakha Lynda, Abdelmoumène Hiba, Bouacida Imane, Farou Brahim
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Stored wheat faces dual threats from intrinsic quality degradation and insect infestations, both of which contribute to substantial post-harvest losses. Existing datasets often address these issues separately, focusing either on grain condition classification or on insect detection, which limits the development of robust and integrated monitoring solutions. In this work, we describe and motivate two complementary types of datasets: those oriented toward wheat grain quality assessment and those focused on insect infestation monitoring. Building on these resources, we present three fusion strategies: (1) Feature-Level fusion, where extracted features are merged into a joint representation; (2) Multi-Task Learning, where a shared backbone network supports separate tasks for grain condition and insect identification; and (3) Decision-Level Fusion (Pipeline Approach), where independent models are combined to produce interpretable and causally informed outcomes. The description and analysis of these dataset categories and strategies provide a solid foundation for a forthcoming comparative study.

title: Hybrid Game Intelligent Control for Large-Scale Electric Vehicle and Power Grids Cooperation
authors: Hengyu Liu, Jiahan Wang, Shuangde Liu, Chongrui Ji, Dawei Hu, Minzhe Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: The unregulated access of large-scale Electric Vehicles(EVs) into the power grid will increase the peak-valley difference and fluctuation of the load, thereby causing voltage fluctuations at distribution network nodes and posing a threat to the safe operation of the power grid. To address this issue, a hybrid game intelligent control strategy for the coordinated operation of large-scale EVs and the power grid is proposed, including a Stackelberg game between the regional distribution system (DS) and EV aggregators (EVA) and a Guided game between EVA and EV clusters. The Stackelberg game is based on the dominant position of the DS in setting electricity prices, coordinating power transactions and guiding the behavior of EVA. The Guided game regulates the charging and discharging behavior of EV clusters by having EVA set the optimal charging and discharging electricity prices. In this paper, a charging demand model is established for a single EV user and the schedulable capacity of the EV clusters is calculated based on Minkowski sum. Considering the interests of the DS, EVA, and EV users, a charging and discharging regulation model for the EV cluster under dynamic electricity prices is established and solved based on modified particle swarm optimization algorithm. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed strategy is verified through simulation.

title: CausaMap: A Semi-Supervised Map for Causal Text Mining
authors: Sami Diaf
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Investigating narratives in specific text-as-data applications has been extensively implemented using hybrid approaches, combining several NLP solutions and statistical techniques. While this direction provides a substantial understanding of key semantic patterns, it remains a challenging task not applicable to all languages. Narratives, especially in social sciences, are often regarded as the study of causality between specific terms, to uncover embedded directed or indirected links for explanation and further hypotheses testing. This paper establishes CausaMap, as a semi-supervised method to unfold causality between predefined terms of interests. It is built on dependency parsers and the use of tree-based structural equation models to quantify direct and indirect causal links. Thus, testing terms' causality in a directed network of parsed, lemmatized words is equivalent to uncovering informative inner structures with preserved semantic properties, without external information. Applied to two different corpora in English and German languages, CausaMap yielded mixtures of directed acyclic graphs and bidirectional edges, forming a narrative picture centered around monetary objectives and macroeconomic goals reported in the Federal Reserve governors' speeches (1996-2024). The German political parties' manifestos (1990-2021) demonstrated a dominance of policy-oriented terms when studying economic and employment debates, where the relatively high number of uncovered connecting nodes explains the causality buildup of pre-defined terms as well as languagespecific morphologies.

title: Analysis of the Structure of the Roughness Profile of the mm-Band Gyrotron Resonator and Comparison With Theoretical Models
authors: D. A. Kotova, A. S. Sedov, D. B. Radishev
abstract: Determining the ohmic <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$Q$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-factor of gyrotron resonators in the millimeter wavelength range requires accounting for the surface roughness, which is incorporated via the effective conductivity coefficient <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${k}_{\textit {skin}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. Although most calculation models for gyrotrons assume this value to be 2, its precise determination for a specific surface remains a relevant task. In this work, a theoretical approach previously developed for a model of the roughness profiles is applied to a real resonator. The surface profile was measured, the obtained data were processed, and the effective conductivity, along with the corresponding <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${k}_{\textit {skin}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> coefficient, was determined. The results obtained can determine both the main parameters of the roughness profile and the limits of applicability of the theoretical model used.

title: A Setting-Free Single-Ended Fault Detection Method for Multi-Terminal DC Systems Based on Active Boundary Control
authors: Jiayi Yang, Guobing Song, Ruidong Xu, Zhongxue Chang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The increasing integration of power electronic equipment into modern power systems has led to the gradual weakening or elimination of boundary components, posing significant challenges for single-ended whole-line fault identification. To address this issue, this paper employs coordinated active boundary control and signal injection control during the fault steady-state to enhance fault characteristics and establish series-connected boundary properties. A fault analysis is established using modal network modeling for both pole-to-ground (P2G) and pole-to-pole (P2P) faults. The results demonstrate that the injected characteristic frequency current magnitude at the protection point increases or remains unchanged in the case of internal faults, while it decreases under external faults. Based on this, a novel single-ended setting-free protection criterion is proposed, which is independent of system parameters, simulations, and complex calculations. Its performance is validated on a multi-terminal hybrid HVDC transmission system. Simulation and experimental results confirm that the method achieves whole-line fault identification under fault resistances up to 500 Ω, noise levels up to 20 dB, and parameter variations, demonstrating high robustness and reliability even in weak or non-boundary scenarios.

title: AIHCIR 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)

title: TOA Estimation based on Multi-band CSI Exploiting the Structure of the Correlation Matrix
authors: Jiawei Gao, Jiancun Fan, Shiyu Zhai, Jie Luo
abstract: In the future generation of mobile communication systems, many scenarios will require high-resolution range-based positioning. However, the accuracy of time-of-arrival (TOA) estimation algorithms for single wideband systems is generally limited due to the constraints of available bandwidth. Currently, leveraging multiple available frequency bands and carrier frequency switching to obtain multi-band channel state information (CSI) for TOA estimation is gaining popularity, as it effectively constructs an equivalent wideband signal. In this paper, we proposed a subspace-based TOA estimation algorithm using multi-band CSI by constructing a correlation matrix and then exploiting its mathematical properties. The proposed algorithm eliminates the need for grid search and thus has lower computational complexity. We analyze the Cramér-Rao Bound (CRB) for the multi-band data model and derive a tighter lower bound for our algorithm. Simulation results show that our algorithm converges to the CRB at high SNR and closely follows the proposed lower bound across all SNR levels. Additionally, our algorithm demonstrates superior performance compared to single-band algorithms and offers advantages in either accuracy or complexity when compared to other multi-band algorithms.

title: Designing Through Dough: The Bagel Studio as a Lesson in Empathy and Play
authors: Bruno Oro, Jason Cornaro, Subinay Malhotra, Ugochukwu Eluchie

title: A Robotic Laser Welding Seam Tracking Method for Complex 3-D Weld Trajectories Based on Geometric Structure Analysis_supp1-3661009.mp4
authors: Juan Liu
abstract: Seam sampling with line-structured-light sensors in laser welding is prone to projection distortion due to the complex 3-D geometry of sheet-metal workpieces, which leads to viewpoint-dependent variations in the sampled seam profile and affects laser incidence direction planning. Moreover, stable target tracking control becomes challenging when the 3-D seam trajectory contains small curvature radii or right-angle transitions. To address these issues, a method is proposed that compensates for projection distortion via deep-learning-based local surface-normal estimation and achieves robust viewpoint control along complex 3-D seam trajectories using prior geometric approximation of the seam trajectory. For normal estimation, seam features are encoded as “point-vector pair” superpixels and extracted by a single-stage deep convolutional network, enabling efficient, robust detection under welding glare, spatter, smoke, and reflections while maintaining boundary stability. Using the “point-vector pair” representation, the laser incidence direction is adaptively adjusted with reference to estimated surface normals to reduce pose uncertainty caused by projection distortion. For viewpoint control, a feedforward leading-direction control algorithm, derived from geometric–numerical analysis of predefined trajectory, ensures stable perception and high-precision continuous tracking along tight curves and right-angle turns. Across two experiments involving different workpiece geometries, the method achieves mean absolute laser focal position errors of 0.16 and 0.20 mm and mean absolute 3-D laser incident-direction errors of 0.99<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> and 1.58<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>, validating its efficiency, stability, and accuracy for sheet-metal laser seam tracking.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology

title: Adversarial Retain-Free Unlearning for Bearing Prognostics and Health Management
authors: Chaewon Yoon, Jiyoung Lee, Hoki Kim
abstract: Prognostics and health management (PHM) systems increasingly rely on machine learning for reliable bearing fault diagnosis. However, data deletion in PHM remains an open problem. Since retraining a model from scratch is often infeasible, this raises a new challenge. Moreover, in most cases, only the pretrained model and the user-requested data are available. Existing approaches under this constraint often fail to achieve proper unlearning and substantially degrade model performance, thereby disrupting the original embedding space and collapsing the structural integrity of the pretrained model. To address this, we propose adversarial retain-free unlearning (ARU). Our framework integrates adversarial samples generated from the pretrained model with a semantic-driven loss to preserve representational stability. Experiments on public and private bearing datasets demonstrate that ARU achieves unlearning efficacy and structural consistency comparable to Retrain while maintaining diagnostic accuracy. We believe that our proposed framework provides a practical and reliable solution for retain-free unlearning in real-world industrial PHM systems.

title: A Microwave-Based Sensing Platform for Microplastic Detection and Quantification: A Machine Learning-Assisted Approach
authors: Mateus I. O. Souza, Leonardo Tarczewski, Brian N. Lahr, Achiles F. Mota, Andrei Leitão, Vinicius M. Pepino, Ben-Hur V. Borges
abstract: This work introduces a low-cost microwave (MW) sensor that achieves the highest sensitivity among MW-based approaches for detecting microplastics (MPs) in water and provides a mechanism for determining polymer types in unknown samples. These achievements address the growing need for sensitive, affordable tools for routine and reliable monitoring of MPs across environmental water matrices. We employ a MW spiral resonator operating at 3.4 GHz to detect and quantify polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), polyvinyl chloride (PVC), and polyethylene terephthalate (PET) in water. The design leverages a highly sensitive spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) mode and a controlled field-sample interaction, enabling tunable coupling and robustness to variations in sample composition. Under maximum interaction (1.6 mL sample volume), the resonator detects concentrations as low as 125 ppm for all polymers. To overcome overlapping responses from similar dielectric properties, we integrate machine learning (ML) to discriminate polymer type. Four algorithms, Random Forest (RF), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Logistic Regression (LR), were evaluated using resonance-derived features: frequency shift, minimum amplitude, 3 dB bandwidth, and skewness. Logistic Regression achieved the best performance, correctly classifying 100 % of samples. After identification, polymer-specific calibration curves are used to estimate the concentration. These findings establish the platform as a sensitive, selective, and low-cost solution for MP monitoring in water. By coupling SSPP-enabled sensing with ML-based classification, this work advances the state-of-the-art in MW environmental monitoring and offers a practical route for addressing MP pollution while enabling both quantification and identification in a single device.

title: ALoRa-Based V2I System for Collision Warning at Blind-Turn
authors: Pratyush Gupta, Shubham Gupta, Theajus Prakash, Ramendra S. Baoni, Anuj Grover
abstract: To mitigate fatal collisions at non-line-of-sight (NLOS) blind curves, this paper presents the Blind Turn Accident Alert System (BTAAS), a low-cost, solar-powered Vehicle-to-Infrastructure (V2I) solution utilizing sub-GHz LoRa. The system uses intelligent nodes at curves to detect vehicles approaching from opposite directions via a motion-gated, bidirectional logic and broadcasting real-time warnings of potential collision risks. Field validation demonstrated a reliable link at a 150 m target distance (RSSI of-91.3 dBm) and robust operation at extended ranges with a negative SNR of-3 dB. Furthermore, an IMU-based Vector-Track algorithm effectively distinguishes between safe and converging trajectories. Offering a 10× cost advantage over radar, estimated at Rs 3500 ($39) per roadside unit and Rs 1000 ($12) per vehicle unit. BTAAS provides a validated, scalable technology for accident prevention in high-risk regions.

title: Distance-Aware Hypergraph and Attention Network With Unimodal Assistance for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
authors: Yibing Wang, Wupeng Xie, Zhutian Yang, Linhan Wang, Mingqian Liu, Yushi Chen, Yue Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) has advanced with deep learning, yet some limitations persist. Early methods can capture intra-sample patterns but fail to model inter-sample structural correlations. While Transformers improved inter-modal modeling, their high computational cost and confinement to pairwise relationships prevent them from capturing the complex, group-wise cues inherent in human expression. To address these gaps, we propose a novel Distance-Aware Hypergraph and Attention Network with Unimodal Assistance (DHAN-UA). Our framework's core is a semantic-proximity-aware hypergraph hybrid convolution. We first introduce an inverse-distance weighting strategy to redefine the hypergraph incidence matrix, ensuring a node's influence within a hyperedge is proportional to its semantic closeness to the hyperedge's conceptual center. This approach embeds crucial categorical and semantic priors into the unimodal features. Building upon this, a synergistic hierarchical attention framework first applies intra-modal self-attention to capture long-range dependencies in the topologically-aware features, then uses an inter-modal attention module for efficient weighted fusion. This design achieves both feature refinement and effective fusion. Finally, to enhance model stability, a unimodal-assisted training strategy acts as a regularizer, ensuring the model retains essential information from all modalities. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our model achieves competitive performance on benchmark datasets while maintaining superior computational efficiency.

title: Demonstration of E/D-Mode Logic Circuits in a Four-Pole 20-nm InGaAs HEMT-on-Silicon Technology using Back-Gate Biasing
authors: Alen Sebastian, Konstantin Kuliabin, Maxime Moulin, Sébastien Chartier, Felix Heinz, Dirk Schwantuschke, Arnulf Leuther, Rüdiger Quay
abstract: We present enhancement- and depletion-mode digital logic gates and circuits with back-gate biasing in a Four-pole high-electron-mobility transistor on Silicon technology. This work uses a 20-nm gate-length InGaAs HEMT-on-Si with the option to place an electron-beam-lithography defined back-gate below the InGaAs channel by wafer bonding. By applying back-gate bias, the threshold voltage is modulated to convert normally depletion-mode devices into enhancement-mode devices, without altering the physical structure or processing steps. The back-gated RF transistors are used directly to implement digital logic gates such as NOT, NAND, and NOR, as well as a D-LATCH circuit, and are demonstrated using the E/D logic approach. Additionally, the effect of back-gate biasing on the power consumption of logic gates is analyzed. The proposed circuits provide the opportunity to integrate logic components with state-of-the-art RF circuits monolithically.

title: A Survey of Repetitive Sequential Pattern Mining
authors: Yan Li, Mengyao He, Wenshuai Guo, Sijing Lu, Youxi Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Sequential pattern mining is an important research direction in data mining, which is derived from frequent itemset mining. This paper first reviews the relevant knowledge of frequent itemset mining and association rule mining. After that, this paper introduces the relevant methods of sequential pattern mining. Traditional sequential pattern mining methods neglect the repetition of a pattern in a sequence. Therefore, several repetitive sequential pattern mining methods have been proposed, such as contiguous sequential pattern mining, sequential pattern mining with gap constraints, and sequential pattern mining with self-adaptive gap. Sequential pattern mining methods with gap constraints also have many forms to count the occurrences of a pattern in a sequence, such as no condition (or general form), one-off condition, non-overlapping condition, and disjoint condition. Finally, we provide prospects for future research on the repetitive sequential pattern mining.

title: Blockchain Technology Model to Support Career Management for a Global Company
authors: Inayatulloh, Hamsinah, Imam Zaenuddin, Dessy Adelin, Siti Julianingsih Nurfitriyani, Muhammad Rizki Sani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Career management in a global company with employees from various countries is a strategic process that manages, develops, and facilitates the professional growth of employees in a multinational environment. This process involves career planning, competency development, and adjustment to cultural and regulatory diversity in various jurisdictions. The diversity of employees from several different countries impacts the competition to fill specific positions, so transparency about employee career paths in the company is needed. Currently, no system supports career management in global companies that provides transparency of employee career paths, so employees do not know the certainty of their careers in the company. The purpose of this research is to develop a blockchain sharding model for a global company that ensures transparency in the career paths of employees from various countries. By implementing blockchain technology, every transaction related to the career paths of employees from several countries will be validated by each node on the blockchain network. Blockchain technology applies peer-to-peer validation to each transaction before storing it on the blockchain network. This research selects blockchain sharding to enhance the efficiency of the validation process, which spans multiple countries. The study employs a qualitative approach that includes a literature review and software simulation using Hyperledger Fabric. Research results show that blockchain technology can increase the transparency of information about employee career levels.

title: YOLO-Based Automatic Segmentation of Prostate Zones on MRI
authors: Nicholas Alven Gandra, Irmawati
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: Prostate magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) segmentation remains a major technical bottleneck in automating prostate cancer assessment, particularly for accurately differentiating the Central Gland (CG) and Peripheral Zone (PZ), which often exhibit overlapping textures and indistinct boundaries. To address these challenges, this study investigates the use of the YOLOv8x-seg architecture on the Prostate158 dataset to achieve precise and computationally efficient prostate zone delineation. Through extensive training and model optimization, the proposed approach achieved Intersection over Union (IoU) scores of 83.44% for CG and 81.37% for PZ, with an overall Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) of 77.19%. These results demonstrate that YOLOv8x-seg effectively captures fine anatomical structures while maintaining real-time inference speed suitable for clinical workflows. Beyond its quantitative performance, this study underscores the adaptability of object detection–based segmentation in medical imaging and its potential to streamline prostate MRI interpretation. Overall, the findings lay the groundwork for integrating AI-driven frameworks into clinical decision support, thereby improving diagnostic precision and efficiency in urological healthcare.

title: Enhanced AMR-Based Modeling of Multi-Role Dialogue Dynamics for Abstractive Summarization
authors: ShiYong Xiong, Chen Yong, Cheng Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Multi-party dialogue summarization requires compressing lengthy conversational content while preserving speaker roles, relationships, and logical consistency. Traditional Abstract Meaning Representation (AMR) graphs, though effective in capturing predicate–argument semantics, struggle to explicitly model interactions among multiple speakers or to represent emotional and intentional dynamics. This paper proposes an enhanced AMR-based approach for dynamic interaction modeling in multi-party dialogues. The method extends the AMR framework by integrating dynamic role attributes—representing emotional and intentional variations—with interaction edge structures that capture behavioral patterns such as “questionanswer” or “proposal-opposition.” A graph attention mechanism (GAT) is employed to jointly learn semantic and interactive information within a unified framework. Experiments conducted on the SAMSum and QMSum datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves consistent and significant improvements in ROUGE- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 / 2 / \mathrm{L}$</tex> metrics, enhancing both the role consistency and semantic coherence of the generated summaries. The results confirm the effectiveness of embedding role dynamics into graph-based semantic representations for abstractive summarization. Moreover, the enhanced AMR graph not only improves quantitative performance but also provides clearer interpretability by revealing how speaker interactions evolve across turns. The framework remains lightweight and generalizable, showing potential for broader adaptation to real-world dialogue summarization scenarios such as customer service analysis and multi-party meeting documentation.

title: Educational Data Mining to support the Design of AI-Powered Tutoring Systems
authors: Ghada A. El Khayat, Lilia Cheniti-Belcadhi, Mohamed El Hadi Benelhadj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: This paper explores the crucial role of data mining in enhancing AI-powered tutoring systems and hence educational systems in general. Data mining techniques enable the analysis of vast amounts of student data to uncover patterns, predict learning performance, and provide personalized educational experiences. By leveraging methods such as clustering, classification, regression, AI tutors can adapt to individual learning styles, recommend targeted resources and identify potential areas of difficulty and improvement. This paper suggests the integration of artificial intelligence and data mining in the field of education to improve learning efficiency, engagement, and academic success. It includes an empirical case study on data of 790 students, where data mining techniques were employed to reveal hidden patterns. The implementation was done using Orange Data Mining and Google Colab. The goal of this analysis is to support the development of AI-powered tutoring systems. This study represents a foundational step towards creating adaptive, data-driven tutoring models informed by a deeper understanding of student behavior. The paper compares the findings with previous research by one of the authors on different data to get more insights. It also makes recommendations to be implemented in AI tutoring systems based on the data mining results to enhance students’ motivation and to reduce the risk of drop out.

title: Snakes in the Spectrum: Deep and Graph Reinforcement Learning Optimizers for Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Haider Farhi, Abderraouf Messai, Tarek Berghout
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Efficient spectrum allocation in Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs) remains a persistent challenge due to the dynamic activity of Primary Users (PUs) and potential collisions with Secondary Users (SUs). Reinforcement Learning (RL) has been increasingly applied to address this issue; however, conventional deep learning models often struggle to capture the relational structure among users and channels. In this study, we investigate the integration of the Snake Optimizer (SO) with two RL paradigms: Deep Q-Networks (DQN) and Graph-based Reinforcement Learning (DGRL). We implement a CRN environment comprising five PUs, ten SUs, and fifteen channels, optimizing Spectrum Utilization Efficiency (SUE), collision rate, and spectral capacity (SC). Each RL agent proposes channel allocations that are refined using SO to enhance convergence and reduce collisions. Comparative experiments demonstrate that both approaches achieve high performance, with DGRL+SO slightly outperforming DQN+SO across all metrics: average reward (0.4621 vs. 0.4586), SUE (66.27% vs. 65.97%), collision rate (0.0060 vs. 0.0105), and SC (9.940 vs. 9.895). These results indicate that modeling CRNs as graphs provides a measurable advantage in allocation performance, and that combining graph-aware RL with metaheuristic optimization can improve robustness in dynamic spectrum access scenarios.

title: A Test Case Generation Approach for ChaoJi Charging Systems Based on FRETish Specification
authors: Wei Yan, Yang Zhang, Le Yu, Zhuangling Yang, Hai Hu, Bing Zhou, Xiaoyu Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The ChaoJi charging standard is a standard that constrains the business processes of charging piles for different enterprises. Currently, compliance testing relies on manual design by experts, primarily targeting individual charging steps. Existing methods that describe standard requirements as formal languages such as linear sequential logic to automatically construct tests place excessive demands on mathematical foundations, making practical application difficult. This paper proposes using FRETish, a temporal specification language with a restricted natural language style, to describe the ChaoJi charging standard. An innovative method is designed to first convert the FRETish specification into a Simulink/Stateflow requirement state diagram, and then automatically generate test cases based on the state diagram. To address the challenge of existing Simulink/Stateflow-based test generation methods for constructing high-coverage tests from nested and concurrent state diagrams, a semantic preserved state diagram execution path unfolding method is proposed. Case study on ChaoJi charging confirms the effectiveness of the proposed testing framework, and experimental analysis also shows that the improved state diagram unfolding algorithm can effectively increase test coverage, particularly in covering the interaction processes among different functional modules.

title: Real-Time Human Detection on an Amlogic S905X Android Set-Top Box Using TensorFlow Lite
authors: Zuhairaja Musheera Tunggorono, Ridho Rahman Hariadi, Arif Bramantoro
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: We present a real-time human-detection system deployed on a mass-market Android set-top box (ZTE B860H V5) powered by an Amlogic S905X CPU, demonstrating that low-cost, fanless TV hardware can run responsive, on-device vision without external accelerators. Using TensorFlow Lite with CPU/NNAPI delegates, we implement an end-to-end RTSP pipeline (buffered ingestion, bounded queues, on-device inference, and event-driven notification) and compare compact detectors: SSD-MobileNet V1/V2 and EfficientDet-Lite (0-2). On the S905X, SSD-MobileNet V1 achieves the lowest average latency ($\sim 450 \mathrm{~ms}$), while EfficientDet-Lite offers higher confidence in cluttered scenes; a fine-tuned SSD-MobileNet V2 raises near-field confidence but exhibits higher, more variable latency. Beyond reporting latencies, we distill model-selection guidance for cost-constrained Android TV devices and show how delegate choice, camera characteristics, and scene type shape practical deployment.

title: A Voltage Sag Localization Method Based on Disturbance Reactive Power Derived from the Clarke Transform
authors: YuXuan Wang, Xiaolei Hou, Chaodai Zhao, Jinghao Teng, Yuhao Ding, Xiangmin Xie
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The accurate location of voltage sag sources is the key to clarifying the responsibilities of power supply and consumption and formulating governance strategies. Aiming at the problem of insufficient adaptability of existing location methods in asymmetric faults and complex grid structures, a new voltage drop source location method is proposed based on positive sequence perturbation of reactive power polarity. Firstly, employing the superposition principle and the method of symmetrical components, an equivalent circuit for the positive-sequence disturbance component during voltage sag was established. The correlation between the direction of disturbance reactive power flow and the location of the sag source was then theoretically examined.. Furthermore, a perturbation reactive power calculation method based on Clark transform is proposed, and a specific calculation process suitable for discrete sampling signals is given. A general positioning criterion is formed that if the positive sequence perturbation reactive power at the monitoring point is regular, the transient drop source is located upstream, if it is negative, it is located downstream. The simulation verification results show that this method has good adaptability to different short-circuit fault types, is not restricted by the grid topology structure, and has a high global positioning accuracy rate, providing an effective solution for voltage sag source localization.

title: EEG Emotion Recognition Based on Gated Recurrent Unit and Adaptive Loss Regulation Mechanism
authors: Chao Li, Bingfeng Liu, Yuqi Chen, Hanyue Li, Xinhong Zheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Subject-independent electroencephalogram (EEG) emotion recognition poses a significant challenge due to the substantial inter-subject variability in brain signals. Compounding this issue is the phenomenon where EEG patterns corresponding to specific emotions, such as negative and neutral states, often exhibit similarities, leading to misclassification and consequently limiting the model's generalization capability. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel Gated Recurrent Unit with Adaptive Loss Regulation Mechanism (GRU-ALRM). Spatial dependencies across EEG electrodes are modeled by a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU)-based lightweight extractor, learning discriminative high-level features. Furthermore, an Adaptive Loss Regulation Mechanism (ALRM) is developed by integrating the Minimum Class Confusion (MCC) principle with cross-entropy loss, which enhances the model's ability to distinguish between easily confused emotion categories. Extensive experiments on the public SEED and DEAP dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance, outperforming several state-of-the-art approaches in subject-independent emotion recognition.

title: Computational and Experimental Analysis of Losses and Thermal State of Rotors of Synchronous Machines in Operating Modes
authors: Kostiantyn Kuchynskyi, Viacheslav Prus
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: This paper briefly describes mathematical models, methods and algorithms for joint electromagnetic and thermal calculations of electrical machines based on the finite element method. The results of corresponding numerical studies in the core elements of massive implicit-pole rotors of powerful full-scale synchronous electric motors used in the oil and gas industry are presented. The values of maximum heating of machine components in stationary and transient modes are analyzed. A comparison of the obtained theoretical solutions with experimental test data under various motor operating conditions is also carried out.

title: Carbon Asset Accounting and Income Analysis of Emission Control Enterprises Considering Transaction Risks
authors: Pengfei Su, Guo Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: A series of trading activities of emission control enterprises in the carbon trading market form carbon assets, and the uncertainty of carbon trading activities forms corresponding trading risks, so it is necessary to formulate trading strategies based on carbon trading risks to maximize carbon assets and returns. Firstly, by analyzing the formation mechanism of carbon quota trading risk, this paper establishes a portfolio model of the carbon trading market, analyzes and calculates the proportion of carbon quotas in different portfolios and the corresponding risks, to provide a theoretical basis for emission control enterprises to formulate trading strategies, then combines trading risks and proposes carbon asset and income accounting methods, and formulates carbon trading strategies of emission control enterprises, and finally takes a thermal power enterprise in Gansu Province as an example for calculation. The results show that with the increase in trading risk, emission control enterprises are more inclined to choose less risky carbon quotas for trading. With the increase of carbon quota trading volume, the growth rate of total carbon revenue of enterprises decreased by 3 percentage points at most, but after considering carbon trading, the total income of enterprises can increase by 5.17 %, indicating that emission control enterprises can achieve enterprise income growth through carbon trading activities, further indicating that the carbon trading market can promote emission control enterprises to achieve energy conservation and emission reduction.

title: LSTM-CNN Hybrid Neural Network for Time-Series Semiconductor Manufacturing Quality Prediction
authors: Hongfeng Song, Boshi Wu, Hao Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Semiconductor quality prediction is challenged by high-dimensional sensor data and severe class imbalance in the SECOM dataset (591 features, 6.6% failures). We propose an LSTM-CNN hybrid model integrating multi-scale convolution, LSTM temporal learning, attention, and ensemble strategies. The model achieves 92.5% accuracy, 96.2% AUC-ROC, and 0.882 F1, outperforming traditional ML and standard deep learning methods, demonstrating strong potential for real-time manufacturing quality prediction.

title: Probabilistic Impact Analysis of the Permeability and Correlation of Wind Power on the Distribution Network
authors: Dengfeng Wang, Yulong Che, Bingke Liu, Xiaoqin Lyu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the rapid development of large-scale renewable energy, the penetration of wind power in distribution networks continues to increase. Due to the inherent randomness and correlation of wind power, the uncertainty in distribution network operation is intensified. This paper proposes a probabilistic load flow (PLF) calculation method based on the three-point estimation method (3PEM) for assessing the impact of wind power penetration and its correlation on the distribution network. First, a probabilistic wind speed model is established. Then, the Nataf transformation is applied to handle wind speed correlations. Finally, the three-point estimation method combined with the Cornish-Fisher series expansion is used to obtain the probability density of output random variables. The impact of wind power on the distribution network is evaluated. Case studies on the IEEE 33-bus system demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in assessing these probabilistic impacts. The proposed method serves as a reference for the operation and planning of wind power integration in distribution networks.

title: Differential Privacy Method with Dynamic Privacy Budget Allocation for Deep Learning
authors: Jiacai Liang, Huawei Song
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: In response to the problem of fixed privacy budget allocation strategy being difficult to balance model privacy and practicality in differential privacy deep learning models, this paper proposes a dynamic privacy budget allocation differential privacy method for deep learning. This method intelligently allocates privacy budget to achieve fine-grained control of noise injection by analyzing the data features and gradient dynamics during the training process, under the constraint of overall privacy budget. This method first uses a layer by layer correlation propagation algorithm to evaluate the contribution of input data features to the model output, and then dynamically adds noise to the features; Secondly, in the model optimization phase, a power function based dynamic privacy budget allocation strategy is introduced to dynamically adjust the noise intensity during the training process. Experiments on the MNIST, Fashion MNIST, and CIFAR-10 datasets show that compared to DP-SGD, AdLM, and other methods, this algorithm significantly improves model accuracy under the same privacy budget.

title: Data Processing Features of Electrical Parameters Recording of Electrotechnical Graphitization Complexes
authors: Dmytro Yarymbash, Mykhaylo Kotsur, Tetyana Divchuk, Yulia Bezverkhnyaya, Iryna Kylymnyk, Igor Kotsur
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The technique of dynamic processing of digital data for recording currents, voltages and active power during graphitization of electrode blanks in AC furnaces was developed. This technique allows reducing the volume of input information, providing computational efficiency and accuracy, and improving the quality of automated control of the operation modes of the electrical graphitization complex.

title: Near-Ideal: A High-Flatness Honeywords Generation Scheme via Felicitous-Difference Total Labeling and Random Forest
authors: Xiaohui Zhang, Wenhao Ji, Tao Feng, Fei Ma, Qiang Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: In recent years, Networked multi-agent authentication systems are widely deployed. However, frequent inter-agent authentication requests create potential attack surfaces. If an agent’s credentials are stolen, the entire system becomes vulnerable to infiltration or sabotage. To counter this, we integrate honeywords technology into multi-agent authentication systems, yet existing schemes often only ideally achieve high flatness in honeywords construction. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a method for constructing high flatness honeywords using labeling techniques. First, we introduce the concept of felicitous-difference total labeling and construct topological matrices corresponding to topological sequences. Next, we combine topological sequences with user passwords to generate high flatness honeywords and incorporate a random forest model to achieve ideal high flatness for the honeywords mixed with user passwords. Subsequently, we validate the proposed scheme through complexity analysis. Finally, compared to other honeywords schemes, our solution demonstrates more significant advantages in terms of honeywords flatness and storage overhead.

title: QuESt Planning: A Long-term Capacity Expansion Planning Tool for Energy Storage Systems & Modeling Flexibility
authors: Cody J. Newlun, Atri Bera, Dilip Pandit, Gustavo Cuello-Polo, Walker Olis, Andres Lopez, Yung-Jai Pomeroy, Tu Nguyen
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Grid planners face the challenge of ensuring that capacity expansion planning (CEP) models sufficiently model a diverse set of generation resources, energy storage systems (ESS), and transmission networks to make reliable, cost-effective investment decisions. However, traditional CEP models often lack the flexibility and robustness to address these complexities. This paper introduces the QuESt Planning tool, an open-source software package that enables scenario-based planning for longterm capacity expansion planning. The tool allows users to customize the spatio-temporal scope (including temporal aggregation and transmission modeling assumptions) and candidate investment options while evaluating ESS technologies based on capital costs, lifetimes, and operational attributes. A case study is performed to demonstrate QuESt Planning’s capabilities and highlight the tool’s potential to address research challenges and enhance decision-making in a dynamic energy landscape.

title: Tomato Maturity Detection Method Based on YOLOv10
authors: Xiantao Tang, Mingjun Li, Yanbo Zhang, Qi Jia
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Small tomatoes, commonly known as cherry tomatoes or cherry tomato, have rich nutritional value and unique taste. During the period of fruit ripening, due to the shelter of tomato leaves, it is necessary to realize high-efficiency automatic intelligent picking and maturity recognition of small tomatoes in complex environments. In this paper, a tomato data set with partially enhanced environmental interference effect is constructed, and an improved yolov10 model is proposed to detect it. By improving the convolution block C2fCIB module, SPPF module and neck module, the ability of feature recognition is enhanced, which provides a more effective model for tomato maturity recognition.

title: Energy Efficiency of Ball Milling at Varying Lifter Number and Mill Speed Using DEM Simulation and Experimental Study
authors: Kit Lester P. Balayo, Evan Myles S. Rapanot, Juren Michael Jay B. Padin, Gernelyn T. Logrosa
abstract: Ball mill grinding is one of the most energyintensive stages in mineral processing reducing gold ore particles to finer sizes. As a key factor in a mill's grinding and energy efficiency, power consumption may be influenced by its rotational speed and lifter number. Yet, most traditional models for estimating mill efficiency overlook effects on finer particles, often leading to inaccuracies. By applying the Discrete Element Method (DEM), this study explores the behavior and interaction of ore particles in local milling setups and validating their operational performance through experiments using P80 of ground gold tailings. Simulation included four lifter numbers-zero lifters, two lifters, four lifters, and eight lifters-at 65%, 70%, and 75% of critical speed. It was found that DEM offers a robust approach by capturing particle motion and interactions within the mill, allowing for better insight into the role of lifter number and rotational speed.

title: Blockchain-Based Traceability of Product Ingredients and Labeling to Combat Overclaiming in the Indonesian Cosmetics Industry
authors: Aurelia Lisdy Nandiya, Herry Irawan, Andry Alamsyah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: The cosmetics industry in Indonesia faces significant challenges related to overclaiming and greenwashing, where product labels often mislead consumers regarding their effectiveness and environmental credentials. This paper designs and evaluates a blockchain-based solution to address these issues by ensuring that cosmetic product labels are connected to verifiable evidence and comply with regulatory standards. The model integrates pre-market claim verification, including certification of ingredients and product safety, with post-market traceability, allowing consumers to verify product claims through a scanning process. The system, tested on a local Ethereum Virtual Machine-compatible network, demonstrated fast and consistent per-transaction confirmation times for product validation and claim verification, supporting its feasibility for operational use. By preventing claim-batch linking when safety test certificates, regulatory authorization, or halal certification are missing or invalid, the model strengthens evidence-based labeling and reduces the opportunity for unsupported claims. The proposed solution strengthens product transparency and regulatory compliance and reduces the risk of overclaiming and greenwashing in Indonesian cosmetics industry. Future research will focus on expanding the system’s capabilities to include periodic testing and status renewal, as well as piloting the solution in collaboration with industry and regulatory bodies to assess its scalability and real-world performance.

title: A Lightweight Evaluation Method for Harmonic Monitoring Indicator Based on High-Order Statistics
authors: Ruining Liu, Xiaolei Hou, Dandan Feng, Yuanyuan Sun, Chaodai Zhao, Xiaolei Yu, Kaiqi Sun
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Harmonic pollution poses an increasingly serious threat to modern power systems, necessitating real-time, highprecision monitoring solutions. Traditional harmonic monitoring methods such as Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) suffer from issues such as high computational complexity, multi-cycle dependency, and poor noise immunity, making them unsuitable for achieving high-precision real-time online monitoring in complex harmonic environments. This paper proposes a lightweight evaluation method for harmonic monitoring indicator based on higher-order statistics (HOS), which achieves real-time online monitoring of harmonic distortion by optimizing data acquisition and processing strategies. This method combines time-domain waveform variance characteristics to construct a lightweight indicator VHD that is independent of harmonic order and phase, effectively reducing computational load and improving data stability. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed indicator exhibits a good mapping relationship with the traditional total harmonic distortion (THD) indicator. Compared with the FFT-based THD method, the proposed method achieves an 87.6% reduction in average calculation time under the same hardware configuration. This lightweight advantage enables real-time online monitoring of harmonic distortion, providing efficient data support for harmonic control and optimization in power systems.

title: Text-to-SQL Generation for Medical Databases via Large Language Models and SQL Decomposition
authors: Yuanchen Ding, Fangjie Wan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: To enhance the reliability and interpretability of medical database queries, this paper proposes a fragment-level self-consistent Text-to-SQL generation method based on large language models (LLMs) and SQL decomposition, specifically designed for integrated clinical and healthcare insurance databases. The method first employs an open-source code-centric LLM with a dual-path prompting strategy—Semantic Example Prompting and Question-Skeleton-based Prompting—to generate multiple candidate SQL queries. Subsequently, the candidate SQL statements are parsed into Abstract Syntax Trees (ASTs) using an SQL parser and uniformly transformed into JSON structures representing clause components. Frequency counting and normalization are performed on corresponding fragments across candidates to construct fragment-level self-consistency confidence scores. Finally, guided by a predefined SELECT syntactic skeleton, high-confidence fragments and database schema information are jointly fed into the LLM to generate a structurally constrained final SQL query, thereby mitigating local errors in multi-table joins and complex conditions. Experimental results on the self-constructed MedInsDB demonstrate that under identical open-source LLM configurations and training data, the proposed method significantly improves execution accuracy over several strong baselines, especially on hard cases involving multi-table joins.

title: Exploring the Potential of Duckweed-Derived Materials in Mercury Removal: A Systematic Review
authors: Lalaine Joan A. Limbago, Althea Leen S. Berganio, Jesslien Kaye A. Cantil, Exelle Megan G. Delima
abstract: Mercury contamination in water poses significant environmental and health risks. Duckweed (Lemna minor), a fastgrowing aquatic plant, has been widely recognized for its ability to treat water bodies contaminated with heavy metals. While numerous studies highlighted duckweed's potential, no systematic literature review currently consolidates findings on duckweedderived materials for mercury removal. This review examined eleven peer-reviewed studies from 2010 to 2025 following the PRISMA framework based on synthesis and modification techniques, removal efficiency, and environmental applications. Findings show that duckweed removes mercury through phosphate and amine functional groups, with optimal adsorption at pH7 - 9 and effective contact times of 10-40 minutes. Mercury removal remained efficient at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 12.0 \mu \mathrm{g}/\mathrm{L}$</tex> (inorganic) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 50.0 \mu \mathrm{g}/\mathrm{L}$</tex> (organic). However, duckweed shows low tolerance to high mercury levels, with plant mortality at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 4 8 8 \mu g/L$</tex>.

title: A Device for Mitigating Three-Phase Load Unbalance via Inter-Phase Power Sharing
authors: Ling Zaixun, Wu Xiaomin, Cui Yibo, Fang Zhijian, Zhao Yunfei, Wang Mengxing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: Due to the uneven load distribution among phases during power grid operation, significant variations in the amplitude and phase of the three-phase currents occur. This increases power losses in the grid and, in severe cases, can cause damage to the grid itself, as well as negatively impact users by increasing the risk of equipment failure. Therefore, compensating for unbalanced loads in the power grid is an urgent issue that needs to be addressed. Traditional methods often rely on reactive power compensation. However, these methods are relatively inefficient, costly, and difficult to implement under conditions of high load imbalance. In light of these limitations—and considering factors such as cost, operational efficiency, and compensation performance—this work summarizes the adverse effects of three-phase unbalanced loads and proposes a novel compensation device and control method based on interphase power balancing. The proposed device compensates for unbalanced loads through interphase power sharing and introduces voltage balancing control. As a result, it is capable of compensating three-phase unbalanced loads even under high imbalance conditions. Additionally, it significantly reduces the required bus capacitance, thereby lowering costs. The effectiveness of this device in compensating for three-phase unbalanced loads has been validated through simulation.

title: Multi-AGV Dynamic Obstacle Avoidance and Path Planning Based on Floyd Algorithm
authors: Jie Gao, Haoya Liu, Weinan Xie, Liang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the increasing demand for logistics, automated guided vehicle (AGV) technology has been widely applied in manufacturing and warehouse logistics. However, existing research still faces challenges in multi-AGV collaborative operation in complex environments with dense task distributions and moving obstacles, particularly regarding path planning accuracy and conflict resolution efficiency. This paper proposes a multi-AGV dynamic obstacle avoidance and path planning algorithm based on Floyd's algorithm, which considers both AGV region density and obstacle avoidance costs. By introducing a bidding strategy and dynamic avoidance priority, the algorithm improves the operational efficiency of multi-AGV systems. The proposed algorithm was tested and analyzed using a Matlab simulation platform. Experimental results demonstrate that, under scenarios with densely distributed task nodes, the algorithm achieves an optimization efficiency improvement of up to 9.5 % compared to the standard Floyd algorithm. Furthermore, in scenarios where AGVs operate at varying speeds, the increase in task completion time remains controlled within 7.7 %, regardless of the number of tasks. Therefore, the proposed algorithm effectively reduces the probability of conflicts between AGVs, enhances task execution efficiency, and exhibits strong adaptability and robustness in complex environments.

title: ManiNet: Manifold Network for Few-Shot Learning
authors: Rui-Qi Wang, Hengcan Shi, Yi Chen, Yaonan Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Few-shot Learning (FSL) aims to learn a model that can be seamlessly adapted to unknown classes with only a few labeled data. A concise but successful way is to learn a robust feature encoder to describe novel classes relying on given supervised data. Under the guidance of such insight, most methods define classes as standard Gaussian distributions with different means in feature spaces, where classification can be performed based on the distances between embedding and class centroids. In spite of considerable achievements, these methods always miss the structural information within classes, resulting in degraded performance. To tackle this problem, we develop a novel yet concise approach named Manifold Network (ManiNet) to perform few-shot classification based on manifolds. Technically, in the ManiNet, each class is represented as a tree rather than an isolated centroids to reserve the structural information. And a simple correction term is introduced to elevate the usage of data by representing each manifold with a graph. Benefiting from such modeling, the probability of unknown data belonging to a class is derived based on the relative energy change before and after adding this data into the class manifolds. Experimental results on popular benchmarks strongly demonstrate that our ManiNet suffices to achieve competitive performance with simpler modeling and higher robustness, compared to baselines.
