title: A Class-Aware Supervised Contrastive Quadratic Neural Network for Imbalanced Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Wei-En Yu, Shiping Zhang, Jinwei Sun, Chenyu Li, Jing-Xiao Liao, Xiaoge Zhang
abstract: Deep learning holds significant potential for bearing fault diagnosis; however, its effectiveness is often hindered by the pervasive issue of imbalanced data in industrial settings, where fault events are inherently rare. To address this widespread challenge, we propose the Class-Aware Supervised Contrastive Quadratic Neural Network (CCQNet), a novel framework combining a class-aware supervised contrastive learning scheme with a quadratic neural network backbone. Our approach introduces two key components to tackle data imbalance: a class-weighted contrastive loss and a logit adjusted cross-entropy loss, which work in tandem to ensure the model pays equal attention to both majority and minority classes. Additionally, we enhance feature extraction through a quadratic convolutional residual network, and provide a novel theoretical analysis linking the function of the quadratic neuron to the principle of autocorrelation in signal processing. Comprehensive experiments on both public and proprietary datasets demonstrate that CCQNet substantially outperforms state-of-the-art methods, particularly in scenarios with extreme data imbalance. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/yuweien1220/CCQNet for evaluation and validation.

title: Intelligent Xn-Based Energy Aware Handover Optimization in 5G Networks via NWDAF-Orchestrated Agent Framework
authors: Mahnoor Sajid, Mohib Ullah Khan, KyungHi Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Energy-aware mobility management in dense Fifth Generation (5G) networks is increasingly challenged by frequent handovers and unnecessary activation of sleeping next-generation NodeBs (gNBs), which lead to excessive energy consumption and degraded mobility reliability. Conventional Xn-based handover schemes rely on static radio thresholds and implicitly assume always-active gNBs, while existing NWDAF-enabled approaches improve stability but do not explicitly account for energy costs during handover execution. To address these limitations, this paper proposes the Intelligent Energy-Aware Handover Framework (IEAHF), a NWDAF-orchestrated architecture that integrates coordinated agent-based control for energy-aware mobility management. The proposed framework introduces an Energy-Aware Handover Optimization Agent (EA-HOA) to guide reliability-driven handover decisions and a Handover Energy Evaluation Agent (HEEA) to assess the energy impact of candidate handover actions, with both agents operating within a closed-loop control process enforced through Operations, Administration, and Maintenance (OAM). By reformulating handover success to incorporate energy inefficiency and enabling per-handover gNB energy reasoning at decision time, IEAHF jointly optimizes service continuity and energy efficiency. System-level simulations demonstrate that the proposed Agent-NWDAF configuration consistently outperforms baseline and analytics-only schemes, achieving tightly concentrated Energy-Aware Handover Success Rates of approximately 0.98, reducing the Energy-Aware Handover Failure Rate to the range of 0.007–0.015, and delivering up to a 32% reduction in average gNB power consumption relative to an always-on baseline. These results indicate that IEAHF provides a scalable and effective solution for energy-efficient mobility management in 5G networks and establishes a foundation for energy-aware handover control in future Sixth Generation (6G) systems.

title: Deep Neighbor Discriminant Binary Embedding for Multi-Label Image Retrieval
authors: Qibing Qin, Mingkun Dou, Wenfeng Zhang, Lei Huang, Jie Nie
abstract: Because of fast retrieval speed and low storage cost, deep hash learning has become a current research spot in multimedia retrieval, which arouses more and more interest and attention. Most of the available deep hashing methods usually employ the mini-batch strategy to construct training batches, and only one mini-batch of samples is valuable at each iteration during model optimization, which fails to explore the original neighbor structure well, leading to sub-optimal embeddings, especially for relatively large datasets. By contrast, the superior performance is achieved by optimizing the SoftMax loss for certain hashing learning, meanwhile, prior research has suggested the normalized SoftMax loss is essentially identical to one smoothed triplet-wise function, in which each class is assigned to one single semantic center. Nevertheless, under real-world scenarios, one class could correspond to multiple local clusters rather than just a single one. To this end, by extending SoftMax loss with multiple semantic centers, a novel deep hashing framework, called Deep Neighbor Discriminant Binary Embedding (NDBE) framework, is presented to generate discriminative hash codes with the original neighbor structure preservation. Specifically, by expanding the size of the last fully connected layer to increase multiple centers for each class, a novel SoftTriple loss is proposed to capture the latent semantic distribution of the original samples and reduce the intra-class variance, which is optimized without constraints sampling. To learn the different numbers of each class center, the class-center aggregation strategy is developed to obtain the compact set of centers while maintaining semantic similarity. Extensive experiments on three public multi-label datasets show that our proposed NDBE framework achieves superior visual similarity search performance over several state-of-the-art approaches. The source code for the implementation of our proposed NDBE framework is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/QinLab-WFU/NDBE</uri>.

title: Image Quality Assessment: Unifying Local and Global Distortions via Fisher Information Geometry and Optimal Sinkhorn Transport
authors: Hangyu Li, Shiyou Liu, Dongzi Wang, You Zhao, Xuekai Wei, Weizhi Xian, Jielu Yan, Xing He, Yong Feng, Jun Luo, Huayan Pu, Qin Mao, Xia Yang, Mingliang Zhou
abstract: A prevailing limitation of existing full-reference image quality assessment (FR-IQA) methods is their segregated focus on either local texture or global structure, preventing a unified analysis of these two critical and interrelated dimensions of human perception. To resolve this, we propose the Fisher Sinkhorn image quality assessment (FSIQA) framework, which establishes a principled unification of information geometry and optimal transport theory. FSIQA leverages a dual-branch architecture: its Fisher branch captures localized, nonGaussian texture distortions by modelling higher-order statistics on a Riemannian manifold, whereas the complementary Sinkhorn branch assesses global structural deformations via entropy-regularized optimal transport. This synergistic framework creates a mathematical continuum for local-to-global degradation analysis and is notably independent of the backbone network architecture, thus requiring no dataset-specific retraining. Comprehensive experiments across five benchmark datasets validate the efficacy of our approach, demonstrating that FSIQA consistently outperforms existing methods and establishes a new state-of-the-art method in the field.

title: Real-time Spatiotemporal Gait Analysis Using a Hip Assistive Wearable Robot
authors: Hyundo Kim, Kyeongsu Shi, Hyunjin Choi, Dong-wook Rha, Kyoungchul Kong
abstract: Wearable robots for lower-limb assistance have advanced significantly in recent years. However, unlike multi-joint assistive robots that support the hip, knee, or ankle joints, hip-only systems are typically equipped with fewer sensors, which limits their ability to support quantitative performance evaluation and gait analysis during use. This study presents a gait analysis algorithm embedded in a hip-assistive wearable robot (SUIT H10, Angel Robotics), designed to estimate key spatiotemporal and kinematic gait parameters using only onboard sensors. The algorithm performs real-time estimation of foot contact event, forward velocity, and knee joint angle using hip joint encoders and inertial sensors. Spatiotemporal gait parameters, including cadence, swing phase ratio, walking speed, and step/stride lengths, are subsequently computed through post-processing after shortwalking trials. The algorithmwas validated through 64walking trials involving 17 healthy adults, and its post-processed gait metrics were quantitatively compared with those from a commercial optical system (OptoGait). These results demonstrate the feasibility of quantitative gait assessment for hip-assistive robots without external measurement systems, supporting performance evaluation in both clinical and real-world settings.

title: Correlation-Based Channel Estimation Scheme for Double-IRS-Assisted MIMO Systems
authors: Shiguo Wang, Shengnan Tian, Rukhsana Ruby, Xiukai Ruan, Qingyong Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Distributed intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRSs) are promising to be deployed in urban environments for enhanced performance in signal coverage, system capacity and delay without additional consumption of bandwidth and transmit power. However, obtaining the individual channel state information (CSI) of the cascaded path through each IRS is challenging, and most existing estimation schemes usually treat all the distributed IRSs as a virtual entity and do not consider the individual CSI of each IRS, which restricts full exploration on IRS-assisted systems and design flexibility. In this paper, for distributed double-IRS-assisted communication systems with multiple users, a novel channel estimation scheme is proposed, through which the individual CSI of the double IRSs is obtained simultaneously, and the performance in accuracy and delay is improved greatly as well. Specifically, leveraging an optimization-based framework with the maximum a posteriori (MAP) approach, we first estimate the overall CSI by turning all the IRS elements on. Then, the individual CSI of each IRS is derived by employing the unrelated characteristics among the elements of different IRSs, which is more efficient and accurate compared to the conventional ones. Numerical simulations are presented to show the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed channel estimation scheme.

title: Predicting the Impact Likelihood of Factors on Higher Education Learners’ AI Usage Readiness in Can Tho, Vietnam
authors: Tuan-Kiet H.V. Truong, Dang Vo Gia Le
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: The objective of this study is to determine the factors that influence the readiness of higher education students in Can Tho, Vietnam, to use AI. This research implemented a quantitative methodology, employing a five-point Likert scale questionnaire to accumulate data through a non-probability sampling approach. The main statistical method for analysing the primary data from 280 respondents is Binary Logistic Regression. The findings suggest that AI utilisation readiness is positively and significantly influenced by factors such as usefulness (US), ease to use (EU), hedonic learning (HE), training advocacy (TA), and interaction (IN). The model demonstrated a predictive accuracy of 97.5%. The results indicate that perceived utility (US) was identified as the most significant determinant $(\operatorname{Exp}(B)=8.249)$, suggesting that a 724.9% increase in the probability of using AI is associated with each unit increase in perceived usefulness. By integrating a training advocacy (TA), hedonic learning (HE), ease of use (EU), and interaction (IN) factors, students’ readiness for AI usage was improved by 538.7%, 392.3%, 298.4%, and 186.2%, respectively. This study has made it clear that willingness to use AI in learning is not only a result of evaluating the tool in terms of effectiveness and convenience, but also reflects a combination of intrinsic motivation, institutional support, and interaction quality. This suggests that universities and technology developers should take a multi-dimensional approach to promoting AI applications, including user-friendly design, ensuring clear usefulness, creating interesting learning experiences, providing structured training, and maintaining transparent and trustworthy interactions

title: Predicting Vehicle Lifecycle Emissions and Hidden Carbon Tax via Multitask TabNet
authors: Christopher Immanuel Widjaja, Ir. Yulyani Arifin, S. Kom
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: An explainable multitask TabNet model is proposed to jointly predict vehicle fuel/energy consumption, tailpipe CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, lifecycle CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, and per-kilometer hidden carbon cost under carbon pricing. The model integrates Canadian Fuel Consumption Ratings, U.S. EPA eGRID intensities, GREET fuel and battery pathways, and World Bank carbon price data to capture crosstask dependencies. Compared with single-task baselines, the multitask approach consistently improves accuracy, most notably reducing errors in tailpipe CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, while providing interpretable feature importance through attention masks. Results show that engine size and vehicle type are dominant drivers, with varying importance patterns across ICE, PHEV, and BEV. The approach combines performance and interpretability, supporting lifecycleaware vehicle emissions analysis.

title: 3D VR Simulation Training Method for Power Transformer Withstand Voltage Testing
authors: Dan Fang, Jiangwei Chen, Lianlian Xu, Bingjie Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: As the core equipment for power transmission and voltage transformation in electrical power systems, power transformers require withstand voltage experiment, which are a key technical means for detecting insulation reliability and preventing operational failures. Traditional training for withstand voltage experiment relies on theoretical instruction from textbooks and a “master-apprentice” model for practical skill transfer. This approach results in suboptimal training outcomes due to limited hands-on opportunities and poses significant safety risks when inexperienced personnel directly operate real equipment. To address the pain points of traditional training methods—poor effectiveness, high costs, and insufficient safety—this paper proposes a virtual reality (VR)-based 3D simulation training method for power transformer withstand voltage experiment. By deeply integrating VR technology with operational training for withstand voltage experiment, this method effectively resolves the challenges of “high cost, high consumption, and high risk” faced by current training centers. Compared to traditional training models, the constructed system demonstrates significant improvements in key metrics such as trainee satisfaction and training effectiveness.

title: Achieving Near-Ideal Flatness in Honeywords via Antimagic Labeling and Machine Learning
authors: Wenhao Ji, Xiaohui Zhang, Jiafang Wei, Fei Ma, Qiang Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: With the proliferation of multi-agent authentication systems, potential threats from inter-agent authentication requests are increasing. If an agent’s credentials are compromised, the entire system becomes vulnerable to penetration or sabotage. To address this challenge, we incorporate honeywords technology into the multi-agent authentication system. However, current scholarly explorations have achieved high flatness honeywords only within theoretical frameworks, and it remains difficult to realize in practice due to constraints imposed by attackers. To address this issue, we propose a novel honeywords generation method based on antimagic labeling. This approach leverages the antimagic labeling of graphs, a fundamental model in complex networks, to construct antimagic graphs, which are then used to generate antimagic sequences for creating honeywords. Furthermore, we introduce a honeywords evaluator powered by a random forest algorithm. This evaluator proactively filters out honeywords that exhibit significant distinguishability from the real password before they can be exploited by attackers, thereby ensuring the honeyword set infinitely approaches the theoretical high flatness and achieving an adaptive and intelligent security assessment. We employ a dual server architecture where the honeychecker alerts the main server upon detecting a login attempt with a honeyword, thus triggering corresponding protective mechanisms. Finally, through a complexity analysis against mainstream attack methods, we demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed scheme.

title: Vulnerability-Based Probabilistic Seismic Performance Quantification of 750 kV Transformer
authors: Yongfu Ma, Lili Wang, Kunning He, Zile Wang, Chengong Li, Qiang Xie
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Transformers play an essential role in substations but tend to be fragile under earthquake excitations. To investigate the seismic vulnerability of the 750 kV transformer, a numerical model was established. Modal analysis and seismic response time-history computation were conducted to identify the weak points of the transformer, a vulnerability evaluation under seismic loading was conducted. The results show that the first two natural frequencies of the structure are 1.12 Hz and 1.43 Hz, respectively. The turret and air-side bushing significantly amplify the acceleration response. At a PGA of 0.2 g, the mean stress at the HV-bushing root reaches 21.78 MPa, with a safety factor of 2.3, and the peak displacement at the top of the bushing can be controlled to within 35 mm. Vulnerability analysis indicates that when the PGA is 0.2 g, the failure likelihood of the HV bushing is below 7%, and the structure remains safe. However, once the PGA exceeds 0.5g, the failure likelihood increases significantly.

title: A Low-Cost Edge ML System for Real-Time Drinking Water Contamination Detection
authors: Asif Ahmed Joy, Mohaiminul Islam, Tarem Ahmed
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost, embedded machine learning (Edge-ML) based system for real-time bacterial contamination risk estimation in drinking water. The proposed solution integrates pH, turbidity, temperature and Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) sensors with an ESP32 microcontroller executing a trained Random Forest classifier. Unlike prior threshold-based or cloud-dependent models, our approach performs fully offline, lightweight classification optimized for resource-constrained microcontroller environments. A publicly available dataset was used to train the model, which was then deployed on-device using micromlgen. Field trials were conducted across diverse water sources. The system achieved 94% test accuracy, with a latency of under 700 ms and a deployment cost below USD 20. With its portability, scalability and low cost, the system serves as an effective early-warning tool for mitigating waterborne disease outbreaks in underserved regions.

title: Breakdown Characteristics of SF6 Gas in Different Electric Field Forms Under Impulse Voltage
authors: Changhong Zhang, Weiguo Li, Xu Yang, Mingyang Li, Shuo Zhao, Youping Tu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The characteristics of SF6 gas in withstanding impulse voltage within UHV GIS and GIL systems determine their insulation design. This paper established an impulse breakdown voltage test platform to investigate the impulse breakdown properties of SF6 gas under varying gas pressures, voltage polarities, and degrees of electric field non-uniformity. Test results indicate: Under slightly non-uniform and extremely non-uniform fields, the polarity effect on SF6 gas impulse breakdown field strength is pronounced. In slightly non-uniform fields, positive-polarity breakdown field strength exceeds negative-polarity values. In extremely non-uniform fields, negative-polarity breakdown field strength surpasses positive-polarity values. As the electric field non-uniformity coefficient increases, impulse breakdown field strength generally exhibits a trend of rapid initial decline followed by gradual decrease. In extremely non-uniform fields with positive polarity voltage, the SF6 switching impulse breakdown field strength exhibits a hump effect with increasing gas pressure, reaching its minimum value at 0.6 MPa.

title: A SPICE-compatible Equivalent Circuit Model for Li-ion Batteries Incorporating Diffusion and Current-Dependent Dynamics
authors: Aryan Chollera, Byunghoo Jung
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Equivalent Circuit Models (ECMs) comprising passive components and a State of Charge-dependent voltage source are commonly utilized to simulate the electrical behavior of Li-ion batteries. Fractional-order Warburg elements are traditionally integrated into ECMs to capture low-frequency diffusion effects, but their analysis and simulation remain quite complex, particularly in tools like SPICE. This paper presents a novel ECM that addresses these limitations by modeling diffusion effects exclusively with linear elements, namely, resistors and capacitors. This approach not only enables transient simulations but also accurately captures the current-dependent impedance behavior at low frequencies, a persistent challenge that remains unresolved even with the use of Warburg elements. The proposed model is applicable to Electric Vehicles (EVs) and Electric Vertical Take-Off and Landing (eVTOL) aircraft and is well-suited for simulating large-scale battery systems.

title: A Multi-Scale, Multi-Channel, and Boundary Feature Enhancement Method for Salient Object Detection
authors: Ruiyue Xu, He Yan, Qile Shen, Jie Min
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Effectively capturing the multi-scale, multi-channel, and boundary features of target images is beneficial for improving the accuracy of salient object detection. A multichannel feature enhancement module (MFEM) is constructed by concatenating two feature maps along the channel, performing convolution activation, dot product addition, and convolution operations. An edge feature enhancement module (EFEM) is constructed by performing an inverse attention mechanism and Gaussian-Laplace transform on the predicted map, channel-wise concatenation, and convolution operations with the feature submap. Multi-scale features are extracted using a SwinTransformer encoder and enhanced using the MFEM. The enhanced features are then fused using a cross-attention mechanism for multi-level features. The fused feature map, the high-frequency feature map obtained by Gaussian-Laplace transforming the predicted map, and the external edge features are input into the EFEM to obtain the salient object detection results. This results in a novel salient object detection model with multi-scale, multi-channel, and boundary feature enhancement: MfENet. Experimental results show that the Mean Absolute Error (MAE), S-measure and F-measure accuracies of our method on the HKU-IS dataset are 0.018,0.924 and 0.959 respectively. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. Furthermore, this work highlights the importance of multi-scale and boundary awareness in enhancing salient object detection, and its contribution to the technical field relevant to the theme of this conference.

title: Scene-Aware Short-Term PV Forecasting with Recency-Effect and Total-Factor Uncertainty Features Via a Multi-View Expert Network
authors: Lixiang Lin, Yuanliang Zhang, Chenxi Zhang, Xin Li, Zixuan Guo, Shuitian Li, Jiajie Deng, Jianqiang Luo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Forecasting distributed photovoltaic (DPV) output is challenging due to its high sensitivity to micro-scale meteorology and the prevalent low-fidelity of available weather data. To mitigate this dependency, this paper proposes a novel, weatherlight paradigm that prioritizes the information embedded in historical power time-series. We introduce two key feature sets: a recency-effect sequence, inspired by working-memory theory, and a set of total-factor uncertainty characteristics (TFC) that systematically quantify intrinsic volatility, mutation, and disorder. A learnable attention module dynamically weights these TFC to generate similar-uncertainty scenarios (SSU), which guide our scene-aware multi-view mixture-of-experts (SAMoE) architecture. SAMoE leverages three complementary views-TFC, recencyeffect, and auxiliary meteorology-and fuses their expert representations via a lightweight self-attention mechanism. Experiments on a real-world DPV dataset demonstrate that our framework achieves superior accuracy and robustness over strong deep-learning baselines, offering a practical solution that significantly reduces reliance on high-precision meteorological inputs.

title: A Fast Adaptive Beamwidth Control Algorithm for Mobile Millimeter Wave Communication Systems
authors: Fangyong Peng, Sheng Hu, Ansheng Duan, Mingwei Xu, Qingsong Ten, Qingwu Wang, Yangfei Xie, Di Wang, Mingyong Li, Minzhu Chen, Xuepiao Wu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: Hybrid analog-digital (HDA) beamforming technology can generate narrow beams with high directivity, which helps millimeter-wave (mmWave) communication systems reduce path loss and improve spectral efficiency (SE). This paper focuses on beam pattern optimization of HDA structures and adaptive beamwidth control to enhance beam coverage and maximize SE in mobile scenarios. First, we perform HDA beam optimization with an ideal sector beam model as the target to concentrate beam energy as much as possible. Then, with the location information of the mobile station (MS), we formulate the maximization problem of the SE. Based on the idea of binary search, we design a fast adaptive beamwidth control algorithm to solve this problem. This algorithm adaptively determines the optimal beamwidth according to the motion of the MS, and combined with the designed optimized beam, it can effectively maximize the SE. Numerical results demonstrate that, in mobile scenarios, the proposed algorithm effectively accelerates convergence to the optimal beamwidth compared to existing approaches while achieving higher SE.

title: A Railway Track Foreign Object Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv11
authors: Qingquan Zhu, Qing He
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper introduces an enhanced YOLOv11 detection algorithm tailored for complex railway scenarios, aiming to improve the accuracy and efficiency of foreign-object intrusion detection by leveraging advances in large models alongside targeted YOLOv11 upgrades. The method addresses key safety risks-objects on electrified contact networks, track intrusions, pedestrians in waiting zones, and real-time identification of ballasted turnouts during operations. To enable comprehensive and fair evaluation, we construct RailX, a multiscenario railway dataset formed by integrating RailFOD23 and RailSem19 via an intra-class sample-selection strategy, increasing diversity and representativeness across scenes and object scales. The model integrates three improvements: (1) DySample as a dynamic upsampling mechanism to sharpen spatial resolution and small-object localization; (2) an iAFF (Iterative Attention Feature Fusion) module embedded in the C3k2 block to strengthen multi-scale representation; and (3) a novel, innovatively proposed loss function-CB-ATFL (Class-Balanced Adaptive Task-Focused Loss)-which combines class-balanced weighting with adaptive, task-focused learning to stabilize training and boost overall detection. Experiments on RailX show a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.6 \% \text{mAP}$</tex> @ 50 gain over the baseline. Small-object performance improves markedly-crossing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+1.6 \%$</tex>, car <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+7.8 \%$</tex>, person <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+7.3 \%$</tex>-while sustaining 148 FPS averaged over five inference cycles. These results underscore the method's effectiveness in realistic railway environments and highlight the new CB-ATFL loss as a key driver of robustness and practical real-time applicability.

title: Hybrid-Norm Constrained Beamforming for Robust Target Detection in Millimeter-Wave Radar
authors: Ruize Xu, Haoran Lan, Zhenshuo Liu, Wenhao Xiang, Hongbin Zhang, Bojian Liu, Ting Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Millimeter-wave radar systems increasingly rely on advanced beamforming techniques address conflicting requirements such as interference suppression, computational tractability, and detection fidelity. In response to this challenge, we introduce a novel beamforming algorithm that incorporates a hybrid-norm constraint, effectively exploiting L1 and L2 regularization principles. The L1-norm encourages sparsity in the weight vector, which is an attribute that can be used to simplify hardware implementation. Meanwhile, the L2-norm enhances the algorithm’s robustness against noise and estimation errors. We develop an efficient iterative optimization procedure, complemented by an adaptive parameter selection strategy to address the issues arising from it. Our simulation results reveal a marked performance improvement compared to conventional techniques. Notably, the proposed method increases the output signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) by 2-3 dB in high-interference scenarios and substantially improves detection capability. For example, in the case of a signal-to-noise ratio of only 0 dB, its detection probability reaches 0.9, which is 2 dB higher than the L1-constrained method and a larger gap than conventional beamformers. These advancements make the algorithm a compelling choice for millimeter-wave radar applications operating in demanding electromagnetic environments where dependable target detection is paramount.

title: Surface design for tunable bandpass frequency selection based on PIN diode transmissive reflection
authors: Bo Zhang, Chengbin Jiao, Feng Song, Shipeng Chen, Yunhuan Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: A Frequency Selective Surface (FSS) is a two-dimensional periodic structure with a selective transmission or reflection function for electromagnetic waves in space. Once a traditional FSS is designed and manufactured, its frequency response characteristics remain unchanged, making it unable to adapt to complex and variable electromagnetic environments. Subsequently, researchers proposed the concept of tunable FSS and successfully realized its design by integrating active devices into traditional FSS units or using dielectric substrates with tunable permittivity. Owing to its ability to actively adjust electromagnetic properties and adapt to complex electromagnetic environments, tunable FSS plays a crucial role in fields such as antenna stealth and electromagnetic shielding. Furthermore, research on tunable FSS has attracted significant attention from many scholars, and this area of study holds important practical significance. Based on different operating mechanisms, tunable FSS can be divided into two categories: switchable type and tunable type. This paper focuses on a series of studies on the design of switchable tunable FSS. Firstly, two different transmission-reflection tunable FSS structures are proposed, adopting parallel feeding and series feeding methods respectively. When the PIN diode is in the on state, both structures exhibit transmission characteristics within a specific frequency band range, presenting a transmission state for incident electromagnetic waves; when the PIN diode is in the off state, they possess the ability to reflect electromagnetic waves. Simulation results show that within a 45° incident angle range, the switching bandwidth of both structures exceeds 20%. Meanwhile, the characteristic curves of the two structures under both TE and TM polarization modes are basically consistent, indicating good polarization stability. In addition, an equivalent circuit model is established, and the working mechanisms of the two structures are thoroughly investigated by analyzing their equivalent circuit models. Finally, a horizontal comparison reveals that the structures designed in this paper exhibit characteristics such as dual polarization, wide switching bandwidth, and low insertion loss.

title: Study on Load Reduction Effect and Influencing Factors of Centralized Maintenance Based on Linear Mixed-Effects Model : A Case Study of Sichuan Province's Practice in 2025
authors: Wentao Chang, Kaiji Liao, Xiaojun Li, Chen Liu, Haoyuan Ma, Xiang Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: To accurately evaluate the load reduction effect and influence mechanism of power grid centralized maintenance measures, this study takes Sichuan Province's centralized maintenance practice in 2025 as the research object. It integrates operational data from the power load management system and meteorological monitoring data, and applies the Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMEM) to quantitatively test the statistical significance of the measure effects. Additionally, it conducts an in-depth analysis of the mechanisms by which user type (detailed in accordance with the National Economic Industry Classification GB/T4754 — 2017), location, and daily maximum temperature affect load reduction.The study finds that:Centralized maintenance measures achieve significant load reduction; user industry type and location are key factors driving response differences, with the impact of location being particularly prominent; the impact of daily maximum temperature is not significant. Industry and regional characteristics can effectively explain the heterogeneous characteristics of individual users. This study not only provides empirical evidence for the refined management of demand response but also offers methodological references for the effect evaluation and optimization of similar demand-side management measures in power grids.

title: Large–Small Model Synergy for High-Recall and Efficient UAV-based Aerial Surveillance
authors: Lingji Dong, Wei Zhao, Zheng Lan, Yanan Liu, Xiupeng Shi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) has become essential for environmental monitoring and disaster response, yet its effectiveness is severely limited by the computational constraints of onboard hardware. Existing systems face a prohibitive tradeoff: lightweight edge models provide real-time responsiveness but suffer from poor detection reliability, while deep learning models offer high accuracy but exceed the energy and processing budgets of autonomous platforms. Here we show a hierarchical "large–small" model synergy that reconciles these conflicting demands by integrating ultra-efficient edge screening with deep cloud-based reasoning. We employ an algorithm-hardware co-designed neural network on the UAV to screen video streams at millisecond latency, utilizing a recall-first strategy to ensure zero missed detections. Potential threats are subsequently verified in the cloud by a specialized segmentation network and a Vision–Language Model (VLM) that interprets scene context. In wildfire detection experiments, our system achieved 100% recall and 99.1% overall accuracy while maintaining onboard inference speeds of approximately 1000 frames per second. These results demonstrate that heterogeneous edge–cloud intelligence can enable efficient, robust, high-fidelity surveillance for safety-critical applications.

title: Sensor Weak Signal Denoising Algorithm Based on Adaptive Wavelet Thresholding and Kalman Filtering
authors: Dewu Yang, Gaofeng Guo, Kezheng Chang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address the problem that sensor signals are susceptible to contamination by strong noise artifacts in complex testing environments, this paper proposes a hybrid denoising method integrating wavelet transform and Kalman filtering-based adaptive filtering. First, the method employs a specific algorithm to screen and locate regions in sensor signals where noise artifacts are present. Next, it conducts wavelet decomposition and reconstruction on these target regions to extract the estimated components of the noise artifacts. Then, the Kalman filtering algorithm is introduced to dynamically predict and calibrate the estimated noise artifacts, thus optimizing denoising accuracy. Finally, the calibrated noise artifacts are subtracted from the original noise-containing regions, allowing for the reconstruction of clean sensor signals. Verification results demonstrate that this method can effectively suppress non-stationary noise while preserving the useful features of the signals, laying a high-quality data foundation for system condition monitoring and fault diagnosis.

title: Fine-Tuning Transformers and LLMs for Fake News Detection in Algerian Dialect
authors: Meriem Sellami, Djihad Arrar, Raid Nedjm Eddine Dekkar, Mohamed El Amine Bouchareb, Nadjet Kamel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: The rapid advancement of internet communication technologies has enabled users to publish news and share opinions widely. While this brings convenience, it also facilitates the spread of fake news. Although extensive research has been conducted on high-resource languages such as English, Arabic—particularly its dialects—remains underexplored due to its complex morphology, lexical ambiguity, and the scarcity of annotated datasets.We investigate the effectiveness of pretrained language models (PLMs) and large language models (LLMs) for fake news detection in the Algerian dialect using the FASSILA dataset. We evaluate a diverse set of PLMs, including the multilingual transformer (mBERT), Arabic-centric transformers (AraBERT, MARBERT, CAMeLBERT-DA, AraELECTRA), and a dialect-specific encoder (DZiriBERT), as well as LLMs (AraGPT2 variants and LLaMA 3). Our experiments compare multilingual, Arabic-specific, and dialect-adapted models, with a focus on analyzing the impact of dialectal pretraining and model scale.The results show that AraGPT2 variants outperform all other models in our experiments, achieving an improvement of approximately 2–3% compared to previously reported results on the FASSILA dataset. These findings highlight the value of large-scale Arabic pretraining for capturing richer semantic patterns and improving performance in downstream NLP tasks, including dialectal fake news detection.

title: A Method for Multi-Scenario Experimental Verification of Automatic Control Software in Power Dispatching Automation Systems
authors: Huiguang Xu, Jiangfeng Qian, Siyuan Zhang, Fang Li, Changjun Zhou, Chen Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: Due to the large-scale grid integration of renewable energy, the operational characteristics of the power grid have become increasingly complex. Significant deficiencies are observed in current testing methods for automatic control software, particularly regarding test depth, breadth of scenario coverage, and the intelligence of scenario generation. To address these deficiencies, a method for multi-scenario experimental verification of automatic control software is proposed in this paper. The closed-loop verification environment is constructed by integrating a mirrored dispatching automation system and a power grid simulation system. Thereby coordinated experiments of state, regional, and provincial dispatching systems are achieved. Furthermore, artificial intelligence technology is integrated. Intelligent generation and comprehensive coverage of experimental scenarios are realized using K-Means clustering and Monte Carlo sampling algorithms. Finally, a closed-loop experimental verification is conducted on the automatic generation control strategy to demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method.

title: Development and Effectiveness Analysis of Virtual Reality on Students' Understanding and Application of Laboratory Safety Procedures in School
authors: Fatimatuzzahro', Khakam Ma'ruf, Rizal Justian Setiawan, Tony Tai-Ting Liu, Nur Azizah, Darmono
abstract: Laboratory environments play a vital role in developing scientific skills, however pose risks due to hazardous materials and inadequate safety comprehension among junior high school students. This study developed and evaluated Virtual Reality (VR)-based instructional media to improve students' understanding of laboratory safety procedures. Using the 4D Research and Development model (Define, Design, Develop, Disseminate), the VR module was assessed through expert validation, pre-test and post-test, and usability analysis. Expert evaluations rated the learning media as highly feasible, with an average score of 4.5 out of 5. Testing with 35 seventh-grade students showed significant knowledge improvement, with mean scores rising from 57.71 to 85.43, equaling a 48.03% increase. Usability, measured using the System Usability Scale (SUS), yielded a score of 85.6, indicating excellent user satisfaction. These results suggest that VR-based learning media is an effective, engaging, and user-friendly tool for enhancing laboratory safety education in junior high school settings.

title: GS-YOLO: Lightweight Small-Target Detection for Photovoltaic Infrared Defects
authors: Zhiyang Xu, Shouhong Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Against the backdrop of global clean energy transition, photovoltaic defect detection is crucial for ensuring the operational reliability of power plants. This paper addresses two major challenges in UAV-based infrared inspection-high missdetection rates of small target hotspots and substantial computational load-by proposing GS-YOLO, a lightweight network. By systematically integrating Depthwise Separable Convolutions (DWConv), a custom LightSPPF module, and a Dual SimAM attention mechanism, the model enhances small target feature extraction capability while maintaining a lightweight architecture. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to the YOLOv8n baseline model, GS-YOLO improves the mAP50 for core defect hotspots from 67.5% to 74.6%, while maintaining model size and overall precision at a comparable level. This provides an effective solution for UAV-based PV inspection, achieving an optimal balance of high accuracy, lightweight design, and real-time performance.

title: A Dual-Path Physics-Constrained Fusion Network for Lithium-ion Battery SOH Estimation
authors: Xinpeng Luo, Zhiwei Xu, Yajie Liu, Tao Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Accurate and reliable estimation of the State of Health (SOH) for lithium-ion battery is crucial to ensure its safe and efficient operation. This paper proposes a Dual-Path Physics-Constrained Fusion Network (DPCFN) for SOH estimation, considering the kinetic asymmetry in the charge and discharge processes of lithium-ion battery. The DPCFN integrates an asymmetric degradation model to separately capture the distinct degradation mechanisms between charging and discharging. The network architecture includes input layer, feature extraction layer, dual-path processing layer, physics-constrained fusion layer, and prediction layer. The dual-path processing layer uses independent bidirectional long short-term memory networks to handle the unique characteristics of charging and discharging. The physics-constrained fusion layer ensures that the prediction results conform to fundamental electrochemical principles. Experimental test demonstrates the effectiveness of the DPCFN.

title: Hybrid Compressed Air Energy System
authors: Jon W. Teets
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: The Hybrid Compressed Air Energy System generates electrical power from stored compressed air, at a temperature of $754 \mathrm{~K}(897 \mathrm{~F})$ and pressure of 49.1bar (712psia). The air properties are acquired via high speed four stage centrifugal impellers, driven by a permanent magnet machine, requiring 906 kW. Stored compressed air is reduced to 4.07bar (59 psia) to drive a radial turbine wheel, attached to a high- speed permanent magnet generator (PMG) to produce 403 kW. Residual turbine exhaust temperature from the turboalternator is then directed to an organic rankine-cycle (ORC) system that produces an additional 51 kW output power. This makes for a total of 454 kW output from the turbo-alternators. An additional energy system, using the initial stored compressed air energy, applies fired hydrogen fuel (via a combustor assembly) to the turbine stage inlet at pressure 4.07 bar (59 psia). This is considered the Hybrid Compressed Air Energy System. Introduction of fired hydrogen fuel increases the turbo-alternator turbine stage inlet temperature from $754 \mathrm{~K}(897 \mathrm{~F})$ to $1255 \mathrm{~K}(1800 \mathrm{~F})$. This is a like system to the prior described. However, with a much higher PMG output of 806 kW from the turbo-alternator. The residual turbine exhaust gas is directed to an ORC system that produces 343 kW. For a total of 1149 kW.

title: Research on Data Trusted Registration System Based on Blockchain Technology
authors: Junhan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Traditional technical solutions face inherent limitations in ensuring data ownership transparency, tamper-proofing, and traceability in data factor markets, particularly regarding core challenges like unclear ownership rights, high transaction costs, and trust deficits. This paper proposes and designs a blockchain-based data trust registration system. The system is theoretically grounded in the “three rights separation” framework of “rights bundles” and architecturally divided into six layers: data resource layer, storage layer, core layer, service layer, consensus and security layer, and application layer. It enables systematic processing and hierarchical interaction of complex data through progressive data trust certification, ownership transfer and traceability, and integrity verification. This establishes a collaborative, efficient, and trustworthy data registration infrastructure. By integrating on-chain and off-chain storage coordination, the system achieves efficient certification and secure balance of data rights. Additionally, the paper explores extended technologies like cross-chain interoperability and zero-knowledge proofs to address future needs for multi-chain coexistence and sensitive data registration. Ultimately, this solution aims to provide core technological support for building a trustworthy, efficient, and open data factor circulation environment, thereby reducing transaction trust costs and unlocking the latent economic value of data.

title: Evaluating Vision Transformers for Robust Feature Extraction in Engineering Applications
authors: Owen Shi, Haiyan Sally Xie, Mangolika Bhattacharya, Rohini Rajgonda Zore
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Automated inspection and infrastructure monitoring require visual representations that remain consistent across varying viewpoints, lighting conditions, and occlusions. Existing supervised approaches often fail to achieve this robustness and demand extensive labeled data. This paper introduces a self-supervised, multi-view Vision Transformer framework that integrates three complementary strategies: SelfDistillation with No Labels (DINO) for global semantic consistency, Masked Autoencoder (MAE) for fine-grained reconstruction, and Segment Anything Model (SAM) for zero-shot segmentation. By leveraging unlabeled multi-view datasets derived from transportation and infrastructure scenarios, the framework minimizes annotation costs while improving viewpoint invariance. Performance is evaluated using Cross-View Classification Accuracy (CVCA), mean Intersection over Union (mIoU), and a novel Viewpoint Robustness Index (VRI). Results demonstrate an average CVCA of 98.97%, $\mathbf{m I o U}$ of 98.97%, and VRI $\geqslant 0.99$ for most cases, representing a 7% accuracy improvement over zeroshot baselines. These findings highlight the potential of selfsupervised Vision Transformers for scalable, reliable inspection systems in engineering applications. Future work will explore multimodal integration and domain-adaptive augmentation to enhance resilience under extreme conditions.

title: TA-MTL: Task-Adaptive Meta Transfer Learning for Few-shot Learning
authors: Xiaobo Sun, Shuai Pan, Haozhu Zhao, Ming Xia, Wenyu Hu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Meta-learning-based methods have been widely adopted to address few-shot learning challenges. After training on a diverse set of tasks, meta-learning models can achieve strong generalization performance on new tasks. However, meta-learning still faces several critical limitations: (1) During meta-training, the base learner is overfitted to the training tasks, leading to insufficient generalization capability on new tasks; (2) Deep neural networks are prone to overfitting when only a small number of labeled samples are available, meta learning therefore often uses shallow networks, which limits its performance on complex tasks. To address these issues, we propose a task-adaptive meta transfer learning (TA-MTL) to alleviate the problem of "catastrophic forgetting" and solve the problem of overfitting. The meta transfer learning optimizes and improves the model's new-task adaptability through contrastive learning, and the task-adaptive algorithm can learn an unbiased base learner and prevent it from over-performing in meta training tasks. TA-MTL learns an unbiased base learner and prevents overfitting to meta-training tasks. Experiments on benchmarks (Mini-ImageNet and FC100) demonstrate that TA-MTL exhibits satisfactory performance in few-shot Learning.

title: Design of a Piezoelectric Vertical-Driven MEMS Electric Field Sensor with Interference Suppression
authors: Gulin Xia, Maoheng Jing, Xiong Xiao, Chun Li, Ruimin Yuan, Shaojun Yang
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Aiming at the problem that MEMS electric field sensors are affected by the same frequency crosstalk noise caused by the excitation voltage, this paper proposes and designs a double-shielded piezoelectric vertical excitation high-sensitivity MEMS electric field sensor sensitive structure with crosstalk suppression. A double-shielded drive structure and static capacitance self-compensation technology are proposed to achieve self-suppression of the drive crosstalk of the MEMS electric field sensor and increase the detection signal-to-noise ratio. The finite element multi-physics field coupling simulation verifies that under an external electric field of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-200 ~\mathrm{V} / \mathrm{m} \sim 200 ~\mathrm{V} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>, compared with the traditional piezoelectric vertically actuated vibration structure, the proposed double-shielded driven structure can reduce the crosstalk by about 73 times. Furthermore, when the static capacitance self-compensation adjustment factor is 0.1, the drive crosstalk can be further reduced by 5 times, providing a reference for improving the key performance indicators such as detection sensitivity and resolution of piezoelectric vertically actuated electric field sensors.

title: A Proactive, AI-Enhanced Safety Framework for Emerging Long-Duration Energy Storage Technologies
authors: Venkat Durvasulu, Timothy Craig Coburn, Christopher G. Searles
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Safety for Long-Duration Energy Storage (LDES) is governed by NFPA 855 (installation), UL 9540/9540A (listing and large-scale fire tests), and IFC §1207. While these have improved, they remain largely incident-driven, creating a “reactive gap” for emerging chemistries and non-battery modalities. We present ProActive-SAFE, a five-stage, assistive framework that (i) anticipates hazards with QSAR-like screening, AI-enhanced FMEA/HAZOP, and physics-informed machine learning (PIML); (ii) enforces a NIST AI RMF-aligned TEVV gate for auditable data/models; (iii) translates results into code-facing artifacts mapped to NFPA 855, UL 9540/9540A, and IFC §1207; and (iv) sustains learning via a neutral, confidential $S^{3} R S$ reporting backbone for near-misses and micro-events. ProActive-SAFE does not replace manufacturer evidence, accredited testing, or AHJ authority; it focuses test plans, clarifies submittals, and enables standardized leading indicators (CII, RRE, HEI, PIF, SOE). We outline pilot KPIs and dissemination to help avoid incident-driven delays while accelerating safe inclusion of new LDES technologies in existing codes and listings.

title: Contrastive Learning for Enhancing Model Selection in Sequential Recommender Ensemble*
authors: Ye Tao, Tianxing Wang, Oliver Qiu, Can Wang, Alan Wee-Chung Liew
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: The field of recommendation systems aims to address the problem of information overload by employing classifier and ensemble-based strategies. Early ensemble models explored approaches that made use of user meta-data to create a performance predictor. This predictor then guided the ensemble model in assigning weights to the base recommenders. However, such approaches are not feasible in real-world scenarios where user meta-data is unavailable, such as in ad hoc searching. In this study, we introduce a novel ensemble model called CRESRec, which is both user-agnostic and instance-aware. CRESRec harnesses the power of contrastive learning to acquire representations of the base recommenders from historical interactions. These representations enable CRESRec to capture patterns and characteristics within the recommenders' outputs. Experimental results demonstrate that CRESRec surpasses state-of-the-art recommender ensembles, achieving improvements of up to 38%. This study emphasizes the importance of constructing representations for base recommenders in ad-hoc inquiries and sheds light on the potential of ensemble learning in sessionbased recommendation systems.

title: Digital Twin for Strategic Carbon Policies Management in Logistic Networks
authors: Manuele Favero, Ece Şirvancι, Chiara Schiavo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: We investigate the management of carbon policies in third-party logistics (3PL) networks by combining a Digital Twin (DT) representation of real transportation systems with gametheoretic decision models. The proposed framework integrates traffic, geospatial, and emission data to construct a weighted logistics graph, enabling the simulation and evaluation of carbon policies impacts under different decision-making schemes. We analyzed two game-theoretic formulations: a static non-cooperative game and a Stackelberg game with leader-follower dynamics. In both settings, equilibria are computed and compared against the centralized optimum using efficiency metrics such as the Price of Anarchy (PoA) and Price of Stability (PoS). Simulation results on a real case study in Central Anatolia, Türkiye, show that selfish routing induces significant inefficiencies in the static case, while the Stackelberg formulation substantially improves performance, reducing PoA to below than 1.01 at high incentive levels. However, the hierarchical structure also introduces utility asymmetries between players due to first-mover advantage. These results indicate that the integration of DT-based simulation with gametheoretic models offers a robust analytical framework to quantify the trade-offs among efficiency, environmental objectives, and fairness in decentralized logistics networks under carbon policy constraints.

title: Electromagnetic Processes Simulation Peculiarities in the Mouthpiece Size Inductor of the Press Mechanism
authors: Dmytro Yarymbash, Mykhaylo Kotsur, Tetyana Divchuk, Yulia Bezverkhnyaya, Iryna Kylymnyk, Tetyana Solodovnikova
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The non-stationary nonlinear mathematical model of an electromagnetic field in size inductor, which considering of the design features is presented. The method of the transformation process to the linear stationary model, which based on ordered enumeration method for calculation of the effective values of the electromagnetic properties with help error minimization criteria of the inductor active power, is developed. The high calculation accuracy, reducing simulation time and computational resources are achieved.

title: Study of a Novel Ultra-High Power Solid-State Protective Device
authors: Qiwei Li, Jun Ding, Haoxiang Li, Wen Cao, Hao Li, Chang Zhai, Chenjiang Guo, Jinyong Fang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In order to meet the need of protecting high-power front door coupling signals in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C}$</tex>-band receivers, a multistage solid -state high power electromagnetic (HPEM) protective device is designed in this paper. The protector adopts a three-stage cascade structure and has the characteristics of high protective power, fast response time and low insertion loss. The results show that the output power of the protector is less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 ~ d B m}$</tex> under the injection of narrowband HPEM signal with 1 kW peak power, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> pulse width and 1 kHz repetition rate. At the same time, in the range of 6 GHz to 7 GHz, the insertion loss is better than 0.8 dB. The protective device can be applied to the HPEM front-door coupling protection of C-band RF receiving system.

title: Proposing Deep Learning Models for Vietnamese Medical Prescription Information Extraction
authors: Dung Lai Tri, Bang Nguyen Anh, Hiep Van Hoang, Phat Huu Nguyen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: The extraction of medical prescription information is important to reduce prescribing errors and enable efficient management. However, the large variability in prescription layouts poses significant challenges to conventional OCR pipelines. In this work, we present a unified deep learning framework that combines scene text detectors (EAST, CRAFT, YOLO) for text localization with recognition engines (TrOCR, VietOCR) for transcription. Based on the VAIPE dataset, our system achieves 94.76% word precision and 97.96% character precision. Moreover, fine-tuning VietOCR on VAIPE reduced its normalized edit distance by 43.5% relative to the pre-finetuning baseline.

publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)

title: Tuning Superconductivity and Transparency via Oxygen Vacancies in Indium Tin Oxide
authors: Oleh Feia, Aaron Parra, Meiyi Li, Danylo Menesenko, Leunam Fernandez-Izquierdo, Elena Zhitlukhina, Andrii Shapovalov, Mikhail Belogolovskii, Manuel Quevedo-Lopez, Ali Aliev
abstract: The fundamental challenge in integrating photonic and superconducting functionalities lies in the coexistence of superconductivity and high optical transparency on a single chip. Indium-tin oxide (ITO) films offer a promising platform spanning the visible, near-infrared (NIR), and mid-IR spectral regions. Understanding the key factors that balance these fundamental properties is essential for advancing related device technologies. Engineering of oxygen deficiency in transparent oxide conductors provides a viable route toward transparent superconductivity with tailored electronic and optical properties. Using density functional theory (DFT), we modeled oxygen-deficient surface states in reduced ITO films. The simulations reveal that the most favorable mechanism for inducing surface superconductivity involves the removal of oxygen atoms from the film surface, which flattens the electronic bands near the Fermi level.

title: Multi-AAVs Collision-Free Flocking and Navigation in Dynamic Environments_supp1-3664039.pdf
authors: Ammar Hawbani
abstract: Multiple autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) system is consisting of multi-AAVs through collaborative techniques to efficiently accomplish various tasks in complex scenarios. Collision free flocking and navigation strategies for multi-AAVs in complex dynamic environments are still challenging in several aspects: real-time collision avoidance of dynamic obstacles, complexity of collaborative multi-AAV path planning, and group stagnation problem due to local optimization in dynamic environments. Traditional flocking algorithms usually rely on strict rules with limited adaptability to different environments. While Reinforcement Learning (RL), as a flexible and model-free framework, is able to solve some of the problems through autonomous exploration, it still suffers from challenges such as sample inefficiency, unstable training process, easy to fall into local optimums of the policy, and difficult to converge in sparse reward scenarios. In order to improve the training efficiency, we propose an improved algorithm called Curriculum-based Heuristic Guidance TD3 (CHTD3) for solving collision-free flocking and navigation of multiple AAVs in dynamic environments. The core idea is to decompose the task of AAV collision avoidance and navigation in dynamic environments into multiple sub-tasks and gradually increase the number of sub-tasks in a staged manner, while combining the heuristic rules with the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic policy gradient (TD3) to solve the problem of prolonged learning cycle commonly found in the traditional RL methods. In addition, to better cope with complex and dynamic environments, we customize a risk assessment criterion and introduce a Human-AAV Collaboration (HAC) mechanism to enable the human operator to intervene at necessary moments to assist the AAV's motion decision. The experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the CHTD3-HAC approach in multi-AAVs collision-free flocking and navigation.

title: Low-profile Chessboard Metasurface for Monostatic and Multistatic Radar Cross Section Reduction
authors: María Guijarro-Maortua, Francisco Luna Valero, David Ramos Somolinos, David Poyatos Martínez, Jesús Grajal, José Manuel Fernández González
abstract: Advances in radar systems have led to the emergence of low observability technologies that minimize target detectability. Among them, metasurfaces and metamaterials appear as promising solutions. In this paper, the analysis and design of a single-layer scattering metasurface with a thickness of λs/10 is presented for both monostatic and multistatic scenarios, simultaneously. The design achieves -12 dB of radar cross section reduction in the band 9.16 - 11.72 GHz, which means a reduction in the monostatic detection distance of 50%. Due to the symmetry and periodicity of the structure, the metasurface is infinitely replicable and the response has been validated for both polarizations. Moreover, the radar cross section reduction is applicable for an incidence range of θ = [−60, 60]°. Several prototypes have been fabricated and measured, and the results have been experimentally validated. For the measurement process, the vector background subtraction technique was used, as well as time gating and cylindrical mode expansion to transform from near-field to far-field.

title: Dual Label Association Recovery for Partial Multi-Label Learning
authors: Xuhuan Zhu, Yu Chen, Xiaozhao Fang, Jie Wen, Jing Zhang, Guoxu Zhou, Shengli Xie
abstract: Partial Multi-Label Learning (PML) deals with a practical scenario where each instance is associated with a set of candidate labels, among which only a subset corresponds to the ground-truth labels while the others are unrelated. Existing PML methods typically employ label association recovery as a structural disambiguation strategy to identify credible labels. However, these methods attempt to recover associations directly from candidate labels, which leads to unreliable disambiguation due to the distorted label structures. To this end, this paper proposes a novel PML method via dual label association recovery (PML-DLAR).The essential strategy is to first eliminate the spurious correlations in label space before recovering dual label associations. Specifically, a biorthogonal transformation is employed to decouple the instance-level and label-level association structures affected by noisy labels. Subsequently, reliable instance-level associations are reconstructed through global geometric structure alignment between feature and pseudo-label spaces. Finally, class specific feature representations are constructed through class prototypes to guide label-level semantic association recovery in label space. Comprehensive experiments validate the superior performance of PML-DLAR over state-of-the-art methods.

title: KnitStructNet: A Structure-Aware Dual-Scale Framework for Stitch-Level Segmentation on Physically Captured Knitted Fabrics
authors: Osman Gumus, Erhan Akin, Ilhan Aydin, Irfan Yolcular
abstract: Semantic segmentation of real knitted fabrics remains challenging due to extreme class imbalance, subtle inter-class similarity, and loop-level geometric variations that synthetic datasets fail to represent. Prior approaches rely heavily on rendered or hybrid imagery and therefore struggle with the photometric and structural irregularities found in physical textiles. This study introduces a fully non-synthetic segmentation framework that reconstructs 13-class stitch instruction maps directly from geometrically aligned, physically captured knitted surfaces. The model integrates multi-scale contextual reasoning with row–column structural modeling to jointly capture fine-grained loop detail and broader directional dependencies. Evaluated exclusively on real knitted samples, the system achieves 95.65% pixel accuracy, a 0.7829 mean IoU, and an 80.2% macro F1-score, demonstrating robust performance across both frequent and rare stitch types. By eliminating reliance on synthetic renderers, the proposed approach establishes a physically grounded baseline for stitch-level instruction-map reconstruction; full program-level instruction-code generation and industrial inspection pipelines are beyond the scope of this work and are left for future study.

title: A 2.4-GHz -247.7-dB Jitter-FoM Ring-VCO-based Injection-Locked PLL with Triple-Point Injection and Injection Timing-Pulsewidth Calibration
authors: Zhen Li, Yuxiao Zhou, Shize Duan, Xing Quan, Yuanyuan Cui, Xiang Yi, Xunying Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: This brief introduces an injection-locked phase-locked loop (ILPLL) with triple-point injection and background injection pulse self-calibration techniques. The proposed triple-point injection scheme effectively eliminates the phase shift induced by conventional direct injection while enhancing injection strength by combining direct injection, cross-coupled complementary injection, and tail injection, thereby achieving low jitter. Furthermore, concurrent calibration of both injection timing and pulsewidth provides further jitter suppression and ensures low spur. Fabricated in a 28-nm CMOS process, the proposed ILPLL achieves an RMS jitter of 135 fs, a reference spur of -70.9 dBc, and a state-of-the-art FoM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">jitter</sub> of -247.7 dB at an operating frequency of 2.4 GHz. The proposed ILPLL consumes 9.3 mW power from a 0.9 V supply and occupies an active area of 0.02 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Creep prediction of fluoroplastics based on Physics Informed Neural Networks
authors: Wuqi Gao, Yinan Ren, Zhidong Yang
abstract: Fluoroplastics are key sealing materials in rocket engines. They can easily fail due to creep during long-term storage, which may threaten the engines’ safety during operation. Accurate prediction of their time-dependent deformation under varying temperature and pressure conditions remains a critical challenge. Existing methods for predicting polymer creep, such as empirical methods and physically-based constitutive models, often depend on fixed working conditions and require material composition analysis. To address these issues, this paper proposes a physics-informed two-stage framework for predicting the extrapolative creep behavior of fluoroplastics. An Extended Burgers Model (EBM) with adaptive parameters is employed to represent viscoelastic behavior under coupled temperature–pressure variations. A hybrid PSO–NLS strategy is developed to robustly identify EBM parameters from 18 experimental creep curves, overcoming initialization sensitivity and ensuring high-quality training labels. Subsequently, a neural network learns the mapping from service conditions (T,F) to EBM parameters, guided by material-specific physical constraints that enforce long-term consistency and extrapolation capabilities. The resulting model generates full creep strain curves ε(t) that are both data-driven and physically plausible. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method surpasses conventional empirical models in extrapolation accuracy, adherence to physical principles, and ease of practical implementation. It combines the strong fitting ability of data-driven models with the interpretability of physics-based models to improve both prediction accuracy and generalization performance.

title: QuantFit-VTON: A Measurement-Conditioned Diffusion Model for Predictable Garment Fit
authors: Mikhail Nikolaev, Lisov Roman, Ilya Makarov
abstract: We address the challenge of realistic, size-controllable virtual try-on—synthesizing images of people wearing new garments that fit correctly in both shape and proportion. Existing diffusion-based systems achieve photorealism but lack explicit control over garment geometry. We augment the VITON-HD dataset with numeric person and garment measurements (e.g., height, length, width) and propose a diffusion-based model QuantFit-VTON with a Measurement Encoder that conditions generation on these values. To remove dependence on the original clothing, we train using a large neutral inpainting region that lets the model adjust length and silhouette freely. Experiments show state-of-the-art perceptual quality (FID comparable to IDM-VTON) and superior geometric accuracy, reducing hem-position error by over 50%. The method delivers realistic, measurement-driven fit control and predictable garment adaptation, enabling practical applications in e-commerce and digital fashion design.

title: Velocity Disambiguation for Video Frame Interpolation_supp1-3667437.pdf
authors: Sizhuo Ma
abstract: Existing video frame interpolation (VFI) methods blindly predict where each object is at a specific timestep $t$ (“time indexing”), which struggles to predict precise object movements. Given two images of a baseball, there are infinitely many possible trajectories: accelerating or decelerating, straight or curved. This often results in blurry frames as the method averages out these possibilities. Instead of forcing the network to learn this complicated time-to-location mapping implicitly together with predicting the frames, we provide the network with an explicit hint on how far the object has traveled between start and end frames, a novel approach termed “distance indexing”. This method offers a clearer learning goal for models, reducing the uncertainty tied to object speeds. We further observed that, even with this extra guidance, objects can still be blurry especially when they are equally far from both input frames (i.e., halfway in-between), due to the directional ambiguity in long-range motion. To solve this, we propose an iterative reference-based estimation strategy that breaks down a long-range prediction into several short-range steps. When integrating our plug-and-play strategies into state-of-the-art learning-based models, they exhibit markedly sharper outputs and superior perceptual quality in arbitrary time interpolations, using a uniform distance indexing map in the same format as time indexing without requiring extra computation. Furthermore, we demonstrate that if additional latency is acceptable, a continuous map estimator can be employed to compute a pixel-wise dense distance indexing using multiple nearby frames. Combined with efficient multi-frame refinement, this extension can further disambiguate complex motion, thus enhancing performance both qualitatively and quantitatively. Additionally, the ability to manually specify distance indexing allows for independent temporal manipulation of each object, providing a novel tool for video editing tasks such as re-timing. The code is available at https://zzh-tech.github.io/InterpAny-Clearer/.

title: Computationally Efficient Predictive Learning Control for Wheeled Mobile Robots: An Offline Sampling Approach
authors: Wenxian Wang, Deyuan Meng
abstract: This article develops a practical predictive iterative learning framework for repetitive trajectory tracking tasks of wheeled mobile robots with lightweight computational resources. To improve input smoothness and reduce computational cost, an offline sampling-based predictive learning control (S-PLC) approach is proposed. Moreover, the design of S-PLC is analyzed through constructing the cost function term by term in accordance with the control objectives of high tracking accuracy, smooth control commands, and minimal computational burden. By leveraging the offline iteration-by-iteration execution property, additional initialization and filtering strategies are incorporated to improve its practical applicability. Both comparative simulations and real-world experiments validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed S-PLC.

title: M2PL-GAN: Multi-View Multi-Level Pathology Semantic Perception Learning for H&E-to-IHC Virtual Staining
authors: Zequn Liu, Liangkuan Zhu, Yining Xie, Xiaoqing Hu, Ziyu Zhang, Jing Zhao, Haochen Qi, Jiajun Chen, Jiayi Ma
abstract: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining is crucial for determining tumor subtypes, obtaining protein expression information, and developing personalized treatment plans. But compared with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining, IHC staining is more complex and expensive. With the advancement of deep learning, converting H&E stained images into IHC stained images has gradually emerged as a solution for obtaining IHC staining. However, current virtual staining processes suffer from difficulties in aligning pathological semantic features, posing significant challenges for network training, which poses significant challenges for network training. To solve these issues, we propose a multi-view multi-level pathology semantic perception learning method for H&E-to-IHC virtual staining (M2PL-GAN). Unlike prior approaches, M2PL-GAN introduces a comprehensive semantic learning paradigm from three views: structural contextual relations, feature distribution, and topology-aware fine-grained semantics. These correspond to the Context-aware Correlation Mechanism (CACM), the Local-aware Distribution Alignment Mechanism (LDAM), and the Graph-aware Bidirectional Contrastive Learning Mechanism (GBCLM) respectively. Among them, CACM enhances contextual consistency by establishing semantic correlations between virtual and real IHC images at local scales. LDAM ensures alignment of semantic feature distributions between virtual and real IHC images, mitigating semantic shifts caused by HE-IHC staining. GBCLM leverages graph neural network to capture topology-aware semantic representations and optimizes semantic feature alignment through bidirectional contrastive learning. Extensive experiments on both public and private datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches in both quantitative metrics and qualitative evaluations. Our code is available in https://github.com/Pikachu-one/M2PL-GAN.

title: Energy-Efficient and Stable Task Offloading and Resource Allocation in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks
authors: Wenwu Zhu, Xiaoheng Deng, Jingjing Zhang, Junyang He, Geyong Min
abstract: Task scheduling in sixth-generation (6G) space-air-ground integrated networks (SAGIN) faces fundamental challenges due to limited terrestrial infrastructure, time-varying wireless links, and stochastic task arrivals. In such dynamic environments, task offloading and resource scheduling decisions are strongly coupled over time through queue evolution, which makes it difficult to simultaneously guarantee service stability and energy efficiency. To address this issue, we formulate a long-term stochastic optimization problem that minimizes the average system energy consumption while stabilizing task queues.
