Leveraging Lyapunov drift-plus-penalty optimization, we develop an online Four-Phase Computation Offloading and Resource Scheduling (FCORS) algorithm that decomposes the per-slot control into four tractable subproblems and operates without any offline training. Simulation results demonstrate that FCORS achieves performance parity with state-of-the-art Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) baselines. Specifically, under heavy traffic loads, it reduces energy consumption by approximately 51.7 (QOA) while suppressing queue backlogs by approximately 90.4 (GEA). Therefore, FCORS provides a robust and lowcomplexity solution for energy-efficient task scheduling in future 6G SAGIN-enabled MEC systems.

title: Faster-than-Nyquist Signaling in the Finite Time-Bandwidth Product Regime
authors: Yong Jin Daniel Kim
abstract: This paper analyzes faster-than-Nyquist (FTN) signaling within a consistent framework based on a fixed time-bandwidth product (TBP), resolving potential ambiguities present in finite blocklength analysis. A key feature of FTN is its ability to increase the number of transmitted symbols in a given time and frequency resource, which can lower the rate penalties inherent in short packet communications. We derive tight bounds on the maximum channel coding rate (MCCR) and demonstrate that FTN’s rate gains over Nyquist signaling can be higher in the finite TBP regime than in the asymptotic case. Performance is benchmarked against the theoretical optimum of transmitting prolate spheroidal wave functions, showing that a well-designed FTN system can closely approach this limit. We present practical design criteria, including the optimal time-acceleration factor for maximizing signaling dimensions, an optimized pulse shape that meets strict out-of-band constraints, and a turbo-equalization-based coding scheme that performs near the derived MCCR bounds. These findings establish FTN as a practical and near-optimal technique for enhancing the rate and reliability of latency-constrained communications.

title: Optimal PV Power Smoothing in Hydrogen–Battery Systems Using an Adaptive Equivalent Consumption Minimization Strategy
authors: Ammar Atif Abdalla, Mohamed Hussien Elimam, Mohamed Shawky El Moursi, Ahmed Al-Durra, Khalifa Hassan Al Hosani
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) power plant variability presents a significant obstacle to grid stability. Hybrid energy storage systems (HESSs), which integrate battery energy storage systems (BESSs) with hydrogen units (i.e., electrolyzers and fuel cells), offer a viable solution to mitigate PV fluctuations and minimize power curtailment. However, existing control strategies are typically rule-based or offline-optimized, lacking the capability to address critical real-time challenges such as nonlinear efficiency characteristics, response delays in hydrogen subsystems, excessive hydrogen consumption, and battery degradation while complying with grid codes. This paper introduces an adaptive Equivalent Consumption Minimization Strategy (ECMS) for real-time, optimal power sharing among hydrogen-BESS systems. The controller minimizes a composite cost function that integrates efficiency-aware dispatch using empirical efficiency curves, health-aware penalties for aggressive state-of-charge (SOC) reversals and hydrogen overuse, delay-resilient compensation for hydrogen subsystem response lags, and degradation-aware control via online BESS aging assessment. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy significantly improves ramp rate (RR) compliance and system longevity. Compared to benchmark techniques, the ECMS reduces BESS degradation by 50% and nearly eliminates RR violations while maintaining hydrogen demand satisfaction and ensuring smooth SOC recovery.

title: PolSAR Super-Resolution by Fusion of LR-PolSAR and HR-SinSAR based on Diffusion Model
authors: Fan Gao, Zun Zhang, Junjun Yin, Xunhao Lin, Dawei Ren, Jian Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Fully polarimetric synthetic aperture radar (PolSAR) provides rich scattering information but is often limited by low spatial resolution. To address this problem, we propose a novel diffusion model-based fusion framework to reconstruct high-resolution PolSAR (HR-PolSAR) images. Our method extends the image restoration stochastic differential equation (IR-SDE) framework by incorporating both low-resolution PolSAR (LR-PolSAR) and co-registered high-resolution single-polarization SAR (HR-SinSAR) as inputs. A dual-source architecture with cross-attention mechanism is introduced to effectively fuse spatial and polarimetric features. Additionally, we employ a training-free hybrid guided sampling strategy, which simultaneously enforces structural consistency derived from HR-SinSAR and maintains polarimetric fidelity from LR-PolSAR. We conduct extensive experiments on both simulated and real-world datasets of vehicles. The experimental results show that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance, especially in recovering fine structural details of man-made targets and preserving polarimetric characteristics.

title: The Impact of Ground Planes Isolation on Inter-Channel Crosstalk in Printed Circuit Boards with Multiple Ground Planes
authors: Yiming Chen, Junjie Lang, Tianmeng Zhang, Jiayu Liu, Chaozong Guo, Jun Xie, Jinchun Gao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the continuous development of high-density printed circuit boards (PCBs), crosstalk suppression is critical for improving signal integrity of electronic systems. In this current work, the impact of ground planes isolation on crosstalk was investigated using theoretical modeling and experimental tests. A three-dimensional (3-D) electromagnetic model of an interconnect structure with split ground planes was developed to analyze the crosstalk suppression effect of two channels with different ground planes isolation strategies. In addition, a series of experimental tests was conducted. Both the simulation and measurement results show that the effect of ground planes isolation on crosstalk is related to the segmentation location. This study provides a theoretical support for developing ground plane segmentation strategies in high-performance PCB design.

title: A New Methodology of a Manifold Decomposition via Iterative PCA for Machine Learning
authors: Chetra Mang, Axel TahmasebiMoradi, Mouadh Yagoubi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: We introduce a novel methodology for parametric domain decomposition in machine learning by leveraging a reduced-order data manifold constructed through an iterative Principal Component Analysis (iPCA) framework. The approach begins by reducing high-dimensional input data to a onedimensional manifold using iPCA, while preserving as much information as possible. Two inverse projection methods are proposed to reconstruct the original data from the reduced representation. A geometric strategy is then developed to construct and stretch a continuous 1-manifold, transforming it into a functional form suitable for segmentation. We introduce a line-similarity-based algorithm (LISSDA) to decompose this manifold into parametric subdomains. These decompositions are used for localized learning, improving model accuracy, particularly in sparse or complex datasets. The effectiveness of the method is demonstrated on a harmonic transport problem, where it significantly outperforms conventional approaches such as global neural networks and standard PCA-based models. Results show that learning in the latent space of the stretched manifold offers both precision and computational efficiency, especially when combined with domain-specific multilayer perceptron (MLP) models.

title: Quantitative Pre-Evaluation Method of Dynamic Coupled frequency Modulation Capability Based on K-Cell CFAR Algorithm
authors: Xiaotong Zhang, Yangyang Ge, Shubo Hu, Bing Xie, Xintong Ma, Ping Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: Aiming at the core problem that the dynamic coupled frequency modulation capability of modern radar in complex electromagnetic environment is difficult to quantify and pre-evaluate in the design stage, which makes it difficult to predict the robustness of actual performance, this paper proposes a quantitative pre-evaluation method based on K-Cell CFAR (Constant False Alarm Rate) algorithm. Firstly, the intrinsic influence mechanism of dynamic frequency modulation coupling factors such as frequency agility range and switching speed on CFAR detection is deeply analyzed; then a dynamic couplingaware CFAR detection framework is constructed. On this basis, a quantitative indicator system is designed to achieve forwardlooking performance scoring. Experiments show that the proposed method can maintain a detection probability of more than 63% when the frequency switching rate is as high as 500 Hz/ms, which is significantly better than CA-CFAR (Cell-Averaging-CFAR) and ADT-CFAR (Adaptive Dual-Threshold-CFAR); the minimum resolvable frequency difference under strong interference conditions is 120 Hz, which is better than other comparison algorithms. At the same time, the attribution analysis results verify the effectiveness of the method in modeling the core coupling factors and predicting performance. This study provides a quantifiable and scalable evaluation tool for evaluating the dynamic frequency modulation adaptability of radar systems during the design phase.

title: Blockchain-Anchored Zero-Trust Communications for Energy Storage Systems
authors: Dennis G Perry, Mariann M Meins, James Taylor, John Dwyier, Conner Haegley
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Energy storage systems are used to provide backup energy to microgrids and other energy users when their main source of power is interrupted. In this paper, we examine energy storage systems and their protection against cyber-attacks. Energy storage systems offer power delivery but are undersecured at the grid edge. We present a three-part architecture that integrates an inline edge security gateway (policy enforcement point) at each energy storage string, a routed zero-trust overlay for authenticated, segmented communications, and a permissioned trust ledger that produces tamper-evident operational logs. The design employs DTLS 1.3 [1], mutual certificate-based authentication, and micro-segmentation; for public-key operations, we adopt NIST post-quantum algorithms (FIPS 203/204/205 [2][3][4]) while retaining AES-GCM 256 or ChaCha20-Poly1305 for record protection and HKDF-SHA256/384 for the key schedule per TLS 1.3 [5] for symmetric protection. We discuss alignment with NIST SP 800-207 [6], IEC 62443-3-3 [7], UL 2900-2-2 [8], California SB 327 [9], and NERC CIP-015-1 [10], and outline a deployment roadmap for utility and defense microgrids.

title: Towards Automated Bias Testing Support for Educational Decision-Makers
authors: Sabrine Rached, Abdelkader Ouared, Abdelhafid Chadli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Ensuring the integrity of AI-driven assessment and fostering trust in AI frameworks for online education is critical for learners and institutions issuing digital credentials. Challenges arise from learners exploiting system vulnerabilities and from algorithmic biases in ML models evaluating outcomes, performance, grades, and engagement, which can compromise fairness and reliability. This paper introduces an ethical process framework called MoSART (Modeling Trust in Student Learning and Analytical Results), a dialogue-based System that guides educational decision-makers in building trustworthy AI systems through automated bias testing in a human-understandable way. MoSART formalizes an end-to-end workflow from stakeholder objectives to three key classes: (i) Data– including data instance contingencies, data provenance, labeling processes, and attribute statistics, (ii) ML Models– the machine learning models applied along with their configurations and parameters, (iii) Learning Outcomes– the results, performance metrics, and logical arguments linking data and model outputs to decisions. The framework integrates automated bias testing and learner profiling to ensure fairness, and transparency. To highlight our approach and demonstrate its usefulness, the MoSART framework is implemented with a supporting tool, along with a representative usage scenario.

title: Developing a Retrieval-Augmented Natural Language Interface for Philippine Demographics and Social Statistics
authors: Marienel N. Velasco, Minera Laiza C. Acosta, Mark Jayson T. Agao, Sheena D. Rom, Alfea Aemiel V. Mingua, Edrian Joshua L. Rosero, Anne Bernadette D. Yaoto
abstract: The study developed a web-based Natural Language Interface (NLI) to improve access to socio-demographic data from the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA), particularly addressing the difficulties citizens face in navigating PSA's platform. By integrating Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a large language model (LLM), the system allows users to ask questions in natural language and receive AI-generated responses with direct link to relevant datasets. Developed using Agile methodology, the system underwent continuous refinement based on user feedback. Functional testing revealed a 71% accuracy rate in responding to queries. Additionally, system evaluation using ISO/IEC 25010 standards and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) reflected high user satisfaction. The findings highlight the system's effectiveness in making statistical data more accessible and understandable, thereby promoting wider engagement with official demographic and social statistics among its target users.

title: Memory-Efficient Information Filtering in Contrastive Learning for Temporal Knowledge Graph Reasoning
authors: Eshani K. Fernando, Michael Adjeisah, Jian Chang, Jian Jun Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Temporal knowledge graphs (TKGs) have emerged as a critical component in modern artificial intelligence systems, enabling machines to reason over dynamic information. However, existing methods for TKG reasoning use massive amounts of information and computational resources, failing to capture minimal essential temporal knowledge with their dynamics while eliminating irrelevant or noisy information for a more precise reasoning process. To this end, we introduce the Forest Fire Contrastive Approach (FFCA), a contrastive learning architecture based on forest fire sampling that presents a preferential attachment mechanism for the extrapolation of TKG. This allows high-degree nodes to attract new connections, improving the pipeline's predictive capability while keeping it compact, leading to an efficient learning and inference process. This approach introduces a degree-biased burn probability that gathers a minimal but highly correlated subgraph relevant to the query as the global view. Simultaneously, a sufficient number of the most recent snapshots were gathered as the local view, preserving task-relevant graph information and removing noise while reducing the computational complexity and memory requirements, improving the sustainability of TKG reasoning models. Experiments on three publicly available benchmark datasets widely used for TKG extrapolation tasks demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves competitive predictive performance to current state-of-the-art methods while demonstrating substantial improvements in memory efficiency, a critical consideration for scalability to large-scale temporal knowledge graph datasets.

title: IoT-Driven Window System for Real-Time Environmental Monitoring and Enhanced Safety for Home 4.0
authors: Khondaker Iffti Hasan Turjo, Debashish Kumar Ghosh, Mustakim Ahmed, Kazi Redwan, Ahmed Hossain, Md. Faruk Abdullah Al Sohan, Abu Shufian
abstract: The evolution of smart home automation has introduced advanced technologies that enhance security, energy efficiency and environmental monitoring. Traditional window systems lack automation and environmental awareness, making homes unsafe and energy inefficient. To address this, this research presents an IoT-driven window system integrating automated control with real-time environmental assessment. The prototype uses an ESP32 microcontroller paired with a TFT display for dynamic WiFi setup. It includes multiple sensors such as SW-420 (vibration), PMS-5003 (particulate matter), MQ-135 (air quality), BME280 (temperature, humidity, pressure) and YL-83 (rain). Gear motors control the window based on sensor inputs, supporting manual and automatic modes via Ubidots. Windows close automatically upon detecting rain or high pollution, enhancing safety and energy efficiency. Users receive instant status notifications and remote-control access with personalized settings. Wi-Fi ensures smart home integration, promoting a healthier indoor environment, reducing heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) needs and providing real-time security alerts for safer living.

title: Optimal Scheduling of Flexible Distribution Networks Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning Considering Soft Normally Open Points
authors: Peng Wu, Zhaohe Yu, Tianyu Li, Kaiyuan Ji, Yini Zhang, Wenhao Yang, Pengfei Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: The growing volatility of large-scale wind, photovoltaic generation, and load demand necessitates enhanced operational flexibility in distribution networks. With the increasing use of flexible interconnection devices, this study introduces an innovative deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-driven approach for optimizing scheduling, integrating soft normally open points (SNOPs) and topology reconfiguration. Firstly, an optimization model for flexible distribution networks with SNOPs is developed, framing the multi-period reconfiguration as a Markov decision process (MDP). Then, a two-layer solution framework is designed, with the outer layer leveraging an improved dueling deep Q-network (dueling-DQN) to determine network topology, while the inner layer optimizes controllable device scheduling based on the chosen topology. Finally, simulations on an improved IEEE 33-bus system show that SNOPs significantly reduce network losses, and the improved dueling-DQN boosts adaptability to renewable and load uncertainties, enhancing both optimization performance and computational efficiency.

title: FFS: Flowspectrum Based Feature Selection for Network Traffic Anomaly Detection
authors: Xiaoye An, Changda Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)

title: Simulation of 5-Phase Cascade High-Voltage Frequency Converters with Space-Vector Modulation
authors: Victor Busher, Dmytro Glazev, Valerii Tytiuk, Andriy Shalyov
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The work is devoted to the development of a 5-phase multi-level cascade frequency converter model as part of powerful high-voltage electric drives and to studying of various modes of its operation. The purpose of the work is to verify the performance of the method of balanced space-vector pulse width modulation in such inverter in normal and emergency modes by modelling the electric drive in MATLAB Simscape Electrical. The paper presents the features of using the method, taking into account the mathematical and geometric interpretation of the motor stator as a symmetrical 5-phase load. The model with a 5-phase synchronous motor from the MATLAB ac8_example.slx database has been used as a basis, which allows the simulation results to be considered reliable. The rules for calculating phase voltages in regular and emergency modes have been formulated and the corresponding units of the model of the inverter control system have been prepared. These values are used for further processing in H-cell control modules with 2- or 3-level pulse-width modulation. Such a model for a 5-phase electric drive has been built for the first time, which constitutes a scientific novelty of the work. Calculations of transient processes have showed the absence of any oscillations, shocks during the system transitions from normal mode to emergency mode and vice versa. It is also important to note that while using the proposed method in a 5phase frequency converter, the balancing is preserved even with a short circuit of one or two phases of the inverter. This significantly increases the survivability of the electric drive, which is especially important in responsible mechanisms and technological processes. The paper provides calculation formulas, diagrams explaining the principles of operation in normal and emergency situations, some of the most important units of the model, which can be used in the design of the microprocessor control system of the power switches of the frequency converter.

title: Cooperative Optimization Control of Variable Lanes and Signal Timing via Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Siyi Wang, Ziyang Zhai, Tianhao Guan, Ruru Hao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional manual or time-based control methods in responding to dynamic traffic conditions, this paper proposes a coordinated optimization control strategy for variable lanes and traffic signals based on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). This method constructs a two-layer control model: the upper layer handles adaptive switching of variable lanes, dynamically adjusting lane guidance functions based on real-time traffic conditions; the lower layer optimizes signal timing based on the upper layer's decisions, achieving coordinated control and global optimization. Simulation tests validated the proposed model's performance and compared it with various traditional and advanced control methods. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed cooperative optimization model effectively alleviates intersection congestion and significantly enhances traffic efficiency. Specifically, average vehicle waiting time decreased by 17.9%, and average queue length reduced by 29%, validating the method's superiority and applicability in complex traffic environments.

title: A Rapid Assessment Method for Satellite Communication Capability Based on an Estimation Model
authors: Ping Jiang, Lei Wang, Mingqi Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Quantitatively evaluating satellite communication capability requires considering numerous parameters. These include satellite count, coverage, capacity, frequency band, transmission delay, and SNR. Typically, a simulation determines the final evaluation of satellite communication effectiveness. Developing an estimation model for satellite communication capabilities can decrease dependence on simulation platforms. This approach employs parametric modeling. It identifies key elements and independent parameters that influence capability layer evaluation metrics. It also includes prior information, enabling a simplified expression of the physical model. It allows quick evaluation of satellite communication effectiveness without needing a simulation platform. This is incredibly valuable for strategic satellite research communications.

title: Research of Relationship between Control Reliability Indices and Complex System Performances
authors: Olha Sushchenko, Oleksander Saluyk
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: This paper deals with the research on the relationship between control reliability indices and motion control system performances. Such an approach allows for an increase in the probability of avoiding false and indefinite failures while tolerancing basic parameters of the system. The expressions for methodical and instrumental constituents of control reliability are represented. The expression determines the interconnection between control reliability indices and operating performances such as accuracy weight and dimension characteristics. The appropriate graphical dependencies for gyroscopic measuring instruments are represented. The obtained results ensure grounded tolerancing of basic parameters of the motion control system at the early phases of designing. Such an approach significantly improves the quality of manufacturing motion control systems.

title: Generic and Domain-Specific AI-Powered Knowledge Graph Construction
authors: Irene Kilanioti, George Angelos Papadopoulos
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Knowledge graphs are structured and interlinked formations that can enhance data expressiveness, as crucial data can be illustrated in a meaningful way that highlights the most significant and impactful affinities among them. In this work, we propose optimized AI-based construction of domain-specific and generic knowledge graphs, including datasets of specific unexplored domains, such as SDGs, and we work on various datasets to prove the generality of our findings, that outperform existent methods. CNN variations and various LLM models improve the performance and reduce training times for the constructed generic and domain-specific knowledge graphs and, hence, facilitate the work of practitioners, who collect and evaluate the data.

title: High-Precision Oil Storage Tank Detection in Satellite Imagery Using a YOLOv5-Based Deep Learning Framework
authors: Yongzhuo Xu, Yuxiang Chen, Haoran Li, Haoran Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper presents an automatic oil storage tank detection framework based on the YOLOv5 architecture. Using high-resolution satellite imagery from the Kaggle Oil Storage Tanks dataset, the model achieves accurate multi-scale detection and classification of floating and fixed roof tanks. The proposed CIoU-based refinement and confidence fusion modules significantly improve localization precision and robustness under complex backgrounds.

title: Cyber Threat Detection in IIoT and IoMT Using DNN-GRU with Multi-Head Attention
authors: Afrah Gueriani, Hamza Kheddar, Ahmed Cherif Mazari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: The growing prevalence of medical and industrial IoT in various domains has intensified the need for robust security mechanisms, particularly real-time systems with minimal false positive rates (FPR). Several solutions have been proposed to address these challenges, with artificial intelligence (AI)-based intrusion detection systems (IDS) emerging as a promising strategy. This study focuses on developing a novel hybrid model that integrates Deep Neural Networks (DNN), Gated Recurrent Units (GRU), and a Multi-Head Attention (MHA) mechanism. The proposed DNN+(GRU-MHA) model is trained on two distinct datasets: CICIoMT2024 for the medical domain and EdgeIIoTset for the industrial domain. To mitigate class imbalance, RandomOverSampler is applied to the medical dataset, while Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) is used for the industrial dataset. Experimental results demonstrate the model's superior performance, reaching 98.22% and 99.78% accuracy rates for medical and industrial datasets, respectively. The model also attained exceptionally low FPR values of 0.002% and 0.0000% in medical and industrial datasets, respectively, with inference times of 0.0001 seconds per instance. These findings support the suggested model's efficacy, emphasizing its ability to perform in real-time and heterogeneous environments while accurately identifying several types of cyber-threats.

title: Eye State Recognition from Prefrontal EEG: A Multi-Domain Feature Fusion Approach
authors: Youqin Lin, Zhengxue Huang, Hao Chen, Zongyuan Liu, Shu Pan, Hongyi Zhang, Xingen Gao, Tingzhu Wu, Zhong Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: To enable efficient and natural human-computer interaction, this study proposes a method for classifying eye-open (EO) and eye-closed (EC) states using electroencephalographic (EEG) signals recorded from the left and right prefrontal regions (AF7 and AF8). We systematically extract three types of features: time-domain features (Central Tendency Measure, CTM, derived from the Second-Order Difference Plot), frequency-domain features (β/α power ratio), and nonlinear dynamical features (Permutation Entropy, PE), and employ a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier for state discrimination. Experiments are conducted on the publicly available PhysioNet EEG Eye State dataset, with a comparative analysis between prefrontal channels (AF7/AF8) and conventional occipital channels (O1/O2). Results demonstrate that our multi-domain feature fusion strategy—specifically tailored to the characteristics of pre-frontal EEG—significantly enhances classification performance, achieving a peak accuracy of 99.10%. This work confirms that, through targeted feature engineering, prefrontal channels can match or even surpass the performance of occipital channels, offering strong technical support for lightweight, wearable brain-computer interface (BCI) systems.

title: HALO: Hyperparameter Auto-tuning via LLM Optimizer for Rapid, Interpretable AutoML
authors: Zhaoyuan Chen, Degang Wang, Yanan Liu, Xiupeng Shi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: The efficacy of machine learning across scientific and industrial domains hinges critically on hyperparameter configuration, a process that dominates model performance yet remains computationally prohibitive. Conventional optimization strategies, such as Bayesian methods, treat models as black boxes; they ignore the semantic context of the data, necessitating expensive cold-starts and exhaustive search to identify viable parameters. Herein, we present HALO (Hyperparameter Auto-tuning via LLM Optimizer), a framework that integrates the semantic reasoning of Large Language Models (LLMs) directly into the optimization loop to transform tuning into an interpretable, knowledge-driven process. By orchestrating a dual-agent system, HALO leverages dataset semantics to formulate informed warm-start configurations and iteratively refines values through natural-language reasoning. On the high-dimensional SECOM industrial dataset, HALO achieves the highest Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC) in just four trials, significantly outperforming established advanced methods. These results demonstrate that infusing optimization with semantic understanding not only drastically reduces computational overhead but also transforms hyperparameter tuning into a transparent, interpretable process.

title: RFRAG: Reader-Friendly Context Refinement for Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Multi-Hop Question Answering
authors: Xiaolong Wang, Yi Liang, Rongsheng Dong, Fengying Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances the performance of generative models by incorporating external knowledge, but the retrieved contexts often suffer from redundancy and fragmentation, hindering the construction of stable multi-hop reasoning. To address this challenge, we propose RFRAG, a Reader-Friendly Context Refinement framework for RAG. The framework consists of three modules: (1) Sub-query decomposition, which refines the original query; (2) Reasoning chain generation, which explicitly constructs intermediate reasoning across evidence; (3) Context refinement, which restructures and filters redundant evidence. Experimental results demonstrate that RFRAG improves the density of retrieved context and the integrity of reasoning chains, leading to enhanced multi-hop question answering performance compared to existing approaches.

title: Adaptive diagnosis of Rotating Machinery using PSO-SVM classifiers based on GAVMD-MPE
authors: Bo Zhang, Wenli Hu, Tao Liang, Chunying Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Traditional fault diagnosis methods of rotating machinery primarily rely on signal preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification. For the issue of small sample data, in this paper, an innovative adaptive and intelligent method for mechanical fault diagnosis is introduced to obtain more significant signal characteristics and improve recognition accuracy. First, variational mode decomposition(VMD) algorithm which established by Genetic Algorithm(GA) is used to decompose the original vibration signal into several intrinsic mode functions(IMFs). Seconedly, calculate the multi-scale permutation entropy(MPE) values of each IMF component to further extract features and construct feature vectors as inputs for the classifier. Finally, the optimal support vector machine (SVM) classifier was established through particle swarm optimization (PSO) to achieve fault diagnosis of rotating machinery. The experiment shows that the recognition rate of 97.7% is achieved by the proposed method.

title: Explainable Enseble Learning-Based Robust Intrusion Detection Framework for Power Systems
authors: Arhaan Godhrawala, Devarsh Patel, Fenil Ramoliya, Shlok Patel, Lakshin Pathak, Rajesh Guptal, Sudeep Tanwar
abstract: Risk of data privacy, security and reliability has been increased by technological advancements. This is where Intrusion detection systems(IDS) perform's its task. They are used to catch the attacks by adapting the user's behavior after logging in. So, we propose a mix of ensemble learning and explainable AI (XAI) based model to reduce such concerns. In our idea, we use a method called Ensemble Learning (EL) to make IDS the main part of the system after using a model called SHAP which is one of the application of XAI to find out if someone is trying to trick the system by pretending to be someone else. This is called a Masquerade attack. An additional layer serves as an alert mechanism as well as feedbackloop in order to strengthen our system. We evaluate the performance of our model by comparing accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score across traditional Machine Learning (ML) models and Ensemble Learning (EL) models. Additionally, we analyze Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curves and Precision-Recall (PR) curves to further assess and visualize the model's effectiveness.

title: Harmonic Coupling Modeling and Analysis of Grid-Connected Inverters Considering Control Loop Effects
authors: Sai Wang, Yahui Li, Yong Wang, Weiming Wang, Shuyi Tong, Junze Lu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: With the advancement of new-type power systems, grid-connected inverters for renewable energy applications are being deployed at an increasing scale. However, the proliferation of such inverters is impeded by persistent harmonic issues, as the distinct dynamic characteristics of various components can excite both high- and low-order harmonics and intensify coupling effects across different frequency bands. To elucidate the influence of component-level characteristics on waveform distortion in grid-connected inverters, it is essential to establish a harmonic model that incorporates these influencing factors. In this context, this paper first examines the mechanisms underlying high- and low-frequency harmonic coupling in grid-connected inverters. Subsequently, a harmonic coupling model that accounts for the effects of multiple components is developed. Finally, simulation studies conducted in MATLAB/Simulink validate the accuracy of the proposed model and provide an in-depth analysis of the harmonic characteristics of the grid-connected inverter.

title: Analysis of DC Overvoltage or Undervoltage Mechanism in Non-Faulted Areas of Flexible DC Grid
authors: Yonghui Song, Hong Cao, Shuyang Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: After a line fault in the flexible DC grid was removed, the system state was changed from closed loop operation to open loop operation. The active power output of each converter station has not changed, and the power flow has been rebuilt under the open loop system, which is prone to cause DC overvoltage and undervoltage problems in the non-faulted areas. To address this issue, the mechanism of DC voltage change after the power flow transfer is revealed in this paper. Firstly, the faulted line is split into two types based on the transmission power of the line, and then, the evolution process of power flow transfer is analyzed under various faults, pointing out that the voltage drop generated by power flow transfer in each line is the root cause of DC overvoltage and undervoltage. The flexible DC grid model was built in PSCAD, and the test results show that when the faulted line with large power transfer is removed, DC overvoltage and undervoltage phenomena are prone to occur, which is consistent with the theoretical analysis.

title: Ultra-Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Model Based on CNN-BiLSTM and Error Correction
authors: Ruifan Li, Na Zhang, Guangchen Liu, Jingyu Li, Bingqian Hu, Yu Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Accurate solar energy forecasting is essential for improving renewable energy consumption and optimizing grid operation. Therefore, this paper pro-poses a photovoltaic power prediction model with an ultra-short time horizon, taking into account similar historical days and error correction. First, select correlation with photovoltaic power as input variables; determine the k-means algorithm, sunny, sunny to cloudy and rainy days are the categories into which the historical data is divided; The photovoltaic power sequence is decomposed using adaptive noise complete ensemble empirical mode decomposition (CEEMDAN). Next, a convolutional neural network (CNN) is employed to pull out the key characteristics of the multidimensional time series data, while a bidirectional long short-term memory network (BiLSTM) dives into the historical output power and meteorological information to capture forward and backward trends, ultimately yielding preliminary power forecasts. Furthermore, an error correction model was established to rectify the initial prediction results. Finally, the historical data of the Alice Springs PV power station in Australia serves for verification and analysis. The findings demonstrate that the model effectively enhances forecasting precision across diverse meteorological scenarios, confirming its validity and efficiency.

title: A Brief Review on Optimal Placement, Operation Strategies and Pricing Mechanism for Pumped Storage Power Stations
authors: Wanbin Wang, Shidong Tian, Wenli Zheng, Tiexuan Lou, Liang Shao, Fushuan Wen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Pumped storage is a crucial component of power systems. This paper explores the key role of pumped storage plants in modern power systems and their optimization strategies. It first introduces the working principles and types of these plants, then analyzes plant configuration and operational optimization strategies, and discusses related policies and electricity market pricing mechanisms. The study aims to ensure a balance between power supply and demand while improving the efficiency of renewable energy use. These analyses provide a solid theoretical foundation for plant optimization and practical guidance for the ongoing transformation of power systems and policy formulation.

title: Quantifying Informativeness in Knowledge Graph-Augmented In-Context Learning for Multiple Choice Query Answering
authors: Maryam Ghanbari, Renata Dividino
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) face significant challenges when attempting to utilize knowledge not encoded during pre-training. In-context learning (ICL) addresses this limitation by embedding relevant information directly into prompts, enabling LLMs to leverage external knowledge without updating their parameters. Recent advances have explored integrating knowledge graph (KG) information into prompts, taking advantage of the structured and factual representations of entities and relationships that KGs provide. A common approach involves identifying key concepts in a task, grounding them to KG nodes, extracting ego-subgraphs centered on these concepts, and incorporating them into prompts. However, not all subgraphs are equally relevant to the task, and selecting appropriate knowledge remains a critical challenge, as irrelevant or noisy subgraphs can reduce model accuracy. This study investigates how KG-based information related to task-specific concepts influences LLM performance on multiple-choice question answering (MCQA) tasks. We implement a four-stage pipeline that (1) identifies key concepts from questions and grounds them in the KG, (2) extracts individual ego-subgraphs for each concept, (3) integrates these subgraphs into prompts, and (4) evaluates their impact on LLM reasoning via probability scoring. Our analysis highlights which concept-specific subgraphs enhance performance, which introduce misleading information, and which have neutral effects. We show that carefully selected KG subgraphs can substantially outperform others in semantic relevance. Furthermore, we examine how the size and connectivity of selected KG subgraphs influence model performance. Overall, this work deepens understanding of KG-based knowledge selection in ICL and informs the design of more effective, targeted prompting strategies. This project's source code is publicly available at https://github.com/maryam-ghanbari/InformativenessOfKGs.

title: Microstructure Design, Encapsulation, and Performance Study of a Dual-Mode Flexible Tactile Sensor
authors: Zeyu Meng, Sihan Zhang, Yang Zuo, Wenhua Gu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Tactile sensors, as core components of electronic skin, hold promising applications and research value in fields such as flexible wearables, bionic robotics, and biomedicine. However, current challenges include complex fabrication processes, high costs, short sensor lifespans, and limited sensing capabilities. This paper presents a dual-modal flexible tactile sensor integrating pressure and temperature sensing. Its fabrication and encapsulation scheme is reliable and straightforward. By introducing circular platform microstructures on the pressuresensing layer, the sensor achieves a maximum sensitivity of 6 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MPa}^{-1}$</tex> across a pressure range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-700 \text{kPa}$</tex>. This sensitivity is 7.5 times that of the unstructured sensor. The designed encapsulation scheme ensures a sensor lifespan exceeding one month. Based on the developed tactile sensor, a sensor data acquisition circuit was designed to enable the collection and display of data from multiple sensors.

title: Estimation analysis and robust identification of photovoltaic model parameters for local shading conditions
authors: Yong Ren, Lei Zhou, Shuang Zhang, Xutao Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: Accurate modeling of photovoltaic arrays is crucial for their performance evaluation and optimization control. However, there are problems in the modeling process such as difficulty in measuring unknown parameters and susceptibility of measurement data to random gross errors, which seriously affect the accuracy of parameter identification. Therefore, this study proposes a comprehensive solution that integrates parameter estimability analysis and robust estimation. Firstly, based on the output characteristics relationship between photovoltaic cells and arrays, an equivalent model under uniform illumination was established, and key parameters that can be uniquely identified were screened through parameter estimability and sensitivity analysis. Furthermore, corresponding array models were constructed for local shading conditions, and the influence of measurement errors on parameter identification was studied in detail. By analyzing and comparing the impact functions and performance of various robust estimators, the optimal estimation method was selected to achieve accurate and robust identification of photovoltaic array parameters under local shading conditions. The results indicate that the framework ensures the rationality of the parameter set through estimability analysis and effectively suppresses data errors using robust estimation, jointly ensuring the accuracy and reliability of the model under different lighting conditions.

title: Aligning Tag-Based Building Operating Systems with RealEstateCore Ontology for Interoperability, Digital Twin Knowledge Graphs and Energy Management
authors: Prerna Juhlin, Michael Kleefisch, Charles Steinmetz, Gösta Stomberg, Matthias Schlöder, Eike Fokken, Philipp Bauer
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Smart buildings collect and integrate information from various building systems towards operational and energy efficiency. In addition, they are also seen to play an important role in decarbonization as well as for providing health benefits to occupants. Information integration requires meaningful information exchange among multiple vendor systems within the facility. The RealEstateCore (REC) ontology based on the Resource Description Framework (RDF) has been developed to enable semantic interoperability across different vendor systems in the building domain. However, leveraging the ontology for existing tag-based systems such as ABB openBOS (Building Operating System) is not automatic. In this paper, we describe the alignment of tagbased openBOS ontology with REC based on extending REC to openBOS+. The corresponding digital twin knowledge graph in Microsoft Azure Digital Twins and related API developments enable interoperable information integration with other systems based on REC. A Model Predictive Control (MPC) application enabled using openBOS+ for building energy management is described towards improving energy performance.

title: Enhancing Integrated Circuit Design: Predictive Modeling and Interpretability in Complex Systems
authors: Wenyan Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The growing scale and complexity of integrated circuits demand more advanced design methodologies. This paper presents a data-driven framework, supported by machine learning techniques and feature engineering, which has shown strong potential in addressing these challenges. The generalized framework leverages predictive modeling to establish the relationship between design parameters and performance metrics. It is applicable to diverse circuits, including Power Amplifiers (PA), Receivers, and Transmitters. In addition, the framework emphasizes the integration of preprocessing, learning algorithms, and interpretability methods. Rather than focusing solely on prediction accuracy, the approach highlights the practical value of models. Furthermore, the underlying reasons for performance differences among models are analyzed and explained through SHAP analysis, enabling better understanding and more informed decision-making in the design process.

title: Evaluation Planning for Academic Self-service University Management System ISDT Product Aspect
authors: Muhamad Idaham Umar Ong, Mohamed Ariff Ameedeen, Ngahzaifa Ab Ghani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)

title: KG-Retriever: Efficient Knowledge Indexing for Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models
authors: Weijie Chen, Ting Bai, Jinbo Su, Jian Luan, Wei Liu, Chuan Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Large language models with retrieval-augmented generation encounter a pivotal challenge in intricate retrieval tasks, e.g., multi-hop question answering, which requires the model to navigate across multiple documents and generate comprehensive responses based on fragmented information. Previous studies adopt iterative retrieval strategies to progressively collect relevant information across multiple documents, but they inevitably incur higher computational costs with multiple retrieval steps. To tackle this challenge, we introduce a novel Knowledge Graph-based RAG framework with a hierarchical knowledge retriever, termed KG-Retriever. The retrieval indexing in KG-Retriever is constructed on a hierarchical index graph that consists of a knowledge graph layer and a collaborative document layer. The associative nature of graph structures is fully utilized to strengthen intra-document and inter-document connectivity, thereby fundamentally alleviating the information fragmentation problem and meanwhile improving the retrieval efficiency in cross-document retrieval of LLMs. With the coarse-grained collaborative information from neighboring documents and concise information from the knowledge graph, KG-Retriever achieves marked improvements on five public QA datasets, showing the effectiveness and efficiency of our proposed RAG framework.

title: PCCE 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)

title: Adaptive Redundancy CRDSA in UAV-LEO Satellite Covert Communication Networks
authors: Zhe Song, Xuyang Zhang, Shuai Wang, Xin Jin, Gaofeng Pan, Jianping An
abstract: With the rapid development of integrated air-space-ground networks, ensuring secure and covert access for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellations has become a critical challenge. This paper proposes the novel adaptive redundancy contention resolution diversity slotted ALOHA (AR-CRDSA) scheme to provide reliable and covert communication for UAV users. The core of the scheme involves a dynamic pairing strategy that conceals a low-power covert user (CU) transmission under the signal of a high-power legitimate user (LU), thereby shielding it from detection by adversarial reconnaissance satellites. A key innovation is the departure from transmitting identical packet replicas; instead, the AR-CRDSA protocol sends two distinct yet complementary packet versions to adapt to fluctuating channel conditions. This adaptive mechanism, combined with multi-satellite selection diversity and an iterative interference cancellation process, is shown through extensive simulations to significantly enhance the effective covert throughput and overall system reliability when compared to traditional random access protocols.

title: Oscillations and displacement of a single domain wall in an alternating magnetic field in bistable microwire
authors: Jozef Onufer, Peter Duranka, Mária Kladivová, Peter Vrábel, Ján Ziman
abstract: New experimental procedures for the study of the dynamics and displacement of a single domain wall in ferromagnetic glass-coated Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">77.5</sub>B<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">15</sub>Si<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7.5</sub> microwire are presented. Measurements of induced voltage as a function of frequency and amplitude of an alternating axial magnetic field and/or of the magnitude of a constant axial magnetic field are performed. From these experiments information about changes in axial magnetic flux are obtained, i.e. about wall position shifting as a function of the applied magnetic field parameters as well as about the dynamic characteristics of wall oscillation motion. Wall oscillations caused by an alternating magnetic field lead to displacement of the wall along the sample wire. Corresponding changes in axial magnetic flux vary for head-to-head and tail-to-tail wall types, but the sign of these changes is always the same. The presence of the unidirectional effect observed in domain wall motion in Fe77.5B15Si7.5 microwires may be the reason for this behavior.

title: Spatial Characteristics and Controlling Factors of Permafrost Deformation in the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau Revealed Through InSAR Measurements
authors: Zhida Xu, Liming Jiang, Zhiping Jiao, Rui Guo, Zhiwei Zhou, Ronggang Huang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accelerated climate warming has intensified permafrost degradation across the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau (QTP), profoundly impacting regional hydrological cycles and carbon fluxes. Surface deformation observed in permafrost areas offers valuable insights into the extent of this degradation. However, the spatial patterns and controlling factors of permafrost deformation across the entire QTP remain poorly understood. This study reveals the long-term and seasonal deformation characteristics of permafrost regions across the entire QTP using Sentinel-1 SAR imagery (primarily from 2019 to 2021) through time-series InSAR combined with a permafrost deformation model. Notably, it provides the first plateau-wide characterization of the spatial distribution of seasonal deformation. In addition, the dominant factors controlling both long-term and seasonal deformation were identified with the geographic detector method. The results demonstrate that permafrost regions experience significantly higher surface subsidence rates (−2.46 mm/year) than seasonally frozen areas (−0.54 mm/year). Spatially, ice-rich permafrost regions with lower thermal stability exhibit higher subsidence rates. The average seasonal deformation amplitude in permafrost areas is 3.85 mm, with larger values concentrated in flat basins and areas surrounding lakes and rivers. Moreover, spatial variability of long-term deformation is mainly controlled by solar radiation and precipitation, and both are positively correlated with deformation rates. Slope is the primary driver of spatial variation in seasonal deformation amplitude. These findings highlight the critical role of surface energy, moisture, and terrain in permafrost dynamics. The study demonstrates the potential of integrating time-series InSAR with geospatial analysis to support large-scale permafrost monitoring and estimation of ground-ice meltwater across the QTP.

title: Enhancing Inclusivity in MOOCs through Adaptive Personalized Technologies for Learning: A Systematic Review
authors: A. Salwa Mrayhi, B. Mohamed Koutheair Khribi, C. Mohamed Jemni
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) broaden access to learning, but inclusivity remains uneven when accessibility and personalization are treated as peripheral rather than foundational design requirements. This PRISMA 2020-aligned systematic review (2018–2025) examines how accessibility by design and Adaptive and Personalized Technologies for Learning (APTeL) are implemented in MOOCs and how they relate to learner outcomes (access, engagement, persistence, achievement). Fifty-six empirical studies met the inclusion criteria by addressing WCAG- or ARIA-based accessibility and/or APTeL mechanisms such as learner modeling, recommender systems, adaptive sequencing, and adaptive feedback. Across this corpus, media-level accessibility was most common, with captions and transcripts reported in 68% of studies, alternative formats (for example, alt text and audio description) in 46%, and assistive technology checks in 34%, while explicit WCAG or ARIA citation (29%) and explicit UDL alignment (21%) were comparatively limited. Personalization efforts centered on learner modeling (54%), recommender systems (41%), adaptive sequencing (36%), and adaptive feedback (30%). Outcome reporting favored engagement proxies (75%) over achievement (38%) and persistence or retention (32%), and only 11% of studies disaggregated outcomes by disability. The clearest benefits appeared when accessible and operable interaction pathways were co-designed with APTeL adaptations explicitly mapped to learning objectives, UDL checkpoints, and assessment strategies. However, heterogeneous conformance reporting, limited assistive technology validation, sparse UDL mapping, and weak outcome validity reduce interpretability and restrict causal claims about impact on learning and persistence. The review specifies design and practice implications for accessibility-aligned APTeL in MOOCs, identifies methodological and ethical gaps that constrain generalizability and equity inferences, and proposes priorities for platform policy and rigorous, disability-aware evaluation frameworks.

title: ZeroSC-SR: Importance-Aware Zero-Shot Semantic Speech Communication over Fading Channels
authors: Zewei Wang, Guanchong Niu, Yao Tang, Tat Ming Lok, Man-On Pun
abstract: This paper introduces an innovative Zero-Shot Semantic Communication System with Speech Reconstruction (ZeroSC-SR), designed to enhance speech transmission and reconstruction in frequency-selective fading channels while remaining compatible with standard digital coding and modulation. By efficiently transmitting phoneme sequences and compressed prompt acoustic features as discrete indices, ZeroSC-SR reduces transmitted data size and achieves a high compression ratio, resulting in lower Character Error Rate (CER) and higher speaker similarity at low transmit power under noisy channels. We quantitatively evaluate the semantic importance of the transmitted phoneme sequences and acoustic features through a task-oriented, mutual-information-based approach. These insights directly guide the development of our Iterative Importance-Aware Allocation and Power Scheduling (IIAPS) strategy, which enhances transmission quality in frequency selective fading channels by allocating subcarriers and power based on semantic importance. Experimental results show that, under fixed power with SNR sweeps, IIAPS outperforms baseline allocation strategies on Word Error Rate (WER) and speaker similarity. Compared to conventional systems, ZeroSC-SR de livers lower CER and higher speaker similarity under varying transmit power, with the greatest improvements occurring at lower power levels.

title: Dual Domain Expurgated Error Exponents for Source Coding with Side Information
authors: Mehdi Dabirnia, Hamdi Joudeh, Albert Guillén i Fàbregas
abstract: We introduce an expurgation method for source coding with side information that enables direct dual-domain derivations of expurgated error exponents. Dual-domain methods yield optimization problems over few parameters, with any sub-optimal choice resulting in an achievable exponent, as opposed to primal-domain optimization over distributions. In addition, dual-domain methods naturally allow for general alphabets and/or memory. We derive two such expurgated error exponents for different random-coding ensembles in the case where the decoder is possibly mismatched with respect to the source and side information joint distribution. We show the better of the exponents coincides with the Csiszár-Körner exponent obtained via a graph decomposition lemma. We show some numerical examples that illustrate the differences between the two exponents and show that in the case of source coding without side information, the expurgated exponent coincides with the error exponent of the source optimal code.

title: Open Access at ACM: 60 Days In
authors: Yannis Ioannidis
abstract: None

title: Progress in Fine Line Metallization: A Statistical Approach to Optimize Laser Processing During Screen Production
authors: Aathira Krishnadas Nair, Srivarshini Upadhya, Jörg Schube, Michael Linse, René Haberstroh, Jale Schneider, Andreas Lorenz, Florian Clement, Ralf Preu
abstract: Currently, the majority of screens manufactured for the screen-printed metallization of industrial-grade solar cells are structured using laser processing. This study aims to enhance the scientific understanding of the interaction between the laser and the screen during the structuring process by investigating the effects of key laser parameters, including laser power, line-to-line pitch, number of passes, and scanning speed, on the quality of screen openings. From the conducted parameter variation tests, a statistical model is developed using the R software to predict screen opening widths. The model focuses on identifying key laser parameters that significantly influence the screen opening width and aims to provide accurate predictions. By establishing a robust predictive model, this work seeks to minimize the need for extensive laser parameter variations conducted on screens and to select optimal laser parameters. The model indicates that laser power and scanning speed exert the greatest influence on screen opening widths. The predicted results demonstrate an accuracy within 1 μm when compared to experimental findings, thereby validating the model's predictive capabilities. Subsequently, the optimal laser parameters are used to structure two screens with the same mesh configuration and which are then used to print on M10-sized tunnel oxide passivating contact substrates with a silver paste. The results are compared with an industry-standard reference screen with similar screen specifications. The mean shading width of the printed contacts from both screens are measured to be 22 and 21 μm, which is comparable with the 20 μm shading width printed from the industry standard screen.

title: DySTop: Dynamic Staleness Control and Topology Construction for Asynchronous Decentralized Federated Learning
authors: Yizhou Shi, Qianpiao Ma, Yan Xu, Junlong Zhou, Ming Hu, Yunming Liao, Hongli Xu
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a potential distributed learning paradigm that enables model training on edge devices (i.e., workers) while preserving data privacy. However, its reliance on a centralized server leads to limited scalability. Decentralized federated learning (DFL) eliminates the dependency on a centralized server by enabling peer-to peer model exchange. Existing DFL mechanisms mainly employ synchronous communication, which may result in training inefficiencies under heterogeneous and dynamic edge environments. Although a few recent asynchronous DFL (ADFL) mechanisms have been proposed to address these issues, they typically yield stale model aggregation and frequent model transmission, leading to degraded training performance on non-IID data and high communication overhead. To overcome these issues, we present DySTop, an innovative mechanism that jointly optimizes dynamic staleness control and topology construction in ADFL. In each round, multiple workers are activated, and a subset of their neighbors is selected to transmit models for aggregation, followed by local training. We provide a rigorous convergence analysis for DySTop, theoretically revealing the quantitative relationships between the convergence bound and key factors such as maximum staleness, activating frequency, and data distribution among workers. From the insights of the analysis, we propose a worker activation algorithm (WAA) for staleness control and a phase-aware topology construction algorithm (PTCA) to reduce communication overhead and handle data non-IID. Extensive evaluations through both large-scale simulations and real-world testbed experiments demonstrate that our DySTop reduces completion time by 46.7% and the communication resource consumption by 48.3% compared to state-of-the-art solutions, while maintaining the same model accuracy.

title: BAYES-GAIT: A Bayesian Deep Neural Network for Continuous Gait Reference Trajectory Generation Using an Enhanced Loss Function
authors: Bharat Singh, Suchit Patel, Ankit Vijayvargiya, Rajesh Kumar, Naveen Gehlot
abstract: Background: Adaptive gait trajectory prediction is essential to achieve natural and stable locomotion in prosthetic limbs and legged robots, particularly under varied conditions such as changing inclines and walking speeds. Although deep neural networks have shown potential for learning complex joint gait trajectory patterns, conventional deep neural networks often suffer from prediction bias and lack mechanisms for uncertainty estimation, which limits their reliability in dynamic or unseen environments. Methods: This study proposes a Bayesian deep neural network framework for predicting continuous joint trajectories in the sagittal plane across diverse ambulation tasks. The approach integrates Bayesian inference to estimate predictive uncertainty and enhance model generalization. A new hybrid loss function is introduced that combines the standard error of the dataset with variational mean absolute error or mean squared error, allowing the model to leverage low-variance gait segments more effectively during training. The framework is trained and evaluated using a dataset that includes varying walking speeds and inclines. Results: The proposed Bayesian model outperforms traditional state-of-the-art methods, particularly deep neural networks and finite state machine baselines, and shows up to 15% and 25% improvements, respectively, in joint trajectory prediction accuracy. Moreover, the Bayesian deep neural network provides 95% prediction intervals, indicating strong reliability and robustness under uncertain conditions. Significance: By incorporating uncertainty quantification and dataset-driven variance into the learning process, this study offers a more reliable and adaptive approach to gait modeling. These improvements support enhanced gait control in assistive technologies and legged robotic systems operating in variable terrain conditions in the real world.

title: Bridging Modulation Gaps: Similarity-Aware Domain-Invariant Learning for Robust Radio Frequency Fingerprint Identification
authors: Rui Wang, Si Chen, Zhenxin Cai, Qin Wang, Chengcheng Liu, Yun Lin, Guan Gui
abstract: Radio Frequency Fingerprint Identification (RFFI) has emerged as a promising technique for enhancing wireless security by uniquely identifying individual devices through their inherent RF characteristics. However, the performance of conventional RFFI methods deteriorates significantly when training and testing involve different modulation schemes, primarily due to the resulting domain shift between modulation types. To address this challenge, this paper proposes Domain-Invariant Adaptive Mixup Enhancement (DIAME), a novel framework that integrates domain-invariant feature extraction with a similarity-aware adaptive mixup strategy to improve generalization across modulation domains. Specifically, DIAME dynamically adjusts the mixup intensity based on inter-feature similarity and incorporates domain alignment and feature matching with pretrained models to promote modulation-invariant representation learning. Extensive experiments on a synthetic RF dataset comprising four modulation types and five devices demonstrate that DIAME achieves an average cross-modulation identification accuracy of 86.18%, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods. These results confirm the effectiveness of DIAME in mitigating domain shift and highlight its suitability for robust RFFI in heterogeneous wireless communication environments.

title: Efficient Incomplete Utterance Rewriting with Modern Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Weilai Jiang, Tianming Dang, Yaonan Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: In recent years, the task of Incomplete Utterance Rewriting (IUR) has become an important research focus within the field of natural language processing. The goal is to restore incomplete utterances in a way that fits the surrounding context, thus enhancing overall understanding. In this paper, we propose a novel and efficient method. Unlike previous studies, we utilize a straightforward fully convolutional architecture to reveal the underlying semantic connections that exist among utterances and make corresponding edits to the original text to restore incomplete utterances. Compared to existing advanced methods, our approach provides a superior balance between efficiency and performance.

title: BlockSecRoute: A Lightweight Blockchain-Based Interdomain Routing Validation System
authors: Juan Cui, Xu Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The Border Gateway Protocol (BGP), as the core interdomain routing protocol of the Internet, suffers from security vulnerabilities such as prefix hijacking, route leaks, and path manipulation. Traditional solutions, including RPKI and BGPsec, face challenges of centralized trust anchors, high deployment cost, and limited scalability. To address these issues, this paper proposes BlockSecRoute, a lightweight blockchain-based interdomain routing validation system that supports prefix - path joint verification. The system adopts a two-tier architecture with an offchain joint verification mechanism and an on-chain Minimal Verifiable Evidence (MVE) model. By performing real-time prefix and path validation off-chain and storing only compact verification proofs on-chain, BlockSecRoute reduces per-update consensus overhead while preserving auditability. In addition, a policy-driven feedback mechanism enables adaptive route management and mitigates abnormal routing behaviors. Evaluations on BGPStream and RIPE RIS datasets show that BlockSecRoute reduces verification latency and on-chain overhead compared with existing blockchain-based solutions. Unlike RPKI, BGPsec, and prior blockchain-based frameworks such as RouteChain and BGPcoin, BlockSecRoute explicitly separates off-chain real-time verification from on-chain minimal evidence storage, achieving policy-aware, low-latency validation with full auditability.

title: Deep Insight Network for Medical Hyperspectral Imaging Segmentation
authors: Wujian Xu, Siyu Liu, Xin Wei, Huan Wan
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Medical Hyperspectral Imaging (MHSI) plays a vital role in accurate tissue segmentation by virtue of its rich spectral-spatial information. However, existing deep learning-based segmentation methods for MHSI still face challenges in effectively extracting and integrating such information, which limits their segmentation performance. To overcome these limitations, we propose an advanced segmentation framework named Deep Insight Network (DeIn-Net), which is designed to comprehensively explore spectral-spatial features and achieve deep fusion for precise MHSI segmentation. The proposed DeIn-Net incorporates four key components: a Dual-view Spectral Feature Extraction module (DvSFE) for capturing both inter- and intra-spectral features, a Feature Seamless Fusion Module (FSFM) for adaptive integration of spectral and spatial information, a Macro-micro Attention Module (MAM) to bridge the semantic gap between encoder and decoder features, and a Feature Geometric Refinement Branch (FGRB) that enhances decoding features through geometric analysis. Extensive experiments on two public MHSI datasets involving six different tissue objects consistently demonstrate that our model achieves superior performance over state-of-the-art methods in segmenting both large and small structures across multiple evaluation metrics.

title: From Video to Weather: Deep Learning-Driven Observation Using Surveillance Cameras
authors: Cuiyan Zhang, Xing Wang, Huiting Lv, Heyueyang Li, A K M Rashid Been Aziz, Nwachukwu Chisom Samson
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Surveillance cameras, with their wide spatial coverage and high update frequency, represent an underutilized resource for weather observation. To investigate their potential as a new type of weather observer, this study constructs a surveillance video dataset covering eleven weather phenomena, and evaluates the performance of eighteen state-of-the-art deep learning models. Experimental results show that the best-performing model achieves 89.6% accuracy, 89.4% precision, 89.6% recall, and $89.4 \% \mathrm{~F} 1$-score. These findings indicate that surveillance videos contain distinguishable visual patterns of weather conditions and that deep learning models can reliably identify them. This study provides a foundation for applying surveillance camera systems to urban weather monitoring and early-warning systems. Moreover, the proposed approach demonstrates the feasibility of integrating ubiquitous visual data with advanced deep learning techniques, offering potential applications in real-time environmental sensing, smart city infrastructure, and autonomous decision-making systems across multiple technical domains.

title: Analysis of Research Progress on New Energy Storage for Enhancing the Resilience of New Power Systems
authors: Yu Zhang, Xi Li, Wei Wang, Yajun Zou, Zhou Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Research on new energy storage technologies involves complex professional knowledge, spans multiple disciplines, and has a high degree of interdisciplinary integration. It is necessary to analyze and assess its development trends using bibliometric methods. This study analyzes the development trends based on global publications from 2019 to 2024. Regarding the publication situation, detailed information is presented from multiple perspectives, including the development trend of the field, the number of papers, disciplinary distribution, country (region) distribution, institutional distribution, hot keywords, and highly cited papers. China is very active in academic research and industrial application in new type of energy storage, achieving abundant results. In the fields of electrochemical, compressed air, flywheel, gravitational, China ranks first in publications, with absolute numbers far ahead, reflecting China's leading role, academic research, industrial applications, demonstration projects, and commercial promotion.

title: MusicScale: Hierarchical Text-to-Music Generation via Multi Scale Autoregressive Modeling
authors: Wenzi Tang, Yan Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Generating music from natural language descriptions remains challenging for existing autoregressive models, which struggle to maintain structural coherence over long sequences. Drawing inspiration from recent work on next-scale prediction in visual and audio domains, we introduce MusicScale, a hierarchical generation framework that treats music composition as a coarse-to-fine process rather than sequential token prediction. The core of our approach is a Music Scale Tokenizer (MST) that breaks down MIDI sequences into four temporal scales-sections spanning 16 bars, 4-bar phrases, single bar motifs, and individual notes. Our Scale Conditioned Transformer (SCT) then generates music progressively across these scales, where each level conditions on text embeddings alongside all preceding coarser representations. When evaluated on the Lakh MIDI dataset, MusicScale demonstrates 38% improvement in text-music alignment and runs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24 \times$</tex> faster than Music Transformer, all while preserving strong structural coherence and melodic quality.

title: Towards a Distributed Processing Approach for SPARQL Queries on Spark Using an RDF Graph-Oriented Model
authors: Siham Benhamed, Ahlem Chalabi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: The volume of RDF data on the Web has now reached the scale of Big Data, enabling machines to process and interpret it. This evolution makes the integration of Big Data technologies essential, particularly for data storage and query processing. Apache Spark is among the most widely adopted distributed computing frameworks for handling large-scale data in such environments, and many systems leverage it to enhance query execution. However, Spark doesn’t account for the specific characteristics of RDF data, which prevents it from efficiently filtering the data to be queried. As a result, it fails to significantly optimize the efficiency and speed of query execution. To overcome this limitation, we introduce a model that generates execution plans for SPARQL queries, specifically optimized for distributed processing with Apache Spark. In the proposed model, RDF data is stored in Neo4j using a structure that maintains the semantic integrity of the data (RDF graph). Subsequently, queries are issued clause by clause over a selected portion of the RDF graph representation (RDF subgraph), guided by the indexing of the data. We experimentally evaluate our model using a synthetic dataset and a variety of queries, thereby demonstrating a significant improvement in response times.

title: Research on Agent-Based Code Generation Intelligence Construction Using AgentGPT
authors: ZhiXiong Li, YanFeng Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper designs and implements AgentGPT, an i ntelligent agent system based on OpenAI's GPT-4 model, aiming t o explore task automation and code generation mechanisms drive n by large language models. Adopting a frontend-backend separation architecture, the system integrates core modules including ta sk generation, scheduling, execution, and feedback. It can automa tically generate executable code based on natural language instru ctions and manage the entire process. Experimental results demo nstrate AgentGPT's high accuracy and stability in code generatio n tasks. It autonomously implements various algorithms includin g recursion, iteration, and dynamic programming, while exhibitin g self optimization and complexity analysis capabilities. These fin dings validate the feasibility and advantages of large language mo del-based agents in automated programming, providing technical references and development directions for subsequent research in multi-task scheduling optimization, reasoning efficiency enhance ment, knowledge augmentation, and multi-agent collaboration.

title: Impact of Large Language Models on Conversational Translation: Towards Translation Agents
