authors: Toru Ishida, Yohei Murakami, Arif Bramantoro
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Despite advances in deep learning, conversational translation has continued to face persistent challenges: ambiguity in short sentences, limited data for colloquial language, and the need for culturally-situated, commonsensebased translations. However, Large Language Models (LLMs) are now leveraging their vast background knowledge to bring about a fundamental shift in the field. In this paper, we first review the various research and technological developments that have attempted to realize conversational translation prior to the emergence of LLMs. We then demonstrate that, with appropriate prompting, LLMs can resolve most of the key challenges identified in conversational translation research over the last two decades. Finally, we outline the future challenges that the research community must address in the era of LLMs.

title: Ecg Signal Classification Using Wavelet Scattering Transform and Hybrid Cnn-Lstm Network
authors: Han Gao, Lihong Yan, Fukui Guan, Xinyu Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper presents a robust method for ECG signal classification by integrating Wavelet Scattering Transform (WST) with a lightweight CNN-LSTM network. Unlike the conventional Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT), which is sensitive to signal shifts and relies heavily on wavelet basis selection, WST provides translation-invariant and deformation-stable timefrequency features with reduced computational cost. The proposed WST-CNN-LSTM model effectively captures multi-scale characteristics of non-stationary ECG signals while suppressing noise interference. Evaluated on the MIT-BIH arrhythmia database, the model achieves 95.5% accuracy, significantly outperforming a CWT-based baseline in both convergence speed and generalization ability.

title: Evaluating Student Satisfaction with Lecturers in Higher Education Through a Business Intelligence Dashboard
authors: Putry Wahyu Setyaningsih, Septian Eka Ady Buananta, Siti Jamiatul Husnaini
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: The evaluation of lecturer performance in higher education often relies on conventional surveys that provide limited analytical depth and actionable recommendations. This study proposes a comprehensive framework for evaluating student satisfaction with lecturers through a BI dashboard integrated with usability testing. The framework is based on a structured questionnaire covering four key dimensions: Assessment & Feedback, Learning Support, Engagement & Motivation, and Accessibility & Approachability. The BI dashboard, developed using Google Looker Studio, visualizes aggregated statistics (average, maximum, and minimum scores), demographic and psychographic comparisons, and detailed lecturer evaluations. Usability and user experience were systematically assessed using UEQ. The results show strong performance in Attractiveness (M = 1.59) and Stimulation (M = 1.57), indicating that users find the system engaging and visually appealing. Perspicuity (M = 1.38), Efficiency (M = 1.43), and Dependability (M = 1.41) achieved above-average ratings, while Novelty (M = 0.99) scored lower, highlighting the need for more innovative features. Therefore, the BI dashboard enhances transparency, usability, and comprehensiveness in lecturer evaluation and provides a positive user experience. This study concludes that integrating BI dashboards with structured feedback and usability testing offers a practical and innovative approach to improving teaching quality and supporting data-driven decision-making in higher education.

title: Making Secret Sharing Smart: A DRL-Driven Framework for Adaptive Parameter Tuning in Intelligent Information Service
authors: Zhenbin Liang, Yuntao Zou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Traditional Secret Sharing (SS) is a key technology for ensuring the security and reliability of PB-scale distributed storage. However, its performance is severely constrained by “static” parameter configurations, which cannot adapt to dynamic cloud network environments, leading to significant computation (high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t$</tex> threshold) and communication (asynchronous overhead) bottlenecks. This paper proposes DASS, a novel Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-driven adaptive secret sharing framework. DASS innovatively combines a DQN decision-making agent with an efficient hbAVSS variant (for communication) and Random Band Matrix erasure codes (for computation). We formally model the complex parameter tuning problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), enabling the DRL agent to observe real-time network states (e.g., latency, load) and dynamically optimize key parameters (such as the threshold <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t$</tex> and erasure code dimensions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m \times k$</tex>). Our evaluation in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex>-node realworld AWS cloud environment shows that, compared to SOTA benchmarks, DASS achieves nearly 15x average speedup (reducing 1TB reconstruction time from 4510 ms to 303 ms), while reducing bandwidth consumption by nearly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 \%}$</tex> (from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 0 M B} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> level to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$302 \text{MB} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>), and increasing system throughput by 31 %. DASS is the first framework to bridge the gap between AI-driven optimization and cryptographic storage, providing an efficient and adaptive solution to the “computation-communication imbalance” problem.

title: Signcryption Scheme Using the Schmidt Samoa Cryptosystem and Ong-Schnorr Shamir Digital Signature to Ensure the Security of Digital Text
authors: Dian Rachmawati, Mohammad Andri Budiman, Amer Sharif, Muhammad Haikal Rahman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Traditional methods that separate encryption and digital signatures for securing digital communication led to inefficiencies in computation, bandwidth, and key management. This research introduces a unified signcryption scheme combining the Schmidt–Samoa Cryptosystem for encryption and the Ong–Schnorr–Shamir algorithm for authentication. The proposed framework leverages the provable security of Schmidt–Samoa, equivalent to the integer factorization problem, while utilizing the computational efficiency of the improved Ong–Schnorr–Shamir signature mechanism. Performance evaluation demonstrates that Schmidt–Samoa achieves 1024 bit key generation in 2.37 seconds, with encryption times ranging from 0.068 to 0.073 seconds, while decryption operates nearly $3 \times$ faster. The Ong–Schnorr–Shamir component delivers superior performance with 512 bit key generation in 0.19 seconds, signing in under 0.00006 seconds, and verification up to $10 \times$ faster. System testing reveals consistent memory usage of $\mathbf{1 8. 5 8 ~ M B}$ throughout operations. This integrated scheme delivers robust security with high computational efficiency, making it ideal for modern digital communication systems.

title: Adaptive Insurance Claim Inference Modeling: Multi-agent RAG with Chain-of-thought Optimization
authors: Dennis Tandelon, Nur Afny Catur Andryani, Yandi Ariffudin, Eka W. Pujiharto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Accurate medical claim documentation and coding are essential for the medical claim reimbursement process. And yet, most of the processes are rely on human decision, which remains time-consuming and error-prone. This research investigates the potential of Agentic AI adoption in supporting medical claim autonomous coding. The proposed method combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and structured agent reasoning. Two prompting strategies were evaluated across three models: GPT, Claude, and Gemini. The simulation’s result indicates that incorporating reasoning substantially improves output fidelity. GPT with RAG + CoT achieved the highest performance with a BLEU-4 score of 0.785 and Semantic Similarity score of 98.9%. Claude also benefited (BLEU-4 score 0.501 and Similarity around 98%). Runtime analysis indicates modest latency (estimated at 1 minute per case) relative to quality gains. Despite strong accuracy, nearly half of the test records still omitted severity level or INA-CBG code fields, reflecting the limitation of text-only inputs without explicit clinical indicators or grouper logic. Building on these findings, future work can explore context-adaptive retrieval and advanced multi-agent collaboration to enhance reasoning depth and domain grounding. The study demonstrates the feasibility and potential of Agentic AI in supporting autonomous standardized medical claim modeling within the Indonesian medical claim ecosystem.

title: A Maneuver Detection LSTM and Fuzzy Neural Network-Based Approach for High-Maneuver Target Tracking
authors: Zhuye Liu, Kailun Shen, Weiming Yuan, Yijie Hao, Beining Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Tracking highly maneuverable targets is challenging due to model mismatches and motion uncertainties. This paper proposes a neural network-based framework for highly maneuverable target tracking, named Maneuver Detection Tracker (MD-Tracker), which consists of a prediction module based on Long Short-Term Memory and a fuzzy neural network update module. The prediction module extracts maneuvering features from residual information via the Maneuver Detection Unit (MDU) to adaptively predict the target state, while the update module employs fuzzy rules to adjust the Kalman gain and correct the estimation. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, a three-dimensional dataset of maneuvering target trajectories encompassing multiple motion models is constructed. Ablation studies and comparative experiments are conducted to evaluate the contribution of the MDU module and the overall estimation performance of the model. In highly maneuverable target scenarios, MD-Tracker demonstrates superior performance compared to current state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: Research on Online Interactive Technology of Digital Twin of Small-Caliber Electromagnetic Railgun
authors: Fan Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Electromagnetic railguns are favored in the military field because of their advantages such as high muzzle velocity, difficulty in interception and strong offensiveness. In recent years, domestic research on railguns has focused on small-caliber railguns, but the transition ablation phenomenon generated by small-caliber railguns during firing has not been effectively controlled. The measurement of rail structural parameters is of great reference significance for the study of the bore wear mechanism and shooting accuracy analysis of small-caliber electromagnetic railguns. In view of the eccentric structure and size limitations of small-caliber rails, the existing structural parameter measurement system has problems such as large reference positioning error and low measurement accuracy. After in-depth analysis of the rail structural characteristics, an online interactive technology framework based on digital twins is proposed to meet the real-time status monitoring and dynamic optimization needs of small-caliber electromagnetic railguns in complex combat environments. Experimental results show that this technology can reduce the system response delay to less than 15ms and improve the parameter prediction accuracy to more than 95%, providing technical support for the intelligent operation and maintenance and rapid decision-making of electromagnetic railguns.

title: Advanced Earth Observation Using Explainable AI for Climate Change Monitoring in the Era of Multisensor Cooperation
authors: Akira Hirose, Gunjan Joshi, Ryo Natsuaki
abstract: Global and comprehensive observation of mountain glaciers is essential not only for providing data for an objective assessment of global warming, but also for evaluating their role as water resources for downstream communities, and assessing the risk of flooding due to glacial lake outbursts. This paper outlines a method we propose to optimize the amount of required data by estimating prominent indices depending on target applications when performing optical multispectral observations of mountain glaciers using satellite-borne sensors. The method is expected to enable observation and evaluation to be carried out quickly and at a low cost, which is of great significance as part of humanitarian activities in the IEEE field.

title: Performance Analysis of Symbol Timing Offset and Carrier Frequency Offset Estimation Based on Training Sequences
authors: Zhenni Wu, Xueyong Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Every communication system requires synchronization between the transmitter and receiver to ensure optimal system performance. In light of this, this work suggests a straightforward training sequence-based estimation technique for symbol timing offset (STO) and carrier frequency offset (CFO). This paper aims to reduce inter-carrier interference (ICI) by maintaining orthogonality between subcarriers in multipath channels to mitigate the impact of symbol timing errors and carrier frequency errors on system performance by maintaining orthogonality between subcarriers. To achieve this, the paper employs a STO and CFO estimation method utilizing a preamble, and the effectiveness of this method is validated through MATLAB simulations. The method is compared with additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) and multipath fading theoretical channels, and its performance is evaluated through different error rates under various signal-to-noise ratios (SNR). It can be observed that by estimating and correcting STO and CFO using this method, the impact of STO and CFO on the system is reduced, resulting in an effective decrease in the system error rate and an optimized system.

title: Data Transmission Scheme in Underwater Wireless Power Transfer Systems for Dynamic Spectrum Access
authors: Koshi Inoue, Urara Tsuchiya, Mamiko Inamori
abstract: This paper investigates the integration of Dynamic Spectrum Access (DSA) into Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) systems, focusing on its ability to adapt frequencies in realtime for more efficient power transfer, especially in challenging environments like seawater. However, seawater's high conductivity reduces underwater power transfer, especially with distance or coil misalignment. To address these challenges, the magnetic field resonant coupling scheme is used for high efficiency at high currents, along with simultaneous data transmission to adjust coil distance and position for stable, efficient power delivery. The proposed 85 kHz WPT system uses S21 parameters from a Vector Network Analyzer (VNA) to model seawater propagation and evaluates Bit Error Rate (BER) via Quadrature Phase-Shift Keying (QPSK) simulations with Wiener filter compensation. This paper demonstrates that integrating DSA with WPT enables smart frequency use and reliable underwater power transfer, benefiting Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) and other marine technologies.

title: Adaptive Resilience Control of DC Microgrids Under Hybrid DoS and FDI Attacks
authors: Jingrui Jiang, Yuanfeng Zhang, Xiaobin Yan, Xing Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Within the domain of DC microgrids, restoring the DC bus voltage to nominal values and ensuring fair power distribution among distributed generation units is a significant challenge, compounded by Denial of Service (DoS) and False Data Injection (FDI) attacks. To address this issue, an adaptive resilient secondary control strategy is proposed in this essay. The findings of the study indicate that this technique is capable of counteracting FDI signals during DoS attacks through the introduction of an adaptive compensation term. As a result, it maintains efficient voltage regulation and equitable current sharing even amid mixed attacks. Furthermore, the system's stability has been meticulously demonstrated using Lyapunov theory. Ultimately, the simulation results unequivocally affirm the practicality and efficacy of the proposed control strategy.

title: Quality Analysis and Evaluation Prediction of RAG Retrieval Based on Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Ruoxin Zhang, Zhizhao Wen, Chao Wang, Chenchen Tang, Puyang Xu, Yifan Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the rapid evolution of large language models, retrieval enhanced generation technology has been widely used due to its ability to integrate external knowledge to improve output accuracy. However, the performance of the system is highly dependent on the quality of the retrieval module. If the retrieval results have low relevance to user needs or contain noisy information, it will directly lead to distortion of the generated content. In response to the performance bottleneck of existing models in processing tabular features, this paper proposes an XGBoost machine learning regression model based on feature engineering and particle swarm optimization. Correlation analysis shows that answer_quality is positively correlated with doc_delevance by 0.66, indicating that document relevance has a significant positive effect on answer quality, and improving document relevance may enhance answer quality; The strong negative correlations between semantic similarity, redundancy, and diversity were −0.89 and −0.88, respectively, indicating a tradeoff between semantic similarity, redundancy, and diversity. In other words, as the former two increased, diversity significantly decreased. The experimental results comparing decision trees, AdaBoost, etc. show that the VMD PSO BiLSTM model is superior in all evaluation indicators, with significantly lower MSE, RMSE, MAE, and MAPE compared to the comparison model. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> value is higher, indicating that its prediction accuracy, stability, and data interpretation ability are more outstanding. This achievement provides an effective path for optimizing the retrieval quality and improving the generation effect of RAG system, and has important value in promoting the implementation and application of related technologies.

title: Smart Cockpit Scenario Service Data Encryption and Security Protection System Based on Voice Interaction
authors: Guangxiu Zhang, Xiaojie Wang, Zhenyu Nie, Jie Yang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: It is difficult to meet the high real-time and multimodal interaction requirements when the deep integration of voice interaction in smart cockpits brings about the triple security threats of end-to-cloud data transmission hijacking, illegal access to local storage, and malicious calls to service interfaces. This study proposes a scenario-driven dynamic security protection framework (SDDSF): first, a multi-dimensional scenario perception model is constructed to identify service scenarios; then, a layered dynamic encryption engine is designed; at the same time, a voice stream fingerprint watermarking technology is developed to embed time-space stamp hashes for traceability and anti-replay; finally, a security policy linkage mechanism is established to coordinate the keys and policies in the cockpit domain. Experiments show that SDDSF achieves an end-to-end transmission delay of 175.3ms with a data volume of 500KB and a replay attack interception rate of 96.2%. Ablation experiments further demonstrate that removing the voice watermark module reduces the interception rate to 80.8% (a decrease of 15.4pp). These results demonstrate that SDDSF combines excellent real-time performance and module collaboration efficiency while ensuring security, providing a scalable and practical security solution for voice- driven intelligent cockpit systems.

title: Towards a High-Level Ontological Approach for Enterprise Stakeholder Representation
authors: Sara Chelbi, Nora Taleb
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Enterprises operate in dynamic and complex environments where diverse stakeholders significantly influence their success and sustainability. Effective understanding and management of these multifaceted relationships are crucial for fostering stakeholder engagement and achieving organizational goals. To comprehensively represent and analyze the Complex relationships of enterprise stakeholders, we propose a High-Level Ontological Approach. This article presents the development of the ontology, guided by the Noy and McGuiness methodology, and its intrinsic evaluation using Protégé. The ontology represents primary and secondary stakeholders, both internal and external, offering a holistic understanding of their roles, interactions. Our approach strikes a balance between granularity and abstraction, ensuring adaptability and precision in stakeholder analysis. By facilitating targeted engagement strategies and informed decision-making, the ontology serves as a valuable tool for organizations. We discuss potential applications and future directions, highlighting the ontology's potential to enhance stakeholder management in diverse industries and contexts.

title: ETransMAE: A Cognitive Stage-Aware Masked Autoencoder for Mild Cognitive Impairment Detection via Clock Drawing Test Videos
authors: Tianchen Niu, Lingjie Fan
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) detection through behavioral biomarkers represents a critical advancement in early dementia screening. This paper presents ETransMAE (EfficientNet-Transformer and Cognitive Stage-Aware Masked Autoencoder), a novel deep learning framework that analyzes Clock Drawing Test (CDT) video sequences for MCI identification. Unlike existing approaches that rely on expensive digital pen devices or static image analysis, our method leverages accessible tablet-recorded drawing videos to capture temporal dynamics of cognitive processes. ETransMAE integrates four key components: video preprocessing, EfficientNet-B0 spatial encoding, multi-head attention temporal modeling, and a cognitive stage-aware masking strategy that aligns with CDT’s three cognitive phases. Experimental results on $\mathbf{1 2 4}$ participants demonstrate superior performance with $\mathbf{9 1. 0 \%}$ AUC (Area Under the ROC Curve), corresponding to a false negative rate of 17.3% and a false positive rate of 13.1%, significantly outperforming traditional methods while requiring only standard tablet devices. The cognitive stage-aware masking strategy contributes 3.2 percentage points improvement, validating the integration of domain knowledge in deep learning architectures. This work further contributes to the fields of AI-driven computer vision and image processing by demonstrating how cognitively informed masking, task-aligned temporal modeling, and lightweight video representation learning can enhance the interpretability and efficiency of behavior-centric video analysis. The proposed framework offers a generalizable paradigm for integrating domain knowledge into vision models, with potential applications across fine-grained motion understanding, cognitive behavior analysis, and clinical video computing.

title: Hydrological Analysis of Upland Reservoirs Using DEM Data
authors: Jieling Wu
abstract: In Japan's mountainous regions, numerous reservoirs have historically played a crucial role in supplying agricultural water. Recently, their importance has been reevaluated in the context of disaster prevention and water resource management. According to the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries (2022), there are over 150,000 reservoirs across the country. In a previous study, we analyzed the hydrological characteristics of Lake Irikasa in Fujimi Town, Nagano Prefecture, using GIS and 10-meter resolution DEM data. While it was possible to delineate the immediate catchment area surrounding the reservoir, the upstream catchment could not be extracted due to data resolution limitations. This study aims to use 5-meter resolution DEM data to delineate a wider upstream catchment area. The results revealed that while the reservoir plays a vital role in water storage and flood regulation, it also poses potential flood risks to downstream areas during heavy rainfall events.

title: Cyber Resilience of Battery Energy Storage System Scheduling under Adversarial Attack on Load Forecasting
authors: Chen Chao, Zixiao Ma, Ziang Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) scheduling plays an important role in the modernization of power grid, yet its growing reliance on load forecasting introduces critical cyber risks. Misled load forecasts can lead to operational costs increase and compromise system reliability and stability. However, the resilience of BESS scheduling to such attacks is largely overlooked. To address this gap, this paper proposes an attack- schedulingvalidation framework to evaluate how adversarial attacks on load forecasting practically impacts BESS scheduling especially in peak shaving. Specifically, we present how to integrate attacked load forecasts into BESS scheduling model. We further propose an optimization-based validation model to assess the operational feasibility of such attacked BESS scheduling under actual load conditions. Experiments show that injecting adversarial attacks on load forecasting can lead to either increased investment costs or power system anomaly during peak hours. These findings underscore the importance to consider cyber resilience in future BESS scheduling.

title: A Robotic Laser Welding Seam Tracking Method for Complex 3-D Weld Trajectories Based on Geometric Structure Analysis_supp9-3661009.mp4
authors: Juan Liu
abstract: Seam sampling with line-structured-light sensors in laser welding is prone to projection distortion due to the complex 3-D geometry of sheet-metal workpieces, which leads to viewpoint-dependent variations in the sampled seam profile and affects laser incidence direction planning. Moreover, stable target tracking control becomes challenging when the 3-D seam trajectory contains small curvature radii or right-angle transitions. To address these issues, a method is proposed that compensates for projection distortion via deep-learning-based local surface-normal estimation and achieves robust viewpoint control along complex 3-D seam trajectories using prior geometric approximation of the seam trajectory. For normal estimation, seam features are encoded as “point-vector pair” superpixels and extracted by a single-stage deep convolutional network, enabling efficient, robust detection under welding glare, spatter, smoke, and reflections while maintaining boundary stability. Using the “point-vector pair” representation, the laser incidence direction is adaptively adjusted with reference to estimated surface normals to reduce pose uncertainty caused by projection distortion. For viewpoint control, a feedforward leading-direction control algorithm, derived from geometric–numerical analysis of predefined trajectory, ensures stable perception and high-precision continuous tracking along tight curves and right-angle turns. Across two experiments involving different workpiece geometries, the method achieves mean absolute laser focal position errors of 0.16 and 0.20 mm and mean absolute 3-D laser incident-direction errors of 0.99<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> and 1.58<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>, validating its efficiency, stability, and accuracy for sheet-metal laser seam tracking.

title: SMoQKE-IDS: Sparse Mixture of Quantum Kolmogorov-Arnold Network Experts for FL-IDS in Edge-IIoT
authors: Jyoti Prakash Sahoo, Binayak Kar, Yi-Leh Wu, Ahmed M. Abdelmoniem, Dimitris Chatzopoulos
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The widespread adoption of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) devices at the network edge necessitates advanced intrusion detection systems to mitigate sophisticated cyber threats. Edge IIoT environments, characterized by heterogeneous data and resource constraints, demand adaptive, scalable, and interpretable security solutions. This study proposes a sparse mixture of Quantum Kolmogorov-Arnold Experts Intrusion Detection system (SMoQKE-IDS), an innovative framework that integrates quantum-classical machine learning, federated learning, and interpretable AI within a Mixture of Experts (MoE) architecture. Unlike existing quantum-enhanced or Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KAN)-based IDS, which typically rely on standalone architectures or dense, unified ensembles, SMoQKE-IDS introduces a Sparse Mixture of Experts (SMoE) paradigm to decouple model capacity from computational overhead. The framework integrates parameterized quantum circuits for high-dimensional Hilbert-space feature mapping, convolutional layers for spatiotemporal feature extraction, and KANs for interpretable univariate nonlinear modeling. These heterogeneous modules are governed by a modality-specific sparse gating mechanism that implements conditional computation by dynamically activating the top-k optimal expert sub-networks for each input. This approach effectively mitigates the computational redundancy inherent in dense models while enhancing adaptability to diverse network traffic profiles. Additionally, federated learning enables privacy-preserving and scalable training, while the inherent interpretability of the framework enhances trust through visual and analytical transparency. Rigorous validation on benchmark datasets within federated environments demonstrates superior detection accuracy, heightened transparency, and robust performance, establishing SMoQKE-IDS as a robust solution for securing edge IIoT ecosystems.

title: ZoomingCOD: Zooming-like behavior for Camouflage Object Detection based on Cross-axis Perception and Adaptive Receptive Field
authors: Feng Xiao, Jianhua Zhang, Guang Yang, Ruyu Liu, Xu Cheng, Shengyong Chen
abstract: Camouflaged object detection (COD) plays a critical role in various applications such as wildlife monitoring, and security surveillance, where accurately identifying targets hidden within complex environments is essential. In COD tasks, the large-scale variation of camouflaged objects often leads to situations where traditional multi-scale methods struggle to capture sufficient contextual information for both small and large objects. Additionally, compared to other detection tasks, the boundaries of camouflaged objects are typically more ambiguous and harder to distinguish, further complicating the process. Although existing methods ZoomNet enhance contextual information by extracting multi-scale information, they fail to adaptively adjust the scale of required context based on the size of camouflaged objects, limiting the effectiveness of the models. To address the issue of significant size and shape variations of camouflaged targets and their varying demands for contextual information, we propose a novel COD method based on the zooming experience of human vision. The model effectively enhances the fuzzy boundary information of the target to be identified through a novel axial perception module and combines the adaptive receptive field selection module to realize a dynamic multi-scale feature adjustment mechanism. We also optimize computational resources with the Mamba state-space model, reducing the global computation through a selective computation mechanism, thus enhancing overall computational efficiency. Through extensive validation on multiple public benchmark datasets and our own custom dataset, our method achieves a 13.5% performance improvement over SOTA wF metrics, demonstrating ZoomingCOD's superior performance in COD tasks.

title: MiNTT: A Family of Module Lattice-based Cryptographic Compression Functions
authors: Riley Vaughn, Mike Borowczak
abstract: A cryptographic compression function is the part of a hash function most responsible for compressing input while avoiding collisions. In recent years [26], [27], NIST has released a Call for Proposals for a variety of cryptographic primitives, albeit there have been no calls specifically for post-quantum hash functions, as classic standards are widely considered secure against quantum threats. Nonetheless, alternative post-quantum primitives should not be disregarded in the event that novel quantum speedups are discovered. The most recent advancement in the development of lattice-based post-quantum compression functions was SWIFFT [5], [21], based on the Ring Short Integer Solution (R-SIS) problem. We propose MiNTT, a family of post-quantum cryptographic compression functions based on the Module Short Integer Solution problem (M-SIS), which brings post-quantum compression up to modern standards.The hardness of M-SIS was established by Langlois and Stehlé [19] via a reduction from the Module Short Independent Vectors Problem (M-SIVP), ensuring strong worst-case to average-case security guarantees. MiNTT leverages the Number Theoretic Transform to enable highly efficient polynomial multiplication. The multiplication of polynomials, which make up the key matrix and input of a MiNTT function, is the primary computational overhead of the function.We provide a range of concrete parameterizations of the MiNTT family and analyze the efficiency and security levels, including optimizations that exploit SIMD parallelism. Parameterizations are tested for concrete security as well as throughput and memory efficiency, and the top preforming variants are suggested for use in a MiNTT-based hash function.

title: Performance Indicators for Shape and Position Assessment in Electromagnetic Inverse Scattering
authors: André Costa Batista, Ricardo Adriano
abstract: Microwave imaging is a low-cost, non-invasive technique for detecting and characterizing objects in inaccessible media through electromagnetic field measurements. The evaluation of algorithms that solve the related electromagnetic inverse scattering problem typically relies on metrics such as Mean Square Error (MSE) or Structural Similarity (SSIM), which primarily assess contrast estimation accuracy but provide limited insight into the recovery of object geometry and position. To address this limitation, this paper introduces two novel performance indicators specifically designed to evaluate electromagnetic inverse scattering algorithms: a shape error indicator that quantifies geometric reconstruction accuracy, and a position error indicator that measures object localization precision. The proposed indicators are applicable to both qualitative and quantitative methods, even when they are combined in an experiment. Comprehensive experimental validation is conducted using traditional algorithms: Linear Sampling Method (LSM), Orthogonality Sampling Method (OSM), Born Iterative Method (BIM), Contrast Source Iterative Method (CSI), Subspace Optimization Method (SOM), and Circle Approximation (CA). Three distinct experimental frameworks are employed: shape recovery studies, position detection studies, and a realistic breast phantom study demonstrating their applicability. Supported by robust statistical analysis, the experiments revealed that: (a) in scenarios with a single scatterer and low Degree of Nonlinearity, BIM achieves the best average performance in object geometry recovery; (b) in scenarios with a single small-sized, high-contrast scatterer, SOM and OSM achieve the best average performance in target localization; and (c) the proposed indicators are less sensitive to contrast estimation errors compared to traditional metrics like MSE and SSIM and to shape reconstruction errors when location detection is considered. These indicators provide useful tools for algorithm development, benchmarking studies, and performance assessment in electromagnetic inverse scattering applications.

title: Deep Unfolding Real-Time Super-Resolution Using Subpixel-Shift Twin Image and Convex Self-Similarity Prior
authors: Chia-Hsiang Lin, Wei-Chih Liu, Yu-En Chiu, Jhao-Ting Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Multi-image super-resolution (MISR) is a critical technique for satellite remote sensing. In the perspective of information, twin-image super-resolution (TISR) is regarded as the most challenging MISR scenario, having crucial applications like the SPOT-5 supermode imaging. In TISR, an image is super-resolved by its subpixel-shift counterpart (i.e., twin image), where the two images are typically offset by half a pixel both horizontally and vertically. We formulate the less investigated TISR using a convex criterion, which is implemented using a novel deep unfolding network. In the unfolding, an embedded simple shift operator trickily addresses the coupled TISR data-fitting terms, and a transformer trained with a convex self-similarity loss function elegantly implements the proximal mapping induced by the TISR regularizer. The proposed convex self-similarity unfolding supermode super-resolution (COSUP) algorithm is interpretable and achieves state-of-the-art performance with very fast millisecond-level computational time. COSUP is also tested on real-world data, for which the subpixel shifts would not be spatially uniform, with results showing great superiority over the official CNES supermode imaging product in terms of credible metrics (e.g., natural image quality evaluator, NIQE).

title: A Probabilistic STA-Bayesian Algorithm for GNSS-R Retrieval of Arctic Soil Freeze-Thaw States
authors: Xiaofeng Meng, Cong Yin, Junming Xia, Feixiong Huang, Weihua Bai, Qifei Du, Dongmei Song, Lichang Duan, Yunlong Du, Guanyi Wang, Rui Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Soil freeze-thaw (F/T) states are a key indicator of the Arctic climate, highlighting the need for their accurate retrieval. Global Navigation Satellite System-Reflectometry (GNSSR) offers a promising approach for retrieving soil F/T states, as F/T states significantly affect soil dielectric properties and reflectivity. Traditional reflectivity-based retrieval algorithms, such as the seasonal threshold algorithm (STA), are highly susceptible to surface interference, including vegetation attenuation and terrain effects, which limits their retrieval accuracy when using a fixed classification threshold. This study proposes an STA-Bayesian algorithm that formulates soil F/T retrieval as a probabilistic inference task. By combining seasonal prior information with GNSS-R observation likelihoods under frozen and thawed states, the algorithm derives posterior probabilities for Bayesian-optimal F/T state decisions, replacing fixed-threshold retrieval with a probabilistic approach that is more robust to surface heterogeneity. The algorithm is applied to GNSS-R data from the Fengyun-3E (FY-3E) satellite and Tianmu-1 (TM-1) constellation. In validation against ERA5-Land soil temperature-derived F/T states, the STA-Bayesian F/T product attains an average accuracy of 92.97%, outperforming both the STA (75.35%) and the SMAP (85.22%). The STA-Bayesian algorithm shows substantial improvements over both the STA and SMAP F/T products in densely vegetated regions (e.g., forests and shrublands) and demonstrates greater robustness to terrain roughness effects. The results demonstrate the STA-Bayesian algorithm's promise for soil F/T monitoring over heterogeneous surfaces and its adaptability to retrieval in the complex environmental conditions of the Arctic.

title: A Novel Multi-Modulation-Order Technique based on Deep Learning for Communication Systems
authors: Thi-Nga Dao
abstract: Autoencoders (AEs) enable efficient data encoding in communication systems by jointly optimizing encoder and decoder. However, conventional AE-based modulation/ demodulation methods typically design AEs for a fixed number of transmission bits per symbol, thus requiring multiple trained models for different modulation orders. To address this problem, we propose a flexible autoencoder (flAE) architecture supporting multiple modulation orders to reduce training time and memory usage. Unlike existing approaches, flAE allows the constellation points across different modulation orders to be overlapped in the space for optimal modulation. The experimental results highlight that flAE is lightweight yet achieves performance comparable to that of traditional AE methods.

title: Covert RIS-Aided THz Communication with Pointing Errors and Imperfect CSI: Joint Beamforming, Phase Shift, and Power Splitting Optimization
authors: Faissal El Bouanani, Zakaria El Allali, Elmehdi Illi
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Covert communication has sparked widespread attention since it seeks to secure potentially undetected transmissions by employing a variety of confusion sources. Despite the highly directed nature of Terahertz (THz) beams, receiver vibration causes beam direction misalignment, lowering communication speeds, and reducing communication covertness. In this research, we present and analyze a resilient reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-aided covert transmission scheme for THz transmissions, in which a multi-antenna node transmits to both a covert user and a public user in the presence of an unfriendly warden, under both perfect and imperfect channel state information assumptions. The proposed approach is based on superimposing the two legitimate users’ signals, confusing the warden node’s detection. Closed-form expressions for the optimal detection threshold at the warden, detection error probability (DEP), and minimum DEP (MDEP) are provided. Simulation findings corroborate the analytical results, and insightful discussions on the monotonicity of the main metrics and their impact on the system’s covertness are included. Then, using an alternating optimization (AO)-based framework, an optimal design of the power allocated to each user’s signal, their respective beamforming vectors, and the RIS phase shifts is carried out to maximize the system’s achievable communication rate subject to power constraints and an MDEP threshold at the warden. The convexity of all intermediate subproblems has been thoroughly examined, and the covertness advantage of the proposed approach has been proven.
