authors: N/A

title: Visual Analyses and Reputation Signals: Role of Facial Characteristics on Sharing-Economy Platforms_supp1-3668118.docx
authors: Yuxin Huang
abstract: Peer-to-peer (P2P) platforms such as Airbnb, where individual providers transact directly with consumers, rely not only on well-studied traditional signals, such as ratings, but also on less-examined visual cues to build trust and facilitate transactions jointly. However, how consumers process and integrate these intertwined multimodal signals remains insufficiently understood. This study, informed by signaling theory and the stereotype content model, uses a dataset from Airbnb to examine the impact of multimodal signals. First, our results provide further evidence that ratings positively influence occupancy rates. More importantly, we show that this effect is not uniform across hosts: warmth-related facial cues—such as smiling and perceived trustworthiness—significantly amplify the impact of ratings, whereas competence-related facial cues exhibit a much weaker moderating role. These interaction effects remain robust across propensity score matching (PSM) and alternative model specifications. Theoretically, this study advances the literature on multimodal signals and complementarity in peer-to-peer (P2P) platforms, particularly by juxtaposing and analyzing the interplay among multimodal signals. In practice, the findings help hosts and the platform understand the value of profile photos, whether amplifying existing advantages or compensating for temporary setbacks in ratings.

title: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
authors: N/A

title: Experimental Determination of Biological Tissue Impedance for Electrosurgical Process
authors: Ievgenii Dziuba, Yuliia Bondarenko, Tetiana Koroliuk, Andrii Dubko, Serhii Stepenko, Oleksandr Bondarenko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The paper represents an experimental study of the biological tissue's impedance properties (phase shift and impedance character) as a load of an electrocoagulator during their electrical welding process. The experimental installation was developed for the study, which allows for changing the operating frequency of electrosurgical equipment, output power levels, and durations of the welding process. It was found that the phase shift between the current through the studied tissues and the voltage does not exceed one electrical degree at the operating frequency of electrosurgical equipment. Thus, it was determined that the character of the biological tissue impedance in the entire studied range can be considered active. This approach allows using lower-order equations for calculations, simplifying mathematical models of transistor converter circuits with biological tissue as a load.

title: Multi-Scale Convolution-Transformer for Long-Term Correlation Prediction of High-Dimensional Time Series
authors: Bofan Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This study investigates the long-term forecasting of high-dimensional time series in finance, energy, and the Industrial Internet of Things. We construct a unified forecasting framework based on the Multiscale Convolutional Transformer (MSCT) core. Using a multiscale convolutional front-end, we successfully extract local and cross-scale patterns. We employ sparse attention and low-rank approximation (Nyström/linear projection) techniques to effectively model long-range dependencies. We combine time- and variable-aware positional encoding with gating techniques to optimize both representational power and computational scalability. Based on the 321-dimensional UCI Electric Load (ECL) dataset, collected every 15 minutes over approximately 3.5 years, we develop a unified preprocessing and evaluation standard. Comparative analysis reveals that the MSCT achieves lower RMSE, MAE, and MAPE than other models, including ARIMA, VAR, 1D-CNN/TCN, LSTM, and pure Transformer. The model also achieves best-in-class performance on the intra-week seasonality retention metric. With a p95 latency of nearly 20.5 milliseconds, nearly 10.4 million parameters, and nearly 8.9 gigabytes of peak video memory, this study successfully achieved an optimal balance between efficiency and accuracy. Multi-scale convolution demonstrated significant effectiveness in suppressing redundancy and non-stationary noise in high-dimensional data. The application of sparse/low-rank attention mechanisms significantly reduced the computational cost of long sequences. Deeply coupling these two mechanisms effectively mitigated the degradation of multi-step predictions while enhancing interperiod stability.

title: SEC-Mamba: An Effective and Explainable CNN–Mamba Model for Intrusion Detection
authors: Tianning Li, Ying Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address the limited detection accuracy of traditional intrusion detection methods under large-scale and complex network traffic, this paper presents SEC-Mamba, a model integrating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with the Mamba state space model for joint feature modeling. The CNN module extracts local feature correlations, while the Mamba module efficiently captures long-range dependencies with linear computational complexity. Additionally, the Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) interpretability framework is incorporated to quantify feature contributions at both global and local levels, enhancing transparency and trustworthiness. Experiments on the UNSW-NB15 dataset demonstrate that SEC-Mamba outperforms conventional methods in Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-score, while providing reliable explanations for its predictions. The results indicate that SEC-Mamba offers an effective and explainable solution for intrusion detection in complex network environments.

title: Converter Station Disconnector Status Detection System Based on Image Object Recognition
authors: Fangbo Xing, Shuangyan Shu, Wei Wang, Ruiliang Zhang, Gang Lv, Hao Liu, Yufeng Wang, Ruiziling Xu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)

title: Comparative Study on Core Loss Prediction Models Based on Modified Empirical Method and Deep Learning
authors: Yuxin Xiao, Lingxiao Li, Mi Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Magnetic components play a crucial role in power converters, and their loss characteristics directly affecting system efficiency and power density. The traditional Steinmetz Equation (SE) exhibits limited prediction accuracy under non-sinusoidal waveforms and multi-factor conditions. This paper comparatively studies magnetic core loss prediction models based on empirical correction methods and deep learning. First, by introducing temperature terms and equivalent frequency transformation, a corrected SE model was constructed. Second, a deep learning prediction model based on BP neural networks was constructed using key features extracted from both the time domain and frequency domain. Finally, the prediction errors of the two models on the test set were compared, with results showing that the relative error of the empirical correction prediction model compared to the traditional SE equation decreased from 29.45 % to 15.36 % under sinusoidal waves, and from 30.5 % to 25.11 % under non-sinusoidal waves. The deep learning model further reduced the prediction error to below 3 %. This research on magnetic core loss provides valuable insights and can serve as a reference for the design of new energy and high-frequency power converters.

title: COSTAR: Cloud-Observed Safety and Trust-Aware Agentic Reasoning for Enterprise Workflows
authors: Sowjanya Pandruju
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Agentic AI systems are moving from lab demos to production use, yet reproducibility, observability, and policy conformance remain open problems. I present COSTAR, a cloud-observed, safety- and trust-aware agentic orchestration layer that couples a deliberative planner with a verified executor, instrumented end-to-end with standard traces and an immutable Action Ledger. COSTAR integrates with Amazon Bedrock Agents for tool-use and knowledge grounding while adding a dual-loop controller: a Deliberation Loop for plan generation and reflection, and an Execution Loop with policy checks, risk budgets, and rollback. I evaluate COSTAR on GAIA, AgentBench, and SWE-bench style tasks, plus enterprise workflows (ticket triage, CRM updates, cloud change requests). COSTAR improves task success under policy constraints while reducing unsafe actions and cost variance versus strong agentic baselines.

title: Clustering Edges Reveals How Vertices Exert Influence in Graph Data
authors: Manohar N. Murthi, Kamal Premaratne
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: How do vertices and edges collaboratively exert influence in graphs? We develop a framework for edge spectral clustering that reveals how both vertices and edges collaboratively accomplish directed influence in directed graphs. In contrast to the ubiquitous vertex clustering which groups vertices, edge clustering groups edges by assigning edges that share a functional affinity to the same cluster thus forming an influence subgraph cluster. With a complexity comparable to that of vertex clustering, this framework presents three different methods for edge spectral clustering that reveal important influence subgraphs in graph data, with each method providing different insight into directed influence processes in directed graphs. Central to the edge clustering methods are three new graph flow Laplacians that capture different directed flow processes and could potentially open new avenues in graph analysis and learning. Several synthetic and real-world examples demonstrate the potential for widespread application of edge spectral clustering in exploratory data analysis.

title: A Two-Stage Hyper-Heuristic with Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Energy-Efficient Distributed Assembly Hybrid Flow-Shop Scheduling Problem
authors: Hua-Dong Bao, Zi-Qi Zhang, Bin Qian, Rong Hu, Wei Chen, Wei-Cheng Gao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: The energy-efficient distributed assembly hybrid flow shop scheduling problem (EEDAHFSP) poses significant challenges due to the strong interdependency between production and assembly operations and the resulting combinatorial complexity. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel two-stage hyper-heuristic with multi-agent reinforcement learning (THHMA), introducing a structured collaborative framework that explicitly decomposes the problem into specialized optimization phases. In the first stage, a dedicated production scheduling agent operates within a constrained solution space to optimize manufacturing sequences, while in the second stage, an assembly-focused agent concurrently refines assembly operations based on the manufacturing outcomes. A joint coordination agent dynamically orchestrates the interaction between these specialized agents, enabling adaptive search space adjustment through a Q-learning-driven high-level strategy. Experimental evaluation on 270 benchmark instances demonstrates that THHMA significantly outperforms five state-of-the-art multi-objective algorithms, achieving superior hypervolume values with a 12.9% average improvement over the best competitor. The proposed two-stage architecture effectively balances local intensification and global exploration, providing an efficient decision-support solution for complex industrial scheduling environments that require simultaneous optimization of production efficiency and energy consumption.

title: Performance Evaluation of Statistical Time Series Models Across Different Stock Volatility Categories
authors: Louis Oktovianus, Ivan Sebastian Edbert, Andry Chowanda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This research aims to model stock prediction analysis using dataset gathered from The Yahoo Finance (i.e. daily closing prices for stocks). There models were proposed, they are ARIMA, SARIMA, and Holt-Winters. Moreover, this research focus on three types of stock categories, such as: slow moving, fast moving and stable stocks. The SMA, RSI, and volatility indicators were used to classify stocks. After data preprocessing, each model is used in a training-testing cycle to predict the next five days. The evaluation methods include MAE, RMSE, MSE, and MAPE. The results reveal that ARIMA and SARIMA achieve the most accurate prediction. For stable stocks, ARIMA achieves MAPE as low as $0.89 \%$. For slow-moving stocks, SARIMA obtained the best result with a MAPE of $\mathbf{0 . 7 0 \%}$. Meanwhile, for fast-moving stocks, SARIMA again provided the best results with a MAPE of $0.73 \%$. On the contrary, Holt-Winters showed a significantly higher error rate in all categories, especially in fast-moving stock. Even though ARIMA and SARIMA have similar accuracy rates, ARIMA is simpler and more suitable for non-seasonal data. All things considered, ARIMA and SARIMA are more reliable compared to Holt-Winters in predicting stock prices

title: Intelligent Monitoring System for Teaching Buildings Based on Multimodal Large Model: Pedestrian and Vehicle Flow Analysis and Safety Report Generation
authors: Hongyun Yang, Wenxin Zhang, Yang Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Traditional video surveillance systems mainly play a passive recording role in campus safety management and lack deep semantic understanding and proactive analysis capabilities for peak class hours. This paper proposes an intelligent surveillance system based on a multimodal large model. By integrating PTZ cameras, CogVLM2, and DeepSeek models, it constructs a ‘perception-understanding-decision’ closed loop. The system uses CogVLM2 to perform fine-grained semantic descriptions and structured storage of human and vehicle targets in teaching building areas, and leverages DeepSeek to conduct deep analysis and automatically generate reports based on natural language rules. Experiments show that the system can effectively identify human and vehicle behavior patterns and potential risks during peak class hours, generate highly readable safety reports, and achieve a transition from passive monitoring to active cognition.

title: Efficiently Pilot Design for Compressed Sensing based Channel Estimation in MIMO-OFDM systems
authors: Shenyang Xiao, Weili Yue, Chao Sun, Liang Ma, Li Li, Depin Lv, Zihao Zhao, Shihao Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Channel estimation based on compressed sensing has recently attracted considerable researches, since it enables the accurate recovery of sparse channel with fewer pilots. However, the performance of reconstruction of sparse channel is greatly bound by sensing matrix associated with pilot pattern. In order to improve channel estimation performance, optimal pilot pattern whose sensing matrix bears small mutual coherence is required. To optimize disjoint pilot patterns over a MIMO system, a pilot pattern design algorithm termed IRS is developed to optimize multiple pilot patterns. The proposed IRS jointly obtains a fixed number of pilot patterns iteratively. Therefore, the propose algorithm is able to efficiently obtain all pilot patterns. Simulation results using Rayleigh model have shown that channel estimation performance can be improved by optimized pilot patterns and the proposed method has better performance than existing algorithms in terms of both normalized mean square error and bit error rate.

title: Impact of Coupled Mesoscale Eddies on Broadband Deep-Sea Acoustic Signal Propagation
authors: Zengyang Wang, Weiming Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: The presence of mesoscale eddies in the deep ocean significantly perturbs the stability of underwater acoustic channels, posing severe challenges for long-range Underwater Acoustic Communication (UAC) and signal processing. Existing research often focuses on sound field simulation under idealized single-eddy conditions, frequently lacking in-depth analysis of complex multi-eddy coupled scenarios and broadband signal response characteristics. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an efficient computational model based on the Finite Difference Adiabatic Normal Mode (FDM-ANM) method to investigate the mechanisms by which coupled multi-eddy environments (coexisting warm and cold eddies) affect broadband deep-sea acoustic signal propagation. Computed normal mode information is utilized to synthesize the frequency-domain transfer function, subsequently obtaining the time-domain Channel Impulse Response (CIR) via Inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT). Simulation results indicate that, compared to single eddies, coupled eddy fields induce more complex distortions in the sound speed profile, significantly altering the location and intensity of deep-sea Convergence Zones (CZ). Furthermore, signal sensitivity to eddy perturbations varies drastically with frequency; high-frequency signals exhibit stronger mode coupling and attenuation characteristics. Channel multipath analysis reveals that, under specific receiver geometries, the presence of eddies can generate a "mode filtering" effect, suppressing the arrival of high-order, slow-propagating normal modes, thereby reducing the channel’s RMS delay spread. The findings of this study provide a theoretical basis for link budget assessment, frequency selection, and signal processing algorithm design for underwater communications in complex dynamic ocean environments.

title: HFPose: Hierarchical Masking and Global-Local Fusion for 3D Human Pose Estimation
authors: Xuguang Liu, Yong Wang, Wenxiu Dan
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Although monocular 3D human pose estimation has progressed substantially in recent years, it still struggles under occlusion conditions because the hierarchical motion dependencies among joints and the discriminative fusion of joint features are often overlooked. Motivated by these challenges, this paper proposes Hierarchical Masking and Global-Local Fusion for 3D Human Pose Estimation (HFPose), which introduces a Hierarchical Masking (HM) strategy and a Global-Local feature Extraction and Fusion (GLEF) module. The HM module partitions joints into three hierarchical levels according to the kinematic chain and performs randomized masking during training, thereby augmenting occlusion-related samples and enhancing spatial modeling performance under joint occlusion conditions. The GLEF module extracts both local and global joint features and introduces scalable adaptive fusion weights to mitigate the imbalance between local and global information, thereby improving the model’s ability to capture complex spatial dependencies among joints. Experiments on Human3.6M, MPI-INF-3DHP, and 3DPW datasets validate the effectiveness of HFPose. Code is available at https://github.com/Lxg-233/HFPose.

title: Efficient Probabilistic Relational Inference for Partially Observable Interacting Dynamical Systems
authors: Yi Ding, Weitao Song, Qi Tao, Xingxing Liang, Longfei Zhang, Guangquan Cheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The neural relational inference model (NRIM) [1] is powerful in capturing complex behaviors of interacting systems from complete trajectory data. However, in real-world scenarios, systems are often partially observable, making NRIM's pretrained models difficult to adapt effectively to new tasks with unseen data patterns. This work addresses the challenge of learning interacting systems from partially observed data points. We propose the Meta-learning Neural Relational Inference Model (M-NRIM), which integrates a meta-learning adaptation module for rapid generalization and a variational EM framework that optimizes a surrogate objective with convergence guarantees. Our model enables fast adaptation to unseen scenarios with partial observations and provides well-calibrated uncertainty estimates for predictions. Experimental results on synthetic physical systems demonstrate that M-NRIM achieves state-of-the-art performance in trajectory prediction, interaction inference, and uncertainty quantification, with its advantages being most pronounced under low-observability conditions.

title: LnHGN: Label and Neighborhood-Aware Heterogeneous Graph Network for Conversational Aspect Sentiment Quadruple Analysis
authors: Chao Gao, Hongxin Zhi, Lixin Ji
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Conversational Aspect-based Sentiment Quadruple Extraction refers to identify fine-grained sentiment quadruples from dialogue data, constituting an important AI application. While existing studies have proposed various methods to enhance dialogue representations, they often fall short in effectively modeling annotation labels and composite relational structures in dialogues. To address these limitations, we introduces a Label- and Neighborhood-aware Heterogeneous Graph Network (LnHGN) to better capture discourse structural information. Our framework incorporates three key innovations: (1) a node type-aware heterogeneous graph reconstruction method that fully utilizes sentiment element annotations to enhance representation learning by reducing intra-class node distances; (2) a neighborhood-aware stacked Simplified Graph Convolution (SGC) model that adaptively weights multi-order meta-paths to effectively aggregate multi-hop relational information while avoiding noise and limited expressiveness from manual meta-path design; and (3) a Focal Loss optimization strategy to mitigate severe class imbalance in entity and relation classification caused by sparse sentiment annotations. The experimental results on two benchmark datasets substantiate the effectiveness of the proposed LnHGN, achieving average improvements of 1.43% and 1.56% respectively over state-of-the-art baselines.

title: Study of Steady State Current Equalization Method for Silicon Carbide MOSFET Devices
authors: Xiaoyan Zhao, Yong Chen, Xu Cheng, Yanqun Liao
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: In this paper, a comprehensive approach integrating multi-physical field coupling analysis and dynamic compensation mechanism is proposed for the steady-state current equalization problem in parallel operation of silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFET devices. It is shown that the on-resistance of SiC MOSFETs can reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 2. 4 \%}$</tex> relative polarity within a batch, and the differences in power loop parasitic parameters (e.g., drain parasitic inductance, source resistance) can significantly exacerbate the unbalanced current distribution, which may lead to localized overheating and reliability risks. The traditional current equalization strategy is difficult to balance between dynamic response and steady-state accuracy. In this paper, by establishing a mathematical model of the nonlinear characteristics of on-resistance coupled with parasitic parameters, we reveal its influence law on current distribution. On this basis, a segmented dynamic compensation algorithm is designed, which combines the driving resistance adjustment with the parasitic parameter optimization strategy, and realizes the balanced regulation under the wide-current condition <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(10 ~\mathrm{A} \sim 100 ~\mathrm{A})$</tex> by matching the eigenvalue of the on-resistance of the device and the branch parasitic resistance in real time. Simulation and experimental verification show that the method significantly reduces the maximum current imbalance from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6. 7 \%}$</tex> without compensation to less than 5%, and is both theoretically innovative and engineering practical. The results provide a key theoretical support and technical path for the efficient and reliable parallel application of SiC MOSFETs, which is of great significance for improving the power density and operational reliability of new energy power systems.

title: Research on Precise Programmable and Reliable Rapid Release/Control Technology for New Energy Storage Based on Chemical Kinetics
authors: Shuya Tan, Ruyi Li, Zhiyuan Lu, Yunzhi Dai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of global energy storage capacity, nearly 70 safety accidents related to energy storage systems have occurred worldwide, involving a total project scale exceeding 4 GWh. The main types of energy storage safety accidents include fires, explosions, and toxic gas releases, with fires being the most common accidents, typically caused by thermal runaway. These fires are characterized by high combustion temperatures, long durations, easy rekindling, and the potential production of flammable and toxic gases (such as hydrogen, carbon monoxide, hydrogen fluoride, etc.). This paper proposes a novel precise programmable and reliable rapid release and control fire suppression device based on chemical kinetics. The system integrates a multi-sensor composite fire detection device and a non-pressure storage solid gas generator fire extinguishing device, capable of rapidly, precisely, and repeatedly releasing clean fire extinguishing agents (perfluorohexanone) to effectively suppress thermal runaway fires within battery packs. Experimental results show that the system can extinguish open flames within seconds after the first discharge, with no rekindling within 24 hours, and can effectively prevent thermal runaway from spreading to adjacent cells. This technology exhibits significant advantages in terms of reliability, environmental adaptability, maintenance-free longevity, and multi-level (pack level, cluster level, cabin level) protection in various energy storage scenarios.

title: EE-YOLO: Improved YOLOv8n for Small Tobacco-Plant Detection
authors: Zhike Wang, Guowei Li, An Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: To address the challenges of detecting and counting tobacco plants during different growth stages—particularly the difficulties in identifying small, dense, and severely missed seedlings in UAV imagery, this study proposes an improved YOLOv8n-based detection method named EE-YOLO. The method incorporates three core innovations: (1) an MSCAM module that integrates multi-scale convolution and attention mechanisms is added to the backbone; by dynamically adjusting channel and spatial weights, MSCAM enhances feature extraction under complex backgrounds; (2) in the detection head, the C2f structure is fused with an Efficient Multi-scale Attention (EMA) module, and through channel–batch recombination and cross-dimensional interaction the model captures richer global information, leading to stronger feature representation; (3) the Wise-IoU (WIoU) loss is employed, whose non-monotonic focusing mechanism guides the model to pay more attention to medium- and low-quality anchor boxes. Under a lightweight design, EE-YOLO outperforms the original YOLOv8n on a tobacco-plant detection dataset, achieving a 1.5% improvement in precision and a 0.5% gain in mAP. On the public C2A- person dataset, precision and mAP further increase by 1% and 0.2%, respectively. These results demonstrate that, compared with one-stage detectors in the YOLO family, the proposed method provides clear advantages in tobacco-plant detection tasks.

title: Adaptive Droop Control Based on SoC Deviation for Communication-Free Battery Balancing in DC Microgrids
authors: Moh. Salahudin, Trias Andromeda, Iwan Setiawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Direct Current (DC) microgrids with Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and renewable sources help improve overall efficiency and reliability. A challenge is State of Charge (SoC) imbalance among parallel batteries, causing unequal current sharing and accelerating degradation. Conventional droop control enables decentralized power sharing without communication but overlooks SoC variation, leading to suboptimal battery use. This paper proposes an adaptive droop control method based on SoC deviation to achieve proportional current distribution without communication. The approach adjusts each battery’s droop resistance according to its deviation from the average SoC. Higher SoC batteries deliver more power when discharging and absorb more current when charging, while lower SoC units are protected from excessive stress. The method was validated in MATLAB/Simulink using two lead acid batteries under charging and discharging. Results show that the SoC difference decreases from 10 percent to less than 2 percent, current sharing error drops from 40 percent to below 5 percent, and the DC bus voltage remains within ±1 percent of the ${7 2 ~ V}$ reference. Results confirm improved SoC balancing, current sharing, and voltage regulation, providing a practical, communication free solution for BESS management in islanded DC microgrids.

title: Research on Compilation Option Setting Method Based on Bayesian Optimization
authors: Leidong Zhao, Pingfei Cui, Kai Nie, Haoran Li, Mengyao Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Selecting appropriate compilation options based on different program structures to enhance program execution efficiency remains a pivotal issue in the research of compilation optimization techniques. To address this challenge, we propose autoOPT, a compilation option selection method based on Bayesian optimization. This method constructs a surrogate model using random forests to characterize the mapping relationship between compilation option combinations and program execution efficiency. It selects the lower confidence bound as the acquisition function and designs a mechanism for dynamically adjusting the hyperparameter kappa, ensuring that the algorithm possesses high diversity exploration capabilities in the initial stage and can accelerate convergence to the optimal solution in the later stage. In the GCC8.3 compiler environment, extensive experiments were conducted on the PolyBench benchmark suite. The results show that compared to compiler -O3 optimization, autoOPT achieves a speedup of 1.10x on the x86 architecture platform, 1.67x on the Sunway 831 processor platform, and a speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.55 x$</tex> on the Sunway 3231 processor platform, validating the effectiveness and applicability of autoOPT on both x86 and Sunway architectures. The autoOPT enhances the quality of selecting effective compilation optimization options and significantly boosts the program execution efficiency on high-performance computing platforms.

title: M-TSINR: Multiscale Implicit Neural Representations via Mamba Encoder for Time Series Anomaly Detection
authors: Ke Liu, Mengxuan Li, Qianqian Shen, Yang Gao, Haishuai Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Time series anomaly detection is a critical task in various domains, including industrial monitoring, healthcare, and finance. Existing time series anomaly detection methods typically rely on discrete grid representations to store and process data, which can lead to issues in capturing the true nature of continuous time series signals. Recently, some researchers use Implicit Neural Representation (INR) to model continuous functions, offering a more natural approach for learning and representing real-world continuous time series signals. However, current INR-based anomaly detection methods suffer from limited generalization and suboptimal performance and efficiency. To address these challenges, we propose M-TSINR, a novel framework that leverages the strengths of INR to improve both the performance and efficiency of time series anomaly detection. Specifically, we design a form of multiscale INR continuous functions, utilizing multiple neural networks to represent different temporal scales of the data, enabling the model to extract hierarchical features from fine-grained to coarse patterns. Additionally, we use a Mamba encoder to generate INR parameters, which significantly improves both the efficiency and the ability to capture complex temporal dependencies. Extensive experiments conducted on five real-world anomaly detection benchmark datasets demonstrate that M-TSINR achieves superior performance compared to other state-of-the-art anomaly detection methods.

title: CREC: A Cross-Domain, Zero-Shot Framework for Real-Time Chinese Named-Entity Correction
authors: Ting Jiang, Ming Song, Xihui Chen, Linjiang Zhang, Tianjie Zhuang, Qingqiu Zhang, Jiang Zheng, Xiaodong Zou, Yu Su
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Spoken queries in Chinese vertical domains remain highly susceptible to cascading errors caused by automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems, particularly on rare proper nouns. However, existing correction pipelines lack not only public benchmarks but also lightweight mechanisms capable of operating on resource-constrained edge devices-where such errors are most critical to address due to limited connectivity and real-time requirements. Moreover, dictionary- and confusion-set-based approaches incur high maintenance overhead, rely on superficial string matching, and suffer from time complexity that grows linearly-or worse-with lexicon size. To address this, we propose an end-to-end plug-and-play framework that achieves industrial-grade accuracy without requiring GPU support or model retraining, making it specifically suitable for deployment on edge computing platforms. First, we curate the Cultural Relic Entity (CRE) dataset, comprising 9,025 entity entries, and augment it with entity instances extracted from CLUENER2020 to construct a new open-domain test set. Together, these form the largest cross-domain benchmark to date for Chinese Spelling Correction (CSC), spanning 11 distinct domains. Second, we introduce Hybrid-Weighted Whole-word Proper-noun Matching (H-WWPM), a zero-shot post-processing method that integrates multi-granularity dynamic windows with joint pinyin-character similarity scoring and an exact-pinyin reward mechanism. This approach corrects a 10 -word query in under 30 ms on a single CPU core, enabling real-time inference at the extreme edge. Experimental results demonstrate that H-WWPM achieves an average sentence-level F 1 score of 70.64% across all domains. When cascaded with existing models such as KenLM, MacBERT, Qwen3-4B, or FASPell, it improves F1 by 59.9% while introducing only minimal additional latency in a distributed micro-service architecture. This work illustrates a cost-effective and horizontally scalable approach to deploying domain-specific CSC across the cloud-edge continuum.

title: Tabular Data into Images techniques for CNN to enhance Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Zahia Benamor, Zianou Ahmed Seghir, Mounir Hemam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: In recent years, the fields of computer networks, the internet, and the Internet of Things (IoT) have experienced rapid advancements. This progress, however, has been accompanied by a proliferation of cyber threats, presenting significant challenges to maintaining security. Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) serve as a critical defense layer to counter the growing number of these cyber threats and attacks. Consequently, the integration of Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) into IDS has become imperative as traditional IDSs struggle to handle the diversity of attacks. While DL models, particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), demonstrate powerful performance with image data, their application is challenged by the inherently tabular nature of network traffic data. This mismatch often leads to suboptimal IDS performance because DL models struggle to process tabular data effectively due to the lack of inherent spatial relationships. Therefore, converting tabular data into image representations has emerged as a promising solution. This paper aims to investigate existing techniques for this transformation within the IDS domain. Our primary goal is to provide researchers with a thorough overview that clarifies current methodologies and enables them to develop innovative, effective techniques to improve Intrusion Detection Systems.

title: Design of a Configurable Multifunctional Systolic Array Based on FPGA
authors: Boyao Shi, Shaosheng Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a configurable universal matrix computation unit based on FP16, achieving a unified integrated design for one-dimensional convolution, correlation, and matrix multiplication. The unit employs a systolic array architecture overall, adapting to diverse computational demands through different PE unit structures and configurable input matrix or vector dimensions. This approach maximizes resource efficiency while maintaining accuracy and throughput. Simulation results demonstrate that the designed unit can perform correlation, convolution, and matrix multiplication operations.

title: Research on Super-resolution Localization Algorithm Based on Shark-MUSIC Algorithm
authors: Jiapeng Zhao, Siyuan Tang, Nannan Liu, Zhi Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: In the future, with the advancement of deep learning, multimodal fusion, and edge computing technologies, super-resolution localization technology will play a core role in many important fields. The research on the super-resolution localization algorithm in this paper aims to better improve the environmental perception ability of sensors. Facing the classical super-resolution localization scenarios and aiming at the problems of low localization resolution and poor accuracy, this paper proposes a super-resolution localization algorithm based on the improved Shark-MUSIC algorithm. It effectively improves the localization accuracy. Experiments prove the effectiveness of the model in this paper, and the super-resolution localization accuracy reaches 98%. This research has important practical significance for the development of multimodal-fusion-based super-resolution localization.

title: A Low-Profile UWB Antenna for GPR
authors: Chen Liang, Shijie Xu, Yue Zhang, Jisheng Tong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In this paper, a low-profile ultra-wideband (UWB) antenna is proposed for ground-penetrating radar (GPR) applications. The antenna is inspired by the coupled modified electrically narrow very low-profile (ENVELOP) antenna and realized by integrating a shorting plate with the ground plane. Two identical TEM horn sections are arranged in a mirror-symmetric configuration between parallel metallic plates, which significantly reduces the ground size and improves the directivity. The proposed antenna achieves a wide impedance bandwidth of 340–<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 7 0 ~ M H z} (\left\vert \mathrm{S}_{\text{1 1}}\right \vert<-\text{1 0 ~ d B} )$</tex> with a compact overall size of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 3 8} \times \text{3 1 4} \times 160 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex>. The maximum realized gain reaches 8.18 dBi, and the radiation efficiency remains above 90 % over the entire band. When the antenna is placed 10 cm above sandy soil, it still maintains good impedance matching and exhibits a clean time-domain response without long-duration ringing.

title: W-Band Carbon Nanotube Switched-Line Phase Shifter MMIC for Phased Array Applications
authors: Murong Zhuo, Hongrong Qiu, Simin He, Defu Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: This paper presents a W-band 180<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup> switched-line phase shifter that uses aligned carbon nanotube (A-CNT) Schottky-diode pairs as selectors between two coplanar-waveguide (CPW) transmission lines. An A-CNT Schottky diode with a 130 nm channel length was fabricated on high-resistivity silicon and exhibits an ON-state current of 4.2 mA at -1.5 V. The extracted small-signal model was embedded in circuit/EM co-design to realize a 1-bit phase shifter occupying approximately 0.2 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The measured results show good agreement with simulation: the phase shift reaches 178<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup> at 90 GHz and remains within 180°±10° over 86–101 GHz, while the amplitude imbalance between the 0° and 180° states stays within ±1 dB from 86 to 98 GHz, corresponding to RMS amplitude and phase errors of about 1 dB and 5<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup>, respectively, over this band. These results demonstrate that CNT-based RF switching networks are promising candidates for W-band beamforming applications.

title: Developing Interactive and Immersive Studying Environments Using AI and Game Engine
authors: Ayana Akutsu, Xiang Li
abstract: In recent years, new productivity tools have gained attention. They include not only basic tools to support works such as to-do lists and timers, but also novel features such as virtual avatars and immersive 2D or 3D environments imitate the inside of a real room. However, as these applications are only been launched recently, their potential and effectiveness are not clear, and they need further research and development. This study focuses on developing an effective virtual application that combines conventional features with unique enhancements. By introducing new elements, the virtual application aims to provide a unique and effective solution for improving productivity and user satisfaction. While some challenges remain, the findings of this study suggest that these new productivity applications have significant potential for improvement and development. However, further research and analysis are needed to fully understand the impact of them on productivity and to improve their design and implementation.

title: A Compact and Lightweight Astronaut Assistance Rover with Robust Validation
authors: Md. Ragib Aunjum, Ali Ahsan, Zareef Jafar, S. M. Masrur Ahmed, Razin Bin Issa, Md. Mahbub Ali, KM Fahim Mahmud, Mohammad Zahirul Islam, Zaber Mohammad, Gazi Musa Al Johan, Md. Saiful Islam, Md. Golam Rabiul Alam, Md. Khalilur Rhaman
abstract: Research on planetary rovers has been active for a decade. Planetary rovers are usually designed for surf landing sites on other planets. Rovers with multiple functionalities make conducting interplanetary explorations in outer space practically possible. Most state-of-the-art interplanetary rovers are 6-wheelers and have difficulty performing a 360-degree rotation. In this research, the design and implementation process of a compact 4-wheeler rover named Mongol-Tori with robust capabilities has been approached which can be applied for the exploration of an unknown planet. Bearing in mind that the weight and volume increase the expenditure in space research, a lightweight modified suspension system has been proposed. An effective electronics system increases the run time and helps the driver of the rover to estimate the mission timeline in teleoperation mode. The vigorous design allows the onboard manipulator to complete any assistance task smoothly.

title: CIT: Context Interaction Transformer for Micro-Expression Recognition in Natural Scene
authors: Haiwen Liang, Jiateng Liu, Yaonan Wang, Hengcan Shi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Micro-expression recognition (MER) holds significant importance in the field of affective computing, due to its extensive applications in medical diagnosis, lie detection, and criminal investigation. However, the existing MER models usually focus on laboratory scenes with fixed views, single illumination and limited subjects, while they often fail for natural scene MER, due to complex lighting and environmental information. To address these challenges, in this paper, we propose a Context Interaction Transformer (CIT) that integrates environmental contextual information into an optical-flo-based transformer framework to more accurately recognize micro-expressions in natural scenes. This model extracts environmental semantics using a pretrained vision model and fuses them with facial motion features through a cross-attention mechanism, and thus leverages rich environmental context to help MER. Moreover, we present an optical flow sparse sampling strategy that selects the most representative motion tokens based on both the environment and facial motion information. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our CIT.

title: ClimateNet: A Multi-Faceted Classifier for Analyzing Climate Change Discourse
authors: Tustee Mazumdar, Adrika Chowdhury, Abu Nowshed Chy, Tarem Ahmed
abstract: Climate change is a pressing global issue, sparking widespread discourse on social media. Understanding public sentiments, stances, humor, and hatred is vital for policymakers and researchers in capturing diverse viewpoints on climaterelated issues. However, limited studies have examined the multiple dimensions of climate change discourse. To address this issue, we leverage recent NLP techniques for analyzing this discourse through classifying social media data across different dimensions. We present ClimateNet, a hybrid neural network model that uses various transformer architectures with sequential learning through BiLSTM and a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) classifier. In experiments on a Twitter benchmark dataset, we observed that ClimateNet (DistilBERT) shows significant improvements over the current state-of-the-art. We also perform the ablation study to validate the contribution of each component of our model. By providing deeper insights into online climate discourse, this research aligns with the SDG-13 (Climate Action) goal in promoting sustainability.

title: Lightweight Breast Histopathological Classification Model Based on Dynamic Multi-Scale Selection
authors: Gaiyan Hong, Wenzhu Peng
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Whole slide image (WSI) analysis is crucial for breast cancer diagnosis, yet its large scale and high computational complexity pose significant challenges. Existing methods typically require fine-grained annotations and involve heavy models that are difficult to deploy clinically. To address this, we propose LightMIL, a lightweight multiple instance learning framework that classifies whole slides using only patch-level features without pixel-wise annotation. Central to LightMIL is a Dynamic Multi-scale Selection (DMS) module, which adaptively integrates features across scales based on semantic content, reducing background noise and highlighting discriminative regions. LightMIL substantially reduces parameters by 30-55% and accelerates inference by 40-60% compared to similar lightweight models, while maintaining strong accuracy.On public WSI datasets, LightMIL achieves 94.2% accuracy (AUC 0.968) with a compact size of 3.2 MB, demonstrating a superior balance of efficiency and performance. This work advances fast, accurate pathological image analysis and offers a practical, deployable solution for low-resource clinical settings, aligning with the pursuit of efficient and applicable AI in medical imaging.

title: Retrieving Relevant Metaverses using Hierarchical Features

title: A Simple Polarization-Diversity Demodulation System Design for Simultaneous Wireless Power and Information Transfer
authors: Jun-Hui Ou, Yuxin Chen, Zhengda Chen, Zhi-Xia Du, Peiming Wang, Bo Zhang, Zhenwen Gui, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Conventional simultaneous wireless information and power transfer methods demodulate the received signal at RF frequency. This part of the RF power can no longer be rectified, which causes a power transfer efficiency deterioration. To overcome this problem, a simple systematic configuration is proposed in this article. The system consists of TX and RX parts. By mapping the communication symbol “1” and “0” into two orthogonal polarizations of the TX antenna, the information can be modulated through the switching between polarization. At RX side, a dual-polarized RX antenna delivers the power to a dual-branch rectifier. By sampling and comparing the output DC voltage of the two branches, the signal can be demodulated, and the power conversion efficiency (PCE) can be maintained high owing to the ultralow-power composition of the sampling components. In the measurement, 64.7% optimal PCE is found at 7 dBm, and a degradation of less than 1% in efficiency is found when receiving the modulated signal with 9600 bps baud rate. With a simple comparator, data rate can reach 2 Mb/s with a bit error rate (BER) of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.4^{\ast } 10^{-3}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. At the TX part, the switching loss brought by the modulation is found 0.5 dB. The whole system is concise and compact. The proposed design is a good candidate for applications that need highly efficient power transmission and reliable communication with lower data rate, such as Internet-of-Things systems.

title: Time-Dependent Breakdown in Schottky p-GaN Gate HEMTs Under Dynamic Gate Stress
authors: M. Millesimo, C. Fiegna, S. Bevilacqua, A. Russo, A. N. Tallarico
abstract: This article presents a comprehensive investigation of the time-dependent gate breakdown (TDGB) behavior in Schottky p-GaN gate high-electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) under dynamic gate stress (DGS). Experiments are carried out over a wide frequency range (10 kHz–1 MHz) and temperature interval ( $30~^{\circ }$ C– $150~^{\circ }$ C). In addition to analyzing the impact of the positive gate voltage level (V ${}_{\text {GSH}}\text {)}$ , the influence of the negative gate voltage level (V ${}_{\text {GSL}}\text {)}$ , applied during the device turn-off phase, is also investigated. This analysis provides guidelines for defining gate-drive conditions that minimize the risk of TDGB and offers physics-based insight into the underlying degradation mechanisms. For temperatures above $60~^{\circ }$ C, the equivalent time-to-failure (ETTF) exhibits a nonmonotonic dependence on switching frequency, attributed to two distinct degradation mechanisms. Mechanism A, dominant at relatively low frequencies, is associated with the time-dependent defect generation duringon-state semiperiods. Mechanism B, prevailing at higher frequencies, arises from electrostatic potential transients occurring in the semifloating p-GaN layer during off-to-on transitions. The value of VGSL modulates Mechanism B, affecting ETTF at high frequency and temperature. A temperature-dependent analysis reveals that the stronger thermal activation of Mechanism A shifts the transition between the two regimes toward higher frequencies as temperature decreases, eventually suppressing the contribution of Mechanism B to TDGB at room temperature within the explored frequency range.

title: An Improved Quadratic Function Negative Definiteness Lemma for the Stabilization of Nonlinear Cyber–Physical Systems With Actuator Faults
authors: Gomathi Dhanabal, Harshavarthini Shanmugam
abstract: This article deals with the stabilization of nonlinear cyber–physical systems (CPS) subject to actuator faults via the fault-tolerant control (FTC) algorithm. First, to tackle the time-varying delays taken into the system, we proposed a novel quadratic function negative determination lemma, which derives the sufficient conditions for the corresponding quadratic polynomials arising in the derivatives of Lyapunov–Krasovskii functional (LKF). For this purpose, we are parting the time-delay intervals into uniformly equal subintervals and the tangents intersection is carried out in the region of each partitioned intervals. Thereafter, from the cross-points of tangents in each subinterval and choosing a freely adjustable parameter within the delay bounds, we attained a novel quadratic function negative definiteness conditions which profits with high system performance. By means of Lyapunov stability theory (LST), the sufficient conditions are derived in the form of linear matrix inequalities that ensure the asymptotic stabilization of the addressed system. Finally, numerical simulations including the realm of autonomous ground vehicle (AGV) problem are performed, and the comparative analysis in the line of negative-definite (ND) lemmas is exhibited to showcase the efficacy of the proposed approach (PA).

title: Zentropy-Enhanced Multigranularity Knowledge Modeling for Robust Feature Selection
authors: Kehua Yuan, Duoqian Miao, Witold Pedrycz, Yiyu Yao
abstract: Multigranularity knowledge modeling is an influential study for information processing and knowledge discovery in artificial intelligence (AI). A central research focus is the multigranularity representation and learning of knowledge structures. Among them, fuzzy rough sets (FRSs) have emerged as a representative method for characterizing uncertain knowledge. However, the existing FRS studies still exhibit two limitations: low robustness in knowledge acquisition and incomplete characterization of uncertainty. Hence, this article proposes a zentropy-enhanced multigranularity knowledge modeling framework for robust feature selection (ZeMG-FS). Specifically, we design a fast and adaptive multigranularity information granulation mechanism based on generalized granular-ball generation to effectively capture data distributions embedded in complex data. Then, the fuzzy rough approximation method is incorporated into the representation of multigranularity knowledge. Furthermore, we analyze the fundamental relationships and structures of the multigranularity knowledge model to introduce a novel multilevel zentropy. Unlike existing entropy measures, the primary consideration of the proposed zentropy is to match and enhance the performance of the proposed model. Finally, we design two feature evaluation criteria grounded in the model and apply them to feature selection. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed methods achieve superior robustness and effectiveness compared with state-of-the-art approaches.

title: A Compact D-Band Power Amplifier Using an Enhanced Slow Wave Structure With 16.2-dBm Output Power and 16.4% Peak PAE
authors: Zeyu Wu, Li Gao, Kaixu Wang, Wen Rao, Xiu Yin Zhang, Qinyu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a fully integrated D-band power amplifier (PA) with high output power and high efficiency, utilizing a two-way power combining technique and implemented in 28-nm bulk CMOS. An enhanced slow wave coplanar waveguide (ES-CPW) proposed in this article can achieve a higher phase constant and lower attenuation constant compared to conventional slow wave coplanar waveguides (S-CPWs), enabling simultaneous device miniaturization and performance enhancement. Based on this structure, a power combiner (PC) is designed to reduce the insertion loss while enhancing performance and minimizing the chip area, which achieves a 0.25-dB insertion loss with broadband operation and compact size. The PA measured results show a 3-dB bandwidth of 18.3 GHz from 122.3 to 140.6GHz. In addition, this PA can achieve a peak gain of 18.5dB, 16.2-dBm saturated output power (Psat), and a peak power-added efficiency (PAE) of 16.4% at 130 GHz. This allows the PA to achieve an average output power of 9.1 (6.5) dBm under 64-QAM (256QAM) modulation with a data rate of 45 Gb/s. To the best of the authors’ knowledge, the proposed PA realized the highest PAE, the widest power bandwidth (1-dB bandwidth), and the maximum data rate in CMOS technology and a similar operating band. Meanwhile, the total chip area is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.32\times 0.31$</tex-math> </inline-formula>mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.
