title: An Unconditionally Stable Stochastic CDI-FDTD Method for Electromagnetic Wave Propagation in Media with Uncertain Material Properties
authors: Guoda Xie, Xiang Wu, Guilin Hou, Gang Wang, Xingang Ren, Yingsong Li, Xianliang Wu, Zhixiang Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: The electromagnetic parameters of practical materials are inherently susceptible to stochastic fluctuations arising from individual variability and environmental perturbations, thereby introducing uncertainty into the electromagnetic response of complex systems. To comprehensively capture such randomness, a stochastic complying-divergence implicit finite-difference time-domain (S-CDI-FDTD) scheme is developed, in which the statistical Delta method is systematically incorporated to quantify and propagate parameter-induced uncertainties. This approach enables the simultaneous acquisition of both the mean and variance of EM fields through a single simulation, thus providing an efficient framework for modeling EM systems involving random media. Numerical analyses confirm that the proposed method strictly adheres to Gauss’s divergence law while maintaining unconditional stability. Several numerical examples further verify the accuracy and effectiveness of the S-CDI-FDTD scheme for EM simulations in random media.

title: A Geographical Information-Based Reflective Collaborative False Data Filtering Scheme in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Zhixiong Liu
abstract: Wireless sensor networks (WSNs) are highly vulnerable to reflective collaborative false data injection attacks (RC-FDIA), where adversaries manipulate legitimate nodes to forward false reports, leading to amplified energy consumption and hidden attack sources. Existing schemes (e.g., geographical-based false data filtering scheme (GFFS)) lack targeted detection for reflective nodes, failing to counter this threat. This paper proposes geographical information based reflective collaborative false data filtering (GRFFS), a lightweight filtering scheme that integrates a four-layer verification mechanism: format, geographical location legitimacy, reflective behavior (targeting reflective nodes), and message authentication code (MAC)/key consistency verification. Theoretical analysis and multi-scenario experiments show GRFFS outperforms state-of-the-art schemes (RC-FDIA filtering rate >50%) with 37% higher compromise tolerance, 29% lower energy consumption, and 1.25 KB optimized storage, well-suited for resource-constrained WSNs under attacks.

title: Resilient Consensus Control of Nonlinear Multiagent Systems Under Hybrid Cyberattacks: A Disturbance Observer-Based Neural Network Approach
authors: Huiyan Zhang, Yu Huang, Ning Zhao, Xuan Qiu, Enrique Herrera-Viedma, Ramesh K. Agarwal
abstract: This article proposes a novel observer-based adaptive neural network-based resilient consensus control approach to address hybrid cyberattacks, disturbances, and nonlinear dynamics in nonlinear leader-following multiagent systems (MASs). Specifically, a dimension expansion methodology is developed to dynamically model and compensate for false data injection (FDI) attacks, while denial-of-service (DoS) attacks are probabilistically characterized via Bernoulli variables, forming a comprehensive hybrid attack mitigation strategy. Then, a cascaded observer is designed, integrating dimension-extended system modeling with disturbance decoupling to simultaneously estimate system states and external disturbances with high precision. Furthermore, an adaptive neural network-based approximation scheme is employed to handle system nonlinearities, eliminating the conservatism of Lipschitz-based methods while enhancing robustness in complex environments. Finally, the simulation result validates that the proposed control method achieves resilient consensus of leader-following MASs under hybrid cyberattacks, disturbances, and nonlinear dynamics.

title: A Predefined-Time Robust Neural Dynamics Controller for Projective Synchronization of Second-Order Chaotic Systems and Its Application
authors: Linju Li, Lin Xiao, Qiuyue Zuo
abstract: Given the high coupling of state variables in second-order chaotic systems, the projective synchronization control schemes for first- or fractional-order chaotic systems are not applicable in second-order chaotic systems. Also, the current control schemes on second-order chaotic systems are difficult to tradeoff between convergence and robustness. To address the above issues, this article develops a predefined-time robust neural dynamics controller (PTRNDC). First, a predefined-time nonsingular terminal sliding mode variable (PTNTSMV) is designed to control the coupling of errors in the projective synchronization of second-order chaotic systems, ensuring the nonsingularity and convergence. Hence, a predefined-time double-integral zeroing neural dynamics (ZNDs) design formula based on a time-base generator (TBG) is devised to ensure that the sliding mode variable attains the desired sliding mode surface swiftly and robustly. The theorems about the stability, convergence, and robustness of the projective synchronization under the PTRNDC are analyzed rigorously, and the comparative simulations further verify the effectiveness of the PTRNDC. In addition, the chaotic sequences generated by the projective synchronization between the second-order chaotic systems are successfully applied in the image encryption, making the original image possess excellent visual distortion.

title: Shunt-controlled resistive state of superconducting wires
authors: K. Harrabi, Z. Alzoubi, L. R. Cadorim, M. V. Milošević
abstract: The use of resistive shunts in superconducting electronics is vast and versatile, to dampen oscillations in junctions, stabilize switching behavior, aid current sensing, divert current during quenches, and protect both the superconductor and the circuit from damage. In single-photon detection by superconducting nanowires, the shunt is crucial for the timely relaxation of the sensor between the events to detect. Here we step out from the superconducting state and discuss the effect of the shunt resistor on the resistive state of a superconducting wire, at elevated currents still below the critical current for the transition to the normal state. We reveal how the shunt resistance controls the system dynamics and the onset of different resistive phases that include hot-spot and phase-slippage events. The accompanying dynamic current redistribution in the circuit also affects the local heating properties and additionally contributes to the control of the resistive state, particularly important at the elevated operation temperatures.

title: A Dual-Branch Temporal Deep-Supervised Framework for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Jingxuan Xu, Lirong Qiu, Ning Shen, Hao Shen, Peifu Liu, Jianan Li
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: In remote sensing applications, change detection serves as a pivotal technology to monitor changes on the land surface by identifying and extracting temporal discrepancies in bitemporal images captured over the same geographical area. Although existing deep learning methods can extract temporal difference features from bitemporal images, these methods often suffer from the attenuation of feature pair distances during long-range encoding and decoding, leading to weakened deep supervision signals. To address this core challenge, this study proposes a pioneering dual-branch temporal deep supervision network, termed DTDNet. The framework employs two independent branches—a front-end semantic branch and a back-end feature mining branch—to achieve multi-dimensional coupling of temporal change features. The former focuses on precise extraction of temporal difference semantics, while the latter is dedicated to effective mining of deep features from bitemporal images. Furthermore, a feature-enhancing Attention Module (FEAM) is innovatively introduced to capture long-range dependencies among token features and learn distance mappings between bitemporal feature pairs, significantly enhancing feature discriminability. Extensive experiments on the MACD, CDD, and SYSU-CD datasets demonstrate that DTDNet achieves breakthrough performance across multiple metrics, substantially advancing the state of the art in change detection technology.

title: Design of a Wound Rotor Brushless Doubly-Fed Machine with Even Pole-Pair Ratio Using Superposition Method
authors: Weidong Pan, Xianfei Xie, Junlong Yang, Kexun Yu, Kaihua Long, Xi Chen
abstract: TThe brushless doubly-fed machine (BDFM) is gaining attention as an attractive topology for variable-speed applications, particularly in renewable energy and transportation, largely due to its high reliability achieved by eliminating rotor slip rings. Among the various rotor types, the wound rotor BDFM offers significant advantages, such as a flexible design and inherently lower space harmonic content. Its rotor winding (RW) plays a critical role in electromechanical energy conversion by enabling interaction between two stator magnetic fields with different pole-pair. However, the conventional ampere-conductor method used for RW design is unsuitable for BDFMs with an even pole-pair ratio, as this method fails to form integral coils due to the unequal number of positive and negative conductors in each phase. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a superposition method that combines two complementary winding schemes, both derived from the ampere-conductor wave method, to form integral coils. First, the fundamental principles and limitations of the ampere-conductor wave method are explained. Then, the superposition method is elaborated, using a wound rotor BDFM with a 2/4 pole-pair combination as an example. Subsequently, a finite element (FE) model of the machine is developed, and its electromagnetic performances are analyzed. Finally, a prototype is manufactured and tested to verify the proposed design scheme.

title:
H
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Optimal Parametric Reduced-Order Model for Fast and Accurate Thermal Analysis of Power Modules Under Varying Cooling Boundaries
authors: Hao Yin, Chong Zhu, Tenghui Dong, Lianbo Yang, Xi Zhang
abstract: Precise temperature monitoring is crucial for ensuring reliability in high-power energy conversion systems, particularly those utilizing power modules. Lumped parameter thermal network (LPTN) model is limited by oversimplification, complex identification and boundary correlation, while finite element method (FEM) is too complicated for practical application. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a parametric model order reduction (PMOR) framework based on matrix projection interpolation (MPI). After extracting the state space representation from a high-fidelity FE model, a Krylov subspace projection with an iterative eigenvalue scheme is employed to identify optimal frequency expansion points, achieving an H2-optimal reduced model. A subspace reference basis projection further aligns reduced-order state variables under varied convective heat transfer boundaries, allowing smooth matrix interpolation without repeated model recalibration. As implemented on a commercial half-bridge power module, the proposed PMOR reduces the system order from 10340 to 10 with experimental temperature prediction errors within 1.99%, while requiring only 0.005% of the original computation time. Comparative experiments under different convective heat transfer boundaries and power losses validate the method's effectiveness and highlight its utility for thermal management in power modules.

title: Nash Equilibrium in Sustainable Finance: Designing a Game-Theoretic DSS for Compliance-Aligned Portfolio Optimization
authors: Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum, Khandaker Mohammad Mohi Uddin, Chanda Rani Debi, Ramona Birău, Virgil Popescu, Md. Abul Kalam Azad
abstract: This work introduces a complete computational framework that combines game theory, sustainability metrics, and regulatory compliance into a unified decision support system for portfolio design. Traditional Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) investing often fails to adequately consider mandatory compliance, which we conceptualize as ESG Compliance (ESGC),an indicator of the degree to which a company meets sustainability standards and reporting regulations. To bridge the gap between financial maximization and regulatory demand, we propose a Game-Theoretic ESG Decision Support System (DSS), which captures the strategic interplay between three distinct investor archetypes: retail, institutional, and regulatory. The workflow encompasses rigorous data cleaning, feature engineering, and game-theoretic optimization based on Nash Equilibrium using a multi-region panel of 4,837 firms.Our empirical results demonstrate that the Game-Theoretic strategy achieves a mean annual return of 25.83% (representing a 117.14% cumulative return over the study period) and a Sharpe Ratio of 1.1623, significantly outperforming the standard Markowitz Mean-Variance benchmark (Sharpe: 0.9652). Furthermore, the framework maintains superior sustainability alignment with a Mean ESG score of 72.51 and a Mean ESGC score of 79.23. When adjusted for sustainability quality, the model generates an ESG-Adjusted Sharpe Ratio of 2.0051, outperforming the ESG-Floor MVO benchmark (1.69). This framework provides a robust tool that integrates financial performance, sustainability, and policy into a single, mathematically rigorous decision-making environment.

title: Efficient Intelligent Early Bearing Fault Detection with Frequency of High Frequency Band Time-Frequency Amplitudes
authors: Xhemajl Mehmeti, Bertan Karahoda
abstract: Bearings are fundamental components of rotating machinery and, due to their wide range of uses, early detection of bearing faults is crucial. In the early stages of a fault, the fault-related components in the vibrational signals are very weak and are often suppressed by other components, making early fault detection a rather complex process. When degradation starts, high-frequency impulsive and transient components typically appear in the vibrational signals at periodic intervals. We detect these components in the early stages of the fault by using the Fourier Transform of high-frequency band Time-Frequency amplitudes. The Time-Frequency amplitudes in the high-frequency band are obtained by the Window Function Shape Independent Short-Time Fourier Transform, and Wavelet Transform, and fault-related features are extracted using the Fourier Transform of these amplitudes. Threshold-based approaches are generally used to detect the onset of degradation. We use Support Vector Machines to achieve optimal separation between healthy and faulty bearing frequency features, and detect faults in the early stages without using thresholds. The proposed model is trained and tested with publicly available run-to-failure experiment bearing vibration signal datasets IMS and XJTU-SY. The model is trained with features derived from all faulty bearing signals from both datasets, and faults are detected efficiently in the early stages, regardless of bearing structure, rotational speed, load, and fault type.

title: Efficient Cooperative Trajectory Planning Using Cognitive-Based Risk Models for Multi-Agent Systems in Non-Convex Environments
authors: Yage Guo, Fanghua Bai, Zhonghao Bai
abstract: This letter focuses on the multi-agent trajectory planning (MATP) problem for car-like intelligent agents operating in high-density unstructured environments and structured scenarios (e.g., urban roads) containing heterogeneous traffic participants. The primary challenge in addressing such problems is obtaining a globally optimal solution rapidly while ensuring safety. The nonlinear kinematic constraints of vehicles, along with the collision constraints that scale with scenario complexity, significantly increase the computational difficulty. Furthermore, the quality of homotopy solutions critically impacts the feasibility of the final solution. To tackle these issues, we propose a hierarchical search and centralized optimization (HSCO) method. Initially, a priority-based search algorithm is employed to rapidly generate an initial guess, enhanced by a time-priority collision pair selection strategy that significantly improves search efficiency. Subsequently, we introduce a novel higher-order warped Gaussian collision risk model integrated with a driver perception field model, effectively reduces the potential collision risk caused by tracking performance uncertainty. Finally, the original MATP problem is linearized into a coupled convex quadratic programming formulation. The proposed collision risk model is seamlessly integrated into the separation plane of potential collision pairs, enabling efficient iterative solution processes that ensure safety, feasibility, and optimality. Experimental results demonstrate that HSCO outperforms existing MATP algorithms in large-scale unstructured scenarios. Moreover, in structured scenarios involving heterogeneous traffic participants, HSCO rapidly generates safe and optimal cooperative trajectories.

title: Evaluation Index System for New Type of Power System
authors: Guangyu He, Yong Deng, Xiang Yu, Renke Huang, Shuai Fan, Zuyi Li, Zeyu Zhang, Yuanbo Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: The evaluation index system for the new-type power system (NTPS) is the basis for scientifically assessing the development level of the NTPS, guiding its construction, development and performance, and reflecting the important contribution of the NTPS in achieving the energy ‘dual carbon’ target. This article first summarizes the essential requirements of the national ‘dual carbon’ strategic goal for the NTPS, which provide sustainable in time, scalable in space, and diversified electricity services, elaborate the key requirements of the NTPS. Next, the article proposes the basic principles to design the evaluation index system for the NTPS and divides the NTPS evaluation index system into core value index set, strategic index set, and capability index set based on three perspectives: core demands, endogenous demands, and capability demands of the NTPS. The article also identifies the primary indicators for the three sets as cleanness, low-carbon, safety, quality, efficiency, structure, flexibility, and synergy index. Then, the secondary indicators for evaluating the NTPS are constructed, the definition, role, calculation method of the secondary indicators are also given. The evaluation index system put forward features several advantages: it fully encompasses the key attributes of the NTPS, has a distinct hierarchical nature, exhibits clear correlations, and is simple to apply.

title: Projection of Temperature Change in Busan City Under Shared Socioeconomic Pathway Scenarios (SSPs)
authors: Ismail Robbani, Jurng-Jae Yee
abstract: This study uses Shared Socioeconomic Pathway (SSP) scenarios and several CMIP6 climate models to evaluate future temperature variations in Busan, South Korea. According to model evaluation, MPI-ESM1-2-LR is the most appropriate for the area. To increase spatial and temporal resolution, statistical downscaling and bias correction techniques such as quantile mapping and the delta approach were used. Under SSP2-4.5, high-resolution predictions suggest a minor cooling tendency in summer temperatures and a considerable warming trend in winters by 2050, with rises of up to 7° C over 2010. There are relatively slight local temperature fluctuations across Busan, according to spatial research. In addition to offering insightful information that helps policymakers and urban planners create successful climate adaptation plans for coastal cities, these findings emphasize the significance of careful model selection and downscaling for accurate urban climate forecasts.

title: Intelligent Route Planning and Collision Avoidance Decision Optimization Based on Transformer and Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Liangchuan Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address the insufficient robustness of long- and short-range perception and collision avoidance decision-making in multi-vessel environments, this paper employs the Transformer-SAC(Soft Actor-Critic) algorithm. This method utilizes a Transformer with a combination of relative position and time encoding to perform a multi-scale spatiotemporal representation of historical trajectories, generating multimodal short-term position predictions and uncertainty covariances. These predictions are then used as state inputs to the SAC continuous control strategy, which outputs heading angular rate and velocity corrections. Training is stabilized through a dual-Q network, temperature adaptation, and experience replay, while a control barrier function is used for minimal intervention safety corrections. Experimental validation using high-fidelity simulation and AIS replay demonstrates that collision rates are only 2.5% and 2.9% under a moderate traffic density (30 vessels/<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{k m}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>) and a 3-second observation delay, with a single-step inference latency of 9.5 ms, improving the robustness of environmental perception and collision avoidance.

title: Calculation of the Optimal Parameters of the Electrical Apparatus for the Irrigation System
authors: Oleksiy Sadovoy, Dmytro Koshkin
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: A current problem in irrigated agriculture is the use of electrical apparatus of a certain class and power. Key factors in selecting electrical equipment for sprinklers include minimizing mass and volume, ensuring moisture protection, and maximizing reliability, efficiency, and operational longevity. Optimizing the design of electrical apparatus can enhance these performance indicators. The paper presents a mathematical model for optimizing a general industrial electromagnetic system based on a comparative analysis method. This method is based on controlled geometric variables, that are independent of electrical parameters. The indicator of the objective function and the ratio of the controlled variables have been obtained. The verification of the mathematical model for finding the optimal parameters of the armour of the electrical apparatus for the sprinkler machine was carried out. The physical models of the electromagnetic systems were constructed according to the geometric dimensions of the real and optimized magnetic starter on the basis of the software solution. The results of the comparative analysis of the distribution of magnetic induction over time were obtained, which allowed the theoretical confirmation of the optimally calculated parameters according to the minimum mass criterion.

title: Development of a Model Device for Induction Heating of Samples to Improve the Performance of Electron Beam Coating
authors: Artemii Bernatskyi, Mykola Sokolovskyi, Taras Nabok, Oleksandr Siora, Volodymyr Lukashenko, Oleh Strelko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: Currently, large number of transformers is installed outdoors with minimal or no protection from elements. At present, the windings of distribution transformers are made from aluminum. Due to the fact that connections of these transformers to communication devices is made using bolt connections, atmospheric moisture can cause corrosion of the aluminum windings at the point of contact. Consequently, transient resistance at the contact point is increased, causing additional heating, which in turn can lead to failure of the distribution transformer. To prevent this, an additional coating of the contact surfaces of the electrical leads is used. The most appropriate method is electron beam coating. However, conventional methods of electron beam coating provide very thin coatings and are time-consuming. To increase the productivity of the sputtering process, the material of the sample to be coated is subjected to preheating. For this purpose, an oscillator system for controlling power transistors and a method for adjusting the oscillating circuit to obtain maximum heating power were developed. Over the course of the study, a model device was created and calibrated for operation in a myriad of operating modes. In result, it was established that it is possible to maintain the maximum heating power of the sample during heating by changing the supply voltage of its LC-oscillator.

title: A Dual-Stage Denoising Framework for EEG Signals Using Enhanced RetNet and Bidirectional GRU
authors: Haiyang Tang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) signals are susceptible to interference from physiological artifacts such as electrooculography (EOG) and electromyography (EMG) signals. Artifact suppression is crucial for ensuring the reliability of disease diagnosis and EEG-based downstream applications, including braincomputer interfaces (BCIs). To address this challenge, this study proposes RGNet, a novel EEG denoising framework consisting of three key modules: a global temporal pattern extractor based on an improved RetNet and Transformer self-attention mechanism, a bidirectional GRU network for modeling the dynamics of contextual signals, and a dual-domain feature extraction mechanism that synergistically processes the time-frequency features of artifacts. By integrating the parallel computing efficiency of RetNet and the sequence modeling advantages of the bidirectional GRU, this framework demonstrates balanced performance in handling both long-term and short-term dependencies. Experiments conducted on the EEGDenoiseNet dataset show that RGNet exhibits superior interference suppression capability compared to five baseline models. It consistently outperforms baseline models across all evaluation metrics. The results confirm that the combination of time-frequency feature fusion and multi-scale temporal modeling can effectively overcome the performance bottlenecks in complex bioelectrical signal processing. This study establishes a new paradigm for robust EEG analysis and holds significant importance for improving the accuracy of neurological disease diagnosis and enhancing the signal reliability of BCI systems.

title: Efficient Estimation of Battery Degradation Bounds from Cycling Data for Use in Planning Tools
authors: David Braese, Alexander Headley, David Rosewater
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Advanced energy storage systems (ESS) are vital for grid stability, peak shaving, and load shifting as power systems change over time. However, evaluating the technoeconomic viability of ESS is complex and often excludes battery degradation, which significantly impacts both lifespan and long-term costeffectiveness. Sandia National Laboratories’ QuESt 2.0 tool assists with ESS analysis but currently lacks integrated degradation modeling. This study introduces a computationally efficient method to incorporate battery degradation into technoeconomic analyses by leveraging real-world cycling data from BatteryArchive.org. The proposed model estimates capacity loss and efficiency decline, enabling more accurate performance forecasting. In addition, this work introduces a percentile band derived from pointwise degradation rates which are used to provide uncertainty bounds around the degradation prediction. These envelopes aid in displaying variability in the expected degradation over time to communicate the range of plausible degradation outcomes and to improve the representation of prediction. This enhancement supports better investment decisions in energy storage technologies.

title: Shielding Plates for Magnetic Pulse Processing of Welded Joints of Non-Magnetic Metal Sheets
authors: Oleksii Karlov, Ihor Kondratenko, Andrii Zhyltsov, Roman Kryshchuk
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: To improve the mechanical properties of welded joints of non-magnetic metal sheets, magnetic pulse processing can be applied. As a result of such processing, an electroplastic effect arises in welded joints. Magnetic pulse processing is based on the local action of a high-density (10^8 A/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) pulse eddy current in a welded joint. A spiral flat inductor is used for magnetic pulse processing. The idea of this study is to place a welded joint of non-magnetic sheet metal under the inductor on a shielding metal plate to achieve an increase in the localized pulse eddy current density in the welded joint. The aim of this work is to investigate the influence of shielding metal plates made of various metals on improving the electromagnetic, energy, and mechanical characteristics when creating a magnetoplastic effect in the welded joint of thin-sheet aluminum alloy. Numerical modeling of the time-varying pulse electromagnetic field using the finite element method is employed for the study. The dimensions and other parameters of the inductor that can be used for processing welded joints of aluminum sheets are proposed. The current in the inductor winding is calculated by jointly solving the magnetic field and electrical circuit equations for discrete time intervals using various parameters of the shielding metal plates under the welded joints. The power source is a capacitor charged to a certain specified voltage. To study the influence of shielding metal plates on currents and magnetic forces in the welded joint, the same amplitude voltage value in the power source capacitor is used. The transient processes of the current density amplitude and the electromagnetic force amplitude in the welded joint area of aluminum sheet with shielding metal plates with different parameters are investigated.

title: Domain Adaptation for Synthetic Bolt Data Generation in Complex Environments
authors: Haibo Liu, Guozhong Cheng, Lufeng Wang, Yan Zeng, Long Li, Wenbin Liu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Bolted connections are widely used in industrial manufacturing and structural steel construction. Efficient and intelligent recognition of bolt components is crucial for achieving smart manufacturing and intelligent construction. However, the scarcity of relevant datasets for intelligent recognition of bolt components in complex scenarios currently hampers related research. Therefore, this study employs domain randomization to parameterize and generate synthetic data across multiple key metrics, including surface shape, surface material, scene layout, lighting conditions, and camera pose. Concurrently, domain adaptation techniques are integrated to eliminate domain differences between synthetic and real data. Experimental results demonstrate that this approach can synthesize data at a rate of 10 frames per second while effectively improving network recognition accuracy by 10%.

title: Research on Remote Operation and Maintenance Methods for Optical Measurement Systems Based on DC Centralized Control
authors: Ning Yu, Wenxin Feng, Junjie Kan, Liwen Zheng, Hao Kiu, Fangbo Xing, Na Yao, Xinrui Chen
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: This paper first introduces the application structure of optical measurement systems in DC centralized control scenarios. It analyzes the operational conditions encountered during the digital and intelligent application of optical measurement system O&M in engineering fieldwork. Subsequently, it summarizes fault diagnosis methods for DC centralized control optical measurement systems based on practical engineering challenges. This provides significant reference value for the subsequent digital and intelligent transformation of measurement systems. This paper addresses the demand for intelligent operation and maintenance in China's southern AC/DC hybrid power grids under the context of building a new power system. It explores the application and fault diagnosis methods of optical measurement systems within DC centralized control frameworks. The article analyzes issues in traditional DC centralized control systems, such as low monitoring efficiency, reliance on experience for fault localization, and lagging emergency response strategies. It proposes an optical measurement system architecture tailored for DC centralized control scenarios, along with a remote operation and maintenance platform, enabling real-time panoramic visualization monitoring of equipment operational status. It highlights key functional modules within the optical measurement system - including the sensing fiber optic ring, sensors, acquisition units, consolidation units, and remote O&M security gateways - while summarizing the performance and impacts of current/voltage transformers under typical faults such as saturation, overvoltage, and partial discharge based on practical engineering challenges. Building upon this foundation, a system solution integrating multiple sensor types (e.g., current/voltage temperature sensors, partial discharge detection sensors) and intelligent early warning modules is proposed. Utilizing a backpropagation (BP) neural network-based algorithm, the system predicts and alerts on measurement system health, enabling self-calibration of transformer measurement data, insulation condition assessment, and remote operation and maintenance support. This system offers significant advantages in enhancing measurement accuracy, enabling remote channel switching, and strengthening equipment condition assessment and fault diagnosis capabilities. It provides a feasible technical pathway and engineering reference for the digital and intelligent transformation of DC centralized optical measurement systems.

title: Research on Fatigue Driving Detection Algorithm Based on PL-YOLO
authors: Yihao Wu, Yang Xue, Qiuhong Lu, ChangCai
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: This study addresses the issue of insufficient recognition accuracy in existing fatigue driving detection models under different feature scales by proposing an improved PL-YOLO fatigue driving detection algorithm based on the YOLO11 model. The optimization strategy of this method primarily involves two aspects: first, a multi-scale gradient attention module (MSGA) was designed to enhance the model's ability to extract feature information at different scales, thereby improving its adaptability under various environmental conditions; Second, a dynamic weighted fusion loss function is designed to combine the advantages of EIoU and WIoU, thereby accelerating model convergence and improving target localization accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that PL-YOLO achieves superior detection performance and robustness in fatigue driving detection tasks, with recognition accuracy reaching 97.5%, representing a 6.9% improvement over the unmodified model, effectively enhancing model performance.

title: Stress Detection Using Multimodal Feature Engineering and Ensemble Learning
authors: V.Gowri Priyaa, B. Bhuvaneshwari, V. Sowmya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Stress affects physical and mental health and overall well-being. Accurate detection is important because stress influences outcomes in education, work, and healthcare. Many existing methods rely on a single source (sensors or surveys) and face issues such as limited feature diversity and class imbalance. This paper presents a lightweight machine-learning framework for stress detection using multimodal data: physiological signals, smartphone usage, sleep quality, and personality traits. Interaction-based features are created through feature engineering, and class imbalance is addressed with Adaptive Synthetic Sampling (ADASYN). Predictive performance is improved using ensemble classifiers, specifically CatBoost and XGBoost. Validation on two separate datasets demonstrates that the method attains classification accuracies of up to 99%, outperforming conventional sensor-based techniques that typically achieve 97%. The framework is designed to be scalable and generalizable for real-world mentalhealth monitoring applications.

title: Dynamic Data Encryption Based on Endogenous Security for Mimic Defense
authors: Bo Zhang, Chuan He, Fuzhong Hao, Yufan Wang, Zhuo Lv, Jinghong Lan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: The rapid evolution of networked environments has exposed static encryption schemes to increasing risks of targeted attacks. To address this limitation, this work applies the endogenous security framework to the design of a dynamic data encryption model for mimic defense. The model integrates multiple heterogeneous encryption algorithms, including DES, 3DES, , and RC4, within a dynamic reconstruction architecture. Three algorithm selection strategies—completely random, manually weighted, and normal distribution–based—are designed to realize dynamic and uncertain encryption switching. An experimental system implemented in Unity simulates encrypted data transmission under different selection strategies. Results from 300 transmission rounds show that the normal distribution–based algorithm achieves an average defense failure rate below 10%, compared with approximately 23% for the random mode and 21% for the manual weighting mode. The findings demonstrate that endogenous security combined with mimic defense effectively enhances data protection by introducing algorithmic diversity and dynamic reconfiguration, achieving higher robustness without prior knowledge of threats.

title: Relatively Robust QoS and QoE Score Aggregation
authors: Thomas A. Weber
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: We present a robust methodology for aggregating Quality of Service (QoS) and Quality of Experience (QoE) scores across multiple criteria in settings where the relative importance of individual metrics (i.e., weights) is unknown or ambiguous. Building on recent advances in relatively robust multicriteria decision theory, we define a Robust Aggregate Score (RAS) based on the worst-case performance ratio with respect to all admissible weight configurations. The method is weight-agnostic, invariant to rescaling of individual metrics, and accommodates criteria with both positive and negative preference direction. We illustrate the approach in the context of video-streaming service evaluation and demonstrate how it enables interpretable and defensible rankings across heterogeneous quality indicators. The proposed framework is broadly applicable to quality assessment tasks that require reliability under weight uncertainty.

title: Novel Ventilation Design and CFD Modelling Validation of an Isolation Operating Theatre in Hong Kong
authors: Angel H.N. Chen, Victor W.K. Wan, Max L.Y. Tang, Ivan K.N. Lee, Melody Y.T. Wong, Sam W.S. Lai
abstract: Active ultra-clean ventilation control plays a vital role in eschewing the opportunistic deleterious risks flowing inward from the surroundings in quintessential specialised care environments, such as the Operating Theatre (OT). In modern OT design, transverse laminar airflow supply is applied to move air in a one-way direction through the clean zone, thereby protecting hospital staff from infection. Following the COVID-19 epidemic, a propaedeutic was initiated to research a discreet ventilation design for an isolation OT in Hong Kong, materialising the influence on (i) infectious protection for clinical staff in the OT and (ii) controlling outward contagious airflow from the OT to general areas during surgical operations in pandemic scenarios. In this paper, a case study of the aforementioned design is exemplified in a local physical conversion for a renovated OT at Pamela Youde Nethersole Eastern Hospital (PYNEH) in Hong Kong. Stringent pressure differentials between the clean zone and the dirty zone are required, along with sub-atmospheric Anterooms and Preparation Room for air-lock purposes. Unlike the conventional design, safeguarding true laminar airflow in an isolation OT may be challenging due to the considerable pressure gradient between a positively pressurised operating room and negatively depressurised air-lock chambers. Accordingly, the aim of this paper is to (i) introduce this novel ventilation design; (ii) conduct a scientific analysis of laminar airflow performance; and (iii) explore the technical feasibility of significant pressure differentials between a highly positive OT and negative ancillary facilities, utilising computational fluid dynamics (CFD) modelling techniques in the early design stage. Adopting a result-oriented approach, the simulation results validated the on-site compliance of this pioneering design with actual renovated conditions, demonstrating the efficacy of CFD's modelling in both design and construction stages. Furthermore, the verification of actual laminar flow performance and the testing results of the pressure gradient are elucidated in this technical paper.

title: Development of a Multi-Channel Image Acquisition Card Using FPGA
authors: Kaige Zhang, Bo Zhang, Linjie Miao, Yusong Mu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: This paper designs an FPGA-based multi-channel image acquisition card for efficient target feature extraction. The system realizes synchronous acquisition of RGB, UV, NIR three-channel CameraLink images, integrating dynamic resolution compression (supporting 1392x1040@15Hz et al.), auto-exposure (AE) and auto-focus (AF) functions. With Xilinx Kintex-7 FPGA as the core and DDR3 SDRAM-based three-frame buffering, it enhances data throughput significantly. Embedded AE/AF algorithms optimize imaging quality in complex environments, and processed data is transmitted to the host via USB3.0 for display and storage. Experimental results show the system features high stability, high frame rate and low latency, with synchronization error <1 pixel clock cycle and USB3.0 bandwidth >3.0GB/s, fully satisfying the stringent requirements of high-speed multispectral image acquisition and real-time processing in industrial inspection.

title: Design and Simulation of a Full-Bridge MMC-Based DC De-Icing Device
authors: Lingmeng Fan, Fuchun Zhang, Tianyu Li, Huazan Zhong, Yanfei Li, Junjie Feng, Guicai Li, Qingming Xin
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional half-bridge modular multilevel converter (MMC) DC deicing devices—namely, narrow DC voltage regulation range, significant inrush current during start-up, and poor harmonic adaptability to the grid—this study first constructs a novel full-bridge submodule (FBSM)-based MMC topology. The circuit structure, mathematical modeling, and operational principles are elaborated. Subsequently, a comprehensive control strategy is designed, incorporating a dq-axis decoupled controller, a DC current controller, modulation and capacitor voltage balancing schemes, and a resonant controller-based harmonic compensation strategy. Parameter design for the de-icing device is then performed. Finally, an electromagnetic transient (EMT) model is developed in MATLAB/Simulink for multi-scenario simulation validation. Simulation results demonstrate that the full-bridge MMC de-icing device achieves wide-range continuous DC voltage regulation, enabling adaptation to transmission lines of varying lengths. A zero-current-impact start-up process is realized. With harmonic suppression enabled, the grid-side current total harmonic distortion (THD) is significantly reduced, indicating effective harmonic compensation. The proposed device overcomes the voltage output limitations inherent in half-bridge topologies. By integrating advanced control and harmonic compensation strategies, it effectively resolves the three major challenges of voltage adaptability, start-up inrush current, and grid interference, providing reliable technical support for power grid security under extreme ice disasters.

title: Optimizing Tropical Mine Dewatering Using IoT-Enabled Hybrid Diesel-Solar PV Pumping with Real-Time Data Integration
authors: Miftahul Fadillah, Erkata Yandri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Open-pit mining in tropical regions requires continuous dewatering to ensure operational safety, yet conventional diesel-based pumping systems are energy-intensive, costly, and unsustainable. To address this challenge, this paper proposes an Internet of Things (IoT)-enabled hybrid diesel-solar photovoltaic (PV) pumping system integrated with realtime monitoring, automated control, and cloud-based communication. A rainfall-driven simulation is developed to evaluate system performance under low, medium, and extreme inflow conditions, using fieldreferenced parameters for pump load and site-specific hydrology. Monthly irradiance values of 2.1-2.9 $\mathrm{kWh} / \mathrm{m}^{2} /$ day (annual mean $2.56 \mathrm{kWh} / \mathrm{m}^{2} /$ day) were applied as the solar input. Results show that the hybrid configuration eliminates diesel use for low and medium inflows, while under extreme inflow, annual diesel consumption is reduced to $\approx \mathbf{8, 3 0 0 ~} \mathrm{L}$ compared with $\approx 8,950 \mathrm{~L}$ for a diesel-only baseline, yielding $\approx 7 \%$ fuel savings. The system achieves renewable energy fractions of $\approx 52 \%$ (low), $17 \%$ (medium), and $7 \%$ (extreme) and maintains monthly photovoltaic contributions of 60$75 \%$ even during variable rainfall. Beyond system modeling, the study incorporates a data-engineering pipeline that enables sensor data acquisition, storage, and visualization, with potential integration of artificial intelligence (AI) for predictive scheduling and anomaly detection. This framework demonstrates the role of applied AI and data engineering in advancing sustainable water-energy management in mining, aligning with Industry 5.0 principles and the broader goals of sustainable development.

title: Multi-Physical Simulation of the Valve Hall of the Offshore DC Converter Platform
authors: Cheng Peng, Junyang Zhang, Chunhua Li, Yijing Chen, Xu Sun, Xiaojiang Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: The VSC-HVDC technology has emerged as the preferred solution for large-scale transmission of offshore wind power generation due to its flexibility, controllability, adaptability, and environmental friendliness. As a core component of VSC-HVDC, the lightweight design of the offshore converter platform plays a crucial role in determining the economic viability of VSC-HVDC. However, high-voltage and large-capacity offshore converter platforms involve numerous internal components and complex spatial distributions of multiphysical fields, posing significant challenges to achieving compact and lightweight designs. This paper aims to deeply explore the distribution laws of multi-physical fields and their influencing factors of the offshore converter platform through multi-physical field simulation technology, provide theoretical support for research in related fields, and offer references for the future design of offshore converter platforms.

title: A GIS-Driven Digital Twin Approach to Enhance Urban Planning Regulation Compliance
authors: Muhammad Fattahulazizan Jaman, P. Ravi Kumar, Uditha Ratnayake, Nor Zainah Siau
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Urban planning enforcement in Brunei Darussalam faces challenges in ensuring building compliance with zoning regulations, height restrictions, and setbacks. This study presents a GIS-driven Digital Twin framework that integrates UAV LiDAR data, cadastral parcels, and planning regulations to improve compliance monitoring. UAV LiDAR data acquisition was carried out with permission from the Town and Country Planning Department, over two selected sites: Lot 338 (Serasa) and Lot 12209 (Sungai Belukut). Data processing included point cloud classification, DEMs/DSMs generation, GCP accuracy validation, and 3D building extraction. An overview of compliance analysis was conducted on building heights, setbacks, and zoning overlays. The results demonstrate sub-decimeter accuracy (RMSE 0.009 m at Serasa; 0.024 m at Sungai Belukut), successful 3D multipatch building modeling, and the identification of planning regulation violations. These technical findings were complemented by stakeholder surveys ($N=27$), which confirmed broad support and strong feasibility for adopting Digital Twin approaches in Brunei’s planning system. This showcases the potential of Digital Twin frameworks utilizing UAV LiDAR and GIS-based methods to enhance urban planning enforcement in Brunei.

title: Parameter Design of DC Transformer based on LC Resonance Principle
authors: Huayong Zhang, Cong Ma, Guanping Jiang, Yu Wang, Yuanmeng Wang, Wengang Xie
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: For photovoltaic-rich areas such as sandy wastelands and deep seas, photovoltaic DC collection schemes can effectively solve the problems of large line losses and low system efficiency in traditional AC schemes. It is an effective solution for efficient photovoltaic collection and long-distance transmission. This article focuses on the core equipment of the DC collection scheme, the DC transformer, and conducts relevant technical research. Firstly, the working principle of the LC resonant DC transformer is introduced. Then, the parameter design methods of its core components, including isolation transformers, support capacitors, resonant circuits, are theoretically analyzed. Finally, based on circuit simulation software, the theoretical calculation results are simulated and verified. The simulation results are basically consistent with the theoretical calculation values, verifying the rationality of the parameter design, and providing a basis for the selection and design of subsequent large-capacity DC transformer devices.

title: Fusing Dense Connections into Hierarchical Transformer for Efficient Image Super-Resolution
authors: Zhongze Luo, Shengling Geng, Mingcong Dang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Lightweight Single Image Super-Resolution methods based on Transformers have made significant progress in recent years. However, they are still constrained by two core bottlenecks: first, the prevalent "information bottleneck" in deep networks, where the progressive degradation of feature information limits the ability to restore high-frequency details; and second, the use of fixed Window-based Self-Attention for efficiency, which restricts the model’s perception of global context and affects the structural integrity of reconstructed images. To address these dual challenges, this paper proposes a new model named Fusing Dense Connections into Hierarchical Transformer(FDCHiT), a novel and efficient SISR model. This model fuses two advanced concepts: it is built upon the Hierarchical Transformer for Super-Resolution backbone, which performs well in efficient receptive field expansion, and integrates the core idea of Dense-Residual Connections from Dense-Residual-Connected Transformer, a method designed to stabilize information flow. Specifically, the model utilizes Hierarchical Windows and a linear-complexity Spatial-Channel Correlation attention mechanism to efficiently construct a multi-scale, large receptive field. To achieve an efficient and adaptive fusion of these densely connected features, we propose a novel "Ultra-Lightweight Gated Fusion"(ULGF) mechanism. Inspired by the conditional modulation concept of Spatially-Adaptive normalization and the efficient spatial attention mechanism of the Convolutional Block Attention Module, the ULGF mechanism utilizes the current feature as a guidance signal to efficiently generate a spatial gate map. This map spatially and adaptively modulates and fuses the historical feature flow, thereby alleviating the information bottleneck. Furthermore, we integrate an Efficient Channel Attention module after the MLP layer within each basic Transformer block. It recalibrates channel-wise features via efficient local cross-channel interaction, refining the feature representation with negligible parameter increase. Experimental results on several benchmark datasets demonstrate that FDCHiT achieves competitive performance with computational overhead comparable to state-of-the-art methods, reconstructing high-quality images with fine textures and sharp edges, thus validating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Students' Perception of Using VR Technology to Improve Electrical and Power Engineering Education
authors: Olena Kuzminska, Mariia Mazorchuk, Svitlana Makarevych, Andrii Petrenko, Viacheslav Osadchyi
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The paper is devoted to the development of the use of interactive simulations through virtual reality (VR) to form the professional skills of future specialists in the field of energy. The results of survey conducted among undergraduate and graduate students (no significant difference was found between the identified groups of respondents) Educational and Research Institute of Energy, Automation and Energy Saving were proved the effectiveness of the use of VR technologies for the formation of professional competencies of future specialists in the field of energy during their studies at the university. The importance of pedagogical design of educational activities using VR technologies is confirmed. In particular, identification of needs are related to the complication or impossibility of developing professional skills in the classrooms of the University, including in the absence of an appropriate energy safety admission group, and the organization of the educational process and appropriate pedagogical support.

title: An Implementation Method of a Ultra-High Speed Variable Block Code Data Encoding and Decoding Structure
authors: Qiang Zhang, Qi Shan, Zhenyu Song
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the increase of satellite communication data, the modem in the ground application system is required to have a higher codec rate and communication capacity. This paper proposes a method for implementing an ultra-high speed variable block code data encoding and decoding structure, which supports serial frame input of correlated ultra-high speed block code data, completes ultra-high speed parallel encoding and decoding, and achieves independent and stable output of ultra-high speed data serial frames. The method proposed in this paper is applicable to FPGA based ultra-high speed variable block code data encoding and decoding, compatible with various block codes such as LDPC, RS, BCH, and multi rate encoding and decoding; Adopting a parallel structure processing approach to rapidly improve the modem throughput of satellite ground application systems; The software calculates the number of parallel loop modules for highspeed data caching, drives the loading of corresponding FPGA bitstreams, quickly reconstructs FPGA encoding and decoding structures, flexibly allocates resources, and improves resource utilization.

title: Research on English Large Language Model for Aviation Professionals Based on Lora Fine-Tuning
authors: Zhiyong Fan, Yalie Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This text addresses the challenges of the scarce existing public datasets in aviation and the high cost of fine-tuning, and studies a lightweight fine-tuning method for a large language model in aviation English, achieving a knowledge question-andanswer model based on aviation English standards centered around ASD-STE100 ISSUE 9. Methods: Dataset construction involves designing prompts to guide Deepseek-r1 in generating question-and-answer pairs related to ASD-STE100 aviation professional English standards; Model selection involves using three general large models for Lora fine-tuning on the constructed dataset, evaluating to select the best model and verifying the results of multiple versions of quantization. Results: The finetuned indexes of BLEU, ROUGE-1 and ROUGE-2 of Qwen2.5-7B are increased by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13.7 \%, 25.6 \%$</tex> and 21.5 %, respectively compared with the base model Conclusion: Our model, built upon a custom dataset, accurately interprets and utilizes aviation English concepts like STE100 to answer user queries. It requires lower fine-tuning costs and computational resources than comparable models, facilitating knowledge dissemination in aviation manuals and advancing the intelligent development of China's aviation industry.

title: Polyphase Sequences with Flexible Zero-Ambiguity-Zone Configurations for Integrated Sensing and Communications
authors: Gangsan Kim, Hong-Yeop Song, Guang Gong
abstract: paper develops the theory and constructions of polyphase zero-ambiguity-zone (ZAZ) sequences for ISAC waveforms, enabling the ZAZ shape to be designed over delay–Doppler regions of interest and supporting flexible (including multi-mode) sensing–communication operation. We first prove that a polyphase sequence with an optimal rectangular auto-ZAZ must be a member of some uncorrelated optimal ZCZ sequence family, and conversely, any member of an uncorrelated optimal ZCZ sequence family has an optimal rectangular auto-ZAZ (Theorems 1, 2 and 3). We generalize the optimality condition on the rectangular ZAZ to that on centrally symmetric convex ZAZs in general (Theorem 4). We propose some constructions of families of polyphase sequences with a strictly or asymptotically optimal rectangular ZAZ (Theorem 1), dual asymptotically optimal rectangular ZAZs (Theorems 5 and 6), and asymptotically optimal rhombic or hexagonal ZAZ (Remarks 5 and 6) from the flexible ZAZ configuration

title: A Physics-Informed Neural Network-Based Scalable Model for GaN HEMTs
authors: Haorui Luo, Zuojun Wang, Yongxin Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: The integration of physical knowledge into artificial intelligence (AI)-based models for gallium nitride high electron mobility transistor (GaN HEMT) modeling is gaining increasing attention, as it reduces the amount of required training data, enhances the model’s physical plausibility, and contributes to the development of explainable AI. In this article, a physics-informed neural network (PINN)-based scalable GaN HEMT model is proposed. The model is trained with channel charge densities as fitting targets, while physical consistency is ensured by incorporating the drain current and gate capacitances into the PINN’s physical constraints. The model architecture and training algorithm are described in detail. The proposed PINN-based GaN HEMT modeling method achieves a high degree of automation in model development, while ensuring excellent prediction accuracy and physical plausibility. The significant self-heating and trapping effects in GaN HEMTs are also embedded into the proposed model. These features contribute to the model’s inherent and strong scalability. The developed GaN HEMT models of various sizes have undergone extensive small-signal and large-signal validation, demonstrating the model’s high accuracy and strong scalability.

title: Impact of MESA Isolation and Passivation on -GaO MOSFET Performance_supp1-3663299.pdf
authors: Saurabh Lodha
abstract: This work presents the electrical characterization of six <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> MOSFET variants designed to address device isolation and leakage challenges. Planar devices without isolation exhibited excessive <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small>-state leakage, with <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>-/<sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small>-current (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\mathrm {ON} }/{I}_{\mathrm {OFF} }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) ratios below 10, primarily due to leakage through the metal–insulator–semiconductor heterostructure formed beneath the gate pad and through the extended active areas. Introducing MESA isolation improved the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\mathrm {ON} }/{I}_{\mathrm {OFF} }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> ratio to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 10^{{3}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> by suppressing these leakage paths. However, plasma-induced sidewall damage during MESA etching introduced new traps, limiting further improvements. To mitigate this, sidewall passivation with a thick (200 nm) sputtered SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> layer was implemented, reducing leakage and enhancing the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\mathrm {ON} }/{I}_{\mathrm {OFF} }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> ratio to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 10^{{7}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. In summary, leakage through the dielectric under the gate pad and from the extended active areas was identified as key degradation mechanisms. These were confirmed through the measurement of two-terminal (source-gate/drain-gate) and transistor output characteristics, which showed suppressed leakage in MESA-etched and sidewall-passivated devices. Among all six variants, the structure with the gate pad on the insulating substrate and passivated MESA sidewalls emerged as the most promising. Additional improvements may be achieved by integrating post-MESA etch annealing and field plates in the fabrication flow for enhanced passivation and electric field management, respectively.
