title: A Review of the Application Research of Artificial Intelligence Technology in Nuclear Power Detection and Operation
authors: Guangxin Zhi, Yunying Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the continuous development of the nuclear power industry and the increasing requirements for safety and reliability, ensuring the safe operation of nuclear plants and efficient inspection and maintenance has become crucial. This paper elaborates on the numerous challenges and key issues faced in nuclear power inspection and maintenance, focusing ons in artificial intelligence technology, particularly in data analysis, pattern recognition, machine learning, deep learning, computer vision, and automated control. It analyzes the current application of these technologies in the field of nuclear power inspection and maintenance, including intelligent inspection, equipment condition monitoring, fault diagnosis, predictive maintenance, and intelligent surveillance. Finally it analyzes the advantages brought by these artificial intelligence technologies, such as improving inspection accuracy, achieving early fault warning, and reducing maintenance costs, which efficiently promote the level inspection and maintenance in the nuclear power industry and ensure the safe and reliable operation of nuclear power plants. By reviewing existing research results, this paper provides theoretical support and guidance for the further application of artificial intelligence technology in the field of nuclear power.

title: Time-Interpolation-Based Phase Detection Method for the Digital Phase-Locked Loop of a MEMS Voltage Sensor
authors: Xiong Xiao, Hui Jin, Gulin Xia, Yukun Xiao, Jiawen Ren, Chun Hu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Digital phase-locked loops (DPLLs) often suffer from limited phase resolution, dead-zone effects, and poor noise immunity when conventional digital phase detectors are used. This work proposes a time-interpolation-based phase error detection method and implements it as a time-interpolated phase detector (TIDPD) for DPLLs. The method combines oversampling, digital filtering, and interpolation to estimate zero-crossing instants of the input signal with high accuracy. In this way, the phase resolution and loop linearity are significantly improved without increasing the main clock frequency. A complete time-interpolated phase detector architecture is constructed and evaluated on a DPLL simulation platform that includes multiple noise sources and non-idealities. Simulation results show that the proposed method effectively suppresses phase noise and quantization errors, improves locking speed, and enhances long-term frequency stability. The method is suitable for closed-loop control systems that require both high accuracy and good dynamic performance.

title: AgriMamba: An Agricultural Visual Intelligence Model Integrating Closed-Loop Control and Hierarchical Memory
authors: Feng Li, Jingqing Li, Shuting Li, WeiLi Wang, JiaQing Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Agricultural disease diagnosis requires accuracy, temporal consistency, and efficiency, yet existing deep learning models rely on single-frame analysis and ignore domain knowledge. We propose AgriMamba, a framework integrating closed-loop control theory with hierarchical temporal-spatial modeling. It introduces Agricultural Guided Control for knowledge-driven memory, TSH-Mamba for multi-scale temporal modeling, SMA for phenology-aware adaptation, and MAFF for multi-level feature fusion. On the Corn Disease & Stress dataset, AgriMamba achieves 91.7 % accuracy, surpasses state-of-the-art by 5.9 %, and reduces complexity and memory by 42 % and 38 %, establishing a resource-efficient paradigm for precision corn disease diagnosis.

title: Adaptive Research on Dynamic Noise Environment
authors: Yunhao Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper tackles the challenge of signal denoising in dynamic noise environments by introducing an enhanced variable step-size leaky normalized least mean square (VSS-Leaky-NLMS) algorithm. The proposed method mitigates the slow convergence and poor stability of conventional NLMS algorithms in nonstationary noise environments through an adaptive step-size mechanisms and a controlled leakage factor. Experimental results demonstrate that under an input condition with a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of 0.16 dB, the improved algorithm achieves an SNR gain of approximately 2.72 dB, while reducing convergence time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 6. 4 \%}$</tex> compared to standard NLMS. This study provides an effective solution for real-time signal processing in dynamic noise environments.

title: Lightweight Deep Learning Model Optimization for Apple Disease Classification Application
authors: M. Aldiki Febriantono, Nasywa Hilmi Cahyono, Brian Marcellino
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Apple fruit diseases reduce yield and quality and are difficult to identify consistently in the field using manual inspection. This study proposes a lightweight deep learning framework for on-device apple fruit disease classification on mobile phones. A new in-field dataset of $\mathbf{3, 9 7 9}$ images from orchards in Poncokusumo, East Java, covering six classes, was collected, expert labeled, augmented and balanced, then split into training, validation and testing sets. Five compact convolutional neural networks were evaluated, namely MobileNetV1, MobileNetV2, MobileNetV3, MnasNet and TinyNAS, trained with a two stage transfer learning strategy. MobileNetV3 achieved the best results with 95% testing accuracy, mean Intersection over Union of 0.6201, and macro precision, recall and F1-score of 0.95. MobileNetV1 and MobileNetV2 reached over 94% accuracy with only a small drop in the other metrics, while MnasNet and TinyNAS showed clearly weaker performance. MobileNetV3 was then deployed in an Android application that provides in situ image based disease diagnosis, visual explanation of the model focus and basic management recommendations, demonstrating the feasibility of accurate and efficient apple disease detection on resource constrained devices.

title: A Wideband High-Isolation Dual-Polarized Antenna Enabled by an AI-Optimized Metasurface
authors: Xuelin Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: In recent years, the WiFi 6E ecosystem operating in the 6 GHz frequency band has continued to develop, and bandwidth-intensive applications have been increasing. Dual-polarized antennas have gradually become a key technology. This paper proposes a wideband high-isolation dual-polarized antenna enabled by an AI-optimized metasurface, which consists of two parts: a lower-layer dual-polarized antenna and an upper-layer metasurface. Among them, the metasurface layer is designed based on the Co-evolutionary Deep Surrogate Model Optimization Framework (CDSM-Opt). This AI-driven framework can efficiently optimize the geometric parameters of the anisotropic units of the metasurface, significantly improve the port isolation performance, and maintain high gain within a wide frequency band. Simulation results show that the antenna has a reflection coefficient lower than -10 dB, an isolation better than -20 dB, and a stable gain between 7.45 and 9.55 dBi in the 6.3-9.6 GHz frequency band.

title: GraphDMIs: Towards Predictive Modeling of DrugmRNA Interactions via Heterogeneous Graph Networks
authors: Tian-Le Long, Ming-Yang Qian, Jia-Wei He, Zhan-Heng Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Predicting drug–mRNA interactions (DMIs) is essential for advancing mRNA-based therapeutics, yet traditional experimental methods are costly and time-consuming. Existing computational approaches often rely on limited data sources and fail to capture multi-scale biological patterns. To overcome these limitations, we propose GraphDMIs, a novel heterogeneous graph network framework that integrates multi-source features—including sequence-based features graph convolutional network (GCN) based features, and Node2Vec feature representations—into a unified predictive model. Experimental results show that GraphDMIs achieves robust and accurate DMIS prediction. The AUC values of all the models reach to 0.85. It is demonstrate that our model can offer a powerful tool for computational drug discovery and the identification of novel mRNA targets.

title: Broad Learning System with Element-wise Adaptive Weighting for Robust Classification under Label Noise
authors: Zhe Zhang, Chongchong Yu, Wenjing Xiao, Can Ding, Haiqiang Chen, Zhenrong Zhang, Keyou Xie, Cuimin Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Broad Learning System (BLS) is efficient but sensitive to label noise. Existing robust variants use sample-level weighting, which underutilizes sample-internal valid information. To overcome this limitation, we introduce an Adaptive Reweighting Broad Learning System (BLS-EAW) for classification scenarios contaminated by label noise. The core improvements of BLS-EAW include an element-wise reweighting strategy that refines the weighting granularity from the sample level to the element level, enabling targeted suppression of noisy elements through weight vectors; a typicality assessment mechanism based on class center distance to identify representative samples close to class centers, enhancing the model's focus on effective information; and a hierarchical iterative framework for element-wise weight updating that gradually suppresses severely noisy elements and adaptively adjusts weights based on residual feedback. The model is optimized by decomposing into subnetworks, each solvable via pseudoinverse. Experiments on UCI datasets show that BLS-EAW outperforms existing robust BLS variants, especially in high-noise scenarios, verifying its efficacy and resilience.

title: Few-Shot Specific Emitter Identification for Different Protocols via Multi-Task Meta-Learning
authors: Haoxuan Men, Hao Wu, Xiaoqiang Qiao, Yihang Du, Jiang Zhang, Tao Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: To address the critical challenge of identifying emitters from different communication protocols under few-shot conditions, this paper proposes a Multi-Task Meta-Learning (MTML) model. The model incorporates dual-stage feature extraction and meta-learning. The feature extraction process employs a shared extractor to learn universal features, while a task discriminator identifies the task type. Task-specific classifiers then capture unique features of the corresponding emitters and achieve joint output. This design effectively addresses gradient conflict in traditional multi-task learning. The feature extraction process is guided under the meta-learning framework, combined with contrastive loss constraints on feature space distribution. On WiSig and LoRa datasets, our method achieves 93% accuracy with only 10 labeled samples per class, enabling efficient multiprotocol specific emitter identification.

title: Multi-Objective Scheduling Strategy for Active Distribution Networks Based on Vehicle-Grid Interaction
authors: Ying Wu, Feng Wang, Zhen Wei, Xiaolin Zhou, Yuanfu Li, Lisheng Li, Wuxia Xu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Under the “Dual Carbon” strategy, large-scale electric vehicles (EVs) as distributed resources help regulate distribution network peak-valley differences but pose challenges to operational economy and security when integrated as loads. A vehicle-to-grid (V2G) scheduling strategy considering the coordination of multiple types of distribution network equipment is proposed in this paper. Firstly, the operational characteristics of EVs are analyzed, and a Ward hierarchical clustering method based on the Monte Carlo method is put forward. The elbow method and silhouette coefficient discrimination method are comprehensively used to determine the optimal number of EV clusters. Secondly, the composition and operation mode of the active distribution network(ADN) are analyzed, and an optimal scheduling framework for the V2G scheduling strategy considering the coordination of multiple types of distribution network equipment is constructed. A multi-objective weighted comprehensive function is defined, and a solution process is proposed in combination with EV-related constraints. Finally, a modified IEEE 33-bus system is selected to conduct multiscenario comparison experiments. The effectiveness of the proposed method has been verified, which has significantly reduced the operational losses of the distribution network. Voltage violations in the power system have been strictly suppressed by stabilizing the voltage within a reasonable range, and the renewable energy accommodation capacity has been remarkably improved.

title: Federated Learning Method Based on Contrastive Representation Knowledge Distillation
authors: Guojuan Zhou, Jiali Zheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Data heterogeneity in federated learning (FL) causes insufficient knowledge transfer, poor feature discriminability, and high communication overhead, limiting FL's application in privacy-sensitive fields. To solve these issues, this paper proposes FedCRKD, a FL method integrating supervised contrastive learning and contrastive representation knowledge distillation into local training, forming a “client-server” architecture with multi-dimensional knowledge interaction. Specifically, the server decomposes the global model into teacher-student models, distributes global knowledge to clients; clients input local data to generate local knowledge and global knowledge on local data (output of the global teacher model on local data), and update local student models via a multi-loss mechanism (cross-entropy for local tasks, supervised contrastive loss for feature alignment, distillation loss for intermediate feature transfer); finally, the server aggregates client knowledge to update the global model, forming a closed loop. Experiments on MNIST,CIFAR-10,and CIFAR-100 (Non-IID data) show FedCRKD outperforms FedAvg, FedProx, and FML in global and local model accuracy, while reducing communication overhead by over 95%. It is well-suited for privacy and real-time-sensitive scenarios like smart manufacturing and medical diagnosis.

title: Motivation Enhancement Method for Students Based on Personality Analysis
authors: Rikuto Fukada, Toru Nakata, Takashi Sakamoto, Toshikazu Kato
abstract: Enhancement and maintenance of motivation is important for students. Encouragement and other types of external interventions will result in specific changes of intrinsic motivation and behavior. Those changes, however, may vary over students, and the best interventions could be different depending on student's personality. E-Learning systems teaching and coaching have to take account of such differences. We conducted an experiment to observe changes in students in an English as a Foreign Language class. Cluster analysis revealed that learners could be grouped according to personality and motivation, such as those who were stability-oriented and prudent, and those who sought personal growth. We found a particular tendency that stability-oriented learners showed a marked improvement in learning attitude after engaging in practical English conversation training. We also observed that learners who were initially encouraged by external prompts tended to develop stronger intrinsic motivation after experiencing success. Furthermore, based on the lessons from the experiment, we propose ‘Five-step cycle of behavior change’ as an effective method to encourage, which consists of 1) providing experience of the action, 2) explanation of value of the action, 3) scheduling for prepare, 4) positive feedback from a system, and other persons, 5) noticing and triggering the chance to next experience.

title: Combined Anti-Jamming Technology for High-Speed Mobile UAV Data Links
authors: Rong Yan, Tong Qu, Pan Zhu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: To address the critical challenges of reliable communication for modern unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) operating under high-speed mobility and within complex electromagnetic conditions, this paper proposes and implements a high-performance data link system. Leveraging a Software Defined Radio (SDR) architecture, the solution seamlessly integrates image transmission, data telemetry, and control functionalities. To mitigate Doppler frequency shifts and multipath fading inherent in high-mobility scenarios, the research focuses on a rapid signal acquisition technique utilizing joint detection of multi-segment PN codes, alongside an efficient Single-Carrier Frequency-Domain Equalization (SC-FDE) method. Furthermore, to counter complex electromagnetic interference, a hierarchical anti-jamming strategy was designed, coordinating multi-stage filtering, frequency hopping, frequency-domain notching, and intelligent spectrum sensing. Simulation results and theoretical analysis show that these key technologies can significantly improve signal acquisition probability, effectively suppress multipath and interference, and ensure the reliability and stability of the data link in harsh environments.

title: Food Security and Emotional Acceptance
authors: Michiko Ohkura, Hidetsugu Nanba, Yasushi Kyutoku, Hiroko Shoji
abstract: In 2023, we conducted a survey and analysis on economic security, with a focus on food security in Japan. We applied both a conventional method and novel approach using generative AI. Based on the findings from both methods, we emphasize the importance of considering emotional aspects when developing food security policies. Furthermore, given the considerable effort and time required by the former method, we strongly recommend the use of the latter for future research. This article presents the procedures and results of each method concerning food security.

title: SCANZIT A Remote Website Inspection Tool to Detect Personal Data Protection Issues
authors: Dk Siti Khairunnisa’ Binti Pg Hj Aliuddin, Serina Haji Mohd Ali, Pg Alias Pg Dp Haji Hidup
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: In an increasingly digitized world, safeguarding personal data has become essential, especially with the introduction of the Personal Data Protection Order (PDPO) 2025 in Brunei Darussalam. This paper introduces SCANZIT, a web-based opensource inspection tool designed to automate the detection of personal data protection issues on websites. SCANZIT supports regulatory efforts by identifying missing cookie banners, unauthorized trackers, absent privacy notices, insecure data collection and more. The system architecture employs a web-based approach using PHP, MySQL, and JavaScript, with Natural Language Processing capabilities for policy analysis. Despite challenges in NLP integration, preliminary testing demonstrates the system’s ability to provide scalable and efficient compliance insights, contributing to regulatory capacity building in Brunei’s digital ecosystem.

title: Selection of Electrical Performance Parameters for ±1100kV UHVDC Line Surge Arresters
authors: Ziming He, Zixin Guo, Xia Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)

title: Leveraging the Ensemble Method to Improve Abstractive Summarization
authors: Saymen, Abba Suganda Girsang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: With the increasing amount of online news, automatic text summarization (ATS) has become a tool to help readers understand key information efficiently. Recent transformer-based models such as T5 and Pegasus have achieved strong results in abstractive summarization, but challenges remain in terms of evaluation scores and summary quality. In this work, we propose an ensemble stacking approach based on the SummaFusion concept, where candidate summaries from T5 and Pegasus are combined with the source article and processed by a T5 meta-learner. Experiments on the CNN/Daily Mail dataset show that our method achieves ROUGE-1 of 0.4236, slightly surpassing Pegasus (0.4211), with competitive ROUGE-2 and ROUGE-L scores, while reducing training time by nearly $90 \%$. The ensemble outputs were also observed to be less repetitive and more faithful to the source text compared to individual models. These findings suggest that fusion-based ensemble stacking can effectively leverage the strengths of different models to balance performance and efficiency. Future work will explore additional candidate models, hyperparameter optimization with Optuna, and experiment tracking with MLflow to further improve reproducibility and performance.

title: Fog image recognition network (FIRNet) with low computational complexity and high accuracy for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle
authors: Chengqiang Huang, Xuemeng Yuan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: The research on fog image recognition of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) aims to address the issues of poor image quality and low recognition accuracy in foggy weather, which is of great significance for enhancing the perception ability of UAVs in harsh environments and expanding application scenarios. Compared with traditional methods, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have advantages such as automatic feature extraction and spatial information retention in fog image recognition. Although conventional CNNs have relatively high recognition accuracy, their computational complexity is extremely high, making them difficult to deploy on mobile devices such as UAVs. Therefore, a fog image recognition network called FIRNet is proposed. In this network, neurons in each layer are trimmed to a number suitable for the binary classification task of images, and depth-separable convolution is used to replace conventional convolution. The fog image dataset HazyDet for UAV is used to train FIRNet. The experimental results show that FIRNet achieves the fog image recognition accuracy of 0.9865 with an ultra-low computational complexity of 48.43 MFLOPS.

title: Noise Resilient Modulation Recognition for Digitalized Power Grids Using SSA and TCN
authors: Dezhi Zheng, Jiawen Ren, Chun Hu, Xiong Xiao, Xiaoxing Wei, Chun Li
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: In digitalized power grids, particularly high-voltage direct current systems, wireless sensor nodes are deployed to monitor voltage, current, and equipment status in real time. However, electromagnetic interference (EMI), arc discharges, and switching transients under high-voltage conditions often degrade signal quality and reduce the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), posing significant challenges to automatic modulation recognition (AMR). We propose a hybrid SSA-TCN architecture fusing Singular Spectrum Analysis (SSA) for adaptive noise suppression and a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) for robust modulation classification. SSA decomposes the trajectory matrix of the received signal to isolate dominant components from noise, while TCN employs dilated causal convolutions to capture long-range temporal dependencies at the symbol level. Designed for deployment on wireless sensing units installed on high-voltage transmission towers, the proposed method enhances recognition accuracy under low SNR or fading conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that SSA-TCN significantly outperforms conventional AMR techniques, making it suitable for edge-side signal processing in smart grid scenarios such as real-time monitoring, fault detection, and condition-based maintenance.

title: Deterministic Compressed Imaging and Efficient Reconstruction for Underwater Target Classification
authors: K Manohar, M. Sabarimalai Manikandan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Compressive imaging plays a vital role in reducing the energy consumption required for sensing and the amount of data to be stored, processed, and transmitted to an edge or cloud computing server. In this paper, we present deterministic compressed imaging and reconstruction for enabling underwater target recognition in the compressed domain without the reconstruction process. This paper also presents the significance of exploring a suitable sparse recovery algorithm with an optimal trade-off between the reconstruction error and recovery time, which can aim to reduce energy consumption and latency at the on-device and edge computing nodes. On standard image quality assessment metrics, results showed that the deterministic compressive imaging provides promising results with low reconstruction error peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) of 26.76-31.74 and structural similarity index measure (SSIM) of 0.768-0.895 and lower recovery time for compression ratios (CRs) of 4 and 8 by using the orthogonal matching pursuit (OMP) with a sparsifying matrix of discrete cosine transform as compared to the L1-optimisation algorithm. Since the reconstructed image had blocking artefacts due to the patch processing involved in the compressive imaging process, we present a post-processing based on the total variation and Tukey window techniques, which produces smoothed images.

title: From Cell to System: Accelerated hpc Simulations of BESS Aging under Frequency Regulation and Arbitrage use cases
authors: Surya Mitra Ayalasomayajula, Michael Starke, Srikanth Allu
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Lithium-ion battery energy storage systems (BESS) packs have emerged as a leading solution for grid-scale energy storage, enhancing resiliency and balancing load fluctuations. Yet, experimental characterization of large-format LIB packs-particularly to assess performance and degradation over hundreds of cycles - demands substantial hardware investment and multi-year testing campaigns. In this work, we couple a hierarchical, physics-based modeling framework agnostic to electrode chemistries with high-performance computing to accelerate systems level evaluation by upto two orders of magnitude. Building on the open-source liionpack platform, we implement cell, module, and pack-scale electrochemical models enriched with mechanistic aging mechanisms and deploy them on an HPC cluster to simulate $150-200 \mathrm{kWh}$ systems over 500 - 1,000 cycles with in days. We subject these virtual B ESS to both constant-current cycling and realistic grid service profiles spanning frequency regulation, ramp-rate support, and energy arbitrage-and quantify the resulting degradation patterns. Our results reveal that localized cell aging can induce substantial nonuniformity at module and pack levels, with service-specific cycling protocols driving distinct aging modes. This rapid, multiscale modeling approach provides a powerful design-space exploration tool for optimizing electrical architecture, control strategies, and operational schedules to prolong pack lifetime and lower total cost of ownership.

title: Performance Comparison of Scaled Conjugate Gradient and Levenberg-Marquardt Algorithms for Pap Smear Pathological Cell Classification
authors: Yessi Jusman, Itsnaini Noor Latif Ismail, Ihsas Nur Alam, Maryza Intan Rahmawati
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Cervical cancer remains a leading cause of cancer-related mortality among women worldwide, making early detection of abnormal cervical cells crucial. Pap smear cytology enables the identification of pathological changes before they progress to invasive carcinoma; however, manual examination is time-consuming and prone to variability. This study proposes a framework for classifying Pap smear cells into five categories: Dyskaryotic, Koilocytotic, Metaplastic, Parabasal, and Superficial-Intermediate using grayscale textural features extracted with the Gray-Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) and a Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP) classifier. The MLP was trained using both the Scaled Conjugate Gradient (SCG) and Levenberg–Marquardt (LM) algorithms and evaluated via $\mathbf{1 0}$-fold cross-validation. The LM-trained MLP with 15 hidden neurons achieved the most outstanding performance, with training, validation, and testing accuracies of $78.84 \%$, $74.31 \%$, and $74.17 \%$, respectively. ROC analysis indicated that classes 1, 2, and 3 were relatively more difficult to discriminate due to subtle textural differences. These results demonstrated that GLCM features combined with MLP provided an effective method for classifying pathological and regenerative cells in Pap smear images, supporting more consistent cytological assessment.

title: IEMol: Image-Enhanced Molecular Representation Learning for Property Prediction
authors: Jiezhou Chen, Pingyu Wang, Linbo Qing, Huan Cao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Molecular property prediction is crucial for accelerating drug discovery, yet prevailing methods primarily rely on molecular graphs, which offer a limited, single-modality perspective. To overcome this, we propose an Image-Enhanced Molecular representation learning framework (IEMol) that enriches graphbased representations with holistic visual patterns from molecular images. Specifically, IEMol integrates these multimodal features through a image enhanced fusion module, enabling a dynamic and comprehensive characterization of molecules. Extensive experiments on multiple MoleculeNet benchmarks show that IEMol achieves state-of-the-art performance on a wide range of classification and regression tasks. The consistent improvements over strong baselines validate that incorporating visual information provides a significant and complementary benefit for molecular property prediction.

title: OPTIMAL-EM: Complexity-Driven Clustering for Optimised Web Accessibility Evaluation
authors: Alexander Hambley, Yeliz Yesilada, Markel Vigo, Simon Harper
abstract: None

title: Safe-Zone Enforced Power Tracking for Hotspot Mitigation in Shaded Microinverter PV Systems
authors: Derick Mathew, J. Prasanth Ram, Young-Jin Kim
abstract: Microinverter-based photovoltaic (PV) systems face reliability risks under partial shading, where bypass diode activation causes shaded submodules (SMs) to dissipate power, creating hotspots and accelerating degradation. Conventional active power tracking (APT) methods often operate in these hotspot-prone zones, resulting in shaded-cell heating, power oscillations when Pref>PMPP, and microinverter restarts. This article proposes a safe zone operation–enforced APT algorithm that enhances the conventional method to ensure oscillation-free regulation while preventing reverse bias stress in shaded SMs. The algorithm dynamically identifies operating regions, halts perturbations under Pref>PMPP, and integrates a minimum power threshold concept to avoid collapse to an open circuit during irradiance drops. Experimental validation on a microinverter prototype, supported by thermal simulations, demonstrates complete elimination of shaded SM dissipation, stable tracking across irradiance and reference variations, and continuous operation without restart. Compared to existing methods, the proposed strategy prioritizes long-term reliability while maintaining tracking accuracy, establishing a safety-driven benchmark for microinverter PV systems.

title: High-Precision Antagonistic Hysteresis Modeling and Compensation for a Nanopositioning Stage With Antagonistic Piezo-Actuation
authors: Haitao Wu, Zhongbo Yu, Jianda Han, Xin Zhao, Yanding Qin
abstract: This article proposes a novel antagonistic Prandtl-Ishlinskii (API) model to accurately describe and compensate the complex hysteresis nonlinearity inherent in antagonistically actuated nanopositioning stages using piezo-actuation. Unlike conventional PI-based modeling strategies that independently treat each actuator, the proposed API model performs nonlinear modeling on the overall coupled output displacement of system. This unified modeling approach achieves higher accuracy with fewer parameters and supports both forward and inverse modeling in a compact structure. To further improve the system’s robustness against unmodeled dynamics and disturbances, an adaptive Kalman filter (AKF) is integrated for real-time parameter identification. Experimental validations confirm that the API model outperforms counterparts in hysteresis modeling with a minimum root mean square error (RMSE) of 129.0 nm. For hysteresis compensation in trajectory tracking, the inverse API model achieves a minimum RMSE of 213.8 nm. The AKF+API<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> method enables accurate nonperiodic trajectory tracking. Even under disturbances, AKF+API<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> method converges quickly within 200 ms, verifying its high-precision trajectory tracking performance.

title: Dynamic Grouping and Aggregation Weight Optimization for Hierarchical Federated Learning with Quantization
authors: Zihao Peng, Nan Zou, Jiandian Zeng, Boyuan Li, Shengbo Chen, Tian Wang, Weijia Jia
abstract: Hierarchical Federated Learning (HFL) alleviates communication bottlenecks by organizing the system into multiple layers: client, intermediate aggregator, and server. Clients and aggregation layers form groups based on connection patterns, and the methods used for grouping and aggregation directly affect convergence performance. Currently, some studies have proposed grouping algorithms to address the non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) characteristics of client data to improve performance. However, these methods do not account for network heterogeneity, such as clients using different quantization levels or adaptive quantization strategies to minimize communication overhead. Moreover, most methods rely on heuristics that blindly explore the combinatorial grouping space, incurring substantial computational overhead. In this paper, we conduct a rigorous convergence analysis and frame the dual challenges of heterogeneous data and quantization heterogeneity in HFL as the joint optimization of aggregation weights and grouping strategy. Specifically, we derive the optimal closed-form solution for the aggregation weights and propose an Alternating Optimization Hierarchical Optimal Weights (AO-HOW) algorithm to compute these weights. Building on this result, we propose DyGHFL—Dynamic Exclusion–Reallocation Grouping for Hierarchical Federated Learning, a structu-reaware and efficient greedy algorithm that reorganizes groups by maximizing structured gain and updating weight coefficients according to current system conditions. Experiments on multiple datasets show that DyGHFL consistently outperforms existing baselines, demonstrating its effectiveness in HFL.

title: Leveraging Generative AI Agent to Promote Teaching Reflection in a K-12 AI Course: Effects on Teachers' Reflection Self-Efficacy, Instructional Design, and Reflective Thinking_supp1-3668051.docx
authors: Tao He
abstract: As generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) rapidly evolves, K-12 education is introducing AI courses whose interdisciplinary complexity exposes teachers' limited readiness. Research suggests that teachers can harness GenAI tools not only to enhance instruction but also to scaffold reflection that drives further teaching improvement. Therefore, this exploratory quasi-experimental study developed a customized GenAI agent system to support teachers' reflection on AI-course teaching and to enhance reflection self-efficacy, instructional design, and reflective thinking. A total of sixty in-service teachers were recruited and divided into two experimental groups, the self-reflection group (SRG) and the peer-reflection group (PRG), both supported by the customized GenAI agent system, and one control group (CG) with conventional technology-based reflection for a four-week AI course reflective practice experiment. The results revealed that the GenAI-agent-supported reflection approach could significantly promote teachers' self-reflection efficacy of two experimental groups compared with CG teachers. However, no significant differences between the SRG and PRG teachers' self-reflections efficacy could be found. Moreover, SRG and PRG teachers also outperformed the CG teachers on mutual-reflection self-efficacy. Furthermore, GenAI approach could significantly boost SRG and PRG teachers' instructional design reflection on methods and behavior, however, no significant differences were observed in instructional objectives or content among the three groups. To further explore the effects of GenAI agent support, Epistemic Network Analysis was applied to examine the coded results of teachers' reflective journals. The findings indicated that SRG and PRG teachers demonstrated broader and higher-order reflective thinking, integrating more dialogic and critical elements, whereas CG networks were predominantly descriptive. Overall, the study confirms that a customized GenAI agent can effectively deepen reflective thinking and practice, offering new insights into fostering teachers' professional development within K-12 AI education.

title: A Modified Multi-Step Frequency-Scaling Algorithm for Ground-Based FMCW-ArcSAR Imaging
authors: Zehua He, Bin Zhou, Jun Pan, Pengding Du, Guangyou Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Arc synthetic aperture radar (ArcSAR) enables panoramic, high-angular-resolution imaging through platform rotation, overcoming the field-of-view limitations of linear-rail SAR. However, conventional ArcSAR algorithms based on low-order series expansions and reference-range approximations suffer from phase errors at millimeter-wave frequencies, resulting in azimuth defocusing. Moreover, as the azimuth bandwidth increases for close-range targets, bandwidth broadening can cause overcorrection when far-range assumptions break down. This paper introduces the Frequency Scaling Algorithm (FSA) into ArcSAR and proposes an enhanced Multi-Step Frequency Scaling Algorithm (MSFSA). Unlike conventional FSA, which performs scaling solely in the range domain, MSFSA incorporates a Doppler-domain frequency-scaling step prior to range cell migration correction (RCMC). Differential and bulk RCMC are then performed based on Taylor-series expansion and series inversion, followed by a second azimuth-frequency-scaling step to restore phase fidelity. With the integration of precise azimuth matched filtering, overall image quality is further improved. Simulation and experimental results using frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radar demonstrate that MSFSA achieves imaging performance comparable to the Range Doppler Algorithm (RDA) with sinc interpolation and the Back Projection Algorithm (BPA), while maintaining higher computational efficiency by avoiding interpolation and point-by-point processing. Compared with existing methods, MSFSA provides superior close-range imaging quality and reduced far-range phase error, making it a promising technique for high-resolution, wide-swath ArcSAR imaging.

title: Local Causal Discovery with Background Knowledge_supp1-3667409.pdf
authors: Yue Liu
abstract: Causality plays a pivotal role in various fields of study. Based on the framework of causal graphical models, previous works have proposed identifying whether a variable is a cause or non-cause of another variable in every Markov equivalent graph by learning only the local structure. However, the presence of prior knowledge, often represented as a partially known causal graph, is common in many causal modeling applications. Leveraging this prior knowledge enables further identification of causal relations. In this paper, we first propose a method for learning the local structure by incorporating several types of causal background knowledge, including direct causal, non-ancestral, and ancestral information. Then we introduce sufficient and necessary conditions for identifying causal relations based solely on the local structure in the presence of prior knowledge. The effectiveness and efficiency of our method are demonstrated through experiments on local structure learning, causal relation identification, and its application to fair machine learning.

title: Tunable Isolator-Free Magnetron Power-Combining System Based on Peer-to-Peer Locking Technique With a Three-Stub Tuner
authors: Shaoyue Wang, Xu Zhu, Xiaojie Chen, Bo Yang, Liping Yan, Naoki Shinohara, Changjun Liu
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Magnetrons are widely used microwave sources in industrial applications. To overcome the power limitations of a single magnetron and achieve large-power output, a compact, high efficiency, and straightforward magnetron power-combining system based on peer-to-peer injection locking is proposed. An <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-plane tee integrated with a three-stub tuner is employed as a coupling network between two magnetrons, with the coupling characteristics adjusted via the insertion depths of the stubs. The injection-locking characteristic is qualitatively analyzed and experimentally verified. Without circulators or external injection sources, the system based on two commercial magnetrons achieves the phase synchronization through peer-to-peer injection locking. The proposed system achieves a maximum microwave efficiency of 90.1% at an output power of 1440 W. It maintains 87.8% efficiency at a peak output power of 1750 W. This work provides a novel perspective on the development of coherent magnetron power-combining systems based on peer-to-peer injection-locked magnetrons.

title: Parameter Analysis and Optimization of Layer Fidelity for Quantum Processor Benchmarking at Scale
authors: Maria Jose Lozano Palacio, Hasan Nayfeh, Matthew Ware, David C. McKay
abstract: With the continued scaling of quantum processors, holistic benchmarks are essential for extensively evaluating device performance. Layer fidelity is a benchmark well-suited to assessing processor performance at scale. Key advantages of this benchmark include its natural alignment with randomized benchmarking (RB) procedures, crosstalk awareness, fast measurements over large numbers of qubits, high signal-to-noise ratio, and fine-grained information [1]. In this work, we extend the analysis of the original layer fidelity manuscript to optimize parameters of the benchmark and extract deeper insights of its application. We present a robust protocol for identifying optimal qubit chains of arbitrary length <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math></inline-formula>, demonstrating that our method yields error per layered gate (EPLG) values <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$40 \% - 70 \%$</tex-math></inline-formula> lower than randomly selected chains for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N=100$</tex-math></inline-formula> qubits. We further establish layer fidelity as an effective performance monitoring tool, capturing both edge-localized and device-wide degradation by tracking optimal chains of length 50 and 100, and fixed chains of length 100. Additionally, we refine error analysis by proposing parameter bounds on the number of randomizations and Clifford lengths used in direct RB fits, minimizing fit uncertainties. Finally, we use layer fidelity to analyze the impact of varying gate durations on layered two-qubit (2Q) errors, showing that prolonged gate times leading to idling times significantly increase these quantities. These findings extend the applicability of the layer fidelity benchmark and provide practical guidelines for optimizing quantum processor evaluations.

title: AFed-CHKD: an Adversarial Federated Learning based Cluster Head Selection and Knowledge Distillation in Vehicular Sensor Network_supp3-3665994.pdf
authors: Shibao Sun
abstract: Federated learning (FL) supports collaborative modeling among clients in vehicular sensor network (VSN). However, the frequent topology changes of VSN lead to high concurrency and poor reliability in the interaction of model parameters between vehicle clients. It leads to reduced timeliness of federal collaborative decision-making and increased vulner-ability to gradient inversion attacks (GIA). To address the above issues, we propose an adversarial FL based cluster head selection and knowledge distillation(KD). We design an adaptive cluster head selection mechanism based on multi-dimensional attributes of vehicle. The cluster head (teacher model) transmits the distilled knowledge (soft label) to the member vehicles within the cluster (student model), compressing interaction parameters. To defend against GIA, the cluster head acts as a generator to construct adversarial examples, and members within the cluster act as discriminators. Based on our designed and configured intelligent vehicle platform, AFed-CHKD is compared with several baseline methods. The results show that the accuracy standard deviation of the model is 1.5%, and the convergence rate of the model is improved by an average of 1.54 times. The reconstructed images by the attacker have blurred boundaries, which proves that AFed-CHKD is resistant to GIA.

title: On Being Demoted to Second Class
authors: Jonathan Bean
abstract: None

title: Outperforming 2D Entropy for Image Segmentation Using Multi-Level Average Grayscale of Neighborhood Corners
authors: Shuheng Zhang, Ri Xv, Yan Zhai, Yunfei Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) entropy thresholding is pivotal for adaptive image segmentation, yet its effectiveness is often constrained by the choice of local descriptors. While conventional 2D histograms typically combine a central pixel's intensity with the arithmetic mean of its local neighborhood, such approaches can inadequately capture critical structural information. To overcome this, we introduce a novel 2D histogram that pairs the central pixel intensity with the average grayscale of a strategically selected local subset: specifically, 3 non-adjacent corners (top-left, top-right, bottom-left) of the 3×3 neighborhood, deliberately excluding the bottom-right pixel to enhance sensitivity to structural integrity. Evaluations on standard datasets from the MATLAB Image Processing Toolbox demonstrate that our method achieves segmentation accuracy comparable or superior to leading contemporary techniques.

title: Analysis and Research on U-I Control Characteristics of $\pm 500 \text{kV}$ Multi-Terminal DC Transmission System
authors: Liyun Zha, Zongzhang Yang, Lei Long, Wenjun Mao, Dongxu Deng, Weiyu Lu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: With the large-scale access of new energy sources and the popularization of HVDC transmission technology, China's receiving power grid is facing multiple challenges brought about by new energy and DC feed-in. The MTDC transmission system has the characteristics of multi-regional power supply and multi-regional load receiving. It has the advantages of converter stations that can transmit power separately, flexible switching of transmission states, and high reliability. It is one of the effective methods to solve the problems of regional new energy grid-connected and consumption. This paper analyzes and researches the U-I control characteristics of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{\pm 5 0 0 k V}$</tex> MTDC transmission system, discusses the control strategies of DC current and voltage, and focuses on the U-I coordinated control characteristics of MTDC transmission system. The results provide a reference for effectively reducing the line overload level under the condition of AC failure at the receiving end, and enhance the safety, stability and operating reliability of the MTDC transmission system.

title: A Weighted Hybrid Approach for Recommender Systems Using Alternating Least Squares and CatBoost
authors: Fiddin Yusfida A’La, Nurul Firdaus, Helmi Imaduddin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This study seeks to enhance the accuracy of movie rating predictions by integrating collaborative filtering and machine learning techniques within a hybrid recommender system. Using the MovieLens dataset, two distinct models are implemented: the Alternating Least Squares (ALS) matrix factorization method and the CatBoost gradient boosting algorithm. The ALS model is designed to capture latent useritem interactions, whereas CatBoost exploits nonlinear relationships through user and item features. The hybrid approach applies a simple averaging of predictions from both models to assess potential performance improvements. Experimental results indicate that the hybrid method outperforms the individual models, achieving Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) scores of 0.810 and 0.645, respectively. These results demonstrate that combining latent factor modeling with treebased learning can effectively reduce prediction errors by leveraging complementary capabilities. The novelty of this research lies in its simple yet effective hybridization strategy, which improves recommendation quality without relying on complex ensemble techniques. The findings suggest that even straightforward model fusion can substantially improve predictive accuracy in recommender systems and can be adapted to other domains where the integration of linear and nonlinear modeling offers benefits.

title: Economic and Environmental Assessment of Optimized PV System Sizing and Vehicle-to-Building Integration
authors: Hironobu Kiuchi, Yoshinori Suzue, Kenta Suzuki, Keigo Ikezoe, Ayumi Yamaki, Yasunori Kikuchi
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Vehicle-to-Building (V2B) integration has gained attention for its potential to reduce electricity costs and carbon emissions when combined with photovoltaic (PV) systems. However, few studies have addressed the practical needs of decision-makers, particularly in determining the optimal PV system size from both economic and environmental perspectives. To address this gap, we developed a simulator that identifies optimal PV system size and generates the V2B charge/discharge profiles using a statistical electric vehicle (EV) behavior model. Simulation results show that V2B enables the installation of larger PV capacity hence enhancing renewable energy utilization. We evaluated two installation cases economically and environmentally; the case of planning PV sizing and V2B installation simultaneously performs better in the long term, achieving lower system life cost and emissions. In contrast, adding V2B after initial PV sizing is more costeffective in the short term, offering an earlier payback period. These findings provide practical guidance for strategic V2B adoption and PV planning.

title: Evolution of Driving Range, Battery Capacity, and Charging Performance in Thailand’s BEV Market (2023-2025)
authors: Pakin Phornphiboon, Sita Serngadichaivit, Supasit Paengson, Kitchaon Ruangjirakit, Yossapong Laoonual
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: The adoption rate of electric passenger vehicles in Thailand reached $13 \%$ in 2024, though still falling short of national targets. The Thai market requires detailed knowledge about battery capacity, driving range, and charging performance evolution to overcome range anxiety and cost barriers. This research examines Thai BEV market developments from 2023 to 2025 through WLTP vehicle specifications, market reports, and secondary literature. The market transition shows a shift toward affordable mid-range BEVs while battery capacities remain stable at $75-80 \mathrm{kWh}$ and charging speeds approach 300 kW. The EV market in Thailand shows signs of achieving equilibrium between cost, range, and charging performance through Chinese brands’ affordable prices and German brands’ diverse model selection.

title: A Mixed-Review Method Analysis of Green Corrosion Inhibitors for Carbon Steel in an Acidic Environment
authors: Lalaine Joan A. Limbago, John Noel R. Cabusas, Kate M. Calderon, Althea Mae C. Ledesma, Leila Kristine T. Villaflor
abstract: The rising demand for eco-friendly solutions has intensified interest in plant-based corrosion inhibitors. However, comprehensive evaluations on their performance, particularly for carbon steel applications in acidic environments, remain limited. This study employs scientometric and systematic reviews to assess naturally derived corrosion inhibitors, the extraction solvents used, and the inhibition mechanism. The review focuses on peerreviewed articles published between 2014 and 2024, sourced from ScienceDirect and Web of Science. Scientometric analysis reveals a rising research output in this field. Results from the systematic review show that alcohol and water-based solvents yield higher inhibition efficiencies, ranging from 79.7% (Rollinia) to 97% (Spice Tree), and 51.33% (Watermelon) to 97.1% (Nappa), respectively. Most plant extracts function as mixed-type inhibitors, exhibiting both physical and chemical adsorption mechanisms. These findings underscore the promise of plant-derived inhibitors as costeffective, biodegradable, and highly effective alternatives to synthetic corrosion inhibitors.

title: Research on Text Recognition in Complex Scenes Based on Dual Attention Mechanism
authors: Chenyang Zhou, Chenyang Zhou, Yaoxing Guan, Haodong Wang, Guojin Gan
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Text recognition in complex scenes remains a challenging problem in computer vision due to factors such as highly variable text morphology and background interference, which often cause traditional Optical Character Recognition (OCR) systems to underperform. This study focuses on improving the accuracy and robustness of such recognition, reviewing the application and evolution of dual attention mechanisms. These mechanisms, typically implemented via spatial-channel or visuallinguistic dual-branch collaboration, address the limitations of single attention modules in noise suppression and semantic understanding. Experimental results demonstrate that on the International Conference on Document Analysis and Recognition (ICDAR) 2019 ArT dataset, the proposed approach outperforms traditional OCR and Convolutional Recurrent Neural Network (CRNN) baselines, achieving accuracy gains of 12.3% to 18.7%. It also shows significantly enhanced robustness in recognizing curved text and under low-light conditions.By transcending the limitations of single-modality analysis and enabling multidimensional feature synergy, this research provides a new paradigm for joint visual-semantic understanding and offers valuable technical insights for the development of advanced visuallinguistic models.

title: Research on Multidimensional Application of Machine Learning in Software Engineering
authors: Haoxuan Zou, Laichao Qu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: In the context of rapid advancements in big data and artificial intelligence, machine learning has found extensive use in software engineering, as well as in fields like data analysis and image recognition. By enabling data acquisition, pattern discovery, and the construction of predictive models, machine learning significantly improves both the efficiency and quality of software development processes, while also driving innovation across multiple sectors. This paper begins by reviewing fundamental machine learning concepts, including major paradigms such as supervised and unsupervised learning, along with standard evaluation techniques like the holdout and cross-validation approaches. Subsequently, it focuses on the practical integration of machine learning within software engineering. The primary objective is to effectively apply machine learning techniques to streamline development workflows and enhance overall productivity.

title: Advances in Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface-Empowered Human Respiration Monitoring
authors: Jingyi Liang, Chenchen Hou, Yukai Wang, Zhirui Qian, Xinyu Li, Jianwei You
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: Human respiration sensing has become a key technology for non-contact health monitoring and smart environments. Recent advances in Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) have enabled intelligent manipulation of electromagnetic waves, offering new possibilities for robust and precise wireless sensing. This paper provides an overview of RIS-assisted human respiration sensing approaches, including non-line-of-sight detection, multi-person monitoring, and integrated identification. Building upon these studies, we introduce a metasurface-empowered multimodal system integrating visual localization, Wi-Fi channel sensing, and programmable electromagnetic control, enabling respiration tracking of moving subjects in indoor environments.

title: A Study on the Performance of Heat-Resistant Fixtures Considering the Capacity Increase of Overhead Transmission Lines
authors: Xianyu Li, Zhijian Liu, Siyuan Li, Xinjie Yue
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the rapid development of the national economy, the demand for electric power loads increases dramatically, which puts forward higher requirements for the maximum transmission capacity of transmission lines. However, the construction cost and engineering volume of opening new transmission line corridors are enormous, so improving the current carrying capacity of existing transmission lines has become one of the leading solutions to the problem of heavy line loads. This paper discusses optimizing the maximum conveying capacity of existing transmission lines by adopting a new heatresistant conductor and fixture technology. The structural parameters of tension-tolerant clamps are taken as design variables. Using an electromagnetic-thermal multi-physics coupling model, we calculate heat-resistant fixtures' hot-spot temperatures and temperature increases under specified operating conditions. Simulating and physically testing the performance of heat-resistant fittings in terms of electrical, thermal, and mechanical properties, and optimizing and improving the structure of heat-resistant fittings.

title: An Improved Malicious Traffic Identification Model with ADASYN and Sparse Attention
authors: Lihui Xiao, JieXiang Zeng, Lin Li, Ronggui Li, Zixuan Qin, Hailing Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the continued expansion of the network scale, the high-dimensional complexity of network traffic and the imbalance problem of attack samples have become increasingly prominent, posing severe challenges for network traffic anomaly detection. Traditional machine learning models exhibit limited detection performance in high-dimensional features and imbalanced samples for malicious traffic identification. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an improved identification model that leverages the collaboration of Adaptive Synthetic Sampling (ADASYN) and sparse attention. ADASYN is utilized to estimate the difficulty of each minority-class sample and adaptively generate additional minority-class samples near the decision boundary based on the sample distribution. By utilizing the generated augmented samples, ADASYN was integrated into a sparse attention-based feature learning model based on TabNet to automatically learn high-dimensional features of network traffic and perform feature selection simultaneously. Bayesian Optimization(BO) algorithm is utilized to determine the optimal hyperparameters of this model. With the learned sparse features as the input, the classifier can classify hard-to-distinguish anomalous samples more effectively and enhance its anomaly detection capability for complex network traffic. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves excellent performance on the UNSW-NB15 dataset, validating its effectiveness and robustness in complex network traffic scenarios.

title: External Damage Vibration Signal Recognition via GBDT with Deep Learning Feature Extractor
authors: Lufan Zhang, Xiaojuan Chen, Xue Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Urban buried All-Dielectric Self-Supporting (ADSS) optical cables are often damaged by external forces like construction machinery and human activities, threatening power grid safety. Existing vibration signal recognition methods rely on single algorithms, which poorly adapt to complex urban environments and yield low accuracy. To solve these issues, this paper proposes a BiLSTM-CNN-GBDT hybrid model. It extracts multi-domain features via a parallel dual-branch structure, dynamically selects high-value features through a gated differentiable fusion layer to avoid redundant interference, and completes recognition with a Gradient Boosting Decision Tree (GBDT) classifier. Experimental results demonstrate that this integrated deep learning feature extraction and GBDT classification framework achieves a recognition accuracy of 96.2%.

title: FICHAD: Fusion of Image Context and Human-Annotated Descriptions for Multi-Modal Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Haodi Ma, Dzmitry Kasinets, Daisy Zhe Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Multimodal knowledge graph completion (MMKGC) aims to predict missing links in multimodal knowledge graphs (MMKGs) by leveraging structural, textual, and visual information. This is critical in real-world applications such as identifying fine-grained visual facts in art, associating damaged goods with vendors in supply chain audits, or connecting medical scans to clinical entities where languageonly models often fail due to outdated or incomplete world knowledge, and vision-language models (VLMs) struggle with reasoning over structured relations. We propose FICHAD, a modular framework that uses pretrained VLMs to generate multimodal context in textual form, which includes link-aware descriptions (LAMM-context), entitycentric summaries, and conceptual hints for relation understanding. This context can be directly injected into existing description-based KGC models (e.g., SimKGC, CSProm-KG) without retraining or architectural changes. Evaluated on three public MMKG benchmarks (FB15K-237-IMG, MKG-W, MKG-Y), FICHAD consistently improves performance; our best variant outperforms prior state-of-the-art MMKGC models by up to 4.6% in MRR and 3.5% in Hits@1 metrics. We further provide quantitative and qualitative analyses demonstrating that VLM-generated context captures relational semantics often missed by baseline methods. Our findings show that FICHAD offers a scalable and effective framework for integrating multimodal knowledge into symbolic reasoning tasks like KGC.

title: Intelligent BIM Object Retrieval by Fusing Image Segmentation and Semantic Recognition
authors: Can Huang, Weijia Liu, Hui Li, Qiuyu Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: The "digital-physical gap" in Building Information Modeling (BIM) hinders linking on-site imagery with digital models. We propose a framework integrating high-precision segmentation with semantic recognition to bridge this gap. The method first uses segmentation for robust, pixel-level component delineation under complex site conditions. A semantic module then classifies these regions and interprets their attributes. Application analysis confirms the framework’s ability to accurately identify diverse components. This fusion of visual and semantic data provides a robust foundation for intelligent BIM object localization and retrieval.

title: An Efficient Attribute-Revocable CP-ABKS Searchable Encryption Scheme Based on LSSS and Key Blinding in Cloud Environment
authors: Chenyu Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper proposes an efficient CP-ABKS scheme for cloud environments, which achieves constant-level pairing operations under the LSSS framework, integrates key blinding and BLS-MHT verification, and simultaneously supports lazy and local attribute revocation. Theoretical proof and experimental results show that the proposed scheme outperforms existing works in terms of efficiency, security, and scalability, making it suitable for large-scale deployment. Future work will explore the integration of lattice-based cryptography to achieve post-quantum security and extend to more complex access policies.

title: Optimal Scheduling of Integrated Energy System Based on Source-Load Dual Response
authors: Kun Zheng, Tao Xu, ChenYu Wang, YanNan Lu, ZeNan Liu, JiaBao Xing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: In order to fully exploit the schedulable resources on both sides of the source and load and improve the economy and low carbon of the operation of the integrated energy system, an optimal scheduling strategy of the integrated energy system considering the bilateral response on both sides of the source and load is proposed. Firstly, the electric-hydrogen system and waste heat equipment are introduced on the source side to construct a source-side response model with flexible output of electric and thermal energy. Secondly, in order to prevent load overresponse, a load-side response model considering load satisfaction level of integrated energy system is constructed on the basis of multi-load interruption and time-shift response on the demand side. The simulation results show that the proposed strategy effectively reduces the operating cost of the integrated energy system.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Throttle-Aware Data on Conditional Imitation Learning for Autonomous Driving
authors: Endang Rusiana, Angga Rusdinar, Inung Wijayanto, Thalita Dewi Rahmaniar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This study investigates how dataset composition affects the learning and generalization performance of Conditional Imitation Learning (CIL) and its Multimodal Variant (MCIL) for autonomous driving. Models trained on a throttle-aware dataset—which includes realistic acceleration, cruising, and deceleration—are compared to the performance of a baseline model trained with a constant-speed dataset. Although models trained on the baseline dataset achieve higher success rates, this dataset is strongly biased toward steady cruising with very limited throttle transitions—an important component of realistic human driving. In comparison, the throttle-aware dataset produces smoother and more stable longitudinal behavior, despite showing slightly lower numerical success rates. MCIL performs better than CIL in most scenarios, with about $\mathbf{1 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 2 \%}$ improvement in tasks like One Turn and Navigation with Dynamic Obstacles. The results demonstrate that throttle-phase diversity improves the naturalness of speed control and exposes the difficulty imitation-based models face when dealing with complex throttle behaviors.

title: YOLOv11-Based Vehicle and Pedestrian Detection
authors: Jun Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This paper addresses challenges in complex traffic scenarios, such as dense pedestrians, small-object clusters, and frequent detection errors, by improving YOLOv11. First, the backbone C3k2 module is replaced with C3k2-Star to enhance feature representation and multi-scale fusion. Second, the neck adopts a RepGFPN structure with Dysample, forming the GDFPN module for more effective multi-scale aggregation. Finally, the loss function is replaced with DIoU to improve bounding box regression. Experiments show the improved model achieves 48.3% mAP50 on COCO—about 3.7 % higher than the baseline—and demonstrates stronger robustness and generalization on VOC.

title: Seismic Response Analysis of The Bent Section of Gas-Insulated Metal-Enclosed Transmission Lines
authors: Yixun Zhang, Qiang Xie
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: To study the seismic performance of gas-insulated metal-enclosed transmission lines, a bent section of gas-insulated metal-enclosed transmission line (GIL) in a certain UHV converter station was selected as the research object. Considering the constraint effects of different types of supports on the GIL enclosure, the different types of connections between the threepillar insulators inside the GIL and the GIL enclosure, as well as the effect of the compensation units on the pipeline, a refined finite element model of the GIL composed of the inner conductor, threepillar insulators, enclosure, compensation unit, and supports was established. The vibration mode characteristics were analyzed, and three-direction ground motions were input for dynamic timehistory response analysis to study the seismic response characteristics of the bent section GIL and the seismic weak locations. The results show that the maximum stress of the bent section GIL occurs at the turning points of the horizontal and vertical pipe sections and the sliding support in the higher horizontal section, which are the seismic weak locations. By setting compensation units in the vertical section to compensate for deformation, the stress on the shell can be reduced, but it will increase the horizontal displacement of the shell. Reasonable setting of the compensation units can to some extent improve the seismic resistance performance of the bent section GIL.

title: Comparative Analysis of Technical Indicators’ Effect on Forecasting Models for LQ45 Stock Prices
authors: Daniel Kevin Jhon Feko Wahyudi, Emmanuel Daniel Widhiarto, Felicia Natania Lingga, Andry Chowanda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Stock price forecasting is crucial in terms of supporting national economic growth. In Indonesia, the LQ45 index serves as a benchmark large-cap stocks with high liquidity, making accurate prediction crucial for market players. This research compares several approaches, such as Ridge regression, ElasticNet, HistGB, LightGBM, XGBoost, SARIMAX, Prophet, and LSTM under two experimental setups, one using raw data OHLCV and another using technical indicators. The evaluation expected to provide insights into model’s accuracy, robustness and practical relevance to Indonesian capital market. The results showed that adding technical indicators only provided a small improvement. Ridge Regression with a 10-day window was performing as the most accurate model, while SARIMAX with technical indicators produced the weakest performance. The Prophet and LSTM models remained consistent but gained no visible advantages. These findings shown that, as a mathematical derivative of price data, technical indicators contain information already represented in lag and rolling features. Therefore, classical linear model remains efficient and stable for short-term LQ45 price forecasting.

title: Design of Adaptive Improved VDCOL Control Strategy for Suppressing Subsequent Commutation Failure in HVDC Transmission Systems
authors: Xun Liu, Tuo Wang, Yechun Xin, Yupeng He, Zhiyuan Chen, Hairun Guo
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The VDCOL control in the High-Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) transmission system can limit the DC current after a fault, thereby reducing the likelihood of subsequent commutation failures in the system. But the parameters of the traditional VDCOL control are fixed and difficult to meet the system recovery requirements under different operating conditions. This paper proposes an adaptive VDCOL control strategy. The parameters of VDCOL control are adjusted according to the degree of AC bus voltage drop, thereby meeting the system recovery requirements under different operating conditions. The simulation results show that under severe faults, the improved VDCOL control strategy can reduce the reference value of current during the initial stage of system recovery, thereby avoiding subsequent commutation failures. Under nonsevere faults, the improved VDCOL control strategy can increase the current reference value on the premise of ensuring that the system does not occur subsequent commutation failures, thus helping the system recover to a steady state quickly.

title: Blood Flow Diffusion Assessment in Cerebrovascular Imaging via Bidirectional Attention Fusion and Joint Loss Optimization
authors: Qingqing Li, Zhe Shen, Tao Li, Li n Wang, Haiyin Du, Qingtao Wu
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Accurate evaluation of cerebral hemodynamics is essential for image-based clinical decision support in moyamoya disease (MMD). However, existing fusion methods struggle to adapt to sparse preprocessed neuroimaging data. To address this issue, we propose a novel sparse image fusion framework that performs multi-scale feature extraction using a lightweight convolutional encoder, combined with a bidirectional attention mechanism and a joint loss function to enhance fusion adaptability. Quantitative results show that the mutual information (MI) index improves by 0.26 and vascular sensitivity reaches 0.96, outperforming existing methods. Pseudo-colorization and threedimensional (3D) visualization further verify its application potential. This approach overcomes key challenges in sparse image fusion and demonstrates the effectiveness of attention-augmented signal fusion, establishing a new paradigm for multimodal neuroimaging processing.

title: Characteristics of Electric Field Distribution on Stator Bar End Surface
authors: Yuheng Meng, Nan Lv, Boyi Li, Yunyi Gao, Jinyan Zhao, Hongyu Mao, Weiqi Yan
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: With the growing demand for electricity in China, the capacity of generators is progressively increasing. The end parts of generator stator bars are prone to corona discharge, which compromises the safe operation of the electrical machinery and the stability of the power system. In this study, a physical model of the stator bar was established using COMSOL Multiphysics software. The actual operating conditions were simulated by applying voltage, and the electric potential and field distribution around the stator under corresponding conditions were calculated. By comparing the results with those without protective measures, it is concluded that the three-segment corona protection layer can effectively slow the rate of electric potential increase along the bar surface and reduce the maximum electric field strength on the bar surface.

title: Comparison of Electricity Consumption in the Obsolete and Modern Steelmaking Process: IOSP+BF+OHF vs Midrex+EAF
authors: Volodymyr Kukhar, Daniil Tymoshenko, Ivan Volovnenko, Oleksandr Kurpe, Volodymyr Pashynskyi, Larysa Shaulska
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The paper emphasizes the urgent need for Ukraine's metallurgy sector, particularly at PJSC "Zaporizhstal," to replace outdated technologies with modern, sustainable steel production methods as part of a global "green transition". The research approach encompassed field measurements, followed by rigorous data processing and analysis. Additionally, a comparative analysis was conducted to enhance the findings. The study compares the electricity consumption at two different steel production methods: (I) iron ore sintering process (IOSP) with an aspiration system + blast furnace (BF) + an open-hearth furnace (OHF) smelting; (II) Midrex process + electric arc furnaces (EAF) smelting. The analysis revealed that the specific energy consumption (SEC) varies significantly across different processes: the Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) requires the highest at approximately 340 kWh/t, followed by the Midrex process at 85 kWh/t. The Open Hearth Furnace (OHF) and Blast Furnace (BF) show lower energy demands at around 4 kWh/t and 3.3 kWh/t respectively, while the Sintering Plant (SP) consumes about 33 kWh/t. It is evident that the "IOSP + BF + OHF" method generates substantially higher CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions than the more efficient "Midrex + EAF" approach. The IOSP is among the most energy-intensive processes, whereas the BF requires less energy. The OHF, though consuming relatively little energy, is less attractive due to its inefficiency. While the Midrex process demands more energy than traditional methods, it compensates with substantial environmental advantages by emitting less CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>. The Electric Arc Furnace (EAF), despite its high energy requirements, excels in the efficient and flexible recycling of steel scrap.
