Research indicates that adopting advanced technologies like Direct Reduction (DR) via the Midrex process, followed by pellet melting in an EAF, is a crucial step toward lowering emissions and enhancing the energy efficiency of modern steelmaking practices.

title: Comparison of Separate Path Planning Algorithms to UAV Control
authors: Oleksii Kucherenko, Tetiana Vakaliuk
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: This article examines the A* and Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) algorithms in order to determine their strengths and weaknesses for UAV navigation. A feature of UAV navigation is the need not only to build a route as a set of geographic points but also to adapt it for use by a UAV. This includes considering the physical features of the drone's movement, namely: control of four motors simultaneously, rotation along the Z axis to capture the required area of observation by the camera, overcoming inertia when changing the direction of movement, etc. In work, a program code in the Python language was developed for planning UAV movement paths based on the A* and ACO algorithms, flight simulations were performed along the routes calculated by these algorithms, the averaged results were presented, and a method for improving the A* algorithm was proposed, considering the peculiarities of the movement of UAV.

title: Positional Electric Drive Based on Induction Motor with Dual-Zone Velocity Control
authors: Oleksandr Derets, Oleksandr Sadovoi, Serhiy Derets
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: An asynchronous electric motor, being relatively simple in design, reliable and energy-efficient, is a nonlinear, non-stationary dynamic object. A vector control system capable of implementing independent control of its magnetic flux and velocity is most effective when using relay controllers. The high accuracy and speed of such a system make it possible not only to synthesize a positional electric drive on its basis, but also to provide dual-zone speed control, which helps reduce the duration of transient processes. To ensure invariance to parametric and coordinate disturbances, the positional electric drive requires feedback on the derivative of the velocity. The N-i switching method used to optimize the electric drive for speed also makes it possible to synthesize a relay controller for an acceleration observer.

title: Design of Cybersecurity Knowledge Graph Systems Based on Large Language Models
authors: Yichun Li, Fei Xiong
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: In cybersecurity analysis, existing intrusion detection techniques can identify attack behaviors but fail to provide deep contextual information such as exploited vulnerabilities, attack techniques, and defense strategies. Traditional entity recognition and relation extraction methods are difficult to apply directly due to the high sparsity and specialization of cybersecurity domain entity relationships, limiting deep analysis capabilities. To address this problem, this paper proposes a large language model-based cybersecurity knowledge graph construction method. The method adopts a three-stage architecture. The first stage constructs a cybersecurity domain ontology framework and establishes a large model-driven entity relation extraction method, utilizing knowledge extraction capabilities, chain-of-thought learning, and in-context learning of large language models, combined with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to achieve precise extraction of specialized cybersecurity entity relationships. The second stage stores extracted triplet relationships into a graph database to construct a structured cybersecurity knowledge graph, enabling efficient management of security data. The third stage designs a front-end and back-end separated visualization system that displays the knowledge graph through a graphical interface and supports interactive query and analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively extract cybersecurity entity relationships, and the constructed knowledge graph provides comprehensive support for security personnel in attack attribution, vulnerability correlation, and defense strategy formulation.

title: Enhancing Healthcare with Generative AI: A Hybrid GAN–Diffusion Approach for Medical Image Augmentation
authors: Abdallah Amirat, Hafed Zarzour
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) has shown significant promise in healthcare, particularly in medical imaging, by improving data augmentation, diagnostic accuracy, and model generalization. However, existing models such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Diffusion Models face challenges in balancing image realism, structural fidelity, and computational efficiency. GANs often suffer from mode collapse and limited diversity, while diffusion models, though high in fidelity, are computationally intensive and slow to sample. To address these limitations, we propose a Hybrid GAN–Diffusion approach that synergistically combines the strengths of both architectures. Our pipeline first pretrains a StyleGAN2-ADA model to capture coarse-to-fine radiographic structure efficiently, then refines the generated samples using a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM) to enhance anatomical plausibility and diversity. This sequential design fuses the sharpness and speed of GANs with the coverage and robustness of diffusion models, yielding synthetic images of superior quality and clinical utility. We evaluate the framework on the Kaggle Chest X-Ray Pneumonia dataset for training and perform external validation on NIH ChestXray14 to assess cross-dataset generalization. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid model achieves an ROC-AUC of 0.984 in-distribution and 0.892 on the external dataset, outperforming classical augmentation, GAN-only, and diffusion-only baselines. The synthetic data quality is further validated by a simulated Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) of 16.2, indicating high realism and diversity. The proposed method establishes a new state-of-the-art in medical image augmentation, demonstrating that hybrid generative pipelines can effectively overcome data scarcity while improving model robustness and generalization.

title: Feature-level vs. Decision-level Ensemble Learning for Automated Tomato Quality Assessment
authors: Nguyen Tran Dinh Ho, Thi-Thu-Hong Phan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This study proposes and systematically compares two ensemble learning strategies for tomato quality classification: post-softmax ensemble (decision-level) and pre-softmax ensemble (feature-level). Three baseline deep learning (DL) models-ResNet50, MobileNetV2, and InceptionV3- are employed, and experiments are conducted on two tomato quality datasets, including a binary-class dataset and a three-class dataset. Experimental results indicate that the post-softmax ensemble does not provide an improvement compared to the best individual CNN model, whereas the feature-level ensemble combined with feature selection consistently achieves superior and more stable performance across accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Specifically, for the binary problem, the Stacking model, which used only $1 \%$ of the most important features, achieved an accuracy of $94.38 \%$. For the three-class problem, the Support Vector Machine (SVM) and ExtraTrees (ET) classifier, trained on $20 \%$ of the selected features, achieved the highest accuracy of $93.96 \%$. These findings demonstrate that combining features before the softmax layer produces a more informative representation, allowing machine learning classifiers to exploit complementary information among deep networks, and that feature selection significantly reduces computational cost while maintaining or improving classification performance.

title: Influence of Hydrogen Production System on Subsynchronous Oscillation Characteristics of Wind-Solar Series Compensation System
authors: ChenYu Wang, Tao Xu, Kun Zheng, YanNan Lu, Zenan Liu, JiaBao Xing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the proposal of the “double carbon” goal in China, wind power and photovoltaic power generation technologies are being widely adopted. However, large-scale wind-solar grid-connected operations increase the possibility of subsynchronous oscillation arising in the system. To resolve this problem, this paper studies the influence of hydrogen production systems on the subsynchronous oscillation characteristics of wind-solar systems.

title: A Study on Breast Cancer HER2 Status Prediction Based on Visformer and Stacking Ensemble
authors: Zixin Kang, Lifeng Deng, Huizhe Wang, Zhengshuai Tan, Ziqi Wang, Xiaochun Guo
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Breast cancer has emerged as one of the most critical global malignancies, and accurate determination of the patient’s HER2 expression status is crucial for guiding clinical treatment. Consequently, using Radiomics to predict HER2 status non-invasively has become an important research topics. This study address the prediction challenge using a small, highly class-imbalanced breast X-ray dataset. To tackle these issues, we adopt the concept of transfer learning, utilizing the Visformer model for deep feature extraction. Crucial biomarkers are then selected from the high-dimensional feature space using a combined approach of the Spearman Correlation Coefficient and Adaptive Lasso. Finally, a Stacking ensemble framework is employed for sample classification and prediction. The proposed framework effectively mitigates the issues of overfitting or underfitting commonly encountered by single model on small and imbalanced datasets, offering a more robust and accurate solution for the non-invasive prediction of breast cancer HER2 status.

title: Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Load-Slip Behaviour in Composite Space Trusses for Sustainable Structural Design
authors: Kaythry P, Sangeetha P
abstract: This paper explores the prediction of horizontal slip behaviour in composite space truss structures using advanced Machine Learning techniques to promote sustainable and disaster-resilient design. A calibrated finite element model simulates the interaction among decking sheets, shear connectors, and concrete slabs under constant load, with key parameters—decking type/thickness, shear connector dimensions, and concrete grade—systematically varied. Four ML algorithms—XGBoost, Random Forest, AdaBoost, and Gradient Boosting Regressor (GBRT)—were used to predict horizontal slip at the concrete–deck interface. AdaBoost achieved the highest accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.9517$</tex>), with all models exceeding 0.93, confirming strong predictive performance. Profile decking led to higher deformation and slip, highlighting the importance of parameter optimization. The results demonstrate the potential of ensemble ML models in modelling nonlinear slip behaviour and informing efficient, sustainable structural designs. The proposed framework also supports resilience by improving structural performance under extreme events such as earthquakes and hurricanes.

title: SplitGuard: A Robust Defense Framework for Split Federated Learning Against Poisoning Attacks via Spatiotemporal Trajectory Analysis
authors: Like He, Yaochi Zhao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Split Federated Learning (SFL) is a promising paradigm for resource-constrained edge intelligence, combining the computational offloading of Split Learning with the parallel aggregation of Federated Learning. However, SFL’s distributed architecture requires transmitting intermediate activations, creating a vulnerability to poisoning attacks that undermine model convergence. Existing defenses, which typically rely on instantaneous statistical features or auxiliary validation data, often fail in SFL due to a lack of historical tracking or privacy constraints. To address this, we propose SplitGuard, a robust defense framework utilizing spatiotemporal feature analysis and an implicit exclusion mechanism. Without accessing raw private data, SplitGuard identifies anomalous trajectories by extracting high-dimensional activation features across two dimensions: Temporal Consistency and Spatial Correlation. Additionally, we introduce a continuous dynamic reputation system with an "implicit exclusion" strategy to permanently isolate persistent malicious nodes. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10 and MNIST demonstrate that Split-Guard significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods (e.g., Smash Defense). Under high-intensity noise attacks (σ = 2), SplitGuard achieves 72.23% accuracy and maintains robustness even with 40% malicious clients. Ablation studies confirm that the implicit exclusion mechanism is critical for ensuring convergence in untrusted environments.

title: Edge-Aware Adaptive Masking for Enhanced Panoramic Gaussian Splatting
authors: Yunbiao Liu, Chunyi Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Panoramic Gaussian Splatting (PGS) extends 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) to model 360° scenes, yet it faces challenges in achieving high-fidelity rendering, particularly in regions with complex textures and fine geometric structures. The standard densification process in 3DGS, which clones or splits Gaussians based on positional gradient, can be inefficient and noisy in panoramic settings due to spherical projection distortions and the immense scale of the scene. To address this problem, we propose a novel framework, Edge-Aware Adaptive Masked Panoramic Gaussian Splatting (EM-PGS). Our method introduces an Edge-Aware Probabilistic Mask that guides a pruning process. First, a panoramic Gaussian rasterization pipeline is used for Gaussian splatting to avoid stitching artifacts in reprojection. The second key component is the view-consistent edge score, which is derived from 2D edge priors across multiple training views. This score facilitates the retention of essential edge Gaussians, effectively mitigating image blurring artifacts. The targeted methodology effectively suppresses noise and artifacts, which, in turn, facilitates the optimal refinement of the Gaussian population within high-frequency detail regions. Experimental validation on benchmark datasets confirms the remarkable visual quality achieved by EM-PGS through its compact representation. Our method yields a PSNR improvement approaching 4dB and achieves a reduction of around 90% in Gaussian count compared with the existing method.

title: Enhancing Robustness in Food Image Classification with Data Augmentation Strategies
authors: Joel Nathan, Lucas Salvacio Husada, Anderies
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This study investigates how training strategies can enhance the robustness of lightweight convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for fine-grained food image classification. While lightweight models such as EfficientNetV2-B0 and MobileNetV3-Large have gained popularity due to their efficiency, their resilience to real-world conditions remains a challenge. To address this, we evaluate the impact of different training strategies on improving generalization and robustness in food image recognition. Models were trained on the Food-101 dataset under five strategies: baseline, basic augmentation, Mixup, CutMix, and a Mixup+CutMix hybrid approach. The trained models were then evaluated across diverse testing conditions, including clean test sets, corrupted datasets, cross-domain evaluation on FoodX-251, and internet-collected samples to simulate real-world scenarios. Results show that CutMix consistently delivers the highest robustness, reaching peak average accuracy of 74.10% on EfficientNetV2-B0 and 71.41% on MobileNetV3-Large, improving average accuracy by up to 2.51% and 3.89% respectively over baseline. Meanwhile, the Mixup+CutMix hybrid achieves the best balance between clean accuracy and robustness. These findings highlight the critical role of training strategies in developing resilient lightweight models suitable for deployment in practical food recognition applications.

title: Safe Coding
authors: Christoph Kern
abstract: None

title: A Robotic Laser Welding Seam Tracking Method for Complex 3-D Weld Trajectories Based on Geometric Structure Analysis_supp7-3661009.mp4
authors: Juan Liu
abstract: Seam sampling with line-structured-light sensors in laser welding is prone to projection distortion due to the complex 3-D geometry of sheet-metal workpieces, which leads to viewpoint-dependent variations in the sampled seam profile and affects laser incidence direction planning. Moreover, stable target tracking control becomes challenging when the 3-D seam trajectory contains small curvature radii or right-angle transitions. To address these issues, a method is proposed that compensates for projection distortion via deep-learning-based local surface-normal estimation and achieves robust viewpoint control along complex 3-D seam trajectories using prior geometric approximation of the seam trajectory. For normal estimation, seam features are encoded as “point-vector pair” superpixels and extracted by a single-stage deep convolutional network, enabling efficient, robust detection under welding glare, spatter, smoke, and reflections while maintaining boundary stability. Using the “point-vector pair” representation, the laser incidence direction is adaptively adjusted with reference to estimated surface normals to reduce pose uncertainty caused by projection distortion. For viewpoint control, a feedforward leading-direction control algorithm, derived from geometric–numerical analysis of predefined trajectory, ensures stable perception and high-precision continuous tracking along tight curves and right-angle turns. Across two experiments involving different workpiece geometries, the method achieves mean absolute laser focal position errors of 0.16 and 0.20 mm and mean absolute 3-D laser incident-direction errors of 0.99<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> and 1.58<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>, validating its efficiency, stability, and accuracy for sheet-metal laser seam tracking.

title: A Single-Layer Wide-Beam Patch Antenna for Ku-Band Wide-Angle Scanning Phased Arrays
authors: Ming Fu, Fulin Xue, Zhenlin Li, Xinhuai Wang, Yin Li, Yin Xu, Xiaowei Shi
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a single-layer, wide-beam patch antenna for Ku-band wide-angle scanning phased arrays. The antenna is based on a U-slot patch and is designed using characteristic mode analysis (CMA). By sequentially loading inverted-L stubs, grounded vias that connect U-shaped strips, and open U-shaped strips on the U-slot patch, the antenna size is reduced, complementary endfire beams are introduced, and endfire radiation is enhanced, respectively, thereby enabling multiple wide-beam characteristic modes. Then, these modes are simultaneously excited at the optimized location to synthesize a hemispherical E-plane pattern. As a critical component of the proposed antenna, the grounded vias that connect the U-shaped strips are configured as two parallel equivalent inverted-F antenna (IFA) structures, enabling resonance on a low-profile single-layer substrate to produce endfire radiation for expanding beamwidth. The fabricated antenna exhibits an E-plane half-power beamwidth (HPBW) of up to 181°. Its 1×8 linear phased array prototype with defected ground structure (DGS) exhibits mutual coupling below –16.8 dB, a scanning range of ±76° for a 3‑dB gain reduction, and a sidelobe level (SLL) below –8.2 dB across the band. These results indicate the antenna's potential as a terminal in satellite-communications applications such as unmanned aerial vehicle terminals and low earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellations after further optimization.

title: The Role of Purchasing in New Product Development under High Uncertainty: The Case of Hydrogen Fuel Cell Vehicles
authors: Losciuto Alessandro, Arcidiacono Francesco, Di Mauro Carmela, De-León Almaraz Sofía
abstract: This study investigates the role that the purchasing function can play to support new product development (NPD) in highly uncertain technological and market environments. The study focuses on projects in the field of hydrogen-powered vehicles for which a supply chain is currently being developed by several manufacturers. An embedded case study involving a components manufacturer operating in the automotive industry and its suppliers constitutes the setting for the study. Findings show that, when involved early in projects, purchasing plays a pivotal role in NPD by integrating supplier knowledge and coordinating internal and external inputs to projects. This enables firms to manage uncertainty, meet technical and regulatory requirements, and support more effective commercialization of new products. Results contribute to research on purchasing and supplier involvement in NPD and provide guidance to executives called to develop supply chains for highly uncertain business fields.

title: Coded Illumination under Quantized Luminosity for Visual Inspection
authors: Yosuke Naruse
abstract: This paper addresses the optimization of illumination and camera imaging conditions in imaging systems equipped with multi-channel illumination for visual inspection. While previous studies have primarily focused on HDR imaging, in practical settings HDR acquisition is time-consuming and typically performed using a single fixed exposure time. To enable the practical application of coded illumination for visual inspection, we extend the method to jointly optimize camera exposure time along with illumination conditions. We propose a unified framework that optimizes both texture and brightness based on BTF, leveraging the full expressive power of the imaging device under the common hardware constraint that illumination luminance is quantized. Furthermore, we demonstrate that the global optimum of Fisher’s LDA, subject to non-negative light intensity constraints, can be computed using SDP, and this solution effectively enhances foreground contrast. Experimental results show strong agreement between simulated and real images in an actual visual inspection hardware environment, thereby validating the feasibility of illumination condition optimization using a digital twin approach.

title: Energy efficiency optimization in relay link transmissions of ground relay stations in satellite mobile communication networks
authors: Yitao Li, Yongtai Zhang, Longhui Li
abstract: Equipped with solar energy and wind energy supply, satellite ground relay stations (SGRS) can be deployed in remote regions without the support of ground power network to provide communication access for ordinary mobile terminals in satellite mobile communications networks. Due to the instability and limitation of solar energy and wind energy supply, to reduce energy consumption and improve energy efficiency of SGRS is very important. Focusing on the unstable and limited energy problem on SGRS, this article proposes a soft actor-critic based joint resource allocation (SAC-JRA) algorithm to improve the transmission energy efficiency of SGRS. The proposed SAC-JRA algorithm includes a multi-dimensional state space, a hierarchical action space, and a dual-weight reward function to balance energy efficiency and user satisfaction. A lightweight dual-branch neural network is used to decouple power and bandwidth allocation, enhancing decision-making efficiency and adaptability. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed SAC-JRA algorithm achieves significant improvements in transmission energy efficiency compared with existing methods, especially in low traffic load and mixed traffic load environments. The results also show the robustness of the proposed SAC-JRA algorithm in dynamic traffic loads, offering a sustainable solution for energy-constrained wireless communications.

title: GeoFL: A Framework for Efficient Geo-Distributed Cross-Device Federated Learning
authors: Maolin Gan, Lanpeng Li, Samiul Alam, Li Liu, Luyang Liu, Mi Zhang, Huacheng Zeng, Zhichao Cao
abstract: In this paper, GeoFL develops a hierarchical federated learning (FL) framework to address the unique challenges in large-scale geo-distributed scenarios. The key idea is to deploy multiple aggregators to geo-distributed clients and aggregate the local model and the global model efficiently and effectively. By assigning each aggregator as a relay layer, GeoFL can elaborately aggregate the geo-distributed clients and systematically determine when to upload the model to the central server based on bandwidth to efficiently update the global model under inadequate and heterogeneous WAN bandwidth constraints. GeoFL designs three key components to optimize the inefficient model aggregation and cope with the non-importance model updates. It further addresses the statistical heterogeneity across geo-distributed aggregators by considering the clients’ graph relationship, delivering an end-to-end clien-taggregator- server architecture for large-scale clients. Compared with existing works, our results on large-scale real-life datasets show that GeoFL speeds up the training process by 1.4×–8× and reduces 6%–80% unnecessary communication rounds between the aggregator and the central server.

title:
F
                    <sup>4</sup>
                    -CKM: Learning Channel Knowledge Map with Radio Frequency Radiance Field Rendering
authors: Kequan Zhou, Guangyi Zhang, Hanlei Li, Yunlong Cai, Shengli Liu, Guanding Yu
abstract: In 6G mobile communications, acquiring accurate and timely channel state information (CSI) becomes increasingly challenging due to the growing antenna array size and bandwidth. To alleviate the CSI feedback burden, the channel knowledge map (CKM) has emerged as a promising approach by leveraging environment-aware techniques to predict CSI based solely on user locations. However, how to effectively construct a CKM remains an open issue. In this paper, we propose F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup>-CKM, a novel CKM construction framework characterized by four distinctive features: radiance Field rendering, spatial-Frequency-awareness, location-Free usage, and Fast learning. Central to our design is the adaptation of radiance field rendering techniques from computer vision to the radio frequency (RF) domain, enabled by a novel Wireless Radiator Representation (WiRARE) network that captures the spatial-frequency characteristics of wireless channels. Additionally, a novel shaping filter module and an angular sampling strategy are introduced to facilitate CKM construction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup>-CKM significantly outperforms existing baselines in terms of wireless channel prediction accuracy and efficiency.

title: Theoretical Benchmarking of Ni/Co/Ti/Mo-Silicide Contact Resistance on N-Type Doped Silicon_supp1-3664775.docx
authors: Jiwon Chang
abstract: In this study, we investigated the contact resistance at the interfaces between silicon (Si) and four transition metal silicides (TiSi2, CoSi2, NiSi2, and MoSi2) for various n-type doping concentrations, using ab initio simulations. Transmission spectrum and contact resistance were calculated through density functional theory (DFT) combined with the nonequilibrium Green’s function (NEGF) method, providing atomistic insights into the contact resistance. Two complementary approaches were employed: the valley filtering method, which estimates the theoretical lower limit of contact resistance by comparing the intrinsic transmission modes of Si and silicides, and two-terminal DFT-NEGF calculations on explicit silicide/Si interface structures that incorporate realistic interfacial effects such as Schottky barrier (SB), interface scattering, and metal-induced gap states (MIGSs). TiSi2 and NiSi2 consistently demonstrated the lowest contact resistance due to superior transmission mode alignment with Si, whereas CoSi2 exhibited the highest resistance for all orientations. MoSi2 showed comparable resistance in the (100) and (111) orientations with NiSi2 and TiSi2, but slightly higher resistance in the (110) orientation. These findings suggest that, in addition to TiSi2 and NiSi2, MoSi2 could be a promising contact silicide for advanced technology nodes where minimizing resistance is critical to device performance.

title: Ensuring Data Integrity for Consumer-Centric Digital Twins in Healthcare 5.0 via a Blockchain-Oriented Signature
authors: Yunfan Hu, Zhisheng Liu, Zeping Ma, Liming Fang, Zhe Liu, Changgen Peng, Abubaker Wahaballa, Shahid Mumtaz, Hu Xiong
abstract: With the rapid development of consumer electronics and the healthcare sector, Healthcare 5.0 has emerged, integrating multiple advanced information technologies to create human-centered systemic healthcare services. Among these technologies, consumer-centric digital twins (CCDTs) provide patients with virtual models, enhancing medical risk management and health guidance, while simultaneously imposing higher requirements on data security and service efficiency. Blockchain addresses this by providing a secure, decentralized framework that eliminates trusted intermediaries. Within this framework, distributed signatures can enable consensus for collaborative multi-party processes to ensure the integrity of medical data, thereby providing a reliable data source for CCDTs in Healthcare 5.0 to a certain extent. However, existing distributed signatures for healthcare systems still struggle with low efficiency and exfiltration attacks. This is due to the unique demands of data-sensitive medical consumer electronics, which necessitate high efficiency to meet strict medical timeliness. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a non-interactive identity-based threshold signature scheme with cryptographic reverse firewalls (CRFs) for CCDTs in Healthcare 5.0 (NI-IBTS-CRF). The scheme eliminates reliance on public key infrastructure via identity-based cryptography and adopts a non-interactive signing paradigm to achieve higher signing efficiency and reduce energy consumption for medical devices. Additionally, the scheme incorporates efficient CRFs that enable fast rerandomization to mitigate exfiltration attacks inherent in existing solutions. Experimental results demonstrate that the CRFs introduce only around 10% additional overhead, while NI-IBTS-CRF still achieves leading signing efficiency compared to existing schemes, thus providing a secure and signing-efficient approach to safeguarding data integrity for CCDTs in Healthcare 5.0.

title: IEEE Electron Device Letters Information for Authors
authors: N/A

title: Construction and Analysis of Cortico-Muscular Coupling Networks Using Transfer Entropy Computed After High-Dimensional Time-Delay Embedding
authors: Jiaming Huang, Yongcheng Li
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Cortico-muscular coupling reflects the interaction between the cerebral cortex and muscles during motor control. This study proposes a transfer entropy model based on time-delay mutual information and high-dimensional signal embedding (HDEmbedDMI-TE) to construct cortico-muscular coupling networks. Electroencephalography and electromyography were recorded from healthy participants and stroke patients during a five-finger grasping (FG) task. After preprocessing, HDEmbedDMI-TE was used to quantify the direction and strength of information flow between cortical regions and muscles. Simulation experiments with known causal directions validated the proposed method, demonstrating accurate detection of directional coupling and a $\mathbf{4. 8}$-fold improvement in computational efficiency compared with conventional Transfer Entropy. Network analysis revealed significant directional coupling during FG, with information predominantly flowing from the motor cortex to muscles in healthy individuals, whereas stroke patients showed impaired motor cortical regulation and compensatory involvement of cognitive-related regions. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of HDEmbedDMI-TE for corticomuscular coupling analysis and its potential value for motor control research and neurorehabilitation assessment. This work advances signal processing methodologies for multimodal data through efficient nonlinear coupling and network analysis.

title: Implementation of a Real-Time Seismic Data Stream Visualization System for Earthquake Monitoring Operations
authors: Sheng Guan, Jianhong Kang, Fan Zhang, Yu Zhang, Hongyan Liu, Xiaodong Pan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: For the visualization of volcanic seismic monitoring data stream, this paper proposes and implements a real-time volcanic seismic data visualization system. The system acquires seismic data streams in real time through the Server-Sent Events (SSE) protocol, stores them in miniSEED format files after parsing, merging, etc., and realizes data visualization relying on VUE and Echarts modules, it supports Windows and Linux systems. Finally, real-time seismic data from 5 stations of Changbaishan Tianchi Volcano Seismic Network were used for validation, and the results show that the system achieves real-time visualization of seismic data and runs stably. The system meets the real-time data stream processing requirements of the seismic monitoring system and provides technical support for volcano monitoring and early warning operations.

title: Truncated Gumbel Distribution-Based Counterfactual Generation for Chinese Judicial Documents
authors: Qinhua Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Counterfactual generation techniques play a pivotal role in judicial scenarios by constructing virtual scenarios that answer “what-if”questions, thereby facilitating legal reasoning, case comparison, and the explanation of judicial decisions. However, Chinese judicial documents, characterized by their stringent professionalism, logical rigor, and domain-specific causal relationships, pose unique challenges: conventional generation methods often fail to modify target elements while preserving legal validity and semantic coherence. To address this, this paper proposes a truncated Gumbel distribution-based approach for counterfactual generation of Chinese judicial documents. Leveraging pre-trained language models, the method reconstructs the probabilistic generation process to enable rigorous causal inference. Firstly, inspired by the Gumbel-max trick, the token sampling mechanism is decomposed into deterministic logits computation and stochastic noise injection, where noise variables follow a truncated Gumbel distribution to constrain semantic deviation between counterfactual and original texts; Secondly A posterior noise inference algorithm is designed to reverse-engineer latent noise parameters from observed judicial documents, ensuring that under intervention, counterfactual texts only alter target causal variables while retaining legal logic and document structure; Lastly Domain-specific constraints are integrated to validate the legal accuracy of generated content, such as the consistency between cited legal provisions and case types. Experimental results on the legal dataset demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms baseline models in terms of intervention precision, legal accuracy, and text coherence.

title: Safety-Guaranteed Robust Control for Lane Changing Path Tracking of Underactuated Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Kuineng Chen, Xiaofang Yuan, Xiangcheng Pan
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Path tracking control is a fundamental function for autonomous vehicle (AV), especially during safety-critical lane changing maneuvers. However, time-varying uncertainties and underactuated characteristics present serious challenges to tracking accuracy, safety and vehicle stability. To this end, this paper proposes a safety-guaranteed robust control strategy, consisting of three stages. First, a safety corridor is established to explicitly constrain the AV’s trajectory within a predefined safe region during lane changing. Second, a diffeomorphism-based state transformation is employed to enable control design beyond state restrictions. The tracking objectives are embedded into equality constraints to formulate a constraint-following task. Third, a matched-mismatched uncertainty decomposition approach is applied to isolate the adverse effects of underactuation. On this basis, a robust control law is developed to ensure constraint-following performance. Extensive CarSim-Simulink co-simulation experiments validate the proposed control strategy’s ability to achieve superior tracking accuracy, enhanced lateral stability and guaranteed tracking safety for lane changing scenarios.

title: Research on Intelligent Processing System for Classroom Videos Based on Multimodal Perception and Self-Recovery Watermarking
authors: Xiaodong Dai
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: With the popularization of smart classrooms, classroom videos have become key data for teaching analysis and evaluation. However, current systems face three major challenges: massive video transmission and storage pressure, content security and integrity risks, and accuracy of automatic student state analysis. This paper proposes an intelligent classroom video processing system that integrates lightweight perceptual multitask compression, temporal robust invisible watermarking, and self-recovering watermarking technology. The system embeds verifiable invisible watermarks before video compression to ensure data authenticity and integrity; during the compression stage, it adopts adaptive rate allocation and optimized H.265/HEVC encoding based on visual saliency to reduce bandwidth consumption; in the post-analysis stage, it constructs a deep learning model based on pose-expression-behavior fusion to assess students' listening state in real time. The innovation lies in the introduction of a self-recovering watermark mechanism, which combines absolute moment block truncation coding and reference sharing strategy, enabling the system to achieve a normalized coefficient of 0.982 for watermark similarity under 10% random frame loss attack, and a peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) of not less than 38.5dB for restored images after video tampering. Experiments based on a self-built classroom video dataset show that the system's end-to-end processing delay is controlled within 142ms, with an accuracy of 96.3% for predicting students' overall listening rate and an F1 score of 0.95. This study provides a low-cost, high-reliability, end-to-end secure technical solution for university teaching management, with significant theoretical value and broad practical prospects.

title: Improving Graph Embeddings in Machine Learning Using Knowledge Completion with Validation in a Case Study on COVID-19 Spread
authors: Rosario Napoli, Gabriele Morabito, Antonio Celesti, Massimo Villari, Maria Fazio
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: The rise of graph-structured data has driven major advances in Graph Machine Learning (GML), where graph embeddings (GEs) map features from Knowledge Graphs (KGs) into vector spaces, enabling tasks like node classification and link prediction. However, since GEs are derived from explicit topology and features, they may miss crucial implicit knowledge hidden in seemingly sparse datasets, affecting graph structures and their representation. We propose a GML pipeline that integrates a Knowledge Completion (KC) phase to uncover latent dataset semantics before embedding generation. Focusing on transitive relations, we model hidden connections with decay-based inference functions, reshaping graph topology, with consequences on embedding dynamics and aggregation processes in GraphSAGE and Node2Vec. Experiments show that our GML pipeline significantly alters the embedding space geometry, demonstrating that its introduction is not just a simple enrichment but a transformative step that redefines graph representation quality.

title: Variable Frequency Simulation Analysis of the Insulation Electric Field of Stator Windings
authors: Yingjie Guo, Xinpei Wang, Xianfeng Li, Jian Wang, Hanwen Ren, Ikromjon Usmonovich Rakhmonov
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The electric field distribution of the stator winding insulation of the auxiliary motor in thermal power plants under the action of variable frequency stress was analyzed. A two-dimensional finite element model was established to study the influence of voltage waveforms and frequencies. The results show that, compared with sinusoidal wave excitation, the electric field generated by square wave voltage increases very sharply, and the increase in frequency causes the surface electric field to increase due to the skin effect. These research results provide guidance for insulation design under variable frequency operating conditions.

title: A Vital Sign Extraction Method for IR-UWB Radar Based on Adaptive Tracking and Sliding Singular Spectrum Analysis Under Micro-Motion Interference
authors: Heng Xu, Die Hu, Long Sun, Cheng Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Non-contact vital sign monitoring is increasingly important in emergency response, security surveillance, and intelligent healthcare. However, conventional fixed range-bin and static-target–based methods degrade significantly when subjects exhibit micro-motions, defined in this study as chest range excursions with peak-to-peak displacement within approximately 0–5 cm and without large body translation, causing respiratory–heart mixing and reduced estimation accuracy. This paper proposes a robust extraction framework for micro-motion scenarios based on adaptive tracking and Sliding Singular Spectrum Analysis (Sliding-SSA). Static clutter suppression and bandpass filtering are first applied to enhance physiological frequency bands. Moving Target Indication (MTI) combined with Ordered-Statistic Constant False Alarm Rate (OS-CFAR) is then used for target detection, and an energy-centroid–driven Kalman filter estimates a continuous human trajectory. A dynamic range window and phase-motion compensation are further introduced to remove macro displacement trends. Finally, Sliding-SSA with soft frequency criteria separates respiratory and heart components. Experiments on 15 measurements within 0.5m–2m demonstrate average errors of 0.7 bpm for respiratory measurements and 1.8 bpm for heart rate, outperforming fixed range-bin methods. The results confirm the robustness of the proposed method and the improved component separation for non-contact vital sign detection.

title: Power Source Improvement for Nanosecond Pulsed Barrier Discharge Generation System
authors: Oleksii Karlov, Vladyslav Bereka, Ihor Bozhko, Ihor Kondratenko, Vasyl Chopyk, Hanna Gai
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: To study the possibility of using a controlled skew bridge in the power source of the ozonizer discharge chamber to create short (~100 ns) voltage pulses of several tens of kV, a low-voltage part with a pulse transformer was developed. Studies of this structure in combination with a high-voltage pulse transformer were carried out for two types of load - active and capacitive, acting as an alternative to the discharge electrode system. Proposed installation generates voltage pulses up to 15 kV, with a duration of 1.25 μs and an energy of ~1 J, which makes it possible to simplify the high-voltage part, reduce the requirements for the following high-voltage sections and their compression in time. The circuit proposed in the article, unlike thyristor parametric systems, allows you to adjust the pulse parameters.

title: A Detection Method for High Impedance Grounding Fault Based on Self-Supervised Contrastive Learning
authors: Yongjia Huang, Zeyin Zheng, Moufa Guo, Wei Gao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: High impedance ground faults (HIGF) in distribution networks present considerable challenges for detection. They typically exhibit minimal observable signatures and display a high degree of randomness. Conventional techniques for HIGF detection often depend on extensive labeled datasets to establish threshold values. This dependency not only incurs significant labor costs but also struggles to extract deeper latent information from fault data, potentially leading to misclassification. To address these limitations, this research introduces a self-supervised contrastive learning framework for HIGF detection in distribution networks. The proposed approach learns generalizable features directly from unlabeled zero-sequence current (ZSC) samples by maximizing similarity between augmentations of the same sample, thereby minimizing reliance on labeled data. Subsequent fine-tuning with a minimal amount of labeled ZSC data enables accurate identification of high impedance grounding faults. Simulations validate that the model effectively learns discriminative features from ZSC waveforms. Notably, it achieves performance comparable to fully supervised models while utilizing only 1 % of their labeled training data. Additionally, it exhibits superior noise resistance in systems with signal-tonoise ratios of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5 d B} \boldsymbol{,} \mathbf{3 0 d B}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 d B}$</tex>.

title: An INN-Mamba Fusion Framework with Two-Stage Training for SAR and Visible Images
authors: Yunzhong Yan, Jun Li, Zhen Liu, La Jiang, Chen Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Existing SAR and visible image fusion methods based on CNNs or Transformers often struggle to capture long-range dependencies and preserve feature fidelity. To address these issues, this paper presents an INN-Mamba fusion framework with two-stage training, integrating the reversible feature decomposition capability of Invertible Neural Networks (INN) with the efficient sequence modeling of the Mamba architecture. An INN-based Feature Decoupling Module achieves lossless separation of high- and low-frequency components, while a Coupled Mamba Module leverages the selective state-space mechanism to perform adaptive cross-modal fusion with linear complexity. A two-stage training strategy, consisting of reconstruction pre-training and fusion training, introduces a complementary reconstruction criterion to guide accurate frequency separation and stable optimization. Experiments on the YYX dataset demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches in both visual quality and quantitative metrics, particularly in preserving edges and structural consistency.

title: Recommendation Method for Electricity Retail Plans Based on Multi-Dimensional Evaluation and Social Network
authors: Xueyan Liang, Huan Xu, Lihong Wang, Lei Yao, Yuxuan Zhou, Yuanqian Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the advancement of electricity market reform, accurate electricity retail plan (ERP) recommendation is key for electricity sellers to enhance their competitiveness. With the advancement of electricity market reform, accurate ERR recommendation is key for electricity sellers to enhance their competitiveness. Existing methods often rely on singledimensional evaluation metrics, fail to fully account for the influence of social trust among customers, or ignore differences in the confidence level of users' evaluation results-these limitations lead to significant deviations in recommendation outcomes. To address this issue, this paper proposes a retail plan recommendation method that integrates multi-dimensional customer evaluations, customer confidence adjustment, and social network trust: it constructs a plan attribute matrix to capture comprehensive preferences, uses the maximum deviation method to determine objective attribute weights and plan scores, and quantifies customer weights through social trust propagation. Experiments conducted on 726 customers in eastern China development zones show that the method's recommendation results for new customers are highly consistent with actual choices, providing support for electricity sellers to accurately recommend ERRs and mine market demands.

title: Mind the Context: Enriching Knowledge Graphs with Rules and Mappings
authors: Veronica Dos Santos, Daniel De Oliveira, Daniel Schwabe, Edward Hermann Haeusler, Fernanda Baiao, Sérgio Lifschitz
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: In exploratory search scenarios, users frequently turn to Knowledge Graphs (KGs) as rich sources of structured knowledge. However, user queries are often imprecise, incomplete, or ambiguous, and KGs themselves are inherently incomplete. Moreover, when KGs are constructed from crowd-sourced content or by integrating heterogeneous data sources of varying quality, the need for mechanisms to assess information reliability becomes critical. This work introduces Context-Augmented Knowledge Graphs (CoaKG), an enriched KG model designed to support context-aware exploration for decision-making and task learning. CoaKG incorporates both explicit contextual mappings and inference rules to derive implicit context, enabling more nuanced query answering. We also present the CoaKG Query Engine, a query processing framework to retrieve contextually enriched answers tailored to users' goals and context.

title: Intent-Driven Smart Manufacturing Integrating Knowledge Graphs and Large Language Models
authors: Takoua Jradi, John Violos, Dimitrios Spatharakis, Lydia Mavraidi, Ioannis Dimolitsas, Aris Leivadeas, Symeon Papavassiliou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: The increasing complexity of smart manufacturing environments demands interfaces that can translate high-level human intents into machine-executable actions. This paper presents a unified framework that integrates instruction-tuned Large Language Model (LLM) with ontology-aligned Knowledge Graphs (KGs) to enable intent-driven interaction in Manufacturing-as-a-Service (MaaS) ecosystems. We fine-tune Mistral-7B-Instruct-V0.2 on a domain-specific dataset, enabling the translation of natural language intents into structured JSON requirement models. These models are semantically mapped to a Neo4j-based KG grounded in the ISA-95 standard, ensuring operational alignment with manufacturing processes, resources, and constraints. Our experimental results demonstrate significant performance gains over zero-shot and 3-shot inference baselines using the pre-trained Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 model without fine-tuning, achieving 89.33% exact match accuracy and 97.27% overall accuracy. This work lays the foundation for scalable, and adaptive human-machine collaboration in smart manufacturing, with promising implications for real-time applications.

title: Impact of Self-Shading on Solar Irradiance for Venlo Greenhouse Agrivoltaics in High Latitudes
authors: Yuanbiao Deng, Tianyue Sun, Ekaterina Terukova, Vadim Davydov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Agrivoltaics integrated into Venlo-type greenhouses present a compelling approach to land-sharing but encounter substantial self-shading issues in high-latitude areas. This study evaluates how structural shading affects the solar radiation reaching photovoltaic (PV) panels. A 3D model of a standard greenhouse in Saint Petersburg, Russia (latitude 59.9 degrees North), was used to simulate hourly shading from October to February, applying the Silva shading factor and Liu-Jordan sky models. The results indicate a pronounced decline in winter energy yield. February exhibited the highest monthly radiation loss at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0}$</tex> percent, despite December having the lowest solar elevation and a lower loss rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 8}$</tex> percent. This counterintuitive trend is explained by local weather conditions-specifically, the ratio of clear-sky days with high Direct Normal Irradiance (DNI) to overcast days dominated by Diffuse Horizontal Irradiance (DHI). Radiation losses are closely tied to atmospheric conditions rather than shading alone. These insights offer a foundation for optimizing the design and assessing the efficiency of agrivoltaic systems in high latitudes region.

title: Implementation of a Cascaded PID Controller for AGV Path Tracking in Intelligent Parking Systems
authors: Pengfei Han
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) plays a crucial role in intelligent parking systems, functioning as essential tools for vehicle transportation and scheduling during parking and retrieval processes, which substantially improves the overall operational efficiency of the system. As a classic control method, Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) control is widely employed in motor speed regulation systems due to its strong robustness and broad applicability. However, few studies have integrated motor control with AGV systems, with most existing research focusing on path planning and multi-AGV coordination. This paper presents the design and implementation of a cascaded PID control system for AGV in intelligent parking environments. The system adopts a dual-loop control structure with an inner velocity loop and an outer angle loop. Experimental results based on a simulated vehicle equipped with an MSPM0G3507 microcontroller, Hall encoders, and an inertial measurement unit (IMU) demonstrate that the proposed method not only effectively suppresses oscillations and improves trajectory tracking accuracy, but also validates its feasibility for AGV control in structured environments.

title: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)-Based, K-Band Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR)
authors: Yaxuan Li, Ali Bekar, Marco Martorella, Michail Antoniou
abstract: The paper proposes the study and development of Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) from compact, high-frequency radar sensors onboard commercial Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs, often referred to as “drones”) to create precision Digital Elevation Models (DEMs). The potential of such InSAR systems, due to the higher operating frequency and target proximity, is quantified, but it is also shown how the same features, combined with the instability of UAV platforms, lead to motion errors that either deteriorate or altogether deny capability. The paper thus quantifies acceptable motion error limits and shows how complex autofocus can restore height estimation performance, through analytical modelling that is verified by simulation and validated through outdoor experiments with a 24 GHz UAV-based demonstrator. To our knowledge this is the first demonstration of a UAV-based InSAR system in this high-frequency band.

title: Electromagnetic, Thermal, and Mechanical Modelling of Thin-Wall HTS Bulks During PFM
authors: Santiago Guijosa, K´evin Berger, Frederic Trillaud, Melika Hinaje
abstract: High-temperature superconductors, especially rare earth barium copper oxide (REBCO) bulks, are promising for superseding permanent magnets in power applications. However, being essentially ceramics, their practical use is limited by their brittleness that turns into mechanical failures during high-field magnetization. These failures often arise from defects that appear during the fabrication process, such as pores and cracks. This fragility may be mitigated by including artificial holes in the bulks. The number, size and distributions of these holes may not just improve cooling, but they may also reduce porosity, and enhance both mechanical strength and flux pinning. Modelling the impact of such holes on the trapping performance of the bulks and their thermo-mechanical response to magnetization, particularly the pulsed field magnetization (PFM) that is considered practical for actual applications, is challenging and requires a multiphysics approach. In that context, the present work introduces a 2D finite element model (FEM) that includes coupled electromagnetic, thermal and mechanical physics to simulate thin-wall REBCO bulks with ring reinforcement. The numerical results provide insight on how to maximize trapped magnetic fields while minimizing mechanical loads with thin-wall reinforced REBCO bulks.

title: A Hybrid ASL/PSC Converter With Oscillation Mitigation Across Switches and Reduced Voltage Stress
authors: Chaofan Cui, Tianmin Liao, Linxiao Gong, Junzhong Xu, Feng Zhang, Yong Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: In photovoltaic system, high voltage gain DC-DC converters are required. Hybrid active switched inductor (ASL) / passive switched capacitor (PSC) DC-DC converters are suitable for photovoltaic applications due to their high voltage gain characteristics. However, when the two inductors or the two parasitic capacitors of the switches in such ASL/PSC hybrid converters are mismatched, significant voltage oscillation occur across the switches, which increase the switch voltage stress. Additionally, the ground potential difference between the input and output ports is not constant, which increases EMI issues. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel hybrid ASL/PSC converter. The proposed converter has no switch voltage oscillation and thus reduces the switch voltage stress. Moreover, the proposed converter has a constant ground potential difference. It also exhibits high voltage gain, lower voltage stress across the output diodes, and higher efficiency. To validate the theoretical analysis, a laboratory prototype was built and tested at an input voltage range of 20V-60V, an output voltage of 400V, and a rated power of 200W.

title: DAMind: Zero-shot Visual Cross-Domain Alignment and Representation for EEG Decoding
authors: Haodong Jing, Yongqiang Ma, Panqi Yang, Haoyu Li, Shuai Huang, Badong Chen, Nanning Zheng
abstract: To efficiently assist humans in various tasks, it is crucial to accurately decode and understand the rich information embedded in brain’s visual cognition. Existing brain-driven research often fails to overcome the challenge of small target data domains, and the lack of explicit semantic, spatial, and other information constraints on feature extractors prevents brain decoding models from learning uniform cross-domain representations, leading to degradation of their performance in unseen domains. To overcome these limitations, we propose DAMind, a multimodal EEG-based model for robust visual cross-domain alignment and decoding. Our approach integrates VLM with brain-inspired cognitive mechanisms, leveraging the strong image-text representation abilities to learn both fine-grained primary visual features and high-level semantic concepts from neural signals, provide effective visual fine-tuning using the visual guidance mechanism. DAMind introduces a stepwise EEG encoding process aligned with visual processing, and employs an instruction-based learning strategy for effective cross-domain zero-shot transfer. Its robust architecture efficiently achieves good generalization performance, enabling the mapping of EEG signals from multiple domains to a unified learning domain. We construct a comprehensive EEG decoding benchmark EBench, DAMind achieves state-of-the-art results on several visual tasks, and outperforms the baseline in zero-shot setting.

title: Redundant Hierarchical Ring All-Reduce in Hypercubes
authors: Huimei Guo, Shuo Quan, Rong-Xia Hao, Jie Wu
abstract: Distributed training of large language models demands efficient gradient synchronization strategies. Existing All Reduce algorithms often fail to fully leverage underlying topological properties, resulting in low edge utilization and unbalanced load in hypercube networks. This paper proposes a Redundant Hierarchical Ring All-Reduce (RHRA) algorithm tailored for hypercube topologies. The algorithm first designs a hierarchical Ring All-Reduce structure with high edge utilization by exploiting the hypercube's inherent high parallelism and symmetry, proving that the optimal number of hierarchical layers is n 2. It then introduces controlled redundancy, dynamically adjusting transmission strategies based on the relationship between the value of bandwidth B and the value of data volume D to reduce communication hops and transmission latency while ensuring reliability. Experiments demonstrate that our algorithm achieves the minimum number of gradient synchronization hops under varying bandwidth constraints, with end-to-end transmission time significantly outperforming existing schemes. Further reliability simulations verify its effectiveness in balancing redundancy to prevent resource waste while maintaining high reliability.

title: Symlectic periodic convolution tracking method: Algorithm and Applications
authors: Jiwang Zhang, Jian Cheng, Renyang He, Rongren Wang, Haiyang Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The periodic pulse features induced by early damage to rolling bearings under a strong noise background are weak, and the signal decomposition method based on the symplectic geometry similarity transformation theory is difficult to solve such feature extraction problems. In this regard, this paper proposes the symplectic periodic convolution tracking (SPCT) method to achieve accurate extraction of weak state features. The SPCT method achieves accurate estimation of the fault period by constructing a reweighted cyclic kurtosis spectrum (RCKS). The RCKS method makes full use of the periodicity and impact of fault features, using the periodic unit pulse sequence as a weight vector to perform convolution operations with the original vibration signal. By plotting a spectral curve of the reweighted cyclic kurtosis value as a function of the candidate period, significant peaks in the curve correspond to the true failure period. Then a truncation matrix is constructed based on the estimated period, and accurate periodic pulse extraction is achieved through SGST. Through simulation and measured signals, the superiority of the period estimation technology based on RCKS are verified, and the SPCT method is verified to be an excellent periodic pulse feature extraction method.

title: Meanshift Shape Formation Control Using Discrete Mass Distribution
authors: Yichen Cai, Yuan Gao, Pengpeng Li, Wei Wang, Guibin Sun, Jinhu Lü
abstract: The density-distribution method has recently become a promising paradigm owing to its adaptability to variations in swarm size. However, existing studies face practical challenges in achieving complex shape representation and decentralized implementation. This motivates us to develop a fully decentralized, distribution-based control strategy with the dual capability of forming complex shapes and adapting to swarm-size variations. Specifically, we first propose a discrete mass-distribution function defined over a set of sample points to model swarm formation. In contrast to the continuous density-distribution method, our model eliminates the requirement for defining continuous density functions—a task that is difficult for complex shapes. Second, we design a decentralized meanshift control law to coordinate the swarm’s global distribution to fit the sample-point distribution by feeding back mass estimates. The mass estimates for all sample points are achieved by the robots in a decentralized manner via the designed mass estimator. It is shown that the mass estimates of the sample points can asymptotically converge to the true global values. To validate the proposed strategy, we conduct comprehensive simulations and real-world experiments to evaluate the efficiency of complex shape formation and adaptability to swarm-size variations.

title: RFHA-YOLO: Dynamic Receptive Field and Adaptive Hybrid Attention for Small-Object Detection in Remote Sensing Images
authors: Xiaobo Liu, Yiting Zheng, Yaoming Cai, Yao Ding, Jun Li, Weijie Kang, Zhihua Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Small object detection in remote sensing images remains challenging due to limited feature resolution and complex backgrounds. Conventional detectors, due to fixed receptive fields and uniform attention, struggle to capture small-target features and suffer from background clutter. To address these limitations, we propose RFHA-YOLO, a novel method designed to enhance small-object feature extraction. RFHA-YOLO integrates two key innovations. Firstly, a dynamic kernel decomposition module with an embedded Receptive Field Attention Block (RFA-Block) is constructed within the backbone as an alternative to the conventional spatial pyramid pooling structure. The core of RFA-Block is the RFAConv layer, dynamically adjusts receptive fields across large-kernel branches to enable fine-grained detail preservation for small targets while suppressing irrelevant background regions. Secondly, a enhanced Hybrid Attention Transformer (AHAT) is introduced in the feature fusion stage, enabling the adaptive integration of channel attention, spatial attention, and window-based self-attention. By learning importance scores at each pyramid level, this approach dynamically balances accuracy and computational efficiency in feature fusion. Extensive experiments on the DIOR, USOD, and AI-TODV2 aerial benchmarks have been demonstrated to show that RFHA-YOLO outperforms several state-of-the-art detectors under similar model complexity. It achieves mAP50 scores of 84.8%, 72.3% and 78.1%, reflecting overall detection accuracy, and impressive mAP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50</sub> scores of 28.8%, 10.0% and 33.8%, which specifically measure performance on small objects. These results confirm the effectiveness of RFHA-YOLO for smcall object detection in remote sensing images, as its integrated RFA-Block and AHAT synergistically enhance small object feature capture and representation refinement.

title: Cross-Image Federated Learning for Hyperspectral Image Classification_supp1-3655587.pdf
authors: Xiangrong Zhang
abstract: The contemporary research paradigm in remote sensing hyperspectral monitoring increasingly relies on multisatellite and multiplatform Earth observation. While the traditional hyperspectral research framework based on single-image processing (SIP) has facilitated the application of idealized scenarios and the development of standardized evaluation benchmarks, it inherently constrains the model’s ability to generalize feature representations across varying spatial and temporal domains. As hyperspectral data applications grow in complexity and data requirements, the limitations of SIP in addressing the demands of modern remote sensing tasks become increasingly apparent. To overcome these research limitations, we utilize the decentralized nature and data security features of federated learning to propose a cross-image hyperspectral image (HSI) federated learning approach for classification tasks. We first develop a client-oriented self-guided knowledge-enhanced personalized learning method that enhances the personalization of the local learning process by leveraging relevant features from other clients, thereby improving the learning efficiency of each client. To address the issue of “bias” in global knowledge caused by uneven data distribution across the federated learning process, we introduce a multiscale semantic aligned dynamic aggregation method to ensure fairness in integrating global knowledge. To our knowledge, this article is the first to explore the joint learning of HSI classification using federated learning. Accordingly, we have constructed open-set and closed-set datasets tailored to this task and have demonstrated the effectiveness of our method on these datasets. The code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Gallipaxi/FedHIC</uri>.

title: AI-Driven RF Detection of Solutes in Water
authors: Manal K. Fattoum, Heba El-Halabi, Sarah M. Ibrahim, Hafez Al Khatib, Nour Alhoda Hajir, Habib El Rahman Shouman, Ziad Doughan
abstract: Water quality monitoring is critical for public health and environmental sustainability. This paper introduces a novel integration of radio frequency (RF) antenna sensing with machine learning to enable low-cost, real-time monitoring of water quality. The antenna, designed in High-Frequency Structure Simulator (HFSS) and fabricated on a Rogers RO4003C substrate (εr = 3.55, thickness = 0.813 mm), is a compact wideband square loop microstrip antenna.The antenna’s copper-to-copper area is only 486.58 mm2 (22 mm × 22.115 mm). The antenna operates across two distinct bands (3.88 – 8.77 GHz and 9.99 – 12.85 GHz), offering a total bandwidth of 7.75 GHz. It functions as a dielectric sensor, detecting salt and sugar concentrations in water by observing shifts in the reflection coefficient (S11) induced by solute-dependent permittivity changes. Measurements were performed using a Vector Network Analyzer (9 kHz–13.6 GHz), and the results confirmed clear correlations between solute concentration and antenna response. To improve solute classification, a hybrid stacking ensemble machine learning pipeline is proposed that fuses extracted antenna-based features with statistical water quality data. Multiple base learners including Random Forest, Logistic Regression, KNN, LightGBM, and CatBoost were optimized and stacked using meta-learner. The ensemble achieved 92% accuracy, outperforming a baseline of a neural network. This work establishes a new paradigm in antenna-based sensing by merging RF hardware with interpretable AI, advancing the development of intelligent, real-time environmental monitoring systems.

title: Radar QR Code based on Metasurfaces with Phase-Gradient Dispersion
authors: Assaf Stav, Dmytro Vovchuk, Konstantin Grotov, Mykola Khobzei, Vladyslav Tkach, Vjaceslavs Bobrovs, Pavel Ginzburg
abstract: Coded radar reflectors are increasingly employed in applications requiring reliable identification, tracking, and improved signal integrity. In contrast to conventional active transponders, passive solutions offer greater robustness by placing the task of information retrieval on the radar interrogation system, thereby supporting the joint communication paradigm of enhancing the capabilities of existing channels. We introduce passive radar QR codes composed of engineered metasurface pixels, designed to interface with automotive FMCW radar systems widely deployed on modern vehicle platforms to enhance road safety. Designed for the 76-81 GHz automotive range, the metasurface pixels generate a strong reflection phase gradient across the band. We demonstrate that FMCW signal processing, which maps phase information to estimated range, interprets a phase chirp as an artificial shift in the detected target distance. Consequently, metasurfaces with distinct phase chirps encode information analogous to Braille, enabling radar systems with adequate angular resolution to decode them as QR codes. We validated this concept by designing and fabricating several phase-chirped metasurfaces on PCB substrates and characterizing their performance in an anechoic chamber. The single-ring metasurface design achieves approximately 330° of phase excursion and yields a measured artificial range shift of 7.43 cm over a 500 MHz band centered at 80 GHz. Multiple QR code patterns were constructed and successfully detected. To expand the dataset, data augmentation techniques were applied, and a custom-designed convolutional neural network (CNN) demonstrated strong detection capabilities for the proposed system. On an augmented dataset of 1024 QR code samples represented as 224×224×3 heatmaps, a ResNet50V2-based CNN exceeds 85% classification accuracy at SNR values of 10-15 dB and achieves a pixel position mean absolute error below 10% at SNR values above 15 dB. Radar QR codes hold promises for serving as passive Vehicle Identification Numbers (VINs), enabling direct interrogation by standard automotive radar systems and paving the way for secure, infrastructure-independent identification in future autonomous mobility scenarios.

title: An End-to-End Learning Model with Quantization Technology for Dynamic Prediction-Decision Joint-Making in Active Distribution Networks
authors: Fenglin Cai, Zhiming Dong, Xiaodong Yang, Jie Li, Zhixiang Hao, Yaoyao He, Helong Li
abstract: Efficient load management and system stability in active distribution networks (ADNs) depend heavily on accurate load forecasting and dynamic decision-making. This paper proposes a novel end-to-end joint learning model for dynamic prediction-decision joint-making in active distribution networks. The model integrates a prediction module, leveraging long short-term memory networks to forecast photovoltaic (PV) generation, active power load, and reactive power load, with a control decision module based on Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3). The forecasting outputs are fed into the decision-making module, enabling dynamic adjustments to network parameters. A feedback mechanism is introduced that incorporates decision losses into the prediction module to enhance forecasting accuracy, thereby creating a tightly coupled, end-to-end joint optimization framework. In addition, a mixed precision quantization technology is adopted to reduce computational overhead and facilitate efficient deployment. Experimental results on distribution networks demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms other methods in terms of load forecasting accuracy and dynamic control effectiveness, resulting in substantial improvements in the operational efficiency and stability of ADNs.

title: ED-FSA-DConvLSTM: A Lightweight Spatiotemporal Model for Ionospheric TEC Prediction Focusing on Capturing Long-distance Spatial Memory with Deformable Convolution
authors: Jian Xiao, Haijun Liu, Jing Yuan, Huijun Le, Rui Zhou, Yan Ma
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

title: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
authors: N/A

title: Leveraging Social Network and Multimodal Graphs for Micro-Video Popularity Prediction
authors: Lilong Liu, Shangheng Chen, Shengsheng Qian, Changsheng Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: With the rapid rise of micro-videos on digital platforms, Micro-Video Popularity Prediction (MVPP) has become a crucial area of research. Micro-videos consist of rich multimodal content, including video frames, text, and social network interaction data, all of which significantly influence their popularity. However, existing methods typically focus on multimodal content features while often overlooking the importance of social interaction data. In this paper, we propose a novel method, Leveraging Social Network and Multimodal Graphs for Micro-Video Popularity Prediction (LSNMG). The key components of this approach include Dynamic Social Perception Pre-calculation (DSPP), which minimizes information loss by propagating the entire multimodal graph, and Retrieval-Driven Enhanced Prediction (REP), which boosts prediction accuracy by retrieving similar videos and incorporating global features. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in prediction performance, validating the effectiveness of combining social interaction data with multimodal content for Micro-Video Popularity Prediction.

title: Making a Champion! Analysing Story-Driven Sports Games to Devise a Design Methodology for Exergames with Stories
authors: Azhan Ahmad, Mas Ayu Mumin, Deenina Salleh, Ibrahim Edris
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Exergames are digital games that combine physical exertion as part of their gameplay such as to control game objects by performing exercise activities. Studies have shown positive effects of combining game elements with exercise. This includes improving users’ affective domain such as motivation to perform physical activities, and general fitness. To the extent of our knowledge, there is still a lack of attention on the use of stories within existing exergames, thus missing out on the opportunity stories can provide such as improved immersion and user satisfaction. In this paper, we will present the analysis of existing story-driven digital sports games where we have identified key narrative and game challenge patterns. These patterns helped us devise a design methodology that can help in designing stories for exergames, which will be introduced in this paper.

title: IRDNet: Invariant and Residual Dynamic Decomposition Neural Network for Time Series Forecasting
authors: Chenyang Li, Xuanzhi Feng, Chao Li, Xuefeng Ding, Yuming Jiang, Shakib Al Hasan, Dasha Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Due to the widespread presence of time series data across various domains, accurate time series forecasting holds significant importance. However, existing complex forecasting models are still affected by issues such as computational efficiency and information bottlenecks. In this paper, we propose a novel deep learning backbone model called IRDNet.
