Additionally, we introduce a method for decomposing time series based on frequency domain characteristics, which IRDNet utilizes to achieve effective information aggregation and modeling of long-term dependencies. We also conduct a comprehensive evaluation of IRDNet's performance across six datasets, demonstrating that IRDNet is an efficient approach in terms of both accuracy and speed, outperforming state-of-the-art deep learning and statistical methods.

title: A Blind Fixed Multi-Beam GNSS Anti-Jamming Method Based on Space-Frequency Domain
authors: Jiaqi Tang, Lin Jia, Zhixiang Huang, Tingyu Long, Chungong Xie, Xianwei Meng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) signals are susceptible to interference, and traditional adaptive nulling algorithms, such as the Power Inversion (PI) criterion, can suppress interference but introduce distortion to the carrier phase. This creates a trade-off between anti-jamming performance and high-precision positioning. This paper presents a novel antijamming technique that does not require prior satellite information, facilitating rapid signal acquisition and highprecision carrier phase tracking, particularly during cold-start scenarios in strong jamming environments. The proposed method employs a blind, fixed multi-beam pointing strategy based on Space-Frequency Adaptive Processing (SFAP) for satellite acquisition, followed by a seamless transition to adaptive beamforming using the Minimum Variance Distortionless Response (MVDR) criterion for accurate tracking. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to the PI algorithm, the proposed method achieves a null of approximately −92 dB in the interference direction while maintaining a 0 dB gain and near-zero phase response in the desired signal direction. The output Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) increases by an average of 4.5 dB, effectively addressing both signal attenuation and phase distortion in jamming environments.

title: Research on Intelligent Scoliosis Orthoses
authors: Jiale Zhu, Guanglin Shi, Wenfeng Wang, Zipeng Ai, Weijie Peng, Yi Cheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Conventional orthoses for adolescent idiopathic scoliosis (AIS) exhibit limitations including single-mode corrective force, poor adjustability, and significant deviation. This study proposes an 'intelligent' solution integrating smart hardware and PID algorithms to upgrade orthotic systems. An intelligent brace was developed with a host computer as the computational core and STM32 microcontroller as the control core, incorporating pressure sensors and a WiFi module. A position-based discrete PID algorithm enables closed-loop control of corrective forces. Validation results show steady-state deviations of 3.97 % for the spine and 4.51 % for the trunk after optimization, with system response time less than 2 seconds. Preliminary case study on 1 Lenke Type 5 AIS patients demonstrated a reduction in T11-L5 Cobb angle from 16.68° to 9.16°. This intelligent PID-controlled system enhances corrective efficacy, providing a controllable and precise non-surgical treatment pathway for AIS.

title: An Intelligent Proofreading Framework for Structured Official Documents Based on Large Language Models
authors: Junyi Zhu, Jiajun He, Chaoyan Zhu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Traditional proofreading of official documents struggles to meet modern office requirements for semantic accuracy, format compliance, and efficiency. To address this challenge, this paper presents an intelligent proofreading framework that integrates fine-tuned large language models with LangChain workflow orchestration, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for knowledge enhancement, TF-IDF semantic matching, and rule- and terminology-based validation. A domain-specific corpus was constructed from high-quality institutional texts, and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) was applied for efficient model tuning. Experimental results show that the proposed framework outperforms mainstream models such as ChatGPT-4, DeepSeek, DouBao, and Qwen3-32B, achieving an average correction accuracy above 80%, an over-correction rate below 10%, and a 30% reduction in response time, while maintaining a Stability Index over 0.9. These results demonstrate that combining knowledge enhancement with rule constraints effectively improves the robustness and reliability of large language models for intelligent official document proofreading.

title: Text-Based Detection of Online Gambling Advertisements on Social Media Using AutoML and CNN
authors: Ari Muzakir, Usman Ependi, Suyanto, Putri Taqwa Prasetyaningrum, Muhammad Ihksan Alfiansyah, Valen Tino Rosi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: The proliferation of online gambling advertisements on social media poses serious threats to digital safety, especially in regions where such activities are illegal. Existing approaches such as keyword matching, rule-based systems, or traditional machine learning models struggle to capture semantic context and are easily evaded through slang or obfuscation. Manual moderation is no longer feasible due to the increasing volume and sophistication of these ads. This study addresses the gap by comparing an automated machine learning framework (AutoML) and a convolutional neural network (CNN) for Indonesian-language gambling ad detection. The dataset comprises 15,286 annotated sentences from Facebook, Instagram, X, and YouTube, including 8,499 positive samples (55.6%) and 6,787 negative samples (44.4%). Data were gathered from text posts, OCR-extracted images, and video transcriptions, then preprocessed with case folding, tokenization, noise removal, and stemming. AutoML used TF-IDF features, while CNN employed pretrained Word2Vec embeddings. Results show CNN achieved 92.80% accuracy, outperforming AutoML at 90.19%, with higher precision and recall. These findings demonstrate the novelty of evaluating AutoML versus CNN on Indonesian text and confirm the effectiveness of deep learning for illicit ad detection. The proposed framework supports real-world content moderation and enhances digital safety.

title: A Review on RGB-Thermal Image Fusion Based on 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Kaiwen Yu, Dong Lin, Pengyi Tian, Na Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3D-GS) has recently emerged as an efficient technique for real-time 3D rendering and novel view synthesis. In parallel, thermal imaging offers unique capabilities for perception in visually degraded environments. This paper reviews the emerging research on fusion of RGB and thermal images for 3D modeling using 3D-GS.Firstly, the fundamentals of 3D-GS and the principle of 3D reconstruction by RGB-thermal image fusion are briefly illustrated. Afterwards, existing 3D-GS approaches used in RGB-thermal image fusion are analyzed and compared in detail. Finally, key findings are summarized and potential challenges are discussed, pointing to promising directions for future research.

title: Paddy AI Chatbot
authors: Saiful Aizam Tahir, Wida Susanty Haji Suhaili
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Rice, also called Oryza sativa, is important worldwide as a food crop, but Brunei Darussalam has difficulty producing enough rice and relies heavily on imports to feed its people. Due to limited resources, an older workforce, and a lack of workers, we urgently need to improve crop production. These problems are worsened by diseases and pests that can significantly affect the quality and yield of rice crops. At the same time, old ways of diagnosing these issues are unreliable and time-consuming. This project aims to create an AI-powered robot for farmers that uses smart technologies to quickly identify diseases and pests affecting rice plants. The chatbot provides quick, accurate diagnoses and personalized management advice to improve resource use, reduce labour issues, and boost the sustainability and efficiency of rice farming in Brunei.

title: Copyright Infringement Risks and Mitigation Strategies in AI-Generated Image Technology
authors: Jikuan Xu, Yuxuan Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the explosive development of AI-generated imagery technologies such as diffusion models, the resulting copyright law dilemmas have become a focal point for global legal and industrial communities. Current AI technologiesincluding Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Diffusion Models, Autoregressive Models, and Transformer models-can generate highly complex, photorealistic, and artistic images based on natural language descriptions. This generative capability is built upon learning and imitating vast amounts of training data. This training data includes a massive volume of copyrighted works, embedding the copyright risks of AI image generation technology within its core mechanisms of data acquisition, model training, and content generation. Passive legal responses are insufficient to keep pace with technological advancement; proactive, forward-looking, and systematic avoidance strategies are imperative. Effective risk prevention should not be the responsibility of a single entity. Instead, developers, users, platforms, and legislators must collaboratively establish a comprehensive governance system. This study aims to strip away the technological veneer, rigorously analyze these risk points from a legal perspective, and construct a comprehensive strategic framework. This framework establishes defenses at every layer, from source to endpoint, to mitigate potential legal conflicts.

title: A Chinese Case Study of Research on Layout Optimization of Wind Farms in Port Areas Based on Wind Resource Assessment and Selected Wind Turbines
authors: Man Zheng, Jinrui Tang, Chen Yang, Haochen Li, Kefang Bao, Yachao Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: As a clean and non-polluting energy, wind energy was used in the construction of green ports to meet the requirement of electric load with rapid growth. The construction of wind farms in the port area has a great impact on the normal operation, maintenance and economic benefits of the port. In this paper, Chuanshan Port area is digitally modelled based on the measured data of the site weather station, the wind spectra of the port area for each month and the whole year. And then the total annual power generation of the wind farm with several wind turbines are calculated. In addition, A comparative study on the selection and layout of wind turbines was conducted for four different types of wind turbines and three preliminary layout points. The final simulation verification shows that the second model of wind turbines arranged at points 2 and 3 is the optimal solution, which can reduce investment costs while ensuring proper wind power generation.

title: Harnessing Machine Learning for Sentiment Analysis: A Comparative Study of Classification Models on Threads Apps User Reviews
authors: Evaristus Didik Madyatmadja, Matthew Hendra, Darren Neoalexis Wiryadinata, Hubert Winton Gunawan, Muhammad Varizan Abinery, Daffa Farras Putra Tarigan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive sentiment analysis study on the Threads app, a new social media platform developed by Meta as a competitor to X (formerly Twitter). The research aims to bridge the gap in understanding user sentiment through machine learning-based text classification, addressing limitations of app store rating systems that fail to dynamically capture user feedback over time. Using 5,000 Indonesian-language Google Play Store reviews, the study evaluates and compares two models: Naive Bayes (NB) and Support Vector Machine (SVM). The methodology involves detailed preprocessing steps—including normalization, stemming, tokenization, and stopword removal— followed by feature extraction using TF-IDF and ngrams. Evaluation metrics include accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Results show that SVM outperformed NB with an overall accuracy of 0.88 and an improved F1-score in negative sentiment classification. This research contributes both methodologically and practically by demonstrating the effectiveness of traditional classifiers for underexplored regional datasets, while providing actionable insights for app developers targeting Indonesian users.

title: Characteristics of Dissociated Gas Products During the Self-Healing Process of Metallized Polypropylene Films
authors: Ruiqiang Wen, Xuke Gao, Cong Wang, Geng Chen, Youping Tu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The gases generated during the self-healing discharge of metallized polypropylene films may lead to capacitor bulging or even explosive failure. In this manuscript, the decomposition pathways and product formation mechanisms during self-healing discharges in metallized polypropylene films are investigated using quantum chemistry calculations and molecular dynamics simulations. The results indicate that the primary decomposition products include <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{H}_{2}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CH}_{4}, \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{4}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex>. Under an applied electric field of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.2 \times$</tex> the rated strength, the total number of gas molecules increases by 72.4 % compared to the rated condition, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{H}_{2}$</tex> production rising by 44.4 %. The quantities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CH}_{4}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{4}$</tex> also exhibit significant growth, while <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex> formation is initiated only when the field strength reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.8 \times$</tex> the rated value. As the applied field strength is increased from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.0 \times$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.2 \times$</tex> the rated value, the number of residual atoms and molecules within the self-healing channel is observed to decrease progressively.

title: A Confidence-Gated Cascade for Efficient Medical Visual Question Answering
authors: Haojie Hu, Jingcan Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: While Large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable performance on medical Visual Question Answering (VQA), their prohibitive computational costs severely limit real-world clinical deployment. We present a novel confidence-gated cascade framework that intelligently routes queries through a heterogeneous ensemble of three VLMs with varying computational demands. Our key insight is that not all medical queries require expensive state-of-the-art models—many can be accurately answered by lightweight alternatives. We leverage model self-assessed confidence scores, elicited through carefully designed prompts, as a dynamic routing signal to escalate only challenging queries to more powerful models. Experiments on VQA-RAD and PathVQA benchmarks demonstrate that our approach achieves 37 % cost reduction while maintaining accuracy within 0.5 % of the strongest model, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$72-74 \%$</tex> of queries resolved by the two cheapest tiers.

title: Illumination-Robust Offset Measurement System Based on Camera
authors: Jiadi Zhang, Jiajia Shi, Yuqi Han, Yanjun Gao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: This work presents a hybrid industrial measurement system integrating deep neural network-based detection with traditional feature engineering into a unified pipeline. Addressing challenges posed by illumination variability and algorithmic divergence, the system combines real-time deep learning localization with precise sub-pixel feature refinement and rotation-invariant descriptors. Equipped with an RGB camera, the deployed system achieves high accuracy in practical industrial environments, significantly improving measurement precision and efficiency for components and instruments.

title: Design and Control of Multi-Port Power Converter for Power Recovery in Power Distribution Network
authors: Miao Yang, Zoujun Wang, Miao Zhao, Jinliang Li, Yujie Deng, Chenhai Long, Jianggui Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In order to achieve rapid power restoration after power outages in power distribution networks, this paper proposes a topology of multi-port power converter and its corresponding control strategy. Firstly, port converters for photovoltaic port, energy storage port and diesel generator port are designed, and corresponding mathematical models are designed are established. Then, control strategies for each port converter are designed according to characteristic of power flow in each port. Meanwhile, in order to achieve power balance in the multi-port power converter, a coordinate control strategy is designed for concerned ports. In the end, simulation and experimental results are given to verify the capability of designed multi-port converter in voltage regulation and power restoration for power distribution network.

title: Garment-Aware Neural Radiance Fields for Generalizable 3D Human Digitization
authors: Wei Yin, Li Liu, Xiaodong Fu, LiJun Liu, Wei Peng
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: HFQuant: Mixed-Precision Quantization with Hessian-Fisher Sensitivity for Neural Networks
authors: Longfei Guo, Hongju Cheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: As the use of neural networks on edge devices increases, model size and inference speed become major deployment bottlenecks. Mixed-Precision Quantization (MPQ) assigns a specific bit-width to each layer, compressing models and reducing inference latency. However, the search space grows exponentially with network depth, and evaluating each bit-width configuration requires quantization and fine-tuning, incurring prohibitive cost. We propose HFQuant, a mixed-precision quantization approach based on Hessian-Fisher sensitivity. We derive a unified HessianFisher Sensitivity (HFS) metric integrating weight and activation quantization errors for per-layer bit-width allocation. A twostage algorithm then assigns weight bit-widths via integer linear programming (ILP), followed by block-wise activation allocation using a sliding window, substantially reducing search complexity. Experiments on ImageNet with ResNet-18, ResNet-50, and MobileNetV1 show that HFQuant achieves a smaller accuracy drop than baseline methods under most configurations with strong robustness across bit-width settings.

title: The Role of Quantum Computing in Transforming Healthcare: A Systematic Literature Review of Quantum Machine Learning Applications in Oncology
authors: Yoseph Adi Nugroho Tansil, Nathanael Gavy, Adrian Hananto, Yohannes Kurniawan
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Quantum Machine Learning (QML) is an emerging field with significant potential to transform oncology. This systematic literature review evaluates the current landscape of QML applications in cancer research, analyzing 21 primary research articles, selected from 410 initially identified, published between 2020 and 2024. The review categorizes QML applications, assesses evidence for quantum advantage, identifies technical challenges, and outlines future directions. Findings indicate robust research activity, particularly in China and India, with hybrid quantum-classical algorithms showing promise in simulated environments for tasks like diagnosis and prediction, especially with small or imbalanced datasets. However, translation into clinical practice remains constrained by hardware limitations and the lack of rigorous real-world validation, resulting in a persistent gap between simulation and clinical application. In addition, most studies adopt quantitative experimental approaches, with limited exploration of qualitative perspectives such as usability, interpretability, and workflow integration. To address these gaps, future research should pursue standardized frameworks, mixed-method evaluation protocols, and cross-disciplinary collaboration between quantum scientists, clinicians, and implementation researchers. Such efforts would provide robust, comparable evidence of clinical utility, ensure trustworthiness in deployment, and accelerate the transition of QML from experimental promise to transformative impact in oncology.

title: Optimization of DSO-SLAM for UAV Based on Monocular Vision
authors: Enchao Jiang, Ce Zhang, Zhiyuan Shi, Taixun Yi, Peiwen Yu, Chenxiao Li, Xiaoyun Xiao, Song Xie, Liyong Fang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: DSO(Direct Sparse Odometry) uses the direct method to calculate the visual odometer, which needs to compare the image information. Therefore, the system assumes that the gray value of three-dimensional points in the same space is unchanged during the image acquisition process. If the illumination conditions change obviously, the system is prone to generate noise and affect the quality of point cloud. In this paper, a back-end optimization method of point cloud based on DoN(Difference of Normal) is proposed. Firstly, the voxel grid method is used to realize the down-sampling of point cloud data, reducing the number of points while preserving the geometric features of point cloud, and then DoN point cloud filtering algorithm is used to remove low-frequency noise. On the premise of ensuring that the map point cloud data does not expand, this method makes the map target more intuitive and the detailed features more obvious. In this paper, the information entropy theory is used to judge the processed results, and the average entropy value of unit points is maintained or improved on the premise of ensuring that the total number of point clouds is obviously reduced. Finally, the SLAM verification platform of UAV monocular vision is set up, and the proposed method is tested and verified. The results show that the map reconstructed by the improved DSO algorithm in this paper has more obvious features and higher information entropy under the similar number of point clouds.

title: Performance Optimization of 10Gb/s NRZ SerDes Link with FFE and DFE
authors: Zhiqiang Chen, Yuqiu Yue, Aiyue Dong, Dongzhu Wang, Shuguang Dong, Dekang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: 10Gb/s NRZ link architecture with variable equalization parameter can be constructed to explore the effect of equalization configuration on link performance. Though analyzing the transient waveform and frequency response of backplane channel, the combination equalizer can be employed into the link, and then optimized by evaluating the eye-opening degree. Simulation results show that for some backplane channel with pre-cursor and post-cursor, 10-tap FFE and 1-tap DFE can achieve 60 ps eye width and 60.5 mV eye height. It is very beneficial for improving the reliability in the SerDes application scenarios, which can be applied to 20G+ serial interface links.

title: Semantic and Structural Drift in Financial Knowledge Graphs: A Robustness Analysis of GNN-Based Fraud Detectors
authors: Rener S. Menezes, Raimir H. Filho
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: The detection of financial fraud with Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) on Knowledge Graphs (KGs) is a state-of-the-art approach. However, the performance of these models in production is threatened by data drift, where the statistical properties of the data change over time. This study provides a foundational analysis of the robustness of different GNN architectures (convolutional and attention-based) when exposed to natural drift in a KG built from the IEEE-CIS Fraud Detection dataset (Kaggle). We trained three models, Relational Graph Convolutional Network (R-GCN), Heterogeneous Graph Transformer (HGT) and Heterogeneous Attention Network (HAN) -on a historical dataset and evaluated their performance degradation in 50 subsequent temporal windows. Our results quantify a significant and volatile performance drop over time for all models, with the F1-score of HGT falling from a baseline of 0.747 to a minimum of 0.455. In contrast, the simpler convolutional architecture of R-GCN proved to be more resistant to drift, achieving a high average F1-score of 0.868 across the monitored windows. We show that architectural choice directly affects the degradation profile, revealing a trade-off between peak performance and temporal stability. This provides strong evidence that drift analysis is a crucial step in the lifecycle of the GNN model for financial applications, establishing a vital benchmark for future work on drift-aware models.

title: Concrete Crack, Spalling, and Exposed Rebar Detection Model Based on Improved YOLOv11
authors: Song Zhang, Lan Wang, Zhonglin Zhang, Guixiong Hu, Zhongjie Su
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To enhance the detection accuracy of typical concrete damage, this study proposes a YOLOv11n-based concrete damage detection model improved with the PaddlePaddle High-Performance GPU Network (PP-HGNetV2) backbone network. Concrete surface damage, including cracks, spalling, and exposed rebar, exhibits multi-scale and irregular distribution characteristics, which pose challenges for accurate detection. To address these issues, a dataset has been constructed using data augmentation strategies such as rotation, brightness adjustment, and blurring. These strategies have improved the model's robustness to variations in visual appearance. The experimental process involved training and evaluating the improved model and comparing it with the baseline YOLOv11n. The results demonstrated that the improved model achieved a 2.1% increase in mAP@50, a 2.7% rise in mAP@50-95, and a 4.2% improvement in recall, while maintaining a parameter count below that of the baseline. These findings validate the effectiveness of the proposed method in improving detection accuracy, recall capability, and model lightweight performance for concrete surface damage.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Explainable AI Framework for Secure UAV-Based Delivery in Telesurgery Systems
authors: Krisha Shastri, Mahek Shah, Kajal Lochab, Priyal Bhinde, Lakshin Pathak, Dhrishita Parvell, Rajesh Gupta, Sudeep Tanwar, Jitendra Bhatia
abstract: The BlockHealth framework proposed in this paper combines Blockchain technology, Machine Learning (ML), and Explainable AI (XAI) to improve the security and trustworthiness of UAV-enabled telesurgery systems. It focuses on identifying and classifying cyber threats, particularly False Data Injection (FDI) and Evil Twin attacks, using four ML models: Naive Bayes, K-Nearest Neighbors, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine. The Random Forest model outperformed the others, achieving a top accuracy of 93%, highlighting its strong capability in detecting these attacks effectively. To provide interpretability, SHAP and LIME methods were employed, enabling a transparent understanding of model decisions. Blockchain technology further ensures data immutability and secure communication between UAVs and healthcare providers. The proposed framework contributes to the secure deployment of UAVs in critical medical environments, offering a robust solution for enhancing trust and resilience in telesurgery applications.

title: Classroom Evaluation Based on Behavior Recognition and Stacked Hybrid Experts
authors: Xiu Ying Tan, Li Ying Wang, Yue Ming Wang, Yan Hua Chu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: With the continuous development of intelligent education, achieving efficient teaching quality evaluation in large-scale classrooms has become an important research direction. To address the limitations of traditional approaches that over-rely on expert judgment and suffer from low efficiency, this paper proposes a classroom score prediction method based on behavior recognition and a stacked mixture-of-experts model. Built on an improved YOLOv10n-LD, the system recognizes four student behaviors in classroom videos—Listening, Bowing, Sleeping, and Using Phone—and computes behavior occurrence rates from frames sampled every 30 s. From these rates, twelve statistical features (such as mean, variance, extremes, and skewness) are extracted and fused with Class size to form a 49-dimensional feature vector. A feature selection mechanism finally retains fourteen optimal features, including Class size and the Listening rate, which are fed into the Stacked-MoE for classroom score prediction. Experiments show that the method achieves RMSE, MAE, MSE, and MAPE of 2.6028, 2.009, 6.7744, and 2.5513%, respectively, outperforming traditional baselines. This study provides an effective solution for intelligent evaluation of university classroom teaching quality and shows strong application prospects.

title: Short-Term Power Load Forecasting Based on VMD-SDAM-Informer Model
authors: Ju Yongsheng, Wei Yong, Wan Xiaohua, Jin Panrun, Zhao Xushan, Wei Xiaoyuan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Existing short-term power load forecasting methods often struggle with low precision, leading to inaccurate predictions. To address this challenge, we propose a hybrid forecasting model that combines Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD), Stacked Denoising Autoencoder (SDAE), and the Informer model. First, VMD is used to decompose the power load time series, reducing fluctuations caused by nonlinearity and non-stationarity. SDAE then extracts key load characteristics, accounting for the influence of weather, seasonality, and time of day. The Informer model is employed to predict and reconstruct the load components, effectively mitigating issues such as gradient explosions and memory overload in traditional algorithms and Transformerbased models. Experimental validation using summer and winter power load data from a city in Northwest China shows that the proposed VMD-SDAE-Informer model significantly outperforms traditional forecasting models. Specifically, the RMSE error is reduced by 0.0936, indicating a substantial improvement in forecasting accuracy. This demonstrates that the model not only enhances prediction precision but also offers a more stable and efficient approach to short-term power load forecasting.

title: Machine Learning Driven-Prediction of Digital Transformation Readiness
authors: Rhisa Adika Putri, Harco Leslie Spits Warnars, Yohannes Kurniawan, Harijanto Prabowo, Sfenrianto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This study presents a systematic literature review on the use of machine learning to predict and analyze Digital Readiness across organizational and social contexts. Eight peerre-viewed articles from 2024-2025 were analyzed to identify ML models, predictive features, and key findings, challenges, and limitations. The review shows that models such as Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Neural Networks, Transformers, K-Means Clustering, and sentiment-based approaches like VADER have been applied. These models rely on diverse datasets, including surveys, human resource data, IoT sensor readings, and social media text, reflecting multiple dimensions of digital readiness: technological infrastructure, human capability, organizational culture, and social perception. This highlights that readiness is a multidimensional construct, not solely technical. Despite promising results, challenges remain, including limited data availability, model interpretability, and difficulty generalizing across domains. Future research should explore Explainable AI (XAI), multimodal data integration, and cross-sector validation to improve model transparency, accuracy, and adaptability. Overall, this study provides insights into how ML can support digital transformation strategies and guide evidence-based decision-making for organizations and policymakers.

title: Impact of Cell Chemistry on the Degradation and Revenue Potential of Energy Storage Systems
authors: Dilip Pandit, Atri Bera, Cody J. Newlun, Tim Wilcox, Yuliya Preger, Tu Nguyen, Raymond Byrne
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Battery energy storage systems (BESS) are widely deployed in power grids for their revenue potential, rapid response capabilities, and support for energy shifting. However, their long-term performance and economic returns are strongly influenced by degradation, which depends on both cell chemistry and market participation strategies. This study examines the market revenue potential of BESS, focusing on degradation across different Lithium-ion cathode chemistries. Stress-factor-based degradation models are formulated for lithium iron phosphate (LFP), lithium nickel manganese cobalt oxide (NMC), lithium nickel cobalt aluminum oxide (NCA) chemistries and validated using real-world cell cycling data. BESS revenues are then evaluated for various market participation strategies (energy arbitrage, frequency regulation, and operational reserves), assessing the economic impacts of chemistry-driven cell degradation. Additionally, this study also identifies the most economically suitable market applications for each cell chemistry.

title: Closed-Loop Detection System for a MEMS Resonant Voltage Sensor Based on Phase-Sensitive Detection
authors: Chun Hu, Dan Yang, Dezhi Zheng, Jinyang Li, Yilin Wang, Chenglin Ren, Xiong Xiao, Wenhao Lu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Voltage measurement is a fundamental requirement for the intelligence and digitalization of modern power grids. Benefiting from their low power consumption and miniaturized design, Micro-Electro-Mechanical System (MEMS) voltage sensors can achieve voltage detection via non-intrusive electric-field measurement, while a high-performance closed-loop detection system is essential to ensure measurement accuracy. This article presents a closed-loop detection system based on phase-sensitive detection (PSD). The sensor electrodes are reconfigured to form a differential detection structure, effectively suppressing common-mode interference and parasitic coupling. A dual closed-loop circuit integrating a phase-locked loop (PLL) and automatic gain control (AGC) are implemented to stabilize the reference signal and enhance the accuracy of PSD. Furthermore, a low-power design strategy is adopted to reduce overall power consumption. Experimental results show that the proposed system achieved a linearity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>) of 0.9989, a minimum relative deviation of 0.01 %, and a total power consumption of 57.6 mW. These results indicate that the proposed system provides an effective balance among precision, stability, and power efficiency, offering reliable technical support for the engineering applications of MEMS voltage sensors in smart grids.

title: Analysis of the Overall Morphology and Deposition Baseline of Soy Protein Solution Spray Based on PIV Image and Statistics
authors: Shuying Chang, Hongjie Fei, Yong Zhang, Chunyu Wu
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Research Background and Significance:With growing environmental concerns, replacing plastics with edible packaging is an inevitable trend. Vegetable paper is a promising, biodegradable option, but it suffers from poor mechanical properties and heat-sealing ability. Spraying a soy protein solution can enhance its performance. This study employs advanced computer vision and imaging techniques to provide a data-driven foundation for optimizing this spray process, establishing a technical paradigm for achieving highperformance coatings. Research Method:To address the impact of spray atomization on uniform coating, this study utilized a Particle Image Velocimetry (PIV) system with high-speed imaging and laser sheet illumination to capture detailed images of the soy protein spray. The captured image sequences were then analyzed using sophisticated computer vision techniques. First, image preprocessing algorithms were applied to denoise and enhance the raw images. Subsequently, segmentation and edge detection algorithms precisely extracted the spray field’s contour from the background. This enabled a digital characterization of the “overall morphology” by quantifying key features like area and perimeter. To determine the “deposition baseline”, pixel intensity distribution analysis was performed at a fixed distance from the nozzle to calculate its width, stability, and centerline offset. Concurrently, a 2D velocity vector field was constructed based on the PIV principle. The experiment systematically varied spraying flow rate, hydraulic pressure, and air pressure, using overall morphology and deposition baseline as the primary indicators. Research Results:Quantitative analysis by the computer vision system identified optimal process parameters. At a spraying flow rate of $50 \mathrm{~cm}^{3} / \mathrm{min}$, an air pressure of 0.08 MPa, and a hydraulic pressure of $\mathbf{0. 1 6 ~ M P a}$, superior atomization was achieved. Under these conditions, the deposition baseline width at 400 mm from the nozzle was highly stable with minimal fluctuation. Furthermore, the baseline’s centerline aligned with the nozzle’s central axis, and the overall morphology area reached its maximum. Research Conclusion:The study concludes that hydraulic pressure, air pressure, and flow rate significantly impact atomization. When the computer vision system detects a stable deposition baseline and a maximized spray area, it indicates an optimal spray effect. This provides a critical process parameter window for obtaining a uniform, high-performance coating on vegetable paper.

title: Improved Measures for Non-Electrical Protection in Busbar Area of Multi-Terminal Flexible HVDC Transmission System
authors: Hangtian Dong, Wanli Shi, Chang Ruan, Yukun Xiao, Sai Ding, Peng Zhang, Xiaoxia Li, Jianhua Lu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Taking the UHV multi-terminal flexible HVDC transmission project - Kunliulong HVDC transmission project as an example, this paper introduces the non-electrical quantity protection configuration and operation strategy for the busbar area at the intermediate station (Liuzhou Station) of a multiterminal flexible DC system. Addressing the issue that the nonelectrical quantity protection operation in the busbar area cannot trigger bipolar DC tripping after Liuzhou Station exits operation, an improved protection scheme for the busbar area at intermediate stations of multi-terminal flexible DC systems is proposed. The feasibility of this improvement has been verified through RTDS simulation and field tests, providing valuable reference for field operation personnel and subsequent multiterminal DC projects.

title: Tuning Method of Node Emergency Support Power Demand Based on Transient Frequency Target Trajectory
authors: Zeyi Zhuang, Jilai Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: The decreasing rotational inertia in new Power Systems exacerbates the issue of frequency stability. When the system experiences a large active power deficit, it is necessary to quickly tune the support power demand of the nodes involved in frequency emergency control to ensure that the transient frequency drop trajectory remains above the target. To address this, this paper proposes a method for tuning the emergency support power demand of nodes based on the control of the transient frequency target trajectory. First, under the premise of meeting inertia indicators of the system frequency stability criteria, an optimal target state is established where the initial transient frequency drop trajectory in any power deficit scenario should remain relatively stable. This target state serves as a benchmark to evaluate the performance of the actual frequency drop at each monitoring node. Second, data is collected based on the short-term frequency drop trajectory at the initial stage of disturbance. The trajectory data is used to assess the difference in equivalent inertia time constants according to its deviation from the target. This difference is then converted into the emergency support power demand for each node, thereby providing significant reference for further allocation of frequency precise control.

title: Ontology-Driven Dynamic Learner Profiling: Towards Fluid Learning
authors: Samia Bousalem, Massinissa Chelghoum, Naila Marir, Fouzia Benchikha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Recent advances in adaptive learning have empha-sized the importance of learner profiles that are not only accurate but also capable of evolving over time. However, most existing profiling approaches rely on static representations that fail to capture the dynamic nature of learners’ preferences and performances. This limitation reduces the ability of adaptive systems to provide truly individualized and responsive learning experiences. In this paper, we propose a dynamic learner profiling approach grounded in an enriched ontology. Rather than introducing new features, our method reuses two existing dimensions—Evaluation and Learning Style (based on the VAK model)—to enable conditional and continuous updates of learner profiles. Evaluation results serve as semantic triggers for re-predicting the learner’s VAK distribution, which is represented as percentages to reflect fluid and overlapping tendencies. SWRL rules formalize the triggering conditions, while a machine learning model recomputes the learning style distribution when necessary. By integrating semantic modeling with adaptive predictions, the proposed framework moves beyond static learner representations and lays the foundation for Fluid Learning, where learner profiles evolve alongside their progress and setbacks.

title: Rolling Optimization and Adjustment Strategy for Virtual Power Plants With Proactive Support Capability
authors: Linna Ni, Zirong Wang, Yuhao Chen, Rongguo Huang, Fushuan Wen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: With the ever-increasing penetration of renewable energy based generation (REG) in power systems, the inherent intermittency and randomness of REG pose severe challenges to system security and stability. This paper proposes an optimization framework for the Virtual Power Plant with Proactive Support (VPP-PS) capability. Unlike traditional passive response modes, VPP-PSs actively predict and compensate for system deviations. A bi-level Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) algorithm is designed to handle regional consistency constraints and inter-regional supply-demand balance through adaptive parameter adjustment. A rolling optimization framework achieves effective uncertainty management via deviation prediction, real-time compensation, and data updates. Case studies demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in achieving optimal resource allocation while respecting system constraints.

title: High-Efficiency Multiband Outphasing Amplifiers Using Streamlined Impedance Synthesis Methodology
authors: Weiwei Wang, Lihe He, Shichang Chen, Hui Gu, Jialin Cai, Zhiwei Zhang, Xin Yu Zhou, Giovanni Crupi, Gaofeng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article introduces a novel network synthesis approach that streamlines the design of multiband outphasing power amplifiers (OPAs). By strategically factoring the global transmission matrix into cascaded subnetworks, the proposed approach reduces the conventional matrix synthesis problem to an adaptive impedance matching procedure, significantly enhancing design flexibility for multiband applications. A simplified real frequency technique (SRFT) is employed to construct adaptive matching networks, achieving high adaptability in dual- and tri-band operations. To experimentally validate the proposed methodology, both a dual-band OPA targeting the 2.6- and 3.5-GHz bands for LTE and 5G applications and a tri-band OPA covering 1.8, 2.6, and 3.5 GHz were fabricated using 10-W GaN HEMTs. Measurement results demonstrate outstanding performance: the dual-band prototype achieved peak output powers of 44.42 and 44.18 dBm with corresponding peak drain efficiencies of 75.4% and 74.6%, while the tri-band prototype achieved peak output powers exceeding 43.8 dBm across all bands with drain efficiencies above 66.8%. Even at a 6-dB output back-off, the tri-band amplifier maintains efficiencies exceeding 48.6% for all bands, effectively showcasing the superiority and practicality of the proposed design approach for high-efficiency multiband OPA realization. To the best of the authors’ knowledge, this is the first nonreconfigurable multiband OPA ever reported.

title: Adaptive Sampling for Continuous Crowdsensing in Unknown Dynamics Environments
authors: Liang Wang, Shan Su, Dingqi Yang, Zhiwen Yu, Fei Xiong, Yao Zhang, Mingjun Xiao, Bin Guo
abstract: Continuous crowdsensing in Mobile CrowdSensing (MCS) involves ongoing monitoring to gather real-time data over extended periods. A key challenge is determining appropriate intervals between consecutive data samplings to capture temporal variations, especially in unknown dynamic environments. Traditional Age-of-Information (AoI) driven methods maintain data freshness but can be costly and result in data redundancy. To address this, we integrate the AoI metric with information entropy difference to create a novel indicator, Composite Data Value (CDV), balancing data freshness and redundancy. Based on it, we investigate the online adaptive sampling problem for continuous crowdsensing in unknown dynamic environments. This problem is challenging due to the vast space of sensing strategies, difficulty in estimating rewards with unknown distributions and varying rates of change, and the degradation of optimal strategies as the environment evolves. Using a multi-armed bandit framework, we propose AdaScs, an online adaptive sampling optimization approach to maximize long-term CDV performance. First, AdaScs develops compact sensing strategies through limited trials. Then, it adaptively performs online sampling based on evolving reward estimations, identifying optimal strategies, detecting environmental drifts, updating strategies, and adjusting cycle lengths. Our results show that AdaScs outperforms all baselines, with accuracy increasing by 22.6% and reaction time at last improving by 54.6%.
