title: A Hardware/Software Co-Optimization of HQC Using Tightly-Coupled Accelerators on a 32-bit Ibex Core
authors: Seog Chung Seo, YoungBeom Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: We present Hardware/Software co-optimization of Hamming Quasi-Cyclic (HQC) enabled by tightly coupled accelerators implemented on a 32-bit Ibex RISC-V core. On the hardware side, we propose a unified multiplier capable of efficiently performing carryless multiplication for both polynomial multiplication over F2[X]/(Xn−1) and multiplication over F28. We also design a Keccak permutation accelerator to support efficient randomness sampling. On the software side, we identify the optimal combination of Toom–Cook and Karatsuba methods for efficient polynomial multiplication on the Ibex core and enhance its performance by minimizing the number of memory accesses during its execution.With our co-optimization strategies, our HQC implementation achieves a performance improvement of several tens of times over the reference implementation.

title: ISRJ Suppression for MIMO Distributed Coherent Aperture Radar Using Bidirectional Chirps and Biorthogonal Fourier Analysis
authors: Mengmeng He, Yizhen Jia, Maria Sabrina Greco, Fulvio Gini, Wen-Qin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Distributed coherent aperture radar (DCAR) operating in multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) mode is highly susceptible to interrupted sampling repeater jamming (ISRJ). Because ISRJ consists of partially sampled slices of multiple orthogonal waveforms, its effective suppression remains challenging. To exploit both the operational characteristics of MIMO DCAR and the formation mechanism of ISRJ, this paper proposes a jamming suppression algorithm that combines up-chirp (UC) and down-chirp (DC) waveform transmission with the biorthogonal Fourier transform (BFT). First, a bidirectional double sliding window pulse edge detector is employed to estimate and compensate for transmission path delay differences among channels. Next, BFT-based analysis is applied to separate UC- and DC-associated components in the received echoes, thereby suppressing cross-term energy leakage in multi-channel matched-filter outputs. Finally, leveraging the time-frequency similarity of jamming slices originating from orthogonal waveforms, the contaminated echo segments are partitioned in a piecewise manner and mitigated via frequency-phase compensation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves effective cross-term suppression and ISRJ cancellation while preserving system coherence.

title: A Robotic Laser Welding Seam Tracking Method for Complex 3-D Weld Trajectories Based on Geometric Structure Analysis_supp6-3661009.mp4
authors: Juan Liu
abstract: Seam sampling with line-structured-light sensors in laser welding is prone to projection distortion due to the complex 3-D geometry of sheet-metal workpieces, which leads to viewpoint-dependent variations in the sampled seam profile and affects laser incidence direction planning. Moreover, stable target tracking control becomes challenging when the 3-D seam trajectory contains small curvature radii or right-angle transitions. To address these issues, a method is proposed that compensates for projection distortion via deep-learning-based local surface-normal estimation and achieves robust viewpoint control along complex 3-D seam trajectories using prior geometric approximation of the seam trajectory. For normal estimation, seam features are encoded as “point-vector pair” superpixels and extracted by a single-stage deep convolutional network, enabling efficient, robust detection under welding glare, spatter, smoke, and reflections while maintaining boundary stability. Using the “point-vector pair” representation, the laser incidence direction is adaptively adjusted with reference to estimated surface normals to reduce pose uncertainty caused by projection distortion. For viewpoint control, a feedforward leading-direction control algorithm, derived from geometric–numerical analysis of predefined trajectory, ensures stable perception and high-precision continuous tracking along tight curves and right-angle turns. Across two experiments involving different workpiece geometries, the method achieves mean absolute laser focal position errors of 0.16 and 0.20 mm and mean absolute 3-D laser incident-direction errors of 0.99<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> and 1.58<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>, validating its efficiency, stability, and accuracy for sheet-metal laser seam tracking.

title: Instance-Semantic Self-Supervised Learning for Object Detection in Remote Sensing
authors: Yuchen Ma, Zhe Dong, Xiangrong Zhang, Yanfeng Gu, Tianzhu Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The paradigm of conducting downstream fine - tuning after large-scale pre-training has been widely applied in various object detection algorithms in remote sensing. However, the datasets used in the pre - training and fine-tuning stages of existing large models are usually different. Especially in object detection tasks, most methods involve pre-training on classification datasets and then fine - tuning with object detection datasets. This difference in data features often causes the pre-trained weights to deviate to some extent from the actual downstream tasks. To ensure the consistency of data features in remote sensing object detection tasks, this paper proposes an Instance-Semantic Self-Supervised Learning (ISSL) framework for object detection in remote sensing. It aims to enable the instance branch and the object semantic branch to learn the spatial and semantic features of objects respectively through contrastive learning, and complete the pre-training on remote sensing object detection datasets. The framework is proposed specifically for remote sensing object detection tasks, eliminating the differences arising from the inconsistency between the pre- training dataset and the dataset for downstream task. This paper evaluates the ISSL on the DOTA and DIOR dataset and compares it with several commonly used pre-training methods. The results show that this method performs better in object detection tasks, and the performance improvement is more significant under the condition of less labeled data.

title: Bandwidth-Aware Quantized-Event Distributed State Estimation for V2X Networks
authors: Haoliang Guan, Xiaoqi Lu, Yuanyuan Chi
abstract: Vehicle-to-everything (V2X) networks enable cooperative perception and control, yet practical deployments are constrained by tight bandwidth budgets, stochastic delays, and packet losses. We propose a bandwidth-aware quantized-event distributed state estimator that integrates local EKF-based filtering, normalized-innovation-squared (NIS) event triggering with multi-level innovation quantization, delay-aware reception (de-duplication and outdated-packet screening), and covariance-intersection (CI) fusion without exchanging cross-covariances. We establish uniform mean-square boundedness and prove monotonic accuracy–communication trade-offs with respect to quantization resolution, triggering thresholds, link reliability, and delay bounds. Simulations in cooperative adaptive cruise control (CACC) and intersection scenarios improve the RMSE–bandwidth Pareto frontier over periodic unquantized EKF+CI, triggering-only, and quantization-only baselines; under a strict per-agent budget (e.g., Bcap = 9 bits/step/agent), admitted traffic remains bounded and budget-aware retuning mitigates scalability-induced RMSE degradation as fleet size increases. Stress tests show enhanced robustness: innovation gating with covariance inflation reduces the divergence rate to zero in the evaluated setting. Profiling indicates that EKF updates and CI fusion dominate runtime with worst-case O(n3) complexity in the state dimension.

title: A Fast Double Null Control Concept Enabling Compact Fusion Reactors
authors: Mark Lafferty, Oliver Bardsley, Cosmas Heiss, Adriano Mele
abstract: The STEP prototype power plant (SPP) will utilize a double null diverted (DND) plasma topology to manage very high divertor heat fluxes within a compact design. The DND configuration provides advantages for divertor power balancing and H-mode access while it is connected, i.e., <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta R_{\text {sep}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> is small. Due to vertical instabilities and limitations of active vertical control, the plasma may deviate from a connected DND resulting in imbalanced divertor heat loads and potentially loss of H-mode, both compromising the viability of the plasma scenario. To mitigate this, we propose a novel double null balance (DNB) control scheme to control <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta R_{\text {sep}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> in conjunction with plasma vertical position control, preventing disconnection. In this study, we test an implementation of DNB control on TCV (tokamak à configuration variable). This controller demonstrates greatly improved control of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta R_{\text {sep}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, maintaining connection during vertical steps of up to +/−3 cm. The controller also demonstrates effective balancing of radiated power during these excursions, preventing radiated power spikes to the inner divertor regions. These positive results are a very encouraging proof of concept for the DNB control, warranting further study and consideration for potential integration into the SPP and other future double-null power plant designs.

title: Modeling and Impact Assessment of Load-Altering Attacks In TSO-DSO Coordinated Power Systems
authors: Yang Gao, Subhash Lakshminarayana, Iason-Iraklis Avramidis, Charalambos Konstantinou
abstract: At the intersection of multi-layer grid operations and emerging cyber-physical threats, lies the critical challenge of ensuring secure and resilient coordination between transmission system operators (TSOs) and distribution system operators (DSOs). While most existing studies focus on the operational optimization of TSO-DSO interactions, the increasing deployment of IoT-controllable loads raises concerns about cyber vulnerabilities. This paper presents a unified modeling framework for evaluating the impacts of load-altering attacks (LAAs) within an AC-AC coordinated TSO-DSO environment. An LAA strategy is developed that originates at the distribution level and propagates upward through TSO-DSO coordination. This “bottom-up” propagation reveals systemic interdependencies and vulnerabilities across grid layers. To capture a range of attacker capabilities, we model representative adversaries (black-box, white-box, and grey-box) each reflecting different levels of system observability and access, and aiming to disrupt grid operations and exploit market dynamics to increase profits from their controlled distributed resources, considering heterogeneity of local resources and contractual arrangements to improve the realism of the market-based coordination framework. Case studies demonstrate how LAAs disrupt locational marginal prices, power flows, and distributed energy resource operations. The results reveal systemic vulnerabilities to coordinated cyber-physical threats and expose hidden weaknesses of grid coordination mechanisms.

title: Modeling Time-Sensitive Causal Uplift in Marketing Campaigns: Evidence from Synthetic and Real-World Data
authors: Shafin Kashem, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum, Erfanul Hoque Bahadur, Khandaker Mohammad Mohi Uddin, Md. Maruf Hassan, Dewan Md. Farid
abstract: Uplift modeling (or incremental response modeling) is a technique used in many business applications to estimate the causal impact of an intervention. In the potential outcomes approach, it has established a conceptual basis for marketing analytics and causal inference studies, enabling more effective targeting of customers whose behavior is affected. Rather than focusing only on who is likely to respond, it looks at why and when responses occur. The approach estimates individualized treatment effects (ITEs) to identify which customers react specifically because of a marketing intervention. The analysis proceeds in two stages. First, a controlled synthetic dataset is designed to simulate real-world variation in demographics, behavior, and treatment assignment bias. This dataset allows performance benchmarking through T-Learner models trained with gradient boosting classifiers. The second stage adapts the Hillstrom Email Analytics dataset to include elements of timing, using customer recency as an indicator of behavioral time. By dividing the data into time bins, the study explores how uplift and the area under the uplift curve (AUUC) change across engagement periods. Results show that treatment effects vary over time, with response gains peaking before gradually falling due to customer fatigue. Discount-based campaigns deliver the highest additional conversions in the synthetic tests, while in the Hillstrom data, recent customers demonstrate stronger causal lift than long-inactive ones. Overall, the framework brings together simulated precision and empirical evidence, showing how time-aware uplift modeling connects predictive analytics with more adaptive, ethically informed marketing decisions.

title: Dynamic Feedback Stabilization of Switched Systems with Limited Communication Capacity
authors: Xiangze Lin, Jianni Cheng, Jingxin Huang, Ju H. Park
abstract: This paper delves into the issue of exponentially asymptotically dynamic feedback stabilization of switched systems with aperiodic sampled-data, taking limited communication capacity into account. Bearing the upper bound on communication bit rates in mind, an event-triggered mechanism, which rules out Zeno behaviours, is put forward to determine the sampling instants and the transmission bits with a lower bound that ensures asymptotic stability at a prescribed convergence rate for switched systems. The control strategy, which not only aims to minimize the number of transmissions but also takes the limited communication capability into account, is an emulation approach where the controller gains have been fixed in advance and the event-triggered rules have to be designed. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is evaluated on the presented simulations and its applications in single-link robotic manipulators.

title: Deep Learning-Based Multi-Condition Transfer Diagnosis of Compound Faults in Electrohydrostatic Actuator
authors: Xiansong He, Yuanhao Hu, Shufeng Zhang, Yibo Song, Xiaoli Zhao, Jianyong Yao
abstract: Electrohydrostatic Actuator (EHA) systems operating under variable conditions suffer from data distribution shifts and feature overlapping of compound faults, leading to degraded diagnostic performance. Existing methods often struggle to align features across different working domains and fail to accurately distinguish coupled fault patterns due to signal entanglement. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Multi-Condition Feature Aligned Capsule Network (MCFACN) for cross-domain compound fault diagnosis. An Adaptive Integrated Maximum Mean Square Discrepancy (AIMMSD) metric, incorporating higher-order statistics and mean constraints, is proposed to capture distribution differences more comprehensively, Furthermore, an aggregated attention routing mechanism is employed to integrate multi-condition source-domain data with convolutional and capsule features, enhancing domain adaptation and feature separability. Extensive experiments on multi-condition transfer tasks demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method. Specifically, MCFACN achieves an average diagnostic accuracy of 99.4%, validating its effectiveness and robustness for reliable aerospace applications.

title: XPoint: A Self-Supervised Visual-State-Space Based Architecture for Multispectral Image Registration
authors: Ismail Can Yagmur, Bahadir K. Gunturk, Hasan F. Ates
abstract: Accurate multispectral image matching presents significant challenges due to non-linear intensity variations across spectral modalities, extreme viewpoint changes, and the scarcity of labeled datasets. Current state-of-the-art methods are typically specialized for a single spectral difference, such as visible-infrared, and struggle to adapt to other modalities due to their reliance on expensive supervision, such as depth maps or camera poses. To address the need for rapid adaptation across modalities, we introduce XPoint, a self-supervised, modular image-matching framework designed for adaptive training and fine-tuning on aligned multispectral datasets, allowing users to customize key components based on their specific tasks. XPoint employs modularity and self-supervision to allow for the adjustment of elements such as the base detector, which generates pseudo-ground truth keypoints invariant to viewpoint and spectrum variations. The framework integrates a VMamba encoder, pre-trained on segmentation tasks, for robust feature extraction, and includes three joint decoder heads: two are dedicated to interest point and descriptor extraction; and a task-specific homography regression head imposes geometric constraints for superior performance in tasks like image registration. This flexible architecture enables quick adaptation to a wide range of sensing modalities. We demonstrate this by training on OPT–TH (optical–thermal) data and fine-tuning on several multispectral settings, including VIS–NIR (visual–near infrared, 0.75–1.4 μm), VIS–IR (visual–infrared, corresponding to long-wave infrared, 8–15 μm), and VIS–SAR (visual–synthetic aperture radar). Experimental results show that XPoint consistently outperforms or matches state-of-the-art methods in feature matching and image registration tasks across five distinct multispectral datasets. Our source code is publicly available at https://github.com/canyagmur/XPoint.

title: Software Vulnerability Detection Method of Hybrid Neural Network
authors: Jianfei Shen, JinZe Li
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: With the continuous growth of software system scale and complexity, software security issues have become a critical bottleneck constraining the development of information technology. Traditional vulnerability detection techniques, which rely on manual rules and struggle to capture complex code semantics, fail to meet the automated security audit demands of massive code in modern software engineering. This paper proposes a hybrid deep learning architecture integrating Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) and Multi-Scale Convolutional Neural Network (MS-CNN) for source code vulnerability detection. The model constructs a joint representation space for code semantics and syntax structure, combined with a gated attention mechanism to dynamically focus on critical vulnerability patterns. Through code slicing preprocessing based on Abstract Syntax Tree (AST), source code is transformed into context-embedded token sequences. The model adopts a hierarchical feature fusion strategy. Bi-LSTM captures long-distance dependencies across function calls, MS-CNN extracts local syntactic patterns and an adaptive attention mechanism weights critical code fragments. Experiments on a hybrid dataset (integrating SARD benchmark and NVD real-world vulnerabilities) containing 124,249 samples demonstrate that the model achieves 97% detection accuracy on the test set, with an F1 score 26.4 percentage points higher than traditional static analysis tools (Checkmarx). Further analysis of attention weight visualization confirms the model's ability to locate sensitive code fragments containing vulnerabilities. This study provides theoretical support for building an intelligent and interpretable vulnerability detection system, demonstrating significant practical value in DevSecOps practices.

title: AI-Enhanced Predictive Modeling of Electric Drive Oscillatory Dynamics
authors: Oleksii Sheremet, Yuliia Sokhina, Kateryna Sheremet
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence, particularly physics-informed neural networks, significantly enhances the predictive modeling of electric drive dynamics. Artificial intelligence allows for precise modeling and control, effectively managing the complexities and real-time demands of electric drives. Physics-informed neural networks combine data-driven insights with embedded physical laws, substantially improving the accuracy of motor behavior predictions under varied operating conditions. This method shows marked improvements in accurately predicting critical motor parameters such as torque, position, and speed. Additionally, the challenges of scalability and adaptability of artificial intelligence models to various electric drive environments are addressed. The enhancements provided by artificial intelligence technologies in electric drive systems are substantiated through practical experiments and validation. These tests confirm that artificial intelligence significantly boosts both the robustness and efficiency of these systems, representing notable progress in this area of engineering. Furthermore, the continued refinement and integration of these technologies are expected to drive future innovations and applications in the field.

title: Research on Classification-Segmentation Enhancement Technology for MRI Localizer Images Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yiyang Li, Jianghong Wu, Xing Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: To address the deficiency of insufficient sensitivity in single classification networks for cardiac and abdominal localizer images in MRI scans, this study combines the parallel multi-branch feature learning advantage of the ResNeXt architecture with the dense connection feature reuse characteristic of the DenseNet architecture to construct a voting-based classification model. Simultaneously, a U-Net-based cardiac segmentation model is introduced, proposing a classification-segmentation enhancement technique and developing a classification-segmentation enhanced network model. Through training and validation on real-world MRI medical image datasets (training and test sets), the results demonstrate that the proposed method can enhance classification accuracy and improve the classification performance for cardiac and abdominal localizer images under conditions of limited training data samples and computational resources.

title: BERT-CNN: An Enhanced Hybrid Encoder Model for Text Multi-Classification
authors: Guanglei Zhou, Wenbo Wei, Yonghua Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: In the face of intense volatility in the global LNG market, industry news and project reports have become critical sources for uncovering risk intelligence. However, their unstructured nature poses challenges for information extraction. To address this issue, this paper designs a two-step task framework capable of simultaneously performing multi-label risk factor classification and Risk propensity classification. The multi-label task aims to identify eight predefined risk factors mentioned in the text, including feedstock supply, geopolitics, and environmental impact. while the risk propensity classification task determines whether the text expresses positive, negative, or neutral stance. The core innovation lies in proposing a hybrid Bert-CNN architecture that integrates a pre-trained language model (BERT [1]) with a convolutional neural network (CNN). This model constructs a feature map containing multi-level semantic information by extracting hidden states from multiple intermediate Transformer [2] layers of BERT, then efficiently captures key local patterns and feature combinations using CNN. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to benchmark methods—including standard fine-tuned BERT and various state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs)—the proposed Bert-CNN model achieves significant performance improvements on both classification tasks. This study confirms that the Bert-CNN architecture enables deeper comprehension of specialized texts in the LNG sector, providing a robust technical foundation for developing intelligent industry risk early-warning and decision-support systems.

title: Efficient Satellite Image Cloud Detection Method Based on Selective State-Space Models
authors: Jingren Yan, Miao Li, Wei An, Longkai Zhang
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Cloud occlusion is a major source of interference in satellite remote-sensing imagery, directly affecting the efficiency of satellite resource scheduling and the accuracy of subsequent ground-object interpretation. The limited computational power of edge devices and the performance drawbacks of traditional detection algorithms further exacerbate the challenges of practical deployment. To address the high computational complexity and insufficient robustness in complex scenes seen in existing clouddetection methods, this paper proposes an efficient cloud-detection approach based on a selective state-space model (SSM). First, a lightweight Mamba backbone network is designed, which integrates the multi-scale feature extraction capability of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) encoder with the local feature enhancement advantages of the dark channel prior to construct a dual-branch feature-extraction module. Together with a feature pyramid network (FPN), this enables adaptive fusion of global semantic information and local details. Then, a selective state-space model (Selective SSM) is employed for efficient global modeling, and accurate cloud masks are produced via skip connections and a segmentation head. Experimental results show that on the satellite dataset, the proposed system achieves a detection accuracy of 86.5%, and the computational complexity is reduced to linear order. Compared with mainstream algorithms such as Transformer- and U-Net-based models, the method maintains superior detection accuracy while achieving linear computational complexity $O(N)$, thereby meeting the real-time deployment requirements of edge scenarios such as on-board satellite devices and providing technical support for optimized satellite resource scheduling and efficient processing of remotesensing data.

title: Industrial Power Load and Electricity-Carbon Classification Based on Improved VMD-DBN Algorithm
authors: Ye Zhang, Qingchun Li, Jian Zhang, Jian Li, Bo Dong, Nan Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Conversion and Control Engineering (PCCE)
abstract: In the industrial field, power load is highly volatile and coupled with carbon emissions. In order to meet the requirements of load forecasting accuracy and electricity-carbon collaborative analysis capabilities, this study proposes an algorithm framework that integrates adaptive variational mode decomposition (VMD) and improved deep belief network (DBN). Firstly, the modal decomposition number K of VMD is dynamically optimized through the sample entropy minimization criterion. Secondly, the Quantum-behaved Particle Swarm Optimization (QPSO) algorithm is introduced in the DBN pre-training stage to adaptively adjust the weight initialization parameters between network layers. Finally, a feature fusion layer based on the attention mechanism is constructed to jointly process the sub-modal components of VMD decomposition and industrial multi-source data, and output load prediction values and electric carbon classification labels. On the dataset of 6 steel enterprises, the load forecasting minimum absolute percentage error (MAPE) of the improved VMD-QPSO-DBN model is only 1.95%; the average electricity-carbon classification accuracy is 98.1%, and the F1 score is increased to an average of 0.977. This algorithm effectively solves the bottleneck problem of load-carbon emission joint modeling under complex industrial scenarios, and provides reliable technical support for "source-grid-load-storage" coordination and low-carbon scheduling.

title: Instantaneous Power Distribution Study in a Doubly Fed Induction Generator in a Rated Mode
authors: Artur Postil, Oleksii Bialobrzheskyi, Maxim Oliynichenko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: An doble feed inductor generator a unique machine that is rationally used in wind power plants. The ability to regulate the stator current frequency with deep changes in the rotation frequency, which the multiplier mechanical transmission is not able to regulate, is realized by regulating the rotor current frequency and amplitude. Many technical solutions for regulating the mode parameters are usually based on the theory developed for synchronous machines. The work goal is to study the instantaneous power indicators of an doble feed inductor generator. As a result of applying the instantaneous power balance for the generator of the designated type, power balance equations are obtained. The obtained results are conjugated with the well-known theory called pq-theory. The belief is formulated that the instantaneous power of each active element of the machine can be used for the appropriate regulation of the machine parameters. A model of an doble feed inductor generator, a system for calculating the instantaneous power of the active elements of the machine is built. The modeling results break the active and reactive instantaneous powers distribution in a active elements of doubly fed induction generator.

title: Research on the Application of Robot Visual Recognition Based on YOLOv3 Algorithm in the Development of Smart Museums
authors: Pengqian Zhang, Jiexuan Du, Beichen Xu, Yonglian Wei, Jinfeng Hou, Zhiyuan Pu, Longbing Tan, Mingzhe Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence, the application of robots in visitor counting, behavior monitoring, and collection security has gradually increased. As one of the key technologies for robots, visual recognition technology can identify and locate information such as visitor numbers, visitor behavior, collection types, and collection preservation status in real time, thereby improving museum management efficiency and service quality. This paper proposes a robot visual recognition solution based on the YOLOv3 algorithm, aiming to optimize object detection accuracy and real-time performance, addressing the impact of complex museum environments (e.g., lighting changes, crowds, dynamic objects) on detection effectiveness. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLOv3 excels in accuracy, real-time performance, and dynamic object detection, showing significant advantages over algorithms like Faster R-CNN and SSD in small object detection and dynamic object tracking. Finally, the study shows that the YOLOv3-based visual recognition can be effectively applied to robots in museum to enhance their performance in practical applications.

title: Interference Analysis 5G Network and LoRa Technology Using Minimum Coupling Loss Method (MCL)
authors: Deni Alva Reza, Rina Amaelia, Soni Joyo Saputra, Alfin Hikmaturokhman, Solichah Larasati, Arrizky Ayu Faradila Purnama
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: The rapid advancement of wireless communication technologies has led to multiple systems operating within the same frequency band. Long Range (LoRa) and Fifth Generation (5G) technologies both utilize the 925 MHz frequency, resulting in potential spectrum interference. This interference can degrade the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SINR), reduce throughput, and impact the reliability of data transmission. This study evaluates the coexistence of LoRa and 5G through simulations using SEAMCAT software with the Monte Carlo method. Key parameters analyzed include Interfering Received Signal Strength (iRSS), Desired Received Signal Strength (dRSS), Carrier-to-Interference Ratio (C/I), and Minimum Coupling Loss (MCL). The MCL method is used to determine the necessary separation between systems operating on the same or adjacent channels. Simulations were conducted with distance variations from 1 to 35 km and transmit power levels ranging from 40 to 43 dBm. Results show that dRSS remains stable around −126 to −127 dBm, while iRSS decreases and C/I improves with increased distance. The highest interference probability reached 34.5% at 1 km with 43 dBm transmit power, while the lowest was 0% at 35 km with 40 dBm. Lower transmit power is recommended to minimize interference between LoRa and 5G systems.

title: Generative Flow Networks for DAG-Aware Multi-Objective Task Scheduling in Edge–IoT Systems
authors: Imene Ayat, Djamila Mechta, Saad Harous
publish: 2025 International Conference on Research in Computing at Feminine (RIF)
abstract: Efficient scheduling of dependent IoT tasks in Edge Computing is challenging due to heterogeneous resources, strict latency requirements, and inter-task precedence constraints. We model task dependencies as a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG), which turns the scheduling problem into a combinatorial optimization challenge with an exponentially ample search space. To address this, we propose a Task-Dependency Aware Generative Flow Network (TD-GFlowNet) that incrementally constructs valid schedules while learning to sample them in proportion to a multi-objective utility function combining latency, energy consumption, and deadline penalties. Unlike traditional reinforcement learning methods that optimize a single trajectory at a time, TD-GFlowNet efficiently explores diverse scheduling solutions guided by the DAG structure. We benchmark our method against policy-gradient RL, Greedy, and Random baselines under physics-based edge settings. Experimental results show that TD-GFlowNet achieves superior latency-deadline trade-offs compared to RL, while being more robust to server heterogeneity than Greedy policies, thus establishing a generative paradigm for combinatorial optimization in edge task scheduling. Our contribution is not only the adoption of GFlowNets but their DAG-feasible, two-head factorization (task/server) with a physics-grounded multi-objective utility.

title: Feedback-Guided Block-Level Encoding Prioritization Using Bandit Learning for Image Transmission
authors: Minyu Jin, Bintao Hu, Hui Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Block-based image transmission is an effective approach for efficient bandwidth utilization, as it allows the sender to transmit only those regions of an image that carry the most relevant visual information. In this framework, an image is partitioned into multiple non-overlapping blocks, each containing different spatial details and levels of perceptual importance. However, the perceived importance of image regions varies among users, depending on their viewing preferences or application requirements. Such diversity creates a need for an adaptive mechanism that can dynamically determine which blocks deserve higher transmission priority. To address this issue, we introduce a feedback-guided prioritization algorithm based on the multi-armed bandit (MAB) model. The proposed algorithm updates the priority of each block according to user feedback by learning the statistical relationship between transmitted content and user satisfaction. Through this continuous interaction, the system gradually identifies the regions that contribute most to user-perceived quality and allocates more transmission resources. Comprehensive simulations verify that the proposed framework can effectively capture user preferences and enhance the overall visual experience.

title: Comparing Attention-Based Architectures for Advanced Vehicle Detection
authors: Cetta Maulana Andhika, Surya Michrandi Nasution, Reza Rendian Septiawan, Saichand Raghupatrini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: With the rapid progress of deep learning and computer vision in intelligent transportation, vehicle detection is now a critical component to tackle. While attention-based models, particularly a variant of Detection Transformer, have shown significant promise, a direct comparative analysis of several state-of-the-art attention-based models: DE-TR, RFDETR, RT-DETR, and DEIMv2, alongside the prominent stage detector, YOLOv12. The models are evaluated on a dataset of 653 images ($640 \times 640$ pixels) captured from a fixed-angle highway CCTV, featuring vehicle classes (car, bus, truck) with significant scale variations. Mean-Average-Precision (mAP) and inference latency were used to benchmark performance. With mAP@.50:.95 of 0.563, the experimental results show that RTDETR outperforms other models in terms of overall accuracy. Additionally, it performs well on medium and small objects with mAP 0.762 and 0.494, respectively. RT-DETR offered the best combination of high accuracy and effective performance, with an inference latency of $\mathbf{5 7. 0 0 ~ m s}$, while RF-DETR recorded the lowest latency, 52.58 ms. In contrast, a critical failure in detecting small objects - with mAP 0.128 - was observed by the baseline DE-TR model. In conclusion, RT-DETR is a strong contender for real-world implementation since it provides the best and most balanced architecture for vehicle detection tasks in highway monitoring scenarios.

title: Predicting Breast Cancer Outcomes Using Integrated Machine Learning Classifiers
authors: Khushi Kansara, Yug Trivedi, Zuzer Chathiwala, Husen Lakdawala, Muktesh Chandra, Sejal Shah
abstract: Breast cancer is a serious world health problem, and it requires precise and understandable predictive models. In this paper, the authors introduce a hybrid ensemble machine learning model to predict survival in patients based on heterogeneous clinical and demographic variables. Five classifiers, i.e., Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM, and KNN, were combined through a soft-voting ensemble after preprocessing the data with label encoding and scaling. The hybrid model had 90.93% test accuracy, beating single models by enhancing generalization and minimizing overfitting. Feature importance unveiled Tumor Size, Survival Duration, T Stage, and 6th Stage as the most important predictors. The model shows excellent predictive capability and clinical value, justifying its applications in oncology decision-making with promise for future deep learning incorporation.

title: Consideration of Inter-Provincial Electricity Carbon Emission Factors Accounting for Regional Dedicated Line Transmission
authors: Shize Zhang, Jinwei Song, Dejun Lou, Yue Yan, Qi He, Shanshan Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: Existing carbon emission calculation methods face several limitations: they use average regional grid emission factors, resulting in coarse geographic granularity and inequity among provinces [1]–[5]. They rely on outdated annual data, failing to capture temporal variations; and they neglect dedicated inter-provincial lines such as UHVDC, leading to overestimation in certain regions. To address these issues, this study proposes a method that accounts for dedicated line transmission. Leveraging high-frequency, accurate power system data, it refines carbon emission factors from the regional level to provincial and monthly scales, fully incorporating inter-provincial line emissions. Compared with traditional regional approaches, the proposed method significantly improves accuracy: mean absolute error (MAE) decreases by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0154 \mathbf{t C O}_{\mathbf{2}} / \mathbf{M W h}$</tex>, mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) drops by 1.952 pp, and coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>) rises from 0.76 to 0.95, demonstrating strong consistency with actual monitoring data.

title: Algorithm for Fire and Smoke Image Detection Based on Multi-Scale Feature Extraction
authors: Xinpeng Zhou, Weilin Wulin, Jiakan Li, Kunsheng Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address the insufficient detection accuracy of fire and smoke images in complex scenarios, this study proposes an optimized multi-scale feature extraction algorithm with YOLOv9s as the baseline model. The improvements are implemented in three aspects: First, the backbone network is reconstructed by replacing the original network with RepViT, which integrates the local feature capture capability of convolution and the global dependency modeling capability of Transformer to enhance the extraction efficiency of fine-grained features of fire and smoke. Second, the feature fusion structure is optimized by introducing BiFPN to construct a bidirectional cross-scale propagation path, which alleviates information attenuation during feature transmission and improves the ability to model feature correlations of smoke targets at different scales. Finally, an innovative C2f_CBAM module is embedded; this module screens key semantic channels through channel attention and locates smoke regions via spatial attention, thereby enhancing feature discriminability. Experimental results demonstrate that through the three-level optimization, the proposed algorithm achieves a 3.5% improvement in mAP50 for fire and smoke detection, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 M}$</tex> reduction in the number of parameters, and effectively controls and reduces the false detection rate. It can thus provide technical support for early fire warning.

title: Dual-band frequency selective rasorber with broadband absorption and transmission
authors: Han Chen, Jue Wang, Pengcheng Wu, Liangzhen Lin
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: In this paper, a dual-band frequency selective rasorber (FSR) with broadband absorption and transmission is proposed. The FSR is consisted of the lossy layer, the lossless layer and an air layer in the middle. The lossy layer consists mainly of dipoles with circular spiral resonators and resistors connecting the dipole at the four corners. The lossless layer consists of three metal layers, with the top and bottom metal layers adopting the same structure, both of which are square ring structures internally connected with bent crosses, and the middle metal layer is a square inductor-type grid. The simulated results demonstrate that the antenna achieves Two passbands with an insertion loss of 3 dB, located in the frequency ranges of 4.62-7.36 GHz and 12.51-15.05 GHz, respectively, with a total relative bandwidth of 64.2%, and three absorption bands with an 80% absorption were realized, located in the frequency ranges of 1.46-4.19 GHz, 9.62-11.92 GHz, and 16.66-16.95 GHz, respectively.

title: Improvement of KNN Algorithm and Research on Fruit Image Classification Technology
authors: Liying Wang, Jiong Zhang, Jia Zhu, Xiaoxia Li, Dai Cao, YanHui Feng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The traditional K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) algorithm often encounters problems such as weak feature expression ability and poor adaptability to fixed K-values in image classification tasks, which affects classification accuracy. Based on this problem, this paper proposes an improved KNN algorithm that combines Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Dynamic K-value Selection, using CNN to enhance feature expression while selecting appropriate K-values based on local sample density. Experiments have shown that the improved KNN algorithm has overcome the performance bottleneck of traditional KNN algorithms in fruit image classification tasks, and also provides a new method for small sample image classification.

title: Multi-Source Perception Fusion Recognition and Hierarchical Repulsion System for Airspace Threats of Transmission Lines
authors: Guogen Li, Yangzhi Wei
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: With the rapid development of the low-altitude economy, the airspace safety of transmission lines is facing severe challenges. Unauthorized “black flight” incidents of drones occur frequently, which are likely to cause conductor collisions and power outages. Traditional safety protection technologies have defects such as low recognition accuracy, high deployment costs, and lack of closed-loop management. To address the above issues, this paper proposes a multi-source perception fusion recognition and hierarchical expulsion system for airspace threats of transmission lines. Integrating spectrum, voiceprint, and visual triple perception technologies, combined with dynamic identity authentication and adaptive expulsion strategies, a closed-loop protection system of “perception - recognition - early warning - expulsion verification” is constructed. The system realizes accurate identification of authorized/unauthorized drones, differentiated disposal of threats, and all-weather monitoring of transmission line corridors. Test results show that the system's recognition accuracy for authorized drones exceeds 99%, and for unauthorized high-risk drones exceeds 95%; the cost of a single set of fixed equipment is reduced by more than 40% compared with traditional equipment, and the response time from target recognition to expulsion activation is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 1.2 \mathrm{s}$</tex>. This technology effectively breaks through the bottleneck of balancing protection cost and efficiency in the industry, and provides reliable technical support for the digital and intelligent transformation of the power system.

title: Engineering Design and Performance Optimization of a Classroom Monitoring and Intervention System Featuring Pose-Invariant Face Recognition
authors: Dongxing Wang, Weixing Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This study aims to design and implement an efficient and reliable classroom monitoring and intervention system to address the issues of insufficient robustness in pose-invariant face detection and the disconnect between learning state perception and teaching intervention in existing classroom monitoring systems. Focusing on the three core areas of algorithm optimization, module integration, and engineering implementation, this paper focuses on the following three aspects of work: firstly, at the detection level, an improved RetinaFace algorithm based on MobileNet v3 and ECANet modules is proposed, which improves the average accuracy (AP) of detecting small-scale and occluded faces to 77.5% on the WiderFace difficult subset, and reduces the parameter count to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4. 2 M}$</tex>; secondly, at the reconstruction level, a multi-scale recursive generative adversarial network (MS-RNNGAN) is designed, which achieves a Rank 1 recognition rate of 81.78% for extreme pose faces with a yaw angle of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 9^{\circ}$</tex> on the Multi PIE dataset, with a reconstruction speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 \text{ms} /$</tex>frame; Finally, at the system level, a five-layer modular engineering architecture integrating the above algorithms was constructed, and through engineering optimizations such as multi-threaded parallelism and TensorRT acceleration, the per-frame processing time was controlled within 30ms, achieving end-to-end real-time processing from video streams to intervention signals, and incorporates modules for state evaluation and intervention triggering based on the Classroom Attention Index (CCI). The experimental results show that the key algorithm modules of this system have excellent performance, and the overall architecture meets the real-time requirements of real classroom scenarios, providing a practical engineering solution for smart classroom construction.

title: Edge-AI Technologies for Sustainability: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Shadi Mahmoodi Khaniabadi, Salviano Soares, Sérgio Ivan Lopes
abstract: Edge-AI offers a promising strategy for implementing distributed sustainable digital systems by offloading computational tasks from centralized cloud-AI infrastructures to local and energy-efficient devices. This study investigates the potential of edge-AI in key domains for sustainability, including smart energy systems, precision agriculture, sustainable smart cities, industrial IoT, and environmental monitoring. Through a literature review, comparative analysis, and evaluation of practical implementations, this research demonstrates that decentralized inference enables substantial sustainabilityrelated gains. Specifically, edge-AI technologies have shown energy savings of up to 28%, latency reductions of 35%, and bandwidth reductions of up to 60% compared to cloud-based models. These improvements are complemented by improved data privacy through on-device processing and reduced reliance on remote servers. Despite these advantages, challenges such as limited hardware capacity, scalability constraints, integration complexity, and lifecycle concerns persist. This study highlights the critical role of edge-AI technologies in promoting green standards to achieve the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UN SDGs) by 2030.

title: A DC-Block-Based Architecture to Enable High Power EV Fast Charging with Built-in Precise Peak Demand Management
authors: Thomas Seo, Jk Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: The exponential rise in energy demand-driven by U.S. policies on local manufacturing and AI-powered data centers-is straining the grid and increasing the need for Distributed Energy Resources (DER). Utilities face constraints from aging infrastructure and distribution upgrade costs, including transmission expansion. Smart DERs help relieve these pressures. This paper presents a DC-Block-based architecture that reduces AC loading on utility distribution lines, particularly for EV fast charging. The system, Clean Virtual Power (CVP), integrates energy storage with telemetry and energy management. Its algorithm, Adaptive Energy Reprofiling Solution (AERS), achieved up to $99.8 \%$ demand management accuracy based on data from commercial and industrial sites. Building on this experience, CVP introduces “HubCharge,” a fast-charging solution designed to avoid utility upgrades during deployment and reduce peak demand during operation. This supports grid stability and EV infrastructure growth.

title: Computational Modeling of the Evolutionary Mechanism of Group Behavior in Social Networks Based on Large Language Models and Graph Neural Networks
authors: Zijing Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Existing research on group behavior in social networks separates the two dimensions of content semantics and relational structure, lacking a unified model that can capture both semantic driving forces and dynamic structural reconstruction. This paper proposes a semantic-structural coupling evolutionary mechanism based on a large language model (LLM) and a graph neural network (GNN). The LLM extracts sentiment, stance, and topic semantic embeddings to construct a temporally recursive semantic state. A dynamic GNN jointly updates node states and edge weights, enabling closed-loop coupling of semantically driven connectivity changes and structurally modulated semantic propagation within the same dynamical system. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed LLM-GNN model achieves an AUC of 0.874 for group trend prediction, an AUC of 0.890 for polarization stage identification, a stage detection accuracy of 0.851, and a semantic causal explanatory power of 0.748. This study demonstrates the evolutionary mechanism of the bidirectional interaction between semantics and structure, providing a new technical approach for group behavior modeling.

title: A Multi-Scale Neural Network Framework for Blind Image Quality Assessment with Double Branch
authors: Jun Wang, Hangyu Jin, Xiaoli Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The existing no-reference image quality assessment methods mainly use the global information of the image and ignore the local information, which affect the extraction of detailed information. To solve this problem,this work proposesa multiScale NR-IQA algorithm based on multi-scale Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with double branch.First of all, taking image patches as input, and usingmulti-scaleconvolution kernelto obtain features of the patches. Then, a weighted average aggregation layer is added to the network. This algorithm integrates the global and local information of the image, which cansolve the influence of different distortion types on the image quality score, meanwhile, it can extract more detailed information and edge information. Experiments on the different databases demonstrate the superiority of the proposed algorithm compared with existing NR-IQA methods. Finally, cross-database evaluation on the TID2013 database and CSIQ databaseshows the high generalization ability between different databases.
