title: EBPersons: A Dataset for Person Detection at the Edges of Buildings
authors: Zitao Gao, Bing Qu, Chunluan Zhou, Junsong Yuan, Zhigang Tu
abstract: With the increasing prevalence of buildings, incidents of falling from heights have become more and more frequent. Accurately detecting individuals at the edges of buildings through surveillance videos is crucial for timely intervention and accident prevention. However, this task, termed Person Detection at the Edges of Buildings (PDEB), presents significant challenges including variations in lighting conditions, occlusions, and small size of person instances. Existing person detection datasets are inadequate for PDEB due to domain gaps. To address this issue, we construct EBPersons, a completely new dataset specifically designed for PDEB. Comprising 1,314 videos captured across over 300 diverse building scenes with diverse lighting conditions, EBPersons provides a rich and challenging benchmark for PDEB research. Furthermore, we propose a baseline method specifically designed for PDEB, named STASH, which includes three key components: a Scale Match strategy to improve small object detection, a Temporal ROI Align Operator to leverage temporal context, and a Sequential-level Semantics Aggregation head to enhance feature representation. Extensive experiments are conducted on EBPersons to compare our method with other detectors, including generic object detectors, pedestrian detectors, and video object detectors. The results demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed STASH, providing a strong baseline for future research on PDEB. Our EBPer sons dataset and the baseline code are publicly available at https://ebpersons.github.io/.

title: Human Pose Estimation in Low-Light Condition With Decomposition and Modulation
authors: Xiao Jin, Chengxu Liu, Yujie Dun, Xueming Qian
abstract: Human pose estimation (HPE) is a fundamental problem in computer vision, aiming to locate anatomical keypoints of the human body in a given picture. Benefiting from recent progress in deep learning, dominant HPE methods can achieve more advanced performance. Unfortunately, these methods rely heavily on large-scale, high-quality datasets captured expensively, resulting in limited learning capabilities in data-constrained low-light situations. Existing methods enhance the model's ability for low-light scenarios by performing intermediate feature alignment between low-light image and its well-lit counterpart. However, these methods fall short in fully exploiting explicit semantic feature exploitation that is independent of lighting conditions, resulting in sub-optimal performance. In this paper, we propose a Progressive Decomposition-Modulation network (PDMNet) for human pose estimation in extremely low-light condition. In particular, PDMNet mainly consists of 1) a semantic-specific decomposition module (SDM) for decomposing reflectance component with rich semantic information, and 2) a semantic-specific modulation mechanism (SMM) that enables the reflectance component to modulate the representation learning of human body parts in a tailored manner. Two closely-related components cooperate with each other to achieve more effective content-specific feature learning in low-light conditions. We further equip them progressively into different scales to enhance the feature learning. Experimental results demonstrate the superiority of PDMNet over state-of-the-art models on publicly available datasets. Our code will be released soon.

title: Synchronized Angle Control for the Thumb and the Index Finger in an Anthropomorphic Hand Actuated by Pneumatic Muscles
authors: Ling Zhao, Chunling Qin, Hongjiu Yang, Tao Wang
abstract: To enhance tracking accuracy and robustness of an anthropomorphic hand actuated by pneumatic muscles, an synchronized angle control strategy for the thumb and the index finger is proposed in this article. The research addresses inverse kinematics problem and tackles challenge of joint coupling, which significantly improves control performance of the multijoint robotic fingers. A simulated annealing Newton–Raphson (SANR) method is employed to efficiently solve the inverse kinematics of the index finger. A nonlinear extended state observer (ESO) is designed to estimate the joint coupling in the thumb and the index finger. A global terminal sliding mode controller is developed to achieve precise synchronized angle control and improve overall control performance. Stability of the nonlinear ESO and the global terminal sliding mode controller is analyzed using Lyapunov methods. Experimental results demonstrate effectiveness of the synchronized angle control strategy in enhancing tracking precision and robustness of the anthropomorphic hand under joint coupling.

title: Angular Consistency Adaptive Filtering and Complex Generalized Gaussian-Based Phase Linking for DS-InSAR
authors: Shuyi Yao, Alejandro C. Frery, Timo Balz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Distributed scatterer InSAR (DS-InSAR) processing is essential for retrieving surface deformation in areas lacking strong point targets. Conventional workflows typically involve selecting statistically homogeneous pixels (SHPs) based on amplitude similarity, followed by phase estimation under the complex circular Gaussian (CCG) model. However, amplitude statistics primarily reflect the backscattering strength of surface targets and may not sufficiently capture differences in decorrelation behavior. For example, when distinct scatterers exhibit similar backscatter strength but differ in coherence, amplitude-based selection methods may be unable to distinguish between them. Moreover, CCG-based SHP selection methods and phase estimators may lack robustness and suffer performance degradation under non-Rayleigh amplitude fluctuations. Focusing on the scale-invariant second-order statistics, we propose the Shape-to-Scale approach for DS-InSAR processing. The method first identifies pixels sharing a common angular scattering structure, which implies having a similar interferometric coherence structure (or equivalently, a similar decorrelation behavior). These pixels are referred to as shape statistically homogeneous pixels (SSHP) and are extracted using an angular consistency adaptive filter (ACAF) based on the complex angular central Gaussian (CACG) distribution. A phase estimation approach (phase-linking) derived from the complex generalized Gaussian (CGG) model is then applied, enhancing robustness to non-Rayleigh scattering. Experiments on both simulated and real SAR datasets showed that the proposed approach yielded more consistent coherence-structure clustering than the considered baselines, and improved the robustness of phase linking while preserving spatial details in heterogeneous and low-coherence areas. By explicitly separating shape (closely related to coherence structure) from scale (amplitude distribution), this work establishes a unified statistical framework that facilitates reliable and interpretable DS-InSAR time-series analysis.

title: Hierarchical Distributed Control with Projected Gradient Descent for Real-Time Congestion Mitigation in MVDC Distribution Systems
authors: Qian Xiao, Yansong Zhao, Wenbiao Lu, Yu Jin, Hongjie Jia, Yunfei Mu, Jiebei Zhu, Chen Shen, Remus Teodorescu, Josep M. Guerrero
abstract: Ineffective congestion management in medium-voltage DC distribution system risks thermal overloads, voltage violations, and systemic instability. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a distributed congestion mitigation method via adaptive real-time control. First, a hierarchical control framework integrating congestion constraints is established, with optimality conditions rigorously derived. Second, the upper layer generates dispatch references to guide lower-layer operations. Projection operators are then introduced to reformulate optimality conditions for controller compatibility, enabling distributed optimal control laws based on projected gradient descent. This approach achieves real-time congestion management and optimal operation. Simulations and comparisons are conducted on a modified IEEE 33 medium-voltage DC distribution system. Compared to model predictive control, the proposed method achieves reduced fluctuations under varying operating conditions. In contrast to distributed robust optimization, it exhibits superior economic performance.

title: Reconstruction of 3-D Sound Speed Fields Based on Tensor Tucker Decomposition
authors: Zhaoying Wang, Hongzhou Chai, Minzhi Xiang, Kaidi Jin, Siyuan Bai
abstract: Sound velocity is a key marine environmental parameter that strongly influences sound propagation in the ocean. In oceanographic research and exploration, the precise reconstruction of 3-D sound speed fields (3-D SSFs) serves as a fundamental prerequisite for improving the measurement and positioning accuracy of marine acoustic equipment and maintaining the reliability of underwater wireless communication. However, conventional empirical orthogonal function (EOF)-based SSF reconstruction algorithms exhibit inherent limitations due to their inability to account for 3-D structural features of ocean SSFs and their inadequate capacity for deep feature extraction. In this regard, this study proposes a modified conjugate gradient (MCG) algorithm incorporating the Tucker tensor decomposition. The integration of the conjugate gradient optimization framework and tensor-based high-order principal component analysis (PCA) can extract spatial structural features of 3-D SSF and robustly estimate missing values through an advanced tensor imputation mechanism. Experimental validation utilizes the Global Ocean Argo gridded data set (BOA_Argo) from the South China Sea, with comparative analyses conducted against conventional EOF and steepest descent (SD) algorithms. The raw data set is partitioned into training and testing sets to independently evaluate the algorithm’s modeling performance and missing-data imputation capability. The proposed MCG algorithm achieves substantial performance improvements, yielding 96.3% and 85.4% enhancements in modeling accuracy relative to conventional EOF and SD approaches, respectively. Furthermore, the algorithm exhibits superior imputation capabilities, with accuracy gains of 84.3% and 80.7% compared to these baseline methods. Crucially, the proposed method maintains exceptional stability and reliability even under challenging acoustic conditions and limited prior information.

title: Degradation of the FY-3D MERSI-II: Cross-Calibration with MODIS and Its Impact on Aerosol Retrieval
authors: Xin Su, Lunche Wang, Xingtao Wang, Yingshuang Li, Shikuan Jin, Yi Wang, Ming Zhang, Lin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The Medium Resolution Spectral Imager II (MERSI-II) onboard the Chinese Fengyun-3D satellite has experienced radiometric degradation in its reflective solar bands since its launch, leading to biases in quantitative retrievals. This study uses high-accuracy MODIS observations as a reference to improve the radiometric calibration performance of MERSI-II and quantifies its impact on aerosol retrieval. First, the ratio of MERSI-II observed top-of-atmosphere (TOA) reflectance to MODIS-simulated MERSI-II reflectance, after angular and spectral matching, is used to evaluate calibration uncertainty and degradation trends based on three pseudo-invariant targets: the Dunhuang desert, DomeC in Antarctica, and deep convective clouds. The evaluation results from 2019 to 2023 show the 550, 650, and 865 nm bands are stable (<0.62%/yr), while the blue bands (λ <500 nm) and shortwave infrared bands (λ >1000 nm) degrade significantly (>1.26%/yr, p-value <0.01), with the 412 nm band showing the highest degradation (4.23%/yr). The degradation trend was fitted with a quadratic polynomial for correction modeling. After correction, seven of the eleven bands exhibit radiometric drift rates within ±0.08%/yr, with all bands below ±1%/yr. Then, the MERSI-II data in 2019–2024 were used for aerosol retrieval with the Land General Aerosol (LaGA) Retrieval algorithm over East Asia. Results show that corrected TOA reflectance improves aerosol optical depth (AOD) and fine-mode AOD (AODF) retrieval accuracy: mean bias improves from −0.045 (−0.075) to −0.001 (0.001), and total errors are reduced by 15% and 27%, respectively. Additionally, the errors in AOD and AODF retrieved from corrected data show no significant temporal trend. Finally, the impact of radiometric degradation on aerosol retrieval was examined, showing that the degradation rate of ~1.9%/yr at 470 nm leads to an increase of 12%/yr (18%/yr) in AOD (AODF) errors. The error propagation factor from TOA reflectance error to errors in AOD and AODF exceeds 9. These results demonstrate that the proposed model significantly enhances MERSI-II’s radiometric accuracy and long-term stability, supporting quantitative and climate applications.

title: A Dynamic Quantum-Resistant Selective Encryption Approach for Agricultural Sensors with Limited Resources
authors: Akshay Kappala, Eric Yocam, Nikil Kayastha, Sai Ram Vodnala, Varghese Vaidyan, Gurcan Comert, Yong Wang
abstract: Agricultural IoT sensors face dual challenges of severe resource constraints and emerging quantum computing threats. Existing encryption schemes either lack quantum resistance or impose excessive computational overhead that is unsuitable for battery-powered field devices. We propose a Dynamic Quantum-Resistant Selective Encryption (DQRSE) scheme that combines the 2D DiscreteWavelet Transform (2D-DWT) with NIST-standardized post-quantum cryptography. The approach selectively encrypts the LL2 sub-band using AES-256-GCM, while protecting high-frequency sub-bands using HMAC-SHA256. ML-KEM-1024 provides quantum-resistant key encapsulation, and ML-DSA ensures authenticated signatures. A novel content-adaptive key rotation mechanism triggers new key generation when Frobenius-norm image differences exceed threshold τ , balancing security against efficiency. Evaluation in the CottonWeedID15 dataset (5,187 agricultural images) demonstrates key selection diversity of 7.88 bits/byte (dynamic) vs 3.32 bits / byte (static), Number of pixels change rate (NPCR) 99.6%, unified average change intensity (UACI) 33.4%, encrypted pixel correlation coefficient <0.005 and minimal computational overhead compared to static approaches 3.32 bits / byte (key selection diversity). DQRSE represents the first quantum-resistant dynamic selective encryption specifically optimized for agricultural sensor networks. Performance evaluation conducted on Debian VMs (4GB RAM, 4 cores) represents idealized lower bounds; the target ARM Cortex-M agricultural sensors (<256KB RAM, 48-168 MHz) will exhibit 25 × higher latency and energy consumption. Future work includes hardware validation on embedded platforms.

title: Intelligent EMS Dispatch via Adaptive Learning with Counterfactual and Risk-Aware Optimization
authors: Wuttipong Kumwilaisak
abstract: Emergency Medical Services (EMS) dispatch demands real-time decisions under uncertainty, requiring simultaneous optimization of response speed, spatial equity, and system resilience. We present PCR COCO, a unified dispatch framework introducing four innovations: (1) Priority-Conditional Risk Control (PCR), which selectively activates tail-risk protection only for critical incidents approaching service-level violations, avoiding the efficiency penalties of uniform conservatism; (2) Counterfactual Opportunity Cost Optimization (COCO), which maintains spatial equity through dispatch-integrated shadow pricing without separate relocation procedures; (3) a context-adaptive learning module using contextual bandits to automatically tune optimization weights across operational regimes; and (4) a scalable architecture with O(k2) candidate pruning and micro-batch assignment for real-time tractability. Rigorous simulation-based validation (n = 25 paired seeds per scenario, bootstrap confidence intervals, Holm–Bonferroni correction) demonstrates statistically significant improvements across four load regimes. Under moderate load, red-priority response time decreases by 10.1% (12.32→11.08 min, p < 0.0001), overall response time improves by 3.7%, and SLA compliance increases from 91.8% to 94.3%. These gains persist under light, heavy, and surge conditions, with zone-level equity metrics showing modest but consistent improvements. The results establish that structured multi-objective optimization with selective risk control can yield measurable performance gains relative to classical heuristic strategies in simulated EMS operations.

title: Multi-Source Data-Driven Active Balancing Study of Ultra-Fast Charging Loads
authors: Wen Wang, Ye Yang, Fan Wu, Xiangliang Fang, Xiujuan Zeng, Tong Liu, Bin Zhu
abstract: Against the backdrop of rapid advancements in multi-source data fusion and intelligent control technologies, ensuring load balance and safety during ultra-fast charging of electric vehicles has become a critical challenge. This paper focuses on the issue of active load balancing in ultra-fast charging and proposes a safe, lithium-plating-free fast charging strategy driven by multi-source data. The proposed method constructs an equivalent circuit model of the lithium-ion battery electrodes and incorporates a dual closed-loop control structure consisting of an anode potential observer and a charging current controller. This enables accurate perception of battery states and dynamic regulation of charging current, effectively suppressing lithium plating and improving charging efficiency. Experimental results show that under various operating conditions, the average observation error of the anode potential is less than 5 mV, with a maximum error not exceeding 10 mV. During current regulation, the anode potential reaches and stabilizes within +1 mV of the set threshold in 26 s, with a steady-state error below 0.05 mV, and no overshoot or oscillation. Compared to the traditional 1C constant current–constant voltage (CC–CV) strategy, the proposed method reduces charging time by approximately 46%. Post-disassembly inspections of the battery revealed no signs of lithium plating, confirming the method's effectiveness and feasibility in enhancing ultra-fast charging performance, ensuring battery safety, and achieving active load balancing.

title: Accurate Characterization and Low-complexity MMSE Equalization of ISCI in Doubly-dispersive Channels
authors: Ziqin Yan, Fan Jiang, Zulin Wang, Zijun Gong, Cheng Li, Xiaofeng Tao
abstract: this paper, we investigate the Inter Symbol and Carrier Interference (ISCI) of doubly-dispersive channels in highly dynamic scenarios from the continuous and discrete perspectives, respectively, and thoroughly analyze its impact on the performance of communications systems. Due to its robustness against the Doppler effect in time-varying channels, Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation has gained significant research interest, with many equalization algorithms proposed. However, existing studies either fail to fully account for ISCI or suffer from prohibitive complexity. In this study, we accurately quantify the ISCI of doubly-dispersive channels and provide an in-depth analysis from continuous and discrete channel models, respectively. Based on this, we propose a low-complexity Minimum Mean Square Error (MMSE) equalization to equalize ISCI in doubly-dispersive channels. The proposed algorithm demonstrates a reduction in complexity of the MMSE equalization from O(M3N3) to O(MNL2 bw), where Lbw denotes the bandwidth of the time domain channel matrix. Concurrently, it has been demonstrated to significantly reduce the bit error rate (BER), thereby enhancing communication performance.

title: Review for "Descriptor: Digital Dataset of Animal Tissues for Macroscopic Pathology in Cancer: Image and Ultrasound Data (DATP-IU)"
authors: N/A

title: When to Harmonize? Evaluating Stage-Specific Harmonization in Federated Brain Age Estimation
authors: Tanurima Halder, Kunal Deo, Nicolás Nieto, Kaustubh R. Patil, Kshitij Jadhav
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is a promising solution for healthcare Artificial Intelligence (AI), striking a balance between patient privacy and the need for diverse datasets. FL enables collaborative model training across institutions, preserving confidentiality and advancing clinical tasks such as diagnosis and treatment planning. However, a key challenge in this setting is the inherent heterogeneity of medical datasets acquired in different institutions, which can undermine the generalizability and performance of the model. This issue is particularly pronounced in neuroimaging applications, such as Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), where site-specific biases arise from variations in scanner hardware, acquisition protocols, and preprocessing pipelines. These differences introduce non-biological variability that can jeopardize downstream analyses and model training. To remove the effect of sites, harmonization techniques are essential tools to improve robustness and reliability. Harmonization techniques are usually applied at the feature level; however, given the limited access to the data possessed by FL schemes, feature-level harmonization may not be enough to remove site effects. In this work, we propose two complementary harmonization strategies within the FL framework: (1) the traditional feature harmonization, by applying ComBat to directly correct the MRI-derived features; and (2) gradient harmonization, which aligns local model updates, particularly the gradients of fully connected layers, across sites to mitigate inter-site distributional shifts before global aggregation. Together, these approaches aim to improve cross-site consistency and improve the model's overall performance in federated medical imaging tasks.

title: Research on CAN Message Data Compression Based on LZW-HF Algorithm
authors: Chuanbo Liu, Jun Zhang, Xin Du, Liye Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the increasing intelligence of automobiles, more and more ECU nodes are mounted on the CAN bus. The application of a large number of ECUs enriches the functions of automobiles, but also generates a huge amount of data, which puts forward higher requirements for the state monitoring and fault diagnosis of automobiles. However, the existing data acquisition devices can no longer meet the multi-bus communication needs of automobiles. To address the problem of long data upload time caused by the large volume of message data, a lossless data compression algorithm is studied. By analyzing the data content and structure of the BLF format message file, it is found that there is a large inter-frame redundancy and pattern redundancy in the file data. Therefore, this paper designs the LZW-Hf combined algorithm to eliminate these redundant features. Considering the resource constraints of the embedded system, the size of the dictionary library of the prefix algorithm LZW in the combined algorithm is optimized. When the CPU load of the system reaches 80%, the size of the dictionary library will be adjusted from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 K}$</tex> to 1K to ensure the stability of the system.

title: A Method for Pricing and Cost Allocation of Capacity Charge for Energy Storage Systems in Electricity Market
authors: Yidi Zhang, Zhou Lan, Ankai Liu, Zikang Shen, Sicheng Xu, Li Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: The high penetration of renewables necessitates flexible resources like energy storage systems (ESS), but their deployment is hindered by inadequate cost recovery mechanisms. Existing pricing methods for ESS capacity charges are often inaccurate, failing to properly account for market-based revenues or the time value of money. To address this issue, a dynamic, rolling determination pricing model for the ESS capacity charge in arecovered investment costs over time. The core of this approach is its ability to quantify the actual revenue shortfall of the ESS by accounting for its market earnings, ensuring that the capacity payment only covers the necessary funding gap. The effectiveness of the proposed model is validated through a case study on a modified IEEE 39-bus system. It is demonstrated that the capacity charge progressively declines over the ESS lifetime as market revenues are incorporated, effectively tracking the cost recovery process. Thus, a more precise and practical framework for facilitating the marketbased cost recovery of ESS is provided.

title: Study on the Suppression of Residual Magnetization and Noise in Nuclear Magnetic Resonance Gyroscope Using a Permalloy-Ferrite Composite Magnetic Shielding System
authors: Qihao Sun, Zekun Wu, Wenxuan Tang, Zhanchao Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: The nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) gyroscopes, with their compact size and high precision, hold significant importance and diverse applications in both military and civilian sectors. Magnetic shielding structures are employed to mitigate external magnetic field interference and ensure stability within the gyroscope's internal magnetic environment. However, the Permalloy material widely used in current magnetic shielding generates magnetic noise within the gyroscope, limiting improvements in gyroscope accuracy. This paper proposes a novel structure based on a Permalloy-ferrite composite magnetic shielding system. By introducing high-resistivity manganese-zinc ferrite into the inner layer of the traditional double-layer Permalloy shield, eddy current noise caused by the high electrical conductivity of Permalloy is effectively suppressed. Simulations and experiments demonstrate that this composite system reduces residual magnetism at the vapor cell center point by 84%, improves residual magnetism uniformity by 16.5%, and significantly lowers the internal noise floor of the gyroscope. Consequently, it markedly enhances the magnetic field stability and measurement accuracy of nuclear magnetic resonance gyroscopes. This work provides novel material combinations and structural optimization approaches for magnetic shielding designs in miniaturized atomic sensors.

title: Airport Lighting Recognition Based on an Improved YOLOv8 Algorithm
authors: Yujie Fu, Yong Yang, Li Sun, Hongda Luo, Kaijun Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: In daily civil aviation operations and flight training, complex environments such as nighttime or low visibility can easily cause airport lighting to trigger visual recognition difficulties, creating potential safety hazards. Addressing the limitations of existing YOLOv8-based object detection in identifying small targets against complex backgrounds, as well as the lack of publicly available airport lighting datasets, this paper proposes an improved airport lighting recognition method. We constructed a specialized dataset containing three common types of airport lights. Using YOLOv8s as the baseline, we introduced an EMA attention mechanism into the backbone network to enhance fine-grained feature representation, thereby mitigating detection accuracy issues for small objects and reducing background interference. Experiments demonstrate that the improved model, YOLOv8s-EMA, achieves 74.5% accuracy—a 1.8 percentage point increase over the baseline—while maintaining comparable parameter and computational complexity. This enables more real-time and robust detection of airport lights, providing robust support for civil aviation operational safety.

title: Research on Maintenance-Free Technology System for UHVDC Transmission Lines in High-Altitude Uninhabited Areas
authors: Zhihui Chen
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: This paper aims to construct a maintenance-free technology system for UHVDC (Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current) transmission lines in high-altitude uninhabited areas, breaking through the three bottlenecks of low operation and maintenance (O&M) efficiency, high response delay, and strong ecological constraints in harsh environments. By integrating a multi-source heterogeneous sensor network (with fiber optic vibration accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.1 \text{mm}$</tex>, infrared thermography temperature measurement error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 1{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, and LiDAR point cloud density of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 \text{pt} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>), an LSTM-ResNet fusion algorithm is developed to achieve edge intelligent diagnosis. Relying on the DTformer model driven by digital twin, a closed-loop architecture of “perception-decision-execution” is built. The application results show that: the state prediction accuracy is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant 92.7 {\%}$</tex>, the diagnosis response time is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 200 \text{ms}$</tex>, the investment cost per unit length is reduced to 1.082 million yuan per tower (a decrease of 42.8%), the surface disturbance area is reduced by 83.1%, and the carbon footprint is reduced by 64.3%. This system realizes the full-life-cycle autonomous operation of UHVDC transmission lines, provides an efficient, reliable, and green technical paradigm for the construction of external transmission channels of clean energy bases, and promotes the strategic transformation of power grid O&M towards minimal human involvement and intelligence.

title: MFCC-Based Multilingual Speech Processing for Depression Classification
authors: Monica Pratiwi, Nur Hayati, Alvin Yohanes Kristianto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: A relatively underexplored research direction in depression detection involves the utilization of multilingual speech data for model development. Advancing multilingual audio-based depression detection systems is increasingly essential, as such approaches can enhance model robustness, improve cross-linguistic generalizability, and support more equitable mental-health assessment technologies on a global scale. This study conducts a comparative analysis to evaluate the effectiveness of Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) with varying dimensionalities specifically 13, 20, 30, and 40 coefficients in distinguishing depressive and non-depressive speech using two multilingual audio datasets: MODMA and DAIC-WOZ. The extracted MFCC features were used to train three deep learning architectures: LSTM, BiLSTM, and GRU. The experimental results show that MFCC-40 consistently achieves the highest performance across models, obtaining an average accuracy of 82.23%, outperforming the lowercoefficient MFCC configurations. Furthermore, among the models trained with MFCC-40, the Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) demonstrates superior classification capability, achieving an average accuracy of 86%. These findings highlight that the combination of MFCC-40 and the BiLSTM architecture provides the most effective framework for multilingual depression detection based on speech.

title: UMT-Net: A Wavelet-enhanced Mamba Network for Abdominal Organ Segmentation
authors: Jinxia Wang, Li Wang, Naixin He, Weidong Zhang, Chaoqing Ma, Lin Li, Xiangyan Feng
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Accurate abdominal organ segmentation from computed tomography(CT) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is essential for automated diagnosis and surgical planning. We propose U-Net with Hybrid Mamba and Wavelet Transformer(UMT-Net), a lightweight framework that integrates bidirectional Mamba modules for efficient global context modeling and stationary wavelet transform (SWT) for multi-scale feature enhancement. Depthwise separable convolutions and high-frequency skip connections are further employed to refine structural boundaries while maintaining computational efficiency. Experiments on abdominal CT and MRI datasets show that UMT-Net achieves Dice coefficients of 0.89 and 0.87, respectively, representing improvements of 2.7% and 4.2% compared to the nnUNet model. Simultaneously, the HD95 value is reduced by over 25%. UMT-Net not only improves segmentation accuracy and boundary accuracy but also significantly enhances efficiency. These results highlight the enormous potential of this architecture in the fields of smart healthcare and robot assistance.

title: Logical Analysis of Fault Restart in the Lu-Gao-Zhao Hvdc Line
authors: Weiyu Lu, Dongxu Deng, Liyun Zha, Jigao Cheng, Song Xu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: The Lu-Gao-zhao HVDC project is the first ± <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 0 k V}$</tex> HVDC transmission system in China that has been converted from a conventional two-terminal configuration into a three-terminal one. For transient DC line faults that are selfclearing, the line fault restart function serves as a critical control and protection strategy to ensure operational reliability. This paper mainly analyzes the fault restart strategy and control logic employed in the Lu-Gao-Zhao three-terminal HVDC transmission system.

title: Structural Optimization and Application of Main Circulating Pump Mechanical Seal for Valve Cooling System
authors: Sai Ding, Chengxi Huang, Yukun Xiao, Chang Ruan, Kai Xiao, Wanli Shi, Peng Zhang, Hangtian Dong
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Aiming at the mechanical seal of the main circulating pump in the high voltage direct current (HVDC) valve cooling system, research on thermal-mechanical coupling calculation, structural optimization design, and engineering application for the mechanical seal friction pair was conducted by employing orthogonal optimization and range analysis methods. The axial deformation and temperature distribution patterns of the friction pair under various factor-level combinations of the mechanical seal structure were analyzed. The results indicate that the primary influencing factors for the maximum axial deformation and the peak temperature of the mechanical seal friction pair in the HVDC valve cooling system are the radial distance from the inner diameter of the end face (V1) and the axial distance from the dynamic ring rubber ring position to the end face (H1), respectively. The optimal factorlevel combination for the mechanical seal friction pair was determined to be “223” (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H} 1=5 ~\text{mm}, \mathrm{H} 2=5 ~\text{mm}, ~\mathrm{V} 1=5.5 ~\text{mm}$</tex>), resulting in a maximum axial deformation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.89 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> for the dynamic ring end face and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.51 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> for the static ring end face. The developed mechanical seal product for the HVDC valve cooling system's main circulation pump has been successfully applied to the power grid of China Southern Power Grid Co., Ltd. for approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 0 0}$</tex> hours, demonstrating stable operation and reliable performance.

title: OPeMer: One-Shot LLM Prompting and Execution-Driven Multimodal Explainable Reasoning
authors: Zuyi Zhou, Dizhan Xue, Shengsheng Qian
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: The Visual Question Answering (VQA) task requires not only accurate answers but also interpretable reasoning processes, particularly in real-world applications where transparency is critical. To reduce annotation and computational costs while maintaining interpretability, the Few-shot Multimodal Explainable VQA (FS-MEVQA) task has been introduced, which aims to generate explanations with limited supervision. In this work, we propose OPeMer (One-shot Prompting and Execution-driven Multimodal Explainable Reasoning), a code-based framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to generate executable Python programs for multimodal reasoning in a oneshot setting. These programs interact with a lightweight Python API to process visual inputs, capture intermediate reasoning artifacts—such as object crops and spatial relations—and optionally call external tools for open-world visual understanding. The resulting execution traces are serialized and provided to the LLM via a secondary prompt, enabling the generation of coherent multimodal explanations grounded in both visual and textual evidence. Designed without reliance on handcrafted rules or large-scale supervision, OPeMer offers an efficient and extensible approach to explainable multimodal reasoning. Experimental results on the SME dataset demonstrate that OPeMer achieves strong answer accuracy and explanation quality, even when using cost-effective LLMs under limited supervision, suggesting its potential for scalable and interpretable VQA.

title: Rolling ARIMA vs Transformer for Megathrust Seismic Magnitude Forecasting: A Two-Region Benchmark on Aceh (ID) and Tohoku (JP)
authors: Herianto, Eka Yuni Astuty, Gibran Qolil Tabrani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Seismic magnitude forecasting in megathrust zones poses a critical challenge for disaster mitigation, necessitating a rigorous balance between prediction accuracy and computational latency. This study presents a comparative evaluation between a Transformer-based Deep Learning model (Informer) and an adaptive statistical method (Rolling ARIMA) for earthquake magnitude prediction. Case studies were conducted in two seismically active regions: Aceh (Sunda Megathrust) and Tohoku (Japan Trench), using USGS catalog data spanning 1990-2024. Experiments were performed across multiple temporal scales (daily, weekly, monthly) and prediction horizons. Results indicate that while the Informer excels in minimizing relative error (SMAPE) on large-volume datasets, Rolling ARIMA demonstrates significant superiority in shortterm accuracy ($\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ up to 0.92 in Aceh) and stability under limited data scenarios. Crucially, the study quantifies a massive divergence in computational efficiency: Rolling ARIMA achieves retraining in under 150 seconds with sub-second deterministic inference, whereas the Informer requires $\gt1000$ seconds for training with unstable inference latency. These findings suggest that for Edge Computing-based Early Warning Systems (EWS), adaptive statistical approaches offer a more pragmatic and reliable solution than complex deep learning architectures.

title: Fire Trapped Personnel Evacuation Optimization Using Coupled PDE-ABM Framework
authors: Rui Cai, Sen Cheng, Jing Li, Chensi Wang, Cen Chang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel coupled PDE-ABM (Partial Differential Equation - Agent-Based Model) framework for optimizing the collaborative evacuation of firefighters, robots, and trapped personnel in fire environments. Traditional evacuation models often assume homogeneous human crowds and neglect critical Human-Robot Interaction (HRI) constraints. Our study overcomes these limitations by establishing a two-way feedback mechanism between a dynamic potential field model and a microscopic simulation of heterogeneous agents. The macroscopic potential field is governed by a reaction-diffusion PDE incorporating agent-based source terms, enabling dynamic adaptation to environmental hazards and robotic guidance strategies. In the microscopic model, human agents are driven by the combined forces of the potential field gradient, social forces, and trust-based HRI forces. We embed this integrated model within a Multi-Objective Bayesian Optimization (MOBO) loop to cooptimize the geometric layout of evacuation pathways and robotic behavioral strategies, aiming to minimize total mission time and hazard exposure while satisfying trust and communication constraints. Simulation results reveal intriguing findings, such as the "Robot-to-Human Ratio Paradox"—where excessive robots degrade performance due to cognitive overload and communication interference—and the emergence of effective "robotic shepherding" coordination. Our results demonstrate that compared to geometry-only approaches, our HRI-constrained model improves mission success rate by 22.3% and significantly enhances exit utilization balance. This research underscores the necessity of deeply integrating human factors and psychology into the fundamental layers of evacuation modeling, providing new theoretical insights and tools for the design of future intelligent emergency response systems.

title: Multi-Label Emotion Classification of Reddit Comments Using a BERT-Based Model
authors: Fransiska Fu, Victoria Simanjaya, Said Achmad, Derwin Suhartono
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: Emotion detection in social media is a key challenge in natural language processing with broad implications for well-being and online safety. This study compares four Transformer-based models—BERT, DistilBERT, ELECTRA, and RoBERTa—for classifying Reddit comments into five emotions: love, anger, sadness, gratitude, and confusion. To address severe class imbalance, a curated subset of the GoEmotions dataset was created using undersampling, ensuring fair evaluation across categories. All models were fine-tuned under the same pipeline, with performance assessed by Precision, Recall, and Weighted F1-Score. Results show competitive performance across architectures, with BERT-base achieving the highest Weighted F1-Score of 0.9260. The model excelled in detecting gratitude (F1: 0.9758) and love (F1: 0.9414), while showing precision-recall trade-offs in anger and sadness. The study contributes a reproducible benchmark, demonstrating that combining balanced data with systematic model comparison yields high accuracy. These findings provide practical guidance for developing emotion-aware systems in areas such as mental health monitoring and online behavior analysis.

title: Research on Fine-Grained Tumor Region Segmentation in Medical Images Based on an Improved U-Net Architecture
authors: Shuaishuai Li, Xuechun Zong, Minghui Ban, Jinhui Yang
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: This study targets fine-grained segmentation of brain tumor regions in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). An improved U-Net architecture is proposed to address low contrast, irregular morphology, and blurred tumor boundaries. The encoder integrates residual multi-scale convolution and channel attention mechanisms to enhance discriminative feature extraction, while the decoder adopts weighted skip-connection fusion with a boundary-constraint loss to refine edge localization. Experiments on public brain MRI datasets show that the proposed method achieves a Dice score of 91.08% and an Intersection over Union (IoU) of 83.74%, outperforming U-Net, U-Net++, and Attention U-Net, with HD95 reduced to 5.98 mm. These results demonstrate the method’s effectiveness for precision medical image analysis, contributing to intelligent imaging and computer-assisted diagnosis technologies relevant to the conference theme.

title: Sentiment Analysis of Public Perception Towards Indonesia’s Free Meal Program (MBG) from X Data Using VADER and IndoBERT
authors: Derren Malaka, Kelsten Wuisan, Rani Puspita
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Makan Bergizi Gratis is a program that was initiated by the Indonesian government in response to child malnutrition or stunting. Social media is one of the ways people share their expressions and perceptions. In this paper, we analyze the sentiment of Indonesian citizens about the new free meal program that was posted in text messages on X platform. The sentiments were classified with VADER, a model that uses lexicon based techniques to label reviews with sentiment using a predefined lexicon. The sentiment is also classified with IndoBERT, which uses a transformer based deep learning model that has been developed specifically for the Indonesian language, which determines contextual understanding from Indonesian text. The model evaluation of VADER achieved 74.24% accuracy, and IndoBERT achieved 86.31% accuracy on MBG tweets. Both models were used to classify the polarity of the tweets. Temporal, geographical, and thematic analysis were also used to reinforce and validate the findings that were obtained through the sentiment analysis process. In conclusion, these research hope to assist the government department in evaluating public concerns, which helps in making future decisions.

title: Implementation of an End-to-End Indonesian Medical Text Simplification System Using IndoT5
authors: Shannon Andrea, Ernawati, Bagus Mulyawan, Janson Hendryli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: The complexity of medical texts in Indonesian creates significant barriers to public health literacy, with existing solutions limited by resource constraints. This paper presents a comprehensive text simplification system using the IndoT5 transformer model. Our contributions include the creation of an expert-validated parallel corpus containing $\mathbf{1, 5 0 0}$ complexsimple sentence pairs focused on 50 prevalent diseases from national health insurance data, subsequently expanded to 5,591 samples through data augmentation. The fine-tuned model demonstrates strong performance with BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) score of 42.76, SARI (System against Reference for Simplification) score of 58.62, and ROUGE-L (Recall-Oriented Understudy for Gisting Evaluation) score of 0.676. Furthermore, we have deployed a fully functional web application using Flask framework that provides real-time medical text simplification for healthcare providers and the general public. This work establishes both a benchmark dataset and a practical tool for Indonesian medical natural language processing, effectively bridging the health information gap through accessible technology.

title: Risk-Aware Short-Term Forecasting of Airport Departure Flow with a Token-Level Graph Convolutional Network for Terminal Areas and the Broader Network Context
authors: Xiangzhi Zhou, Haoru Guo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: This work addresses short-term forecasting of airport departure flow in terminal areas while accounting for the broader network context through recent congestion signals and calibrated uncertainty. We cast the task as multi-step distributional forecasting and present TokenGCN, a graph convolutional network that outputs the P10, P50, and P90 trajectories for the next <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 0}$</tex> minutes in one pass. A 48-hour history per airport is sliced into overlapping tokens; a k nearest neighbor graph in token space captures local shape similarity, and graph convolutions propagate information among similar short-horizon patterns while preserving longer-range structure. Inputs fuse scheduled departures known in advance, near-term taxi-out statistics that summarize surface congestion, and calendar encodings. Training with the quantile loss produces calibrated predictive intervals without intermediate decomposition. On 2019 operations data, TokenGCN achieves stable errors and informative uncertainty widening during perturbed periods, which is useful for risk-aware tactical decisions. Compared with a transformer reference, TokenGCN attains a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency, and its compact design is easy to extend with exogenous factors such as weather and runway configuration.

title: Enhanced Underwater Target Recognition via Multidimensional Structured Acoustic Feature Representation
authors: Yi Yin, Tieshu Li, Shengyan Liu, Lei Yue, Yu Liu, Qiming Mao, Pengrui Dai
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: Underwater acoustic target recognition is an essential yet challenging task in ocean engineering, primarily due to the complexities of underwater acoustic propagation and the absence of stable and structured patterns in signals acquired through passive listening. This paper presents a novel approach for underwater acoustic target recognition that leverages multidimensional structured acoustic feature representation. The proposed method combines graphical representations with the construction of multi-dimensional acoustic features and employs deep learning techniques to model underwater acoustic targets through multi-domain feature fusion. Experimental results demonstrate that on the DeepShip-main dataset, this method achieved a peak target identification accuracy of 92.00%. This success was purely a function of initial experiments carried out as proof-of-concept validation of the potential of this technique for engineering application, indicating a high likelihood that it will serve a pivotal role with regard to continued advances in the space of underwater acoustic recognition. The proposed system has attained near end-to-end underwater acoustic target recognition.

title: Research and Implementation of Air Interface Conformance Testing Methods for Private Wireless Networks Based on EPDT Protocol
authors: Jing Shen, Dongjiao Xu, Linmao Wan, Gongming Li, Yongjie Li, Gang Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: To guarantee interoperability in China's self-reliant EPDT (Electrical-power professional data transmission) wireless private networks, this paper presents a conformance testing method for the air interface protocol. By capturing and analyzing over-the-air signals, the approach extracts and decodes physical layer bursts and data link layer PDUs, subsequently comparing them with standard signaling flows. Validation results confirm its effectiveness in verifying critical procedures like terminal registration and unicast data transmission, pinpointing discrepancies from the protocol standard. This work offers a valuable tool and reference for the development and deployment of EPDT network equipment.

title: Research on Reactive Power Support Effect of CLCC Under the Condition of UHV AC/DC Tight Coupling
authors: Ling Xu, Xiaohu Zhang, Mengyao Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: Considering the objective situation of the reverse distribution of energy resources and power load centers in China, East China, as the most economically dynamic and power load-concentrated region in the country, has successfully built the world's first UHV AC ring network. This paper centers on the UHV transmission line ring network in East China. A simulation model is established using PSD-BPA to analyze and compare the coupling characteristics of the UHV ring network during voltage fluctuations under different grid structures. The research shows that 1000 kV UHV transmission lines exhibit strong coupling, which aggravates the propagation of reactive power and voltage fluctuations. Through the coordinated control of the main branch and auxiliary branch, CLCC technology actively avoids commutation failure in terms of mechanism, provides reactive power support, and maintains the voltage of the UHV ring network.

title: Enumerative Sphere Shaping-PAS Architecture for Reliable Transmission in 64QAM-OFDM 400 GHz Photonic THz Communication System
authors: Tong Li, Jianguo Yu, Shenao Cai, Shuhui Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: We demonstrate a 400 GHz photonic Terahertz (THz) 64QAM-OFDM system utilizing Enumerative Sphere Shaping (ESS) based Probabilistic Amplitude Shaping (PAS). ESS mitigates the finite-blocklength rate loss inherent to Constant Composition Distribution Matching (CCDM) by leveraging the full multidimensional sphere. Experiments show that at an Achievable Information Rate (AIR) of 5.1 bit/2-D, ESS-PAS achieves Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) gains of 0.81 dB and 0.31 dB over uniform signaling and CCDM, respectively. At a Frame Error Rate (FER) of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup>, ESS improves power efficiency by 0.98 dB over the uniform baseline.

title: Electromagnetic Phenomena in the Atmosphere and their Impact on the Ecosystem
authors: Andrzej Krawczyk, Ewa Korzeniewska, Krzysztof Politowski, Małgorzata Serwach, Józef Mróz, Alyona Nikitina
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: Since the beginning of life on Earth, we have been dealing with the impact of our planet's electromagnetic, magnetic and electric fields, as well as the activity and influence of the Sun. These interactions have a significant impact on maintaining the balance of our ecosystem. Both the Earth's magnetic field, solar storms, and lightning are occurring electromagnetic phenomena that affect the atmosphere. The Earth's magnetic field protects it from harmful solar radiation and charged particles. It is considered a protective barrier that stops high-energy cosmic radiation particles from reaching the planet's surface and also affects weather patterns and climate. The differences in the Sun’s activity are also the source of solar storms which have an impact on the Earth's atmosphere by explosions and sending charged particles towards our planet. Such particles interact with the atmosphere which can be observed as the wonderful and gorgeous auroras, especially in the Scandinavian countries. Such phenomena can influence the work of transmission lines and power systems. Research such electromagnetic phenomena is very important for understanding the possible consequences of affecting the natural form of electromagnetic fields in our ecosystem. Such knowledge is important from the point of view of our health and functioning on our planet.

title: Computational Fact-Checking of Online Discourse: Scoring Scientific Accuracy in Climate Change Related News Articles
authors: Tim Wittenborg, Constantin Sebastian Tremel, Oliver Karras, Sören Auer
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Democratic societies need reliable information. Misinformation in popular media, such as news articles or videos, threatens to impair civic discourse. Citizens are, unfortunately, not equipped to verify the flood of content consumed daily at increasing rates. This work aims to quantify the scientific accuracy of online media semi-automatically. We investigate the state of the art of climate-related ground truth knowledge representation. By semantifying media content of unknown veracity, their statements can be compared against these ground truth knowledge graphs. We implemented a workflow using LLM-based statement extraction and knowledge graph analysis. Our implementation can streamline content processing towards state-of-the-art knowledge representation and veracity quantification. Developed and evaluated with the help of 27 experts and detailed interviews with 10, the tool evidently provides a beneficial veracity indication. These findings are supported by 43 anonymous participants from a parallel user survey. This initial step, however, is unable to annotate public media at the required granularity and scale. Additionally, the identified state of climate change knowledge graphs is vastly insufficient to support this neurosymbolic fact-checking approach. Further work towards a FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) ground truth and complementary metrics is required to support civic discourse scientifically.

title: Small Target Detector for Power Scenarios via Self-Aligned and Adaptive Feature Sampling Pyramid
authors: Zhenyu Chen Big Data Center, Huaguang Yan, Jianguang Du, Yi Chen, Qi Li, Shuai Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)

title: Application of a Lightweight CNN Combining Batch Normalization and Dropout in Garbage Classification
authors: Ziyi Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the gradual implementation of waste classification policies, achieving efficient and accurate automatic recognition of waste images has become a research focus. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have demonstrated excellent performance in image classification tasks and are therefore widely applied to waste classification. Based on the TrashNet dataset, this study constructs a lightweight CNN baseline model and further integrates Batch Normalization and Dropout techniques to enhance the model's convergence speed and generalization capability. Experimental results show that the improved CNN model achieves an approximately 5 % increase in classification accuracy on the test set compared with the baseline model, reaching a maximum accuracy of 87.2 %. The study demonstrates that even under limited data conditions, structural improvements to a basic network can yield significant performance gains, offering a feasible approach for the automation of waste classification.

title: Electricity Consumption Forecasting for Agency Electricity Purchasing Based on LSTM Networks
authors: Wenjie Sun, Fangmei Bie, Jun Yang, Fuzhang Wu, Yafang Tong, Ting Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This paper proposes a method for forecasting agency electricity consumption based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural networks. With the deepening of the power market reform, grid companies are required to purchase electricity on behalf of a large number of industrial and commercial users. The amount of electricity purchased is mainly forecasted based on the users’ electricity consumption and typical load curves. Therefore, accurate and scientific electricity consumption forecasting is crucial for ensuring user electricity safety, stabilizing grid power supply, and reducing the cost of electricity purchase deviation. Although significant progress has been made in the field of electricity consumption forecasting, there are still deficiencies in data governance and model optimization. This paper first mines more relevant features from the historical load sequence of agency electricity purchasing users, measures the impact of these features on the load using the Pearson correlation coefficient, and performs prediction based on hyperparameter optimization and feature selection. Finally, the daily electricity consumption is calculated according to Simpson’s rule. Experimental results show that the proposed method can effectively improve prediction accuracy even in the absence of data for external influencing factors, providing a more scientific basis for decision-making in agency electricity purchasing by grid companies.

title: Computer Vision-Based Indonesian Sign Language Recognition System Using MediaPipe and SVM on Raspberry Pi
authors: Ivan Alexander, Endra, Muhammad Mirza, Daniel Geraldo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This research presents the development of a real-time Indonesian Sign Language (BISINDO) recognition system that utilizes a camera and Raspberry Pi 5 to facilitate communication for individuals with hearing impairments. The system focuses on recognizing static hand gestures corresponding to the 26 alphabet letters ($\mathrm{A}-\mathrm{Z}$) in BISINDO, providing a low-cost, embedded solution for sign language translation. The core components of the system include OpenCV for image preprocessing, MediaPipe for extracting 3D hand landmarks, and a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier for gesture recognition. A publicly available dataset consisting of 312 labeled images (12 images per alphabet) was used for model training, covering all BISINDO hand signs for letters A to Z. To evaluate system robustness, testing was conducted under three lighting and background conditions: natural, plain white, and low light. The trained model achieved high recognition accuracy across all environments, with the highest accuracy recorded under well-lit, high-contrast conditions. The results confirm the effectiveness of combining lightweight embedded hardware with computer vision and machine learning techniques in recognizing hand gestures with minimal latency and low computational cost. This study contributes a scalable framework that can be expanded to dynamic gesture recognition or real-time sentence-level interpretation in future work, enhancing communication accessibility for the deaf community in Indonesia.

title: IEGWO: An Improved Evolutionary Grey Wolf Optimizer for Quadcopter Single-Shot Long Trajectory Optimization
authors: Lin Zhao, Jun Cai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Trajectory optimization is particularly important for quadcopter flight in obstacle-intensive environments. This paper proposes an improved evolutionary grey wolf optimizer (IEGWO) algorithm to solve quadcopter long trajectory optimization problems. Using a limited-memory evolutionary strategy to enhance the convergence of the grey wolf optimizer (GWO). Additionally, a covariance matrix is employed to improve the search direction for the solution. Within the elliptical sampling area, candidate solutions gradually converge toward the optimal solution. This paper presents experiments using benchmark functions and a quadcopter trajectory simulation. The effectiveness of IEGWO is evaluated against LBFGS and GWO. For single-shot long trajectory optimization in quadcopters, the experimental results demonstrate improved convergence accuracy and reduced computational time.

title: Hardware Computing Capabilities for Industrial Informatics Systems
authors: Inna Zhulkovska, Oleg Zhulkovskyi, Oleksandr Sadovoi, Yuliia Sokhina, Oksana Lebid, Hlib Vokhmianin
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: Floating point computing is an important tool in industrial informatics for process control and monitoring, machine vision and image processing, data analysis, process simulation and modelling and decision-making based on precision computing. They enable you to solve complex problems and improve the efficiency and reliability of systems in industry. Increasing the accuracy of arithmetic operations by increasing the digit capacity of numbers is the primary means of performing rounding-critical calculations. As the result of the research the general formulas for calculation of the values of minimum and maximum normalized and subnormalized numbers and also for calculation of the maximum absolute rounding errors of the basic format numbers are received. The conducted research shows the abilities of modern general-purpose processors and compilers of high-level languages to support the formats of increased accuracy for increasing the efficiency of mathematical modeling in general.

title: Research on D2D Communication Power Control Algorithm Based on Multi-Strategy Fusion
authors: Xingyu Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: Device-to-device (D2D) communication sharing spectrum with cellular users introduces complex intra- and cross-layer interference. Efficient power control is the core challenge determining its performance. To address this issue, this paper proposes a power control optimization algorithm based on the Multiple-Strategies Fusion (MSF), which integrates three power allocation strategies. They are channel sensing, interference coordination and throughput maximization. An intelligent fusion mechanism dynamically selects the optimal power scheme. This scheme employs weighted scoring to intelligently match D2D users with cellular users, thereby maximizing the overall system efficiency while ensuring the communication quality of cellular users. As shown in the simulation results, the game theory scheme and the basic optimization scheme (BOS), the MSF algorithm has respectively increased the D2D user throughput by 45.2%, 25.8% and 18.5%. The performance loss of cellular users is constrained within a reasonable range by using an adaptive constraint mechanism. This research provides a new approach to solving the interference management problem in D2D communications.

title: Insole-Derived Plantar Pressure Variability Reveals Dual-Task Gait Differences in Early-Stage Parkinson&#x2019;s Disease_supp1-3667847.docx
authors: Na Young Kim

title: Causal Prompting for Implicit Sentiment Analysis With Large Language Models
authors: Jing Ren, Wenhao Zhou, Bowen Li, Mujie Liu, Nguyen Linh Dan Le, Jiade Cen, Liping Chen, Ziqi Xu, Xiwei Xu, Xiaodong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Implicit sentiment analysis (ISA) aims to infer sentiment that is implied rather than explicitly stated, requiring models to perform deeper reasoning over subtle contextual cues. While recent prompting-based methods using large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in ISA, they often rely on majority voting over chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning paths without evaluating their causal validity, making them susceptible to internal biases and spurious correlations. To address this challenge, we propose CAPITAL, a causal prompting framework that incorporates front-door adjustment into CoT reasoning. CAPITAL decomposes the overall causal effect into two components: the influence of the input prompt on the reasoning chains, and the impact of those chains on the final output. These components are estimated using encoder-based clustering and the NWGM approximation, with a contrastive learning objective used to better align the encoder’s representation with the LLM’s reasoning space. Experiments on benchmark ISA datasets with three LLMs demonstrate that CAPITAL consistently outperforms strong prompting baselines in both accuracy and robustness, particularly under adversarial conditions. This work offers a principled approach to integrating causal inference into LLM prompting and highlights its benefits for bias-aware sentiment reasoning. The source code and case study are available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/whZ62/CAPITAL</uri>.

title: Using a Hybrid SMF/MMF Cable for Deploying Optically-Powered B5G Multitechnology Optical/Wireless Systems
authors: F. B. F. Pinto, S. B. Vasconcellos, E. S. Lima, E. L. Anderson, Arismar Cerqueira S.
abstract: This paper reports an innovative hybrid optical cable-based Radio-and Power over Fiber (RPoF) system to simultaneously support diverse access networks, i.e., 5G New Radio (NR) in millimeter wave (mmWave), visible light communication (VLC), gigabit passive optical networks (GPON) and WiFi 6. A 200 m hybrid optical cable was idealized and manufactured with 06 conventional  multimode fibers (MMFs) and 02 single-mode fibers (SMFs) aiming to simplify the deployment of optically-powered beyond 5G (B5G) multitechnology optical/wireless systems. SMFs are deployed to transport data signals, whereas MMFs are used in the PoF system to power the B5G radio remote unit (RRU) composed of multiple access networks. In total, 40 W optical power is transmitted over 5 MMFs to deliver approximately 14.03 W electrical power, which is used to simultaneously power 05 devices (02 RF amplifiers, a photodetector, a GPON modem and a laser from the VLC system) with margin of more than 7 W. To evaluate the RoF-based B5G network, the root mean square error vector magnitude (EVM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</sub>) is utilized. The proposed optically-powered multitechnology system is capable of providing up to 1.45 Gbps total throughput, using a 64 QAM modulation scheme within the 3GPP EVM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</sub> threshold. Furthermore, a stability analysis of the PoF-delivered electrical power and GPON modem throughput demonstrates strong potential and high reliability for applying the PoF technology to power diverse components of optical/wireless B5G systems.

title: A multi-source information fusion anomaly detection framework based on denoising diffusion probabilistic model for rotating machinery
authors: Jing-Yang Zheng, Zhi-Pan Ren, You-Ming Ge, Yong-Jian Yu, Jing Zhu, Li-You Xu, Qing Ni, Ke Feng
abstract: Rotating machinery fault detection is critically important for industrial systems. However, current methods relying on single-modal signals often yield incomplete feature representation and limited robustness. Denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPMs) offer promise for anomaly detection due to their strong generative capabilities and ability to train solely on healthy data. To overcome these limitations, this paper introduces MFE-DDPM, a novel rotating machinery anomaly detection method integrating multimodal feature fusion with an attention-enhanced DDPM. Our approach significantly enhances detection accuracy and robustness. First, vibration and acoustic signals are converted into time-frequency spectrograms via time-frequency analysis and a hyperparameter-free encoding method, while temperature data are transformed into grayscale images. These modalities are fused at the data level by mapping them to the R, G, and B channels of an RGB image, respectively. Subsequently, the attention-enhanced DDPM detects anomalies based on the reconstruction error of samples generated from the fused input. Validation on the Ottawa Multimodal Bearing Dataset shows that MFE-DDPM achieves a highest anomaly detection accuracy of 99.50%, with an average accuracy of 98.65% over ten repeated trials, outperforming both conventional single- and dual-modal methods and multimodal approaches without attention mechanisms.

title: AI in Satellite Remote Sensing of the Ocean
authors: Xiaofeng Li, Qing Xu, Haoyu Wang, Haoyu Jiang, Xiaobin Yin, Shanshan Mu, Xiaolong Li, Hua Su, An Wang, Yi Yang, Yanjun Wang, Yibin Ren, Xudong Zhang, Yingjie Liu, Chong Wang
abstract: Satellite remote sensing plays a fundamental role in observing oceanic processes by providing large-scale, long-term, and continuous measurements. With the increasing availability of multisource satellite data, challenges such as data gaps, complex environmental conditions, and the limitations of conventional retrieval methods have become more evident. In recent years, artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a practical and effective approach to address these issues. This article reviews the development of AI techniques in satellite ocean remote sensing, focusing on three main application areas: parameter retrieval, data reconstruction, and image-based ocean phenomenon detection. For geophysical variable retrieval, AI models such as convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Transformer architectures have improved the accuracy of ocean waves, sea surface, salinity, wind, and ocean color estimates, especially under extreme or noisy conditions. In the field of data reconstruction, AI methods enable the completion of missing data in both surface and subsurface ocean layers, offering finer spatial–temporal resolution and better consistency than traditional interpolation approaches. For image interpretation, deep learning (DL) models have been applied to detect and segment dynamic ocean features such as mesoscale eddies, internal waves, sea ice, and tropical cyclones (TCs), achieving high efficiency and precision. This article also highlights the integration of AI with physical knowledge, the use of multisource fusion, and the trend toward near real-time (NRT) applications. These developments indicate that AI will play an increasingly important role in future satellite-based ocean observation and environmental monitoring.

title: Joint AoI and Handover Optimization in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Network
authors: Zifan Lang, Guixia Liu, Geng Sun, Jiahui Li, Jiacheng Wang, Weijie Yuan, Dusit Niyato, Dong In Kim
abstract: Despite the widespread deployment of terrestrial networks, providing reliable communication services to remote areas and maintaining connectivity during emergencies remains challenging. Low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellations offer promising solutions with their global coverage capabilities and reduced latency, yet struggle with intermittent coverage and limited communication windows due to orbital dynamics. This paper introduces an age of information (AoI)-aware space-air-ground integrated network (SAGIN) architecture that leverages a high-altitude platform (HAP) as intelligent relay between the LEO satellites and ground terminals. Our three-layer design employs hybrid free-space optical (FSO) links for high-capacity satellite-to-HAP communication and reliable radio frequency (RF) links for HAP-to-ground transmission, and thus addressing the temporal discontinuity in LEO satellite coverage while serving diverse user priorities. Specifically, we formulate a joint optimization problem to simultaneously minimize the AoI and satellite handover frequency through optimal transmit power distribution and satellite selection decisions. This highly dynamic, non-convex problem with time-coupled constraints presents significant computational challenges for traditional approaches. To address these difficulties, we propose a novel diffusion model (DM)-enhanced dueling double deep Q-network with <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</u>ction decomposition and <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</u>tate transformer encoder (DD3QN-AS) algorithm that incorporates transformer-based temporal feature extraction and employs a DM-based latent prompt generative module to refine state-action representations through conditional denoising. Simulation results highlight the superior performance of the proposed approach compared with policy-based methods and some other deep reinforcement learning (DRL) benchmarks. Moreover, performance analysis under various system settings verifies the robustness of the proposed approach.

title: Fuzzy State Estimation of Space 2-D Nonlinear Parabolic PDE Systems Utilizing a Mobile Sensor
authors: Xiao-Qiong Li, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Zi-Peng Wang, Jin-Liang Wang, Zhou-Yang Liu
publish: IEEE Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Magazine
abstract: This paper presents a design method for the fuzzy state observer of spatial two-dimensional (2-D) nonlinear parabolic partial differential equation (PDE) systems under the utilization of a mobile sensor. Initially, a Takagi-Sugeno (T-S) fuzzy model is established, as it is capable of representing nonlinear systems. Subsequently, by employing the Lyapunov direct method, a fuzzy state observer and a guidance law of the mobile sensor are proposed to ensure the asymptotic stability of the state estimation error system. Ultimately, numerical simulations are conducted to demonstrate the viability of the developed approach.

title: RGB-Event HyperGraph Prompt for Kilometer Marker Recognition based on Pre-trained Foundation Models
authors: Xiaoyu Xian, Shiao Wang, Xiao Wang, Daxin Tian, Yan Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems
abstract: Metro trains often operate in highly complex environments, characterized by illumination variations, high-speed motion, and adverse weather conditions. These factors pose significant challenges for visual perception systems, especially those relying solely on conventional RGB cameras. To tackle these difficulties, we explore the integration of event cameras into the perception system, leveraging their advantages in low-light conditions, high-speed scenarios, and low power consumption. Specifically, we focus on Kilometer Marker Recognition (KMR), a critical task for autonomous metro localization under GNSS-denied conditions. In this context, we propose a robust baseline method based on a pre-trained RGB OCR foundation model, enhanced through multi-modal adaptation. Furthermore, we construct the first large-scale RGB-Event dataset, EvMetro5K, containing 5,599 pairs of synchronized RGB-Event samples, split into 4,479 training and 1,120 testing samples. Extensive experiments on EvMetro5K and other widely used benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach for KMR. Both the dataset and source code will be released on <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Event-AHU/EvMetro5K_benchmark</uri>

title: Interdisciplinarity in HCI: What Happened in Our Big Tent?
authors: John M. Carroll
