title: Review for "Descriptor: Digital Dataset of Animal Tissues for Macroscopic Pathology in Cancer: Image and Ultrasound Data (DATP-IU)"
authors: Ali Yassine

title: AI-Driven Competency Pattern Mining for Digital Literacy in Adaptive Learning Environments
authors: Yeni Anistyasari, Suparji, Ekohariadi, Shintami C Hidayati, Agus Wiyono, Awwalia Arofatun Nikmah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Digital literacy is a critical competency in higher education, yet its multidimensional nature makes it difficult to assess and model using traditional approaches. This study proposes an AI-driven framework for mining digital literacy competency patterns within adaptive learning environments. Data from 230 undergraduate computer science students were collected through surveys, LMS behavioral logs, and performance records. A deep autoencoder was used for representation learning, followed by clustering algorithms (KMeans, DBSCAN) to identify homogeneous student profiles. Supervised models (Decision Tree, Random Forest) were then applied to predict engagement and performance. Finally, explainable AI techniques (SHAP, LIME) provided interpretable insights. Results revealed three distinct profiles—Technically Skilled but Critical-Weak, Balanced Digital Users, and Critical Creators-that significantly correlated with engagement and academic outcomes. Random Forest achieved the best performance (ROC-AUC $=0.86)$. SHAP analysis highlighted critical literacy and projective literacy as dominant predictors of success. These findings demonstrate how AI-based pattern mining can generate actionable insights for adaptive learning design, bridging computational methods and educational practice.

title: A Multimodal Feature Alignment Approach for Algal Image Recognition and Semantic Explanation
authors: Xigang Wang, Zhenyu Liu, Daming Sun, Xiao Han, Na Chu, Huan Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: This study targets algal microscopy images in hydrological scenarios and addresses three issues: high annotation cost, fine-grained discrimination difficulty, and cross-modal semantic mismatch. We develop a multimodal feature-alignment approach that combines source-domain self-supervised pretraining, visual-description text encoding, and semi-supervised transfer. SimCLR is first used for source-domain pretraining, followed by light supervision to obtain a domain-sensitive image encoder. A dual-agent pipeline (GPT-5 for generation and Gemini-2.5 for auditing and de-redundancy) produces visual descriptions for each class, which are encoded by the CLIP text branch to form semantic representations. During training, the CLIP RN50 vision branch is replaced by the source-adapted encoder and optimized jointly with the CLIP text branch through contrastive learning. At inference, classification and explanation are driven by an image-description similarity vector. For target-domain transfer, the method combines 5% human labels with K-Means pseudo-labels and applies confidence filtering to suppress noise. Experiments on Diatom (32 genera) and an in-house microscopy dataset (33 genera) report mAP@5 of 0.88 and 0.93, significantly exceeding CLIP RN50 zero-shot and linear probe baselines (p<0.01), and consistently producing explanations aligned with genus-level criteria. The approach shows strong applicability and reproducible gains under small-sample professional settings.

title: Influence of Three-Dimensional Dimensions of MO Crystals on MO Crystal Magnetic Field Sensors Under Magnetization Effect
authors: Haiping Luo, Mengjiao Xue, Liqiang Sun, Zhijie Zhao, Yang Zhan, Mengting Su, Xing Fan, Zhehao Pei
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: Magneto-optic crystal magnetic field sensors based on the Faraday effect possess advantages such as passivity, wide bandwidth, and high insulation, exhibiting broad application prospects in magnetic field measurement of power systems. However, the magnetization effect of magneto-optic crystals under external magnetic fields leads to uneven internal magnetic field distribution, and traditional light intensity modulation formulas cannot accurately describe their response characteristics. This study focuses on the influence of magnetization effect on the sensor transfer function, simulating and analyzing the influence of threedimensional dimension changes of magneto-optic crystals on internal magnetic field distribution and sensor sensitivity under magnetization effect. The results show that changes in the threedimensional dimensions of the crystal significantly affect the distribution of internal demagnetizing fields, thereby altering the sensor's sensitivity and linearity. This research provides a theoretical basis for the structural optimization and performance improvement of magneto-optic crystal magnetic field sensors.

title: Local Partial RZF in Cell-Free Massive MIMO: A Deterministic Equivalent Analysis
authors: Jiafei Fu, Peng Jiang, Pengcheng Zhu, Hien Quoc Ngo, Michail Matthaiou, Xiaohu You
abstract: We consider a cell-free massive multiple-input and multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) system, where we derive a deterministic equivalent (DE)-form of the ergodic sum spectral efficiency (SE) based on local partial regularized zero-forcing (LP-RZF) precoding with statistical channel state information (S-CSI) by leveraging large-dimensional random matrix theory. Thanks to this derivation, the previously challenging issue of precoding design based on S-CSI is now resolved, particularly in scenarios where CSI is limited to local information at each access point (AP). Moreover, as the central processing unit (CPU) now only needs to transmit an optimized regularization parameter to the APs, the computational overhead can be reduced, which naturally enhances the system scalability. Driven by these advantages, we then introduce a joint user association, power allocation, and precoding design (i.e., regularization parameter optimization) scheme aimed at maximizing the ergodic sum SE and minimizing the sum power consumption. This is achieved through two optimization problems: one for the ergodic sum SE maximization using weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE)-based processing and another for the sum power consumption minimization employing a block coordinate descent (BCD)-based algorithm. Numerical results demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed PRO-LPRZF scheme.

title: Recommendation Method for Retail Electricity Packages Based on FAHP-2I and FTOPSIS-2I
authors: Xinyi Jiang, YuXuan Zhou, Yuanqian Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power System and Renewable Energy (PSRE)
abstract: In the retail electricity market, the high personalization of user demand and the fuzzy, uncertain nature of evaluation information pose major challenges. Existing methods struggle to capture user preferences and seasonal variations, limiting recommendation accuracy. To address these issues, this paper proposes a retail electricity package recommendation method combining an improved Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process with Type-2 Intervals (FAHP-2I) and an improved Fuzzy Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution with Type-2 Intervals (FTOPSIS-2I). First, a K-means-based model identifies similar user groups, where distances between users and cluster centers determine their decision weights. Second, FAHP-2I derives attribute weights by integrating group preferences. Third, FTOPSIS-2I applies crossseason fuzzy envelopes and complete fuzzy computations to comprehensively rank and recommend candidate packages. A case study using real regional data verifies that the proposed method effectively captures uncertainty and dynamics in user demand, substantially improving recommendation accuracy and providing reliable decision support for personalized electricity services.

title: Modeling and Performance Analysis of Nanocrystalline Magnetic Shielding Considering Weak Magnetic Field
authors: Yuxuan Wang, Xueping Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue that variations in the permeability of nanocrystalline materials under weak magnetic field conditions affect the accuracy of magnetic shielding performance evaluation. The static magnetic characteristics of the nanocrystalline material were measured experimentally, and its permeability in weak magnetic fields was obtained by fitting based on the Rayleigh model. Combined with analytical calculations and simulation analysis of a multilayer magnetic shielding structure, the results indicate that, compared to using a fixed permeability for calculating the shielding factor of the magnetic shielding device, incorporating the permeability of the nanocrystalline material under weak magnetic fields effectively improves the accuracy of the shielding factor calculation, lowering the error from 21.8% to 6.2%. This verifies the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method for evaluating the performance of nanocrystalline magnetic shielding devices, providing an important reference for the design of high-performance magnetic shielding apparatus.

title: Modeling and Suppression of Core Loss in Amorphous Alloy Motor Stator Cores
authors: Shaoze Wang, Xueping Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Communication, Sensing and Electromagnetics (ICSE)
abstract: Due to the property of amorphous alloy materials featuring low loss at high frequency, their application in the stator core of permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) can reduce core loss. However, under high-speed operating conditions, amorphous alloy motors still generate significant losses, causing temperature to rise and having a certain impact on the performance. To accurately calculate stator core loss and suppress the loss, we propose a variable coefficient core loss separation model considering skin effect, temperature, and lamination coefficient, and achieve loss suppression by optimizing the stator structure. Based on the loss model, key structural parameters are screened through sensitivity analysis, and the genetic algorithm is combined to optimize the geometric structure such as stator slot width, slot opening, slot bottom, and air gap length, to achieve the design of minimizing core loss. Finite element analysis and experimental validation are subsequently conducted. The results show that the optimized design reduces core loss by up to 22.4% under no-load conditions, and the maximum error between the theoretical analysis and the experimental results is approximately 8%, verifying the accuracy of the proposed model and the effectiveness of the structural optimization method.

title: Research on High-Precision Tracking of Tobacco Logistics Based on Two-Factor Authentication and AES Encryption
authors: Guanggen Guo, Rui He, Yankun Huang, Yuyang Tang, Bowen Zheng, Lei Lu, Xianwei Niu, Hua Long
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To address the need for enhanced timeliness and accuracy in logistics tracking, this study proposes an integrated high-precision tracking system for tobacco logistics, incorporating two-factor authentication and Advanced Encryption Standard (AES)-256-GCM. The system employs dual-factor verification alongside AES encryption to securely store unique identification serial numbers and reader identifiers in a server database, thereby safeguarding sensitive logistics data. Additionally, laser point cloud technology is introduced to accurately extract target features for tracking purposes. A database management system (DBMS) is utilized for structured data storage, while an XML communication adapter enables seamless connectivity with logistics tracking terminals, enabling real-time data exchange. Experimental comparisons demonstrate that the proposed approach not only effectively prevents unauthorized data access through robust authentication mechanisms but also achieves highprecision tracking, reduces temporal deviations in tracking information, and significantly improves the timeliness of logistics monitoring.

title: Exploiting Temporal Redundancy with Generative Priors: A Transformer-based Framework for Low-Light Image Enhancement
authors: Miaomiao Tian, Shenshen Luan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Low-light image enhancement is crucial for improving the quality of applications such as nighttime photography and security surveillance. The core challenges lie in significant noise amplification due to weak signals, severe detail loss from underexposure, and motion blur caused by handheld shooting, all of which compromise image clarity and realism. Existing multi-frame low-light enhancement methods typically focus on pixel-level fusion, lacking a holistic grasp of the final image’s perceptual quality. Inspired by the GPP-LLIE framework, we are the first to integrate generative perceptual priors from Vision-Language Models (VLMs) with Transformer-based temporal fusion. By employing an adaptive alignment strategy and a novel perceptual prior-guided cross-frame attention mechanism, our method achieves effective information alignment and complementary enhancement across burst frames. The proposed method demonstrates superior performance on the SID dataset, excelling in both quantitative metrics and visual quality.

title: Global Review of Long Duration Energy Storage Market Participation Models: Insights for Enhancing U.S. Market Rules
authors: Venkat Durvasulu, Todd Levin
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Long-Duration Energy Storage (LDES) technologies are essential for a reliable and resilient electric grid, yet current U.S. market frameworks create investment barriers by undercompensating their value. Existing market models often fail to reward the full suite of services from diverse LDES technologies, particularly multi-hour and multi-day reliability. This paper conducts a global review of LDES market participation models, analyzing their merits and shortcomings through international case studies. We diagnose critical design gaps in the U.S. system, such as duration-agnostic capacity accreditation and the lack of resilience-focused products. Based on this review, we propose best practices and a policy roadmap for the U.S. to foster technologyinclusive LDES adoption by better aligning market incentives with system needs.

title: Hierarchical Knowledge Distillation for Cross-Lingual Stance Detection
authors: Qiuli Zhou, Jingyuan Yao, Shengeng Tang, Weidong Chen, Lechao Cheng, Jun Tang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Stance classification, a core Natural Language Processing task, determines the stance (e.g., favor or against) a speaker expresses towards a given entity or claim. It is vital for applications ranging from analyzing social media to conducting market research. These viewpoints are commonly expressed in various languages, which depend on user and platform. However, the majority of stance detection researches to date have primarily focused on monolingual settings, with limited exploration of cross-lingual approaches. To address this challenging issue, we propose a new method of hierarchical knowledge distillation for cross-lingual stance detection, which not only helps the student model generalize its stance detection capability from source languages to other target languages, but also improves its capacity to mimic the teacher model’s classification performance for different stances. Evaluations on diverse multilingual stance detection datasets confirm that our method achieves better performance than existing competitive baselines.

title: Semi-Supervised Remote Sensing Image Change Detection with CT-SWBCE Loss and Reliable Pseudo-Labeling
authors: Yan Yang, Meiqi Jia, Yanning Wang, Jihui Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Remote sensing image change detection (RSICD) is crucial for environmental monitoring, disaster early warning, urban planning, etc. To improve high-resolution (HR) RSICD performance, this paper proposed a semi-supervised method based on reliable pseudo-labeling and CT-SWBCE loss. A mIoU-OA metric is desined to generate high-quality pseudo-labels from unlabeled data. The CT-SWBCE loss is introduced to mitigate class imbalance. On the LEVIR-CD and WHU-CD datasets, our method shows consistent gains, improving IoU by 1.3-8.5% and 3.5-24.5%, and OA by 0.09-0.5% and 0.24-1.11%, respectively.

title: Implementation of Long Short Term Memory Algorithm in Building Wind Speed Prediction Model: Case Study in Banda Aceh
authors: Dedek Ismail, Suriadi Suriadi, Tarmizi Tarmizi, Melinda Melinda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Prediction wind speed plays an important role in the development of generator electricity power wind. Prediction model speed of the wind can determine patterns and designs for efficient wind. This role can be realized when the prediction model speeds up with a level of high accuracy. This study aims to develop and to evaluate predictive models of speed wind with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) algorithm, which builds model prediction speed wind to count the magnitude of speed wind every month. Election LSTM algorithm in building a predictive model speed wind Because very relevant with characteristics data speed wind. The dataset used consists of historical data on the speed of wind-based on heights of 10 m and 50 m in the city of Banda Aceh. Research This uses two dimensions of LSTM layer configuration, function Rectifier Linear Unit (ReLU) activation at each layer dimension, and Adam Optimizer to speed up rate model convergence. The results of this study indicate that data normalization techniques, number of neurons, variation of activation functions, and epochs contribute to the accuracy of model predictions. The findings study describes that the prediction model speed wind at a height of 10 m with the configuration of 64 neurons in the first layer dimension and 32 neurons in the second layer, resulting in the best RMSE value of $\mathbf{0. 7 3 9}$.

title: Gaussian Support Vector Machine Models Based Texture Analysis using Gabor Filter for Concrete Crack Classification
authors: Yessi Jusman, Ahmad Zaki, Agil Priambada, Abi Arga Gumilang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: Concrete cracks are critical indicators of structural deterioration that may compromise the safety and service life of civil infrastructures. Conventional visual inspections are subjective, labor-intensive, and inefficient for large-scale monitoring, creating an urgent need for reliable and automated crack classification methods. This study proposes a machine learning framework that integrates Gabor filter-based texture analysis with Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifiers to improve the accuracy and consistency of crack identification. The preprocessing stage included grayscale conversion, contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE), and image sharpening to enhance texture visibility. A multiorientation Gabor filter was applied to extract spatial frequency and directional information from concrete surface images, producing distinctive texture descriptors. These features were classified using three Gaussian-based SVM models: Fine, Medium, and Coarse. Experiments were conducted on 528 images, categorized into three groups: severe cracks, fine cracks, and non-cracked surfaces. The Medium Gaussian SVM achieved the highest accuracy of $88.77 \% \pm 0.33$, demonstrating strong generalization and low variability. The primary contribution of this study lies in establishing an efficient and stable texture-based classification framework that combines Gabor features and SVM kernels for accurate assessment of crack severity. The proposed approach provides a practical and computationally efficient solution for automated structural health monitoring of concrete infrastructures.

title: Analysis of a Large-scale Flywheel Energy Storage System with a Focus on Power Loss Assessment
authors: Fatemeh Ghanbaridoust, Martin Zschau, Frank Hänel, Frank Will
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Flywheel energy storage systems have received renewed attention due to their advantages, such as high-power density, long cycle life, and environmentally friendly characteristics. Energy is stored in a flywheel as kinetic energy by spinning a rotor at high speeds. In this context, a specific configuration of a single-rotor flywheel system with a capacity of 500 kWh was constructed in Boxberg/O.L., Germany. This demonstrator features a 42-tonne rotor, delivers 500 kW of power, and operates at a maximum speed of $3000 \mathrm{~min}^{-1}$. This study aims to evaluate the system’s performance by analyzing its components and inherent losses, which are calculated using analytical methods. The demonstrator, along with measurement data, serves as the basis for validating the mathematical model. The results highlight the potential and the challenges of largescale flywheels, indicating that a vacuum system, effective sealing, and appropriate bearings can reduce the power losses of 370.5 kW at $1700 \mathrm{~min}^{-1}$ by about $71 \%$.

title: TLA2eBPF: Real-Time Protection for Kernel Memory Out-of-Bounds Security
authors: Zi Wang, Yuqing Lan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Memory out-of-bounds (OOB) vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel threaten system integrity by enabling privilege escalation, information leakage, and arbitrary code execution. Formal verification techniques such as TLA+ can mathematically guarantee memory-safety invariants but cannot enforce runtime monitoring, whereas eBPF provides real-time kernel instrumentation but relies on manually crafted heuristic rules. This work presents TLA2eBPF, the first semantics-preserving framework that automatically converts TLA+ security specifications into executable eBPF monitoring code. We design (1) bidirectional semantic-mapping rules between TLA+ state machines and eBPF runtime states; (2) a TLAPS-based bisimulation proof system ensuring behavioral equivalence; and (3) an optimized eBPF runtime including cache acceleration, batched event delivery, and probabilistic sampling. Experiments on real CVEs and Syzkaller cases demonstrate 100% detection coverage, 0% false positives, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<3.5 \%$</tex> runtime overhead, achieving both formal completeness and practical real-time protection.

title: A Probabilistic Fusion Method for Channel Estimation and Signal Detection over Rayleigh Fading Channels
authors: Jie Gu, Dazhuan Xu, Huan Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing and Communication Technology (SPCT)
abstract: This paper investigates the Rayleigh fading channel and proposes a probability-fusion-based approach for joint channel estimation and signal detection. A novel Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) system model is established, which operates without requiring explicit channel state information (CSI). Under the assumption of Gaussian input sources, we derive the upper bound expression of the corresponding channel capacity. Comparisons with the channel capacity under perfect CSI, the achievable rate of BICM, and the conventional MMSE method demonstrate that the proposed PFISAC approach achieves a distinct improvement in Gaussian capacity. The results provide new theoretical support for the design, modeling as well as performance optimization of ISAC systems over Rayleigh fading channels.

title: MAW-FEDformer: Meteorological-Aware Weighted Fedformer for Cooling Load Forecasting in Integrated Energy Stations
authors: Tianle Chen, Haifang Hu, Yang Luo, Yang Zhou, Mingxuan Zou, Wenjun Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Accurate cooling load forecasting is essential for optimizing energy scheduling and carbon reduction in Integrated Energy Systems (IES). However, the cooling load exhibits complex nonlinear dynamics influenced by fluctuating meteorological conditions, which limits the performance of conventional statistical and deep learning models. To address this issue, this paper proposes a Meteorological-Aware Weighted FEDformer (MAW-FEDformer) for hourly cooling load forecasting. The model introduces a Meteorological-Aware Weighting (MAW) module that dynamically learns the time-varying influence of meteorological factors through a time-conditioned attention and gating mechanism. By integrating this module with the frequencyenhanced decomposition structure of FEDformer, the model captures both intrinsic temporal-frequency dependencies and external weather impacts. Experimental results on real-world data from a comprehensive cooling station in Hangzhou, China, demonstrate that MAW-FEDformer achieves the lowest prediction errors among state-of-the-art models, reducing MSE and MAE by 17.9 % and 14.7 %, respectively, compared with FEDformer. These findings highlight the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed approach for meteorology-sensitive cooling load prediction and its potential in supporting energyefficient operation of Integrated Energy Systems.

title: Automating Brunei’s Labour-Census Cleaning with Excel Power Query and VBA
authors: Lee Zi Hao, Arif Bramantoro, Aliana Abdullah
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Brunei Darussalam’s annual labour-force census has historically required months of manual cleaning across ~150,000 records and 60 fields. We developed an Excel-native automation that combines Power Query for deterministic transformations and reference-table merges (e.g., BDSOC occupation codes, ISO country codes) with targeted VBA procedures for cross-field validation and orchestration. The workflow first standardises key categorical fields and code formats, then applies rule-based checks on age, employment type, work-pass expiry, salary ranges by BDSOC group, identity-document consistency, qualification plausibility, and commencement-date validity. Business Activity entries are preprocessed and split from semicolon lists to single-code rows to enable precise lookups. Each rule emits an explicit flag column to support focused human review, and a connected Excel dashboard presents refreshed summaries on each run. Evaluated on the 2023 census, the pipeline processed the full dataset in $\approx 5$ minutes, replacing months of manual effort while improving consistency, traceability, and staff productivity. Because it reuses familiar spreadsheet tooling and requires no new licences, the approach is readily adoptable and provides a replicable model for data-rich public-sector settings. We discuss integration with stricter front-end form validation and optional off-loading of heavier joins or anomaly detection to a database or Python/R for future scalability.

title: Optimizing Pour-Over Coffee Extraction with Deep Reinforcement Learning: A Proximal Policy Optimization Approach with Curriculum Learning
authors: Zhahirah Jeffery, Arif Bramantoro, Nor Zainah Siau, Siti Noorfatimah
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Optimizing complex, multi-variable processes in real-time is challenging, especially when manual tuning or fixed rules cannot adapt to changing conditions. This study explores the use of Proximal Policy Optimisation (PPO), a deep reinforcement learning algorithm, to optimise key brewing parameters in pour-over coffee extraction. The task is framed as a structured case study in process optimisation. A shaped reward function was designed to give continuous feedback based on how closely the agent’s outputs match roast-specific brewing targets. This supports smoother learning relative to sparse/binary rewards. The agent observes roast level, brew ratio, brew time, and water temperature, and makes continuous adjustments to the latter three. To improve training, Curriculum Learning (CL) was applied in three phases: light roast only, light and medium roasts, and all roasts. These conditions were compared with a PPO baseline trained without CL. Results show that the shaped reward improved performance and training stability, while CL accelerated learning and improved generalisation. The final agent consistently produced high-reward outputs and roast-compliant brews. This research highlights how reinforcement learning can optimize dynamic, real-world processes, with potential applications beyond coffee.

title: Research on Autonomous UAV Power Inspection Method Based on Visual SLAM and Multi-View 3D Reconstruction
authors: Meiyan Li, Shiling Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: Current UAV power inspection systems face significant bottlenecks, including low automation, GPS dependency, and a lack of quantitative analysis capabilities. This paper presents an integrated solution combining visual SLAM, autonomous navigation, multi-view 3D reconstruction, and 3D defect measurement to achieve full-process automation. Our method features a robust visual SLAM strategy with hybrid pointline feature fusion for centimeter-level localization in GPS-denied environments, an intelligent path planning strategy with automatic orbiting trajectories for optimal data acquisition, and a quantitative defect measurement framework operating directly on 3D models. Experimental results demonstrate that our system achieves 0.052 m localization accuracy in GPS-denied conditions, a 96.5% model completeness score, and measurement errors below 2.0% for both damage area and conductor sag quantification. This work provides a comprehensive technical solution for autonomous power inspection, enabling the transition from manual operation to intelligent, data-driven grid maintenance.

title: Overview of Control and Protection Strategies for Three-Terminal DC Converter Valve Cooling Systems
authors: Song Xu, Minghao Pan, Qiang Zhang, Shi Shan, Liyun Zha, Weiyu Lu
publish: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
abstract: This paper introduces the main components of the cooling system for three-terminal DC project converters, the primary logic structure of the control and protection system, as well as the control and protection strategies.

title: Graph Neural Network-based Prediction of Three-Dimensional Stress Fields of Solid Propellant Grains under Multiple Operating Conditions
authors: Zuo Chuanzhen, Li Liangliang, Lv Zhigang, Wang Peng, Duan Leiguang, Feng Hanxiao
publish: 2025 International Symposium on AI-Driven Image Processing and Computer Vision Technology (AIPCVT)
abstract: To reduce the high computational cost of threedimensional stress-field prediction for solid rocket motor propellant grains under solidification cooling, long-term storage, ignition pressure, and acceleration, this paper presents an end-toend surrogate based on a U-shaped graph neural network (GNN) with offline hierarchical pooling and adaptive feature fusion. A parameterized finite element (FE) model is built from geometric and operating parameters, and an optimized Latin hypercube design is used to generate geometry-load cases for Abaqus simulations, producing steady-state stress fields on a unified mesh. The resulting data are encoded as graphs, and the proposed network integrates offline pooling (KeepPool), gated adaptive fusion (GAF), and multi-condition multi-head regression to predict nodal full-field stresses. Unlike TopKPooling-based GNNs, KeepPool precomputes fixed pooling indices on the unified mesh, enabling index-consistent downsampling and scatter-based unpooling across samples, while GAF improves recovery of stress concentrations. The model achieves Avg. $\mathrm{R}^{2}$ of $0.950,0.977,0.995$, and 0.993 for ignition, curing-cooling, long-term storage, and acceleration, respectively, with high-stress-region PAE of $\mathbf{6 5. 9 8 \%}-\mathbf{7 5. 9 6 \%}$. Compared with full FE analysis, inference is accelerated by tens to hundreds of times across the four conditions, enabling rapid integrity screening and preliminary optimization.

title: Research on Decoupling Middleware Services and Peak Shaving of Traffic Based on AMQP
authors: Wensen Qiu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: With the rise of distributed systems and the growing adoption of the microservices architecture,message-oriented middleware is capable of providing efficient and reliable message delivery,and has become an indispensable component in distributed systems. By leveraging message-oriented middleware,applications or distributed components can engage in reliable asynchronous communication, thereby reducing intersystem coupling.thereby enhancing the overall scalability and availability of the system. Therefore,message-oriented middleware is also recognized as a crucial technology in system development. Leveraging this technology,it enables key characteristics such as functional decoupling, asynchronous send/receive, and traffic peak shaving,ultimately delivering message communication capabilities on standard server, storage, and network hardware platforms,achieving the goals of high reliability, high availability, and eventual consistency.

title: Robust Bayesian Diffusion LMS with Adaptive Clustering Over Multitask Networks
authors: Jiahang Zhou, Fuyi Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: To mitigate the impact of sudden disturbances such as impulsive noise on the performance of multitask networks, this paper proposes a Robust Bayesian Diffusion Least Mean Square Adaptive Clustering(R-B-DLMS-AC) algorithm. This algorithm derives variable step sizes through Bayesian learning and enhances robustness by using the squared norm constraint of the weight vector difference. The algorithm also introduces a clustering strategy-based approach, enabling the nodes in the network to find cooperative partners based on the similarity of tasks. Simulation results show that the R-B-DLMS-AC algorithm outperforms the comparison algorithms in multitask learning scenarios.

title: An Optimized Hybrid FFT Framework for the Sunway SW26010-Pro Many-Core Processor
authors: Zechuan Zhang, Junmin Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Computer Science, Electronic Information Engineering and Intelligent Control Technology (CEI)
abstract: The Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) possesses unique mathematical properties, enabling the conversion of sequences between the time and frequency domains through discrete sampling. It has found extensive applications in fields such as digital signal processing, physics, and mathematics-for example, in signal analysis and convolution operations. Based on the architectural characteristics of the SW26010PRO heterogeneous manycore processor developed for the domestic Sunway OceanLight supercomputer, this paper proposes an efficient many-core parallel approach. By integrating the Cooley-Tukey and Stockham algorithms, we present a novel FFT computation framework tailored for many-core architectures. Furthermore, we introduce a inter-core data exchange paradigm specifically designed for this new FFT framework and optimized for many-core architectures. By integrating the aforementioned optimization techniques, our implementation achieves an average speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60.9 \times$</tex> over FFTW running on a single host core, when executed on a core group consisting of one master core and 64 slave cores.

title: A Comparison of Deep Learning Algorithms for APT Malware Detection
authors: Alifya Kayla Shafa Susanto, Ilham Achmad Fiqrin, Ravi Kumar Patchmuthu, Sharul Tazrajiman Bin Haji Tajuddin, Mardin Syah Putra Zai
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Applied Computational Intelligence in Information Systems (ACIIS)
abstract: Due to its ability to remain hidden in a system for an extended period while gathering sensitive information, Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) is one of the most dangerous types of cyberattacks. This study aims to compare several deep learning (DL) models, including CNN-1D, CNN-2D, ANN, DNN, RNN, LSTM, and Bi-LSTM, for the detection of APT malware using the DAPT2020 and Unraveled datasets, and their performance is evaluated with standard classification metrics. The goal of this research is to identify the most effective model for detecting APT malware. Accuracy, recall, F1-score, and precision are used to evaluate the model. The CNN-1D model achieved the highest accuracy, with scores of $98.64 \%, 96.57 \%$, $98.95 \%$, and $97.74 \%$ for accuracy, recall, F1-score, and precision, respectively, on DAPT2020, while LSTM achieved the highest on the Unraveled dataset with scores of 99.95%, 97.91%, $98.82 \%$, and 98.36%.

title: DPA-Net: Enhancing Fluorescence Microscopy Object Detection via Density-Guided Spatial Attention and Perceptually-Aware Diffusion
authors: Chunyan Zhao, Hengyi Zeng, Jie Li, Lei Wei, Xiuxian Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image, Signal Processing and Communication Technology (ISPCT)
abstract: Automated object detection in fluorescence microscopy presents fundamental challenges stemming from inherent imaging constraints, annotation scarcity, and significant inter-laboratory domain variations. These challenges severely compromise the generalization capabilities of conventional detection architectures. To address these limitations, we propose DPA-Net, a novel framework that synergistically integrates domain-adaptive generative synthesis with a biologically informed spatial attention mechanism. Our approach makes three key contributions: (1) a perceptually-aware two-stage diffusion pipeline that generates structurally coherent objects followed by Retinex-based illumination diversification, producing high-fidelity training corpora from limited annotated data; (2) a Density Perception Attention (DPA) module that explicitly models the inherent spatial clustering properties of biological specimens to guide feature learning through an auxiliary density regression task; and (3) a comprehensive theoretical analysis demonstrating why density priors are particularly effective for clustered object detection. Extensive evaluations on both proprietary and public benchmark datasets demonstrate that DPA-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming existing methods by 1.9% in mAP@0.5 on our primary fluorescence microscopy task. Our code will be made publicly available.

title: Student’s attitudes toward artificial intelligence and its impact on academic procrastination: A selfefficacy mediation test
authors: Pamela Hendra Heng, Regine Caitlyn Suwu, Nivia, Reni Juniarsa Pranata, Nikole Edmonda Yapputro, Henny Triyani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering and Sciences (ICAIDES)
abstract: This study explores how students’ attitudes toward Artificial Intelligence (AI) influence academic procrastination, highlighting the important role of self-efficacy as a mediator in this relationship. AI is increasingly integrated into education, providing benefits in personalizing learning and improving efficiency, but it also has the potential to increase student dependence and reduce thinking skills. Self-efficacy plays a crucial role in shaping students’ academic behavior patterns, including their tendency to postpone tasks. This study uses a non-experimental quantitative design with a survey method through questionnaires administered to 500 junior high school students at School X and School Y. The variables in this study were measured using the Students’ Attitudes Toward Artificial Intelligence (SATAI), Academic Procrastination Inventory (API), and Self-Efficacy Scale (SEC). Data analysis techniques included descriptive analysis, correlation tests, and mediation tests using the Baron and Kenny approach. The results of this study are expected to provide insight into how students’ attitudes toward AI contribute to academic procrastination, with self-efficacy as a factor that has the potential to reduce the negative impact of attitudes toward AI.

title: Analysis of Fire Safety Sensors and Controls with Design Scenarios for a Reliable Residential and Commercial Vehicle to Grid Integrated Energy Storage Systems
authors: Anuradha Munshi
publish: 2026 IEEE Electrical Energy Storage Applications and Technologies Conference (EESAT)
abstract: Recent safety concern for EV batteries raise concern for reliable Vehicle to Grid distributed energy system. This paper analyzes sensors monitoring temperature change, controls and components of batteries to detect safety issues sending alert before battery malfunction. Features like AutoShutOff while battery device is charging or discharging for peak grid demand impact integrated energy storage dependability to implement better V2G technologies where EV battery sensor detects safety risks early to disconnect from grid to eliminate fire danger. Further propose design scenarios of stable V2G integrated energy storage system to evaluate the performance for reliable residential or commercial distributed energy system. The objective is to compare design scenarios of large scale or microgrid with V2G integrated energy storage system with better monitoring sensors and controls tracking history of thermal, component and environmental factors to minimize impact of safety issues over lifecycle.

title: Development of A Secure API Authentication Mechanism with JWT and Data Encryption
authors: Krisna Widatama, Putry Wahyu Setyaningsih
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Information System and Information Technology (ICISIT)
abstract: Currently, the use of Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) has become essential and widely adopted in both web and mobile applications to support data integration and service interoperability. However, the growing reliance on APIs also introduces significant security risks, particularly in terms of authentication and data confidentiality. This research focuses on developing a secure API authentication mechanism that combines the JSON Web Token (JWT) with the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES-256) for payload encryption. JWT is widely used in modern applications as an authentication method, but it remains vulnerable to token hijacking and data exposure when the payload is not encrypted. In this study, a prototype web application was developed to implement and evaluate the proposed authentication method. Several test scenarios were conducted, including valid login, token modification, token expiration, and sniffing attacks. The results demonstrate that integrating AES-256 encryption within JWT significantly enhances data confidentiality and resilience against token-based attacks, while maintaining acceptable system performance. This approach provides a practical and more secure solution for API communication in sensitive domains such as healthcare and finance.

title: Stability of Electric Drives Optimized by Nonlinearity
authors: Ivan Biliuk, Dmytro Shareyko, Oleg Savchenko, Dmytro Koshkin, Oleksandr Maiboroda, Andrii Rudenko
publish: 2024 IEEE 6th International Conference on Modern Electrical and Energy System (MEES)
abstract: This paper investigates the electric drive with a nonlinear correction device optimization with a dynamic change of link parameters. The time constants dependences of thyristor converter and executive motor in this case were calculated. The stability conditions of the built automatic control system and conditions for the occurrence of self-oscillations are calculated. On the basis of conducted studies, the possibility of such optimization has been proven.
